a shining beacon (iomedae).name: A Shining Beacon (Iomedae)
a shining beacon (iomedae).description:
You carry a burning hatred in your heart for all things demonic, and have studied their weaknesses carefully.

Requirements: Iomedae

Benefits: You deal additional damage equal to your weapon's critical hit modifier when you score a successful critical hit with a weapon on a demon. This damage is added only after the damage is totaled, and is not multiplied by the critical hit multiple.

contextNotes.text: On critical hits vs demons, add damage equal to your weapon's critical hit modifier. This damage is not multiplied by the critical hit multiple.

touched by divinity (wrath of the wright.name: Touched by Divinity (Wrath of the Wrighteous)
touched by divinity (wrath of the wright.description:
As long as you can remember, you’ve had an unexplainable interest in one deity in particular. One of your parents may have been a priest of this deity, or you may have been an orphan raised by the church, but these alone cannot explain your deep connection to the faith. You’ve always felt calm and at ease in places holy to the deity, and often have dreams about the god or goddess visiting you—most often in the form of a sacred animal or creature. Your faith is strong, even if you don’t happen to be a divine spellcaster—if you are a divine spellcaster, you should be a worshiper of this deity.

Benefits: You begin play with a silver holy symbol of your chosen deity for free. In addition, choose one domain associated with your chosen deity. You gain the use of that domain’s 1st-level domain spell as a spell-like ability usable once per day (CL equals your character level).

Associated Mythic Path

Hierophant

Multiple Characters

If other characters choose this trait, you should all work together to decide what deity you’re associated with—it should be the same deity shared by all of you. You might even share the same dreams.

bestial wrath (rovagug).name: Bestial Wrath (Rovagug)
bestial wrath (rovagug).description:
When you strike hard, you strike deep.

Requirements: Rovagug

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on critical confirmation rolls.

shadow child (council of thieves).name: Shadow Child (Council of Thieves)
shadow child (council of thieves).description:
Westcrown has long suffered under a peculiar curse, a blight that rises every night, bringing with it fell beasts that hunt the shadows. No one can say from whence these night-horrors come—though some blame the mysterious wizards of Nidal, other claim that it’s some curse laid by fallen Aroden, while still others suggest some shadowy mastermind manipulates an ebon brood from the depths of Westcrown’s northern ruins. Whatever the case, the people of Westcrown have long feared the night, but not you. You’re goal is to reclaim the darkness from the beasts that hide within. You’ve acclimated yourself to the dark, and thus act with greater precision in the shadow than most.

Benefits: When attacking targets in areas of dim light, you do not suffer the standard 20% miss chance on attack rolls for being in the poorly lit area.

contextNotes.text: When attacking targets in areas of dim light, you do not suffer the standard 20% miss chance on attack rolls for being in the poorly lit area.

alchemical prodigy (qadira).name: Alchemical Prodigy (Qadira)
alchemical prodigy (qadira).description:
In your magical studies, you have learned to substitute the careful application of alchemical principles for spell knowledge.

Requirements: Qadira

Benefits: Choose one 1st-level spell from the bard, cleric, druid, or sorcerer/wizard spell list. If you have the Brew Potion feat, you may create potions of this spell as if it were on your spell list.

sharkchild (skinwalker; wereshark-kin).name: Sharkchild (Skinwalker; Wereshark-Kin)
sharkchild (skinwalker; wereshark-kin).description:
In your youth, you swam into the ocean as deep as you could, and had it not been for your wereshark ancestry you would have surely drowned.

Requirements: Wereshark-Kin Skinwalker Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Swim checks. As long as you have a swim speed (such as that granted by your bestial form), you can use the run action to move up to five times your swim speed while swimming straight down from or straight up toward the surface of the water.

contextNotes.text: As long as you have a swim speed (such as that granted by your bestial form), you can use the run action to move up to five times your swim speed while swimming straight down from or straight up toward the surface of the water.

loyalty across lifetimes.name: Loyalty across Lifetimes
loyalty across lifetimes.description:
You and your eidolon share a link that seems to span multiple lifetimes.

Benefits: Your eidolon treats its Constitution score as if it were 2 points higher for the purposes of determining when its negative hit point total sends it back to its home plane. In addition, your eidolon gains a +1 trait bonus on Will saves against enchantment spells and effects.

Usage: You can add a new ability feature to your eidolon and create a context note for this trait. Choose Constitution Checks and Will saves in the target of the context notes.

fatal trapper (zyphus).name: Fatal Trapper (Zyphus)
fatal trapper (zyphus).description:
Your patron grants you expertise at creating or disabling traps.

Requirements: Zyphus

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Craft (traps) checks and a +1 trait bonus on Disable Device checks made to disable traps. 

Usage: Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add Craft (traps).

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to disable traps.

highlander (hills or mountains).name: Highlander (Hills or Mountains)
highlander (hills or mountains).description:
You were born and raised in rugged badlands or hills, and you've become something of an expert at evading the predators, monsters, and worse that haunt the highlands.

Requirements: Hills or Mountains

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Stealth checks, and Stealth is always a class skill for you. This trait bonus increases to +2 in hilly or rocky areas.

contextNotes.text: Increase this trait's normal +[[1]] Trait bonus  to a +[[2]] Trait bonus   in hilly or rocky areas.

chip on the shoulder.name: Chip on the Shoulder
chip on the shoulder.description:
When someone pushes you, you push back.

Benefits: Whenever a creature fails an Intimidate check against you, you gain a +2 trait bonus on your next Intimidate check against that creature.

Intimidate becomes a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on your first check against creatures a creature fails an Intimidate check against you.

stubborn (vampire; vetala).name: Stubborn (Vampire; Vetala)
stubborn (vampire; vetala).description:
The angry child inside bolsters your will, and you often prove utterly defiant.

Requirements: Vetala Vampire Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against attempts to control you, such as command undead, halt undead, or channeled energy to turn or control undead.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against attempts to control you.

questing hellknight (lawful).name: Questing Hellknight (Lawful)
questing hellknight (lawful).description:
You are a quick study when it comes to local laws.

Requirements: Lawful alignment

Benefits: Once per day, you can attempt a Knowledge (local) check untrained to recall information about local laws, rulers, and popular locations. Additionally, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (local) checks, and Knowledge (local) is a class skill for you. You must be of a lawful alignment to take this trait.

the pathfinder's exile (council of thiev.name: The Pathfinder's Exile (Council of Thieves)
the pathfinder's exile (council of thiev.description:
Westcrown's dilapidated Pathfinder lodge of Delvehaven has long excited your imagination. Forcibly closed by the order of the city's diabolical rulers, the Pathfinders of Westcrown were exiled, forcing them to leave behind untold knowledge and the treasures of countless expeditions. Today, Delvehaven lies under the pale of fearful rumors and dark magic, and bureaucratic red tape has prevented trespass on the lodge's well-protected grounds. Having secretly and illegally contacted agents of the Pathfinders, you've expressed your interest in aiding their ventures in Cheliax - with a particular eye toward investigating Delvehaven, the source of your long-standing adventuresome interests. To your surprise, some days ago you received a message back from an unnamed Pathfinder operating underground in the country.

Benefits: Along with encouraging you to investigate the lodge and report your findings to the Grand Lodge in Absalom, he's sent along a battered and tarnished, yet still functional @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-wondrous.Item.SyKirMypun1K59Tx]{wayfinder}. You've promised yourself to pay back your unknown contact the 500 gp cost of the item someday, but until then, it's yours to use. A wayfinder is a magical compass that grants you a +2 circumstance bonus on Survival checks to avoid becoming lost, and can be commanded to emit @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.ip9onjfp6nf1vlv9]{Light}as the spell (CL 5th) as a standard action. Further details on the Pathfinder Society and wayfinders can be found in the Pathfinder Campaign Setting or Pathfinder Chronicles: Seekers of Secrets.

blackthorn rancher (lambreth).name: Blackthorn Rancher (Lambreth)
blackthorn rancher (lambreth).description:
Domestic animals from Lambreth are known for their resiliency.

Requirements: Lambreth

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Handle Animal checks and Handle Animal is a class skill for you. Your animal companion or bonded mount gains a permanent +3 trait bonus to its maximum hit point total.

skymetal affinity (numeria).name: Skymetal Affinity (Numeria)
skymetal affinity (numeria).description:
Requirements: Numeria

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Fortitude saves against the negative effects of skymetals, as well as a +2 trait bonus on Craft checks made to create weapons or armor out of skymetals.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against the negative effects of skymetals.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to create weapons or armor out of skymetals.

ambush training (pathfinder society).name: Ambush Training (Pathfinder Society)
ambush training (pathfinder society).description:
You’ve learned that taking an enemy by surprise can end a combat before it begins.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on initiative checks and a +1 trait bonus on weapon damage rolls during any surprise round in which you act.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on weapon damage rolls during any surprise round in which you act.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on weapon damage rolls during any surprise round in which you act.

giantslayer scion (giantslayer).name: Giantslayer Scion (Giantslayer)
giantslayer scion (giantslayer).description:
You grew up listening to tales of a famed ancestor’s mighty battles against giant foes. Just the mention of your ancestor’s name is enough to strike fear into the heart of the most stalwart giant warrior, and you’ve resolved to follow in your forebear’s footsteps.

Benefits: You take no penalty on Intimidate checks against creatures with the giant subtype that are larger than you, and you gain a +1 trait bonus to the DC of any fear-based effect you use against a creature with the giant subtype.

contextNotes.text: You take no penalty against creatures with the giant subtype that are larger than you, and you gain a +1 trait bonus to the DC of any fear-based effect you use against a creature with the giant subtype.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to the DC of any fear-based effect you use against a creature with the giant subtype.

demoralizing presence (zon-kuthon).name: Demoralizing Presence (Zon-Kuthon)
demoralizing presence (zon-kuthon).description:
Your very existence is unsettling to your foes.

Requirements: Zon-Kuthon
Benefits

You gain a +2 trait bonus on Intimidate checks made to demoralize opponents.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to demoralize foes

betrayed (curse of the crimson throne).name: Betrayed (Curse of the Crimson Throne)
betrayed (curse of the crimson throne).description:
You were hardly a model citizen as a child or young adult. Your reasons for turning to a life of crime may be varied, but what matters is that you eventually fell in with a certain well-connected and notorious crime lord named Gaedren Lamm. His reputation as a snake and a treacherous scoundrel was known to you, but for reasons of your own, you chose not to turn him down when he offered you a chance to work for him. You may have assumed you were an exception, or that you’d be able to handle him, or perhaps even planned to betray him. As it worked out, though, Gaedren got the upper hand and took you down you first. You may have served time in jail, may have been beaten by his thugs and left for dead, or could simply have had your profits stolen out from under you. Whatever the cause, Gaedren wronged you, and you are eager for the chance to get revenge.

Choose one of the following benefits:

Hungry for Revenge

You’ve never forgiven Gaedren for his betrayal, and have vowed to make him pay for what he did. Whether that’s seeing him rot in jail or a shallow grave, you hope to taste vengeance someday. Whenever you deal damage with a melee weapon on a creature that is flat-footed, you gain a +1 trait bonus on the damage roll.

Reformed Criminal

You’ve given up the life of crime, and managed to talk your way out of any repercussions such as jail time or fines. You’ve told yourself that you would rather leave your past behind, yet the concept of seeing Gaedren Lamm pay for his crimes still appeals to you. You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks, and Diplomacy is a class skill for you.

Usage

  1. Select Hungry for Revenge, or Reformed Criminal when you take this trait.

  2. if you select Hungry for Revenge: Update the target of the untargeted Context note on the changes tab of this trait to reflect Attacks> Melee Weapon

  3. if you select Reformed Criminal:

    a) Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect Diplomacy; and

    b) Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect Diplomacy.

  4. Delete any unused change or note

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on the damage rolls whenever you deal damage with a melee weapon to a creature that is flat-footed. (Hungry for Revenge)

the rider.name: The Rider
the rider.description:
You are born under the auspice of the mounted caravan guard, whose very presence deters danger.

Requirements: 14 Desnus - 20 Sarenith

Benefits: Once per day, when you succeed at a Ride check, you can attempt an immediate Intimidate check against the nearest foe. If you fail this check, the DC of your next Intimidate check against that opponent does not increase (as it normally would).

outlander - missionary (any campaign).name: Outlander - Missionary (Any Campaign)
outlander - missionary (any campaign).description:
You are not from town; you’ve recently come from somewhere else and are hoping to make your fortune here. Pick one of the following reasons to be a newcomer to the area.

Benefits: Pick one of the following reasons to be a newcomer to the area:

Missionary

You have come to town to see about expanding the presence of your chosen faith after receiving visions that told you your faith is needed in the region—what that need is, though, you’re not quite sure.

You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (religion) checks, and Knowledge (religion) is a class skill for you.

If you cast divine spells, pick three spells on your spell list. You are particularly adept at casting these spells, so they function at +1 caster level when you cast them, and their save DCs (if any) gain a +1 bonus.

Usage: If you cast divine spells, you must pick 3 from your spell list when you select this trait.

You must edit these three spells independently once you have added them to your actor.

Special Note

This is one of three versions of this trait. Other versions can be found here:

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.KVrNf7eTgJbtA8gl]{Outlander - Lore Seeker (Any Campaign)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.HSzL4QzbRr2BtTjJ]{Outlander - Exile (Any Campaign)}

arcane malignancies (changeling).name: Arcane Malignancies (Changeling)
arcane malignancies (changeling).description:
As changelings sense the awakening of the eerie powers they inherited from their hag progenitors, they are often mortified to discover terrifying and debilitating alterations to their physique. While the call seizes the changelings’ minds around the time their bodies begin maturing, the arcane malignancies that mar their features and extremities have nothing to do with puberty.

Not every changeling develops a malignancy, and when a changeling does develop a malignancy, it is often unrelated to whatever traits her hag mother has. These transformations are more akin to a magical allergy, manifesting in response to the well of arcane energy bubbling away in the changeling’s core.

Effects: A changeling character can take an arcane malignancy as a drawback in order to select an additional trait at character creation. Randomly determine the arcane malignancy by rolling a d% and consulting the table below.

d%

Effect

1–2

The smallest three toes on each of your feet have fused together. You take a –2 penalty on Acrobatics, Climb, and Perform (dance) checks.

3–4

Your incisors grow so long and sharp that you have difficulty eating. Eating takes twice as long. Consuming a potion or other magical foodstuff requires a full-round action.

5–7

You have an extra digit on each hand, which hampers your fine motor skills. You take a –2 penalty on Craft, Disable Device, and Sleight of Hand checks.

8–10

Your limbs and torso are covered with piscine scales that you must constantly moisten. If more than 1 day passes without you applying at least a gallon of water to them, you take 1d3 points of damage each hour until you do so.

11–13

Your skin bears a sizable scarlet mark that flushes and lightens with your moods. You take a –2 penalty on Bluff and Disguise checks.

14–16

One of your eyes has grown enlarged and developed a pale, milky film. Daylight seems uncomfortably bright to you. You take a –2 penalty on saving throws against light-based spells and effects, and you are automatically dazzled for 1d6 rounds when exposed to bright light or sunlight.

17–19

The soles of your feet are flat, and you tire quickly. You can hustle for only a half hour before becoming fatigued, and every 4 hours spent traveling by foot in a day deals 1 point of Constitution damage.

20–22

Your already pallid complexion grows unusually sensitive to the rays of the sun. Exposure to direct sunlight for more than 1 hour at a time makes you sickened for 24 hours.

23–25

The tip of your tongue is forked and stiff. You have difficulty making yourself understood. You take a –2 penalty on Bluff, Diplomacy, and Disguise checks to fool or persuade others.

26–28

The painful and stiff swelling of your joints puts you at a disadvantage in combat. You take a –2 penalty on combat maneuver checks and Escape Artist checks to free yourself when grappled or pinned.

29–31

Your ears have stretched to an abnormal size, making you overly sensitive to loud noises. Whenever you are dealt sonic damage, you take 1 additional point of damage, and you are deafened for 1 minute whenever you take damage from any sonic effect.

32–33

The pupils of your eyes resemble a cat’s, and beasts cannot abide your presence. You take a –2 penalty on Handle Animal and Ride checks.

34–35

You grow velvety-soft fur along your back, arms, and legs that chafes and catches under clothing and armor, increasing the armor check penalty of any armor by 1 and applying a –1 armor check penalty to ordinary clothing. Clothing and armor can be specially fitted for you, eliminating the increased penalty but increasing their price by 50 gp.

36–37

You grow an extra row of sharp, crooked teeth. The uneven set of your jaws gives you constant headaches. You take a –2 penalty on concentration checks and saving throws against pain effects.

38–40

It is nearly impossible to find armor that fits your concave chest and unusually narrow, sloping shoulders. The armor check penalty of any armor you wear increases by 1, and you take a –1 penalty to AC when wearing medium or heavy armor.

41–42

Your throat balloons out like a frog’s, and your voice comes out as an odd croak. You take a –2 penalty on Diplomacy checks and on Perform (act, oratory, and sing) checks.

43–45

An oozing sore has developed on your face that refuses to heal and makes you more susceptible to illness. You take a –2 penalty on saving throws against disease.

46–48

Your flesh grows bark, and your major joints have become gnarled and knotted like tree limbs, causing you to move slowly and stiffly. You take a –2 penalty on Reflex saves.

49–51

Your thin, almost translucent skin is delicate and tears easily. You take 1 point of bleed damage from slashing attacks that deal at least 1 point of damage to you.

52–54

A massive patch of multicolored warts covers much of your face. People cannot help but stare, and they always remember you once they’ve seen you. You take a –4 penalty on Disguise checks.

55–57

Your unruly hair grows with alarming speed. If you do not spend 1 hour trimming and grooming your hair every 48 hours, it snarls in nearby foliage whenever you are outdoors, imposing a –2 penalty to your Armor Class and on attack rolls and Stealth checks.

58–60

Your limbs are thin and frail. You take a –2 penalty to CMD against disarm, grapple, and trip attempts.

61–63

Your bones shatter as easily as glass. You take 2 additional points of damage each time you are hit by an attack made with a bludgeoning weapon.

64–65

One arm is turning to stone, though you retain limited mobility. You take a –4 penalty on all skill checks requiring the use of two hands and on attack rolls when wielding a two-handed weapon or fighting with two weapons.

66–67

You develop an allergy to one spell per spell level from the witch spell list (determined by your GM). Casting or being the target of a spell you’re allergic to causes you to break out in a painful rash. This rash deals 1d4 points of damage per spell level,and imposes a penalty on your Charisma equal to the level of the spell. The rash fades after a number of hours equal to the level of the spell.

68–69

The joints in your unnaturally long thumbs have fused, and you often fumble objects. When you retrieve a stored item, you have a 10% chance of dropping it.

70–72

Your knees bend backward like a bird’s, which makes many athletic movements difficult to master. You take a –2 penalty on Climb and Swim checks, and your running speed is only three times your regular walking speed.

73–75

A tenacious patch of fungus has sprouted on your back, weakening your system against toxins. You take a –2 penalty on saves against poison effects.

76–77

Your misaligned eyes affect your ability to judge distances. You take a –2 penalty on ranged attack and damage rolls.

78–80

When you get flustered, heat builds up in your face until it glows with inner fire. You take a –4 penalty on Diplomacy and Perform checks when in the presence of 5 or more creatures.

81–83

Your ears curl up like dried leaves, and you experience difficulty picking up sounds. You take a –2 penalty on hearing-based Perception checks.

84–86

Your curving, clawlike fingernails grow so long so quickly that they impede you in dangerous situations. You take a –2 penalty on Escape Artist and Sleight of Hand checks.

87–89

One of your legs measures noticeably longer than the other, and you lose your balance easily. You take a –2 penalty to CMD against bull rush and trip attempts.

90–92

Your eyes have swollen to mere slits. You take a –2 penalty on sight-based Perception checks.

93–96

You develop a shaggy pelt about your neck and shoulders that makes you overheat quickly. You take a –4 penalty on checks and saves to resist environmental heat effects.

97–98

You grow a tail that you can’t easily conceal and that thrashes violently at inconvenient moments. You take a –2 penalty on Disguise and Stealth checks.

99–100

You grow vestigial gills. While they grant you no ability to breathe in water, they make it harder for you to catch your breath. When you are fatigued, it takes 12 hours of complete rest to recover. When you are exhausted, it takes 2 hours of complete rest to become fatigued.

bouncy (goblin).name: Bouncy (Goblin)
bouncy (goblin).description:
Your bones, flesh, and skin are a bit more elastic than those of most goblins, when you fall, you tend to bounce a little better as a result.

Requirements: Goblin

Benefits: Whenever you take falling damage, the first 1d6 points of lethal damage suffered in the fall are automatically converted to nonlethal damage. You also gain a +2 trait bonus on all Reflex saves made to avoid unexpected falls.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to avoid unexpected falls.

comparative religion (silver crusade).name: Comparative Religion (Silver Crusade)
comparative religion (silver crusade).description:
Your allies within the Silver Crusade have taught you a lot about Golarion’s deities and their followers.

Requirements: Silver Crusade

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (religion) checks, and Knowledge (religion) becomes a class skill for you.

honored provider (ghoran).name: Honored Provider (Ghoran)
honored provider (ghoran).description:
Nex depends upon the food produced by ghorans, and you inspire gratitude in creatures you feed.

Requirements: Ghoran

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Bluff, Diplomacy, and Sense Motive checks to interact with a person to whom you have given needed food in the past 24 hours.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on checks  with a person to whom you have given needed food in the past 24 hours.
+[[2]] Trait bonus on checks  with a person to whom you have given needed food in the past 24 hours.
+[[2]] Trait bonus on checks  with a person to whom you have given needed food in the past 24 hours.

cold fortitude (kobold).name: Cold Fortitude (Kobold)
cold fortitude (kobold).description:
The perpetually chilly environment of your youth made you exceptionally resilient in arctic climes.

Requirements: Kobold

Benefits: You gain a +4 trait bonus on Fortitude saving throws against nonlethal cold damage.

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Trait bonus against nonlethal cold damage.

disgraced noble (war for the crown).name: Disgraced Noble (War for the Crown)
disgraced noble (war for the crown).description:
Your noble family used to matter, until your father took a stand against Maxillar Pythareus, the commander of Taldor’s military. True or not, the accusations Pythareus leveled against your family in return destroyed your reputation and isolated you from the society you grew up in. Now the only thing that matters to you is clawing your way back up the social ladder, either for your own quality of life or to clear your family’s name.

Benefits: You’ve had to practice deception as you began working your way back into Taldan social circles; you gain a +2 trait bonus on Bluff checks to conceal your identity and a +2 bonus on Linguistics checks to spot or produce forgeries, and one of these skills is always a class skill for you. Once each day, you can choose a single humanoid you believe to have been involved in the conspiracy to destroy your family; you gain a +1 morale bonus on attack and damage rolls against that NPC for a number of rounds equal to your character level. At 10th level, this bonus increases to +2.

Usage: Select Bluff or Linguistics when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: Once each day, you can target a humanoid believe to conspire to destroy your family;you gain a +[[1]] Morale bonus on attack and damage rolls against that NPC for a number of rounds equal to your character level. At 10th level, this bonus increases to +2.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to conceal your identity.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to spot or produce forgeries.

chainbreaker (andoran).name: Chainbreaker (Andoran)
chainbreaker (andoran).description:
You’re an expert at rescuing slaves. You know that a few slaves freed from their chains can help turn the tide during a boarding action.

Requirements: Andoran

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Disable Device checks made to open locks, Strength checks made to break chains or bars in order to free slaves, and combat maneuver checks made to sunder chains or bars in order to free slaves.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus  to open locks.
+[[1]] Trait bonus  to break chains or bars in order to free slaves.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to sunder chains or bars in order to free slaves.

back for more.name: Back For More
back for more.description: Benefits: Anytime an opponent confirms a critical hit against you, you gain a +2 morale bonus on attack rolls and damage rolls against that foe until the end of your next turn.

contextNotes.text:
Anytime an opponent confirms a critical against you,
gain +[[2]] Morale bonus against that foe until the end of your next turn.

Anytime an opponent confirms a critical against you,
gain +[[2]] Morale bonus against that foe until the end of your next turn.

Anytime an opponent confirms a critical against you,
gain +[[2]] Morale bonus against that foe until the end of your next turn.

boarded in the shackles (serpent's skull.name: Boarded in the Shackles (Serpent's Skull)
boarded in the shackles (serpent's skull.description:
Your last home was in the Shackles, a dangerous land of pirates and treachery, but also of fantastic opportunity. A life of weal and woe on the sea and amid the pirate controlled lands has toughened you to a variety of hardships.

Benefits: Pick one of the three categories of saving throws - you gain a +1 trait bonus on all saving throws of that type.

Usage: Select Fortitude, Reflex, or Will when you take this trait.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

tireless logic.name: Tireless Logic
tireless logic.description:
Your curious mind figures out even the most complex problems.

Benefits: Once per day when you make an Intelligence-based skill check or ability check, you can roll twice and take the better result.

contextNotes.text: Once per day when you make an Intelligence-based skill check or ability check, you can roll twice and take the better result.
Once per day when you make an Intelligence-based skill check or ability check, you can roll twice and take the better result.

instant friendship (suli).name: Instant Friendship (Suli)
instant friendship (suli).description:
You inherited your janni parent’s knack for reversing a tense situation.

Requirements: Suli

Benefits: Once per day, you can treat an Intimidate roll to improve a creature’s attitude toward you as if you had used Diplomacy instead. You must use this ability after making the check but before the GM has revealed the result. If you continue threatening the target after making the check, the check automatically fails.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you can treat an Intimidate roll to improve a creature’s attitude toward you as if you had used Diplomacy instead. You must use this ability after making the check but before the GM has revealed the result. If you continue threatening the target after making the check, the check automatically fails.

Once per day, you can treat an Intimidate roll to improve a creature’s attitude toward you as if you had used Diplomacy instead. You must use this ability after making the check but before the GM has revealed the result. If you continue threatening the target after making the check, the check automatically fails.

provider (erastil; halfling).name: Provider (Erastil; Halfling)
provider (erastil; halfling).description:
Few things exalt your faith more than feeding the hungry with your own honest sweat.

Requirements: Erastil; Halfling

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on any d20 roll to acquire food or water for others. Though this bonus most often applies to Survival checks, it could also apply to attacking monsters that are safe and nutritious to eat (mainly animals and plants). However, Erastil takes a dim view of waste and of those who scorn his favor, and if you abuse this gift (for example, by using the bonus to defeat a bear, then only eating one bite of it), he punishes you with a -2 penalty on attacks, checks, and saves for 24 hours.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on any d20 roll to acquire food or water for others.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on any d20 roll to acquire food or water for others.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on any d20 roll to acquire food or water for others.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on any d20 roll to acquire food or water for others.

missing child (curse of the crimson thro.name: Missing Child (Curse of the Crimson Throne)
missing child (curse of the crimson thro.description:
You suspect that a child you know has been abducted by Gaedren. Whatever the relationship, you’ve heard rumors about Gaedren's “Little Lamms,” and of how the old man uses children as pickpockets and agents for his crimes. You’ve even heard rumors that the child you’re looking for has been spotted in the marketplaces in the company of known cutpurses and pickpockets. Although the Korvosan Guard has been understanding of your plight, yet it has its hands full with “more important” matters these days, it seems, and has not yet been able to learn anything more about Gaedren. No one else is interested in bringing Gaedren down and rescuing his victims— that task falls to you. Yet where could the old scoundrel be hiding?

Benefits: Choose one of the following benefits:

Missing Sibling: The missing child is a brother or sister. Although everyone else has given up hope, you believe your sibling still lives out there. Your constant search for the missing sibling has developed into great skill at rumor mongering and finding information from others. Gather Information and Sense Motive are always class skills for you.

Missing Son or Daughter: The missing child is your own son or daughter, a niece or nephew, or the son or daughter of your superior or employer and one you had been charged with protecting. The child was abducted during a trip to the market or some other daily event. Long hours spent searching for rumors and your stubborn will to continue grant you a +1 bonus on Will saves.

Usage:

  1. Select Missing Sibling or Missing Son or Daughter when you take this trait.

  2. If you select Missing Sibling: Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect Gather Information* and Sense Motive. then Delete the untargeted Change on the changes tab

  3. If you select Missing Son or Daughter: Update the target of the untargeted change on the changes tab of this trait to reflect Saving Throws>Will

Special Notes: *Gather Information from the 3.5 Ruleset were combined into Diplomacy in the Pathfinder ruleset.

This is the 3.5 Ruleset version of this trait. To view the updated version for the Pathfinder rules click the link below:

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.17mo4Jjxp01HsKnN]{Missing Child (Curse of the Crimson Throne)}

goblin watcher (rise of the runelords).name: Goblin Watcher (Rise of the Runelords)
goblin watcher (rise of the runelords).description:
You grew up in Sandpoint staring off the cliff across the Varisian Gulf. Spending so much time there at Junker’s Edge watching the goblins below as they scrounged through the discarded junk and seeing what they made out of the garbage, you developed an eye for spotting the most useful and valuable discarded items.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Perception and Appraise checks, and a +5 trait bonus on Appraise checks to determine the most valuable item visible in a treasure hoard.

contextNotes.text: +[[5]] Trait bonus to determine the most valuable item visible in a treasure hoard.

honor-driven (human; ulfen).name: Honor-Driven (Human; Ulfen)
honor-driven (human; ulfen).description:
Your honor is such a part of you that you can sense it (or its lack) in others.

Requirements: Ulfen Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks, and Sense Motive is always a class skill for you.

scarred.name: Scarred
scarred.description:
An injury left you horribly, visibly scarred, making it more difficult for you to hide your true face, and also making most people distrustful of you merely due to your appearance.

Effects: You take a –5 penalty on Disguise checks and a –2 penalty on Bluff checks.

shipboard caster (riddleport).name: Shipboard Caster (Riddleport)
shipboard caster (riddleport).description:
You feel an affinity with ships and the sea, and the rolling rhythm of the waves helps focus your mind.

Requirements: Riddleport

Benefits: When on a ship or other water-going vessel, you gain a +3 bonus on concentration checks.

contextNotes.text: +[[3]] bonus when on a ship or other water-going vessel.

hex-proof.name: Hex-Proof
hex-proof.description: Benefits: You’ve dealt too often with cruel fey magic, and so gain a +2 bonus on Will saves against hexes and fey creatures’ spells and spell-like abilities.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Bonus against hexes and fey creatures’ spells and spell-like abilities.

spooked.name: Spooked
spooked.description:
You had a traumatic experience with a spirit at a young age that colors your reactions to such creatures even to this day.

Effects: Whenever you perceive a fey, outsider, or undead from within 60 feet, you become shaken for 1d4 rounds unless you succeed at a Will save with a DC equal to 15 + the creature’s Charisma modifier. Immunity to fear effects does not allow you to forgo this saving throw; even if you are immune to fear, you must roll the Will saving throw for this drawback when applicable, albeit with a +4 bonus.

contextNotes.text: Whenever you perceive a fey, outsider, or undead from within 60 feet, you become shaken for [[1d4]] rounds unless you succeed at a Will save with a DC equal to 15 + the creature’s Charisma modifier. Immunity to fear effects does not allow you to forgo this saving throw; even if you are immune to fear, you must roll the Will saving throw for this drawback when applicable, albeit with a +4 bonus.

good folk (any city).name: Good Folk (Any City)
good folk (any city).description:
People’s faith in you tends to run deeper than their trust of others.

Requirements: Any City

Benefits: You gain a +2 bonus on Charisma checks and Charisma-based skill checks with creatures that have a friendly or helpful attitude towards you.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus with creatures that have a friendly or helpful attitude towards you.
+[[2]] Trait bonus with creatures that have a friendly or helpful attitude towards you.

rivethun adherent.name: Rivethun Adherent
rivethun adherent.description:
Rivethun is an ancient tradition of dwarven shamans who learned to listen to and affect the world around them by reflecting on the friction between body and spirit. As someone who feels a similar friction between your inner spirit and physical body, you have picked up some old rivethun meditation techniques, even if you aren’t a dwarf.

Benefits: While you suffer a physical ailment, such as disease, poison, or ability damage to Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution (but not including hit point damage), you gain a +2 trait bonus on Will saving throws. While you suffer a mental ailment, including insanity, any charm or compulsion effect, or ability damage to Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma, you gain a +2 trait bonus on Fortitude saving throws.

contextNotes.text: While you suffer a mental ailment, you gain a +2 Trait bonus.
While you suffer a physical ailment (not including hit point damage), you gain a +[[2]] Trait bonus.

demon smiter.name: Demon Smiter
demon smiter.description:
You grew up around those who fight the demons, and from their stories you learned about the demons’ weaknesses. You likely have joined that nation’s cause as a crusader.

Benefits: Once per day when fighting demons, you gain a +4 trait bonus on a single attack roll.

thrune loyal agent (hell's vengeance).name: Thrune Loyal Agent (Hell's Vengeance)
thrune loyal agent (hell's vengeance).description:
Note:

This is a special trait that can only be gained by progressing through the Hell's Vengeance Adventure Path. It is not available for normal character creation

You have sworn your body to Queen Abrogail and House Thrune in a ritual ceremony, and are willing to lay down your life in service to Thrune.

Benefits: Once per week, when reduced to fewer than 0 hit points, you automatically gain fast healing 1 for 1 minute. Once this ability has been used, you instead gain a +4 trait bonus on Constitution checks to stabilize when reduced to negative hit points.

In addition, you receive an invisible @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.n8k4pdmjjg8xuv7l]{arcane mark} of the encircled cross emblem of Cheliax as a symbol of your loyalty. This mark does not fade and is visible only to other creatures with this trait, or creatures with the ability to perceive the invisible (via or true seeing, for example). You can make this mark visible to other creatures for 1 round as a swift action. If the mark is dispelled or removed (such as with erase), it can be replaced with another casting of arcane mark, which thereafter functions as detailed in this trait.

contextNotes.text:
Once per week, when reduced to fewer than 0 hit points, you automatically gain fast healing 1 for 1 minute. 
Once this ability has been used, you instead gain a +[[4]] Trait bonus on Constitution checks to stabilize when reduced to negative hit points.

insider knowledge (grand lodge).name: Insider Knowledge (Grand Lodge)
insider knowledge (grand lodge).description:
Venture-Captain Valsin likes to keep abreast of situations within the Pathfinder Society, and you do your best to emulate him.

Requirements: Grand Lodge

Benefits: Choose either Diplomacy or Knowledge (local). You gain a +1 trait bonus on all checks for the chosen skill, and the chosen skill becomes a class skill for you.

Usage

Select Diplomacy or Knowledge (Local) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

hunter’s knack.name: Hunter’s Knack
hunter’s knack.description:
When you watch and wait before attacking, your strike is true.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls against your favored enemies when you make such attacks as part of a readied action.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attack rolls against your favored enemies when you make such attacks as part of a readied action.

blood of dragons (any).name: Blood of Dragons (Any)
blood of dragons (any).description:
Long ago, your ancestors’ blood mixed with that of dragons.

Requirements: Any race

Benefits: Choose one of the following: gain a +1 trait bonus on Perception checks, gain low-light vision, or gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against effects that cause sleep or paralysis.

Usage: Select one (1) of the three options available when you take this trait.

If you choose the bonus to Perception:

  1. Update the Formula of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to "1".

  2. Delete the note on the changes tab.

If you choose to gain Low-light vision:

  1. Delete the change on the Changes tab of this trait.

  2. Delete the note on the changes tab of this trait.

  3. On your actor's Attributes tab, edit the "senses" and check the box beside "Low-light Vision" and click the Save Changes Button.

If you choose to gain the bonus to saving throws:

  1. Delete the change on the Changes tab of this trait.

  2. Update Target of the note on the Changes tab of this trait to "All Saving Throws".

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against effects that cause sleep or paralysis.

pitaxian improvisation (pitax).name: Pitaxian Improvisation (Pitax)
pitaxian improvisation (pitax).description:
Your quick hands can salvage even the most botched performance.

Requirements: Pitax

Benefits: Once per day after you attempt a Perform check and before the results of the roll are revealed by the GM, you can attempt a Bluff check and use the result of that check in place of your Perform check. You must use the result of that Bluff check, even if it is lower.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, after you attempt a Perform check, and before the results are revealed, you can attempt a Bluff check and use the result of that check in place of your Perform check. You must use the result of that Bluff check, even if it is lower. ([[@resources.pitaxianImprovisationPitax.value]] remaining uses)

scholar of balance (the concordance).name: Scholar of Balance (The Concordance)
scholar of balance (the concordance).description:
You have studied the balance of the elements and its effects on natural and planar forces.

Requirements: The Concordance

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (nature) and Knowledge (planes) checks, and one of these skills (your choice) becomes a class skill.

Usage

Select Knowledge (nature) or Knowledge (planes) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

expert boarder (besmara).name: Expert Boarder (Besmara)
expert boarder (besmara).description:
Besmara rewards those who board other vessels and take what they want.

Requirements: Besmara

Benefits: While fighting on the deck of a ship, you gain a +1 bonus on all attacks made with one-handed weapons.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] bonus on all attacks made with one-handed weapons while fighting on the deck of a ship.

xenophobic.name: Xenophobic
xenophobic.description:
You have a hard time understanding and trusting those with unfamiliar ways and appearances.

Effects: You take a –2 penalty on Diplomacy and Sense Motive checks made against creatures of a different race or from a different culture.

contextNotes.text: -[[2]] penalty  vs creatures of different race or culture.
-[[2]] penalty  vs creatures of different race or culture.

scholar of the ancients (rise of the run.name: Scholar of the Ancients (Rise of the Runelords)
scholar of the ancients (rise of the run.description:
Growing up with your nose in books, you’ve had a great interest in past cultures and ancient history. Furthermore, having grown up in Varisia, you know the monuments dotting the landscape belong to an ancient civilization known as Thassilon.

Benefits: From your life of study and dogged research, you’ve pieced together the language and partial history of this once-great empire. You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (arcana) and Knowledge (history) checks, and begin play able to speak and read Thassilonian.

haunted.name: Haunted
haunted.description:
Something from your past—or a dark secret you presently hold—makes it difficult for you to ever be at peace, and your chronic worry that you might fall to evil influence has become a self-fulfilling prophecy.

Effects: You take a –2 penalty on saves against spells with the evil descriptor.

contextNotes.text: -[[2]] Penalty against spells with the evil descriptor

intrepid volunteer (halfling).name: Intrepid Volunteer (Halfling)
intrepid volunteer (halfling).description:
Like many free half lings, you volunteered readily for military service or an adventuring career and met other half lings who showed you how to make up for your small size.

Requirements: Halfling

Benefits: Choose one Strength-based skill or one combat maneuver. You can use your Dexterity modifier in place of your Strength modifier to calculate your total modifier for that skill check or combat maneuver check.

Usage: Select one Strength-based skill or one combat maneuver when you take this trait.

Update the chosen skill on the Skills Tab of your Actor to reflect your choice or, if you have chosen a combat maneuver, select dexterity as the attack ability when you add a combat action to your actor for the chosen Combat maneuver.

eager combatant (riddleport).name: Eager Combatant (Riddleport)
eager combatant (riddleport).description:
You’re so excited to board other ships that you’re frequently the first one over the rail, regardless of the danger.

Requirements: Riddleport

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls made while onboard an enemy ship.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attack rolls made while onboard an enemy ship.

thrill-seeker (desna).name: Thrill-Seeker (Desna)
thrill-seeker (desna).description:
Desna has blessed you not just with a love of taking chances, but also the fortune to come out of such risky situations unscathed.

Requirements: Desna

Benefits: Once per day, when you attempt an Acrobatics check, you can roll twice and take the better result. You must choose to use this ability before making the check.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, when you attempt an Acrobatics check, you can roll twice and take the better result. You must choose to use this ability before making the check.

artifact hunter (mwangi expanse).name: Artifact Hunter (Mwangi Expanse)
artifact hunter (mwangi expanse).description:
You know the legends of the Mwangi Expanse and the places where great treasures were rumored to have fallen.

Requirements: Mwangi Expanse

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (geography) and Knowledge (history) checks, and one of them is always a class skill for you. 

Usage

Select Knowledge (geography) or Knowledge (history) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

nanite revival (android).name: Nanite Revival (Android)
nanite revival (android).description:
You can instinctively activate your nanites to save your life.

Requirements: @UUID[Compendium.pf1.races.Item.9Fe6mDt0oP52m6Jl]{Android}

Benefits: Once per day as an immediate action, you can command your nanites to stabilize you while you are dying, even while unconscious or helpless. This does not count as a use of your nanite surge ability.

foreign opportunist (mummy's mask).name: Foreign Opportunist (Mummy's Mask)
foreign opportunist (mummy's mask).description:
You’re not a native Osirian, but the opportunity to explore the tombs of Ancient Osirion—and “liberate” the treasures they hold—is too good to pass up. Whether or not you’re interested in the history of this land, you’re def initely interested in the wealth that’s lain hidden in dusty crypts for millennia— such as the tombs in the newly opened necropolis of Wati.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Appraise checks, and that skill is always a class skill for you. In addition, your contacts in the antiquities markets allow you to sell relics of Ancient Osirion for 60% of their listed price, rather than the normal 50% value. What is considered a relic of Ancient Osirion is left to the GM’s discretion.

secret shame.name: Secret Shame
secret shame.description:
You have a terrible fear of the public at large, a group, or an important person (such as your order of knights, your liege lord, your family, or your lover) learning a shameful truth about you.

Effects: You take a -1 penalty on saving throws against fear effects, and the DC of any Intimidate check to demoralize you is reduced by 1. If you would normally be immune to fear, you do not take these penalties, but instead lose your immunity to fear (regardless of its source).

contextNotes.text: -[[1]] penalty against fear effects. If you would normally be immune to fear, you do not take this penalty, but instead lose your immunity to fear (regardless of its source).

wary (aasimar).name: Wary (Aasimar)
wary (aasimar).description:
You grew up around people who were jealous of and hostile toward you. Perhaps your parents were not pleased to have a child touched by the divine,they may have berated or beaten you, or even sold you into slavery for an exorbitant price. You grew up mistrustful of others and believing your unique appearance to be a curse.

Requirements: Aasimar

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Disguise and Sense Motive checks.

voices of solid things (witchmarket).name: Voices of Solid Things (Witchmarket)
voices of solid things (witchmarket).description:
You traveled with the Witchmarket for a time, either willingly or as a result of a bargain made by some other buyer. In your time in the Witchmarket, you learned to listen to the whispers of items as though they were living creatures, giving you clues as to their function.

Requirements: Witchmarket

Benefits: Choose Appraise, Craft, Disable Device, or Spellcraft. You apply your Charisma modifier on checks with that skill instead of the skill’s normal ability score. That skill is a class skill for you.

Usage: Select Appraise, Craft, Disable Device, or Spellcraft when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

Update the Skills tab of the actor to reflect Charisma rather than the default ability for the chosen skill.

fury (rovagug).name: Fury (Rovagug)
fury (rovagug).description:
The flood of battle and destruction stirs deep within you.

Requirements: Rovagug

Benefits: When wielding a greataxe, you receive a +2 trait bonus on attack rolls to confirm critical hits.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to confirm critical hits when wielding a greataxe.

bastard.name: Bastard
bastard.description:
You were born out of wedlock. You have always been an outsider in society, and in your own family. This perspective has sharpened your insight.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks, and Sense Motive is always a class skill for you.

issian (kingmaker).name: Issian (Kingmaker)
issian (kingmaker).description:
You were raised northern Brevoy, a land of misty shores and harsh hill lands, of snowy vistas and violet-hued mountains. You are descended from an able and intelligent people, and you have grand ambitions, a mind alert for opportunity, and the tenacity to fight for your goals no matter the challenge. You care for little more than achieving your aspirations and opportunities to win wealthy and grandeur, for which few costs prove too great. You see yourself as a citizen of Brevoy through and through. The call for champions willing to help take back your country's rightful holdings in the Stolen Lands has inflamed your dreams of profit and possibilities, so you have joined an expedition to quest south.

Benefits: Your agile mind grants you a +1 trait bonus on all Will saves made to resist mind-affecting effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to resist mind-affecting effects.

winter warrior (irrisen).name: Winter Warrior (Irrisen)
winter warrior (irrisen).description:
You grew up in the icy lands of Irrisen, and surviving in the cold snows of winter is second nature to you.

Requirements: Irrisen

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Stealth and Survival checks in ice- or snow-covered terrain.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus in ice- or snow-covered terrain.
+[[1]] Trait bonus in ice- or snow-covered terrain.

acupuncturist (chu ye).name: Acupuncturist (Chu Ye)
acupuncturist (chu ye).description:
You have studied the ancient art of acupuncture, and use it to pursue enlightenment and healing.

Requirements:Chu Ye

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on all Fortitude saves made against poison effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus  against poison effects

shrouded casting.name: Shrouded Casting
shrouded casting.description:
You have learned the secret to casting some spells without lesser material components.

Benefits: Choose one school of magic when you pick this trait. You can cast spells from that school as if you had the @UUID[Compendium.pf1.feats.Item.ElqchQT5WB625HPg]{Eschew Materials} feat.

contextNotes.text: You are considered to have the Eschew Materials feat for the school of magic you've chosen.

child of infamy (council of thieves).name: Child of Infamy (Council of Thieves)
child of infamy (council of thieves).description:
Your family has long been in show business. Perhaps it was your parents, or an older sibling, or an uncle or aunt,whatever the case, one of your close relatives is, or was, a well-known and well-loved actor or actress. That this relation died in some compromising and embarrassing way has done little to alter your family's name and fame, if anything, the unfortunate death has increased it.

When folk learn your last name, they are quick to assume you live a wild life like your ill-fated relative, and whether you bask in this notoriety or do your best to hide your heritage, the years of association with the acting scene have had their effect. You've certainly inherited your relative's talents, and are a larger-than-life figure, a melodramatic attention-hound, or a sly manipulator of emotions.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Perform (act) skill checks, and the skill is always considered a class skill for you. Furthermore, you've inherited some of your relative's funds, and begin play with a nest egg of 300 gp that you can spend however you wish.

Usage: Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the Perform (act) skill.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect Perform (act).

Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect Perform (act).

observant archer (aquatic origin).name: Observant Archer (Aquatic origin)
observant archer (aquatic origin).description:
Growing up in an aquatic environment, where three-dimensional movement and perception were necessary, has given you keen observational skills and the ability to notice and target unusual positions with ranged attacks.

Requirements: Aquatic origin

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on ranged attack rolls when your target is at least 10 feet above or below you.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on ranged attack rolls when your target is at least 10 feet above or below you.

tusked (half-orc, orc).name: Tusked (Half-Orc, Orc)
tusked (half-orc, orc).description:
Requirements: Half-Orc, Orc

Benefits: Huge, sharp tusks bulge from your mouth, and you receive a bite attack (1d4 damage for Medium characters). If used as part of a full attack action, the bite attack is made at your full base attack bonus –5.

Usage: Add a Bite attack from the Monster Abilities Compendium to your Actor

Modify the bite attack damage formula from sizeRoll(1, 6, @size) to sizeRoll(1, 4, @size, 4)

skymetal smith (iron gods).name: Skymetal Smith (Iron Gods)
skymetal smith (iron gods).description:
The fires atop Torch Hill have long been a boon to smiths and metalworkers, and your family is no exception. Whether you grew up in Torch or simply made several trips here with your parents to use the fire, this was to be your first time to use the torch for your own project. You managed to use the fires to craft a small weapon or piece of armor from skymetal, but not long thereafter the fires went out. The violet flames are as much a part of your upbringing as anything else, and their loss distresses you; you hope to find a way to rekindle the torch below the hill.

Benefits: You’ve long hoped to work with skymetal, and begin the game with a small metal bauble made of the skymetal of your choice—you made this item yourself. The item is nothing more than a valuable art object worth 100 gp. You can sell it to gain an additional 100 gp when creating your character, but if you keep it, your pride in its crafting grants you a +2 trait bonus on Will saving throws made against emotion and fear effects. You lose this bonus if you willingly sell or give up the item, but if it is destroyed or lost through no fault of your own, you retain a +1 trait bonus on such Will saves.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus made against emotion and fear effects as long as you retain your Skymetal bauble. You lose this bonus if you willingly sell or give up the item, but if it is destroyed or lost through no fault of your own, the bonus becomes +1 instead.

venicaan medic (qadira).name: Venicaan Medic (Qadira)
venicaan medic (qadira).description:
You have studied at the College of Medicaments and Chirurgery, and know by sight the signs of ailments and toxins you might encounter.

Requirements: Qadira

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus to Heal checks to treat diseases or poisons, and Heal is always a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to treat diseases or poisons

boar resilience (skinwalker; wereboar-ki.name: Boar Resilience (Skinwalker; Wereboar-Kin)
boar resilience (skinwalker; wereboar-ki.description:
You were victimized by bullies or even abusive adults. Although the harassment got worse when your wereboar features became more obvious, those features also gave you fortitude that helped you resist it.

Requirements: Wereboar-Kin Skinwalker Heritage

Benefits: You are unimpaired by being at exactly 0 hit points.

In addition, you can ignore the Endurance prerequisite for the @UUID[Compendium.pf1.feats.Item.O0e0UCim27GPKFuW]{Diehard} feat.

oral historian (human; shoanti).name: Oral Historian (Human; Shoanti)
oral historian (human; shoanti).description:
You learned numerous mnemonic devices to more easily recall information imparted by your quah’s master storyteller.

Requirements: Shoanti Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (history) and Perform (oratory) checks, and one of those skills (your choice) is always a class skill for you.

Usage: Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the Perform (oratory) skill.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect Perform (sing).

Select Knowledge (history) or Perform (oratory) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

screaming leap (realm of the mammoth lor.name: Screaming Leap (Realm of the Mammoth Lords)
screaming leap (realm of the mammoth lor.description:
The rocky foothills of your homeland are ideal for staging leaping ambushes that leave your victims battered and routed.

Requirements: Realm of the Mammoth Lords

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on melee damage rolls when you make a charge attack from higher ground. Once per day, when you make a charge attack from higher ground, you can use the Intimidate skill to demoralize your target as a swift action.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on melee damage rolls when you make a charge attack from higher ground

clockwork surgeon (aesocar).name: Clockwork Surgeon (Aesocar)
clockwork surgeon (aesocar).description:
Thanks to your medical innovations, you can perform especially comprehensive or speedy surgeries.

Requirements: Aesocar

Benefits: You begin play with a special healer’s kit at no cost and can fully restock your kit by expending materials worth 10 gp and spending 8 hours assembling medical devices. When using this healer’s kit to treat deadly wounds, you can either halve the time required to perform the action or add your Intelligence modifier to the number of hit points healed.

contextNotes.text: When using your special healer’s kit to treat deadly wounds, you can either halve the time required to perform the action or add [[@abilities.int.mod]] to the number of hit points healed.

helpful.name: Helpful
helpful.description:
You always know the best way to assist your companions, be it assisting them with a task, defending them in battle, or helping them place a well-aimed strike.

Benefits: When using the Aid Another action, you grant your ally a +3 bonus instead of a +2 bonus.

swamp recluse (swamp).name: Swamp Recluse (Swamp)
swamp recluse (swamp).description:
Requirements: Swamp

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against poison, and you can safely eat raw fish and game without any effort spent cooking or cleaning them.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against poison.

naive.name: Naive
naive.description:
Your perception that everyone is innately good does not bode well in the heat of battle, when less scrupulous foes might take advantage of your misguided optimism.

Effects: You take a –2 penalty to AC against attacks with improvised weapons and a –2 penalty to CMD against dirty trick combat maneuvers.

contextNotes.text: -[[2]] penalty to AC against attacks with improvised weapons.
-[[2]] penalty to CMD against dirty trick combat maneuvers.

strength foretold.name: Strength Foretold
strength foretold.description:
You can channel extra power into one of your bloodline spells.

Benefits: Once per day as a free action, you gain a +1 trait bonus to the DC of the saving throw of one of your bonus bloodline spells.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you may gain a +[[1]] trait bonus to the DC of the saving throw of one of your bonus bloodline spells.

fortified (shadow lodge).name: Fortified (Shadow Lodge)
fortified (shadow lodge).description:
Some members of the Shadow Lodge aren’t completely convinced of the Decemvirate’s sincerity and fear potential reprisals.

Requirements: Shadow Lodge

Benefits: Through alchemical techniques and endurance training, you gain the ability to negate a critical hit or sneak attack scored against you once per day. This ability functions as the @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-special-qualities.Item.ESa4zBfizx2LWPGx]{fortification} armor special ability, with a 20% chance of success.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you may attempt to negate a critical hit or sneak attack scored against you with a 20% chance of success. ([[@resources.fortifiedShadowLodge.value]] remaining uses)

hard to pin down (tiefling).name: Hard to Pin Down (Tiefling)
hard to pin down (tiefling).description:
Only more pressing dangers kept bullies from victimizing you.

Requirements: Tiefling

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus to AC against attacks of opportunity made by opponents whom you flank and opponents denied their Dexterity bonus to AC against you.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to AC against attacks of opportunity made by opponents whom you flank or whom are denied their Dexterity bonus to AC against you.

keleshite trader (kelesh, qadira).name: Keleshite Trader (Kelesh, Qadira)
keleshite trader (kelesh, qadira).description:
Your cosmopolitan upbringing has given you familiarity with many cultures.

Requirements: Kelesh or Qadira

Benefits: You gain two bonus languages and a +2 trait bonus on Knowledge (local) checks.

Usage: Select two bonus languages when you take this trait.

Update the Granted Proficiencies>Languages section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choices.

ustalavic noble (ustalav).name: Ustalavic Noble (Ustalav)
ustalavic noble (ustalav).description:
You come from an aristocratic family, though your family has long since lost any power or prestige it once had.

Requirements: Ustalav

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy and Knowledge (nobility) checks. In addition, your starting money is increased by 100 gp, your "birthright," such as it is.

child of the moon.name: Child of the Moon
child of the moon.description:
Your lunar gift becomes much more powerful when the moon is full.

Benefits: Choose either Climb, Stealth, or Swim. You gain a +1 trait bonus on checks made with the chosen skill. On dates during and after the first quarter of a lunar phase but before end of the last quarter (the half of the lunar cycle where the moon is fuller), this bonus increases to +2. During the full moon, this bonus increases to +4.

contextNotes.text: You gain a +[[1]] trait bonus on Climb, Stealth, or Swim checks. +[[2]] during waxing and waning gibbous phases. +[[4]] during a full moon.
You gain a +[[1]] trait bonus on Climb, Stealth, or Swim checks. +[[2]] during waxing and waning gibbous phases. +[[4]] during a full moon.
You gain a +[[1]] trait bonus on Climb, Stealth, or Swim checks. +[[2]] during waxing and waning gibbous phases. +[[4]] during a full moon.

besmara's strength (besmara).name: Besmara's Strength (Besmara)
besmara's strength (besmara).description:
The Pirate Queen sometimes answers your calls when you tread dangerous waters.

Requirements: Besmara

Benefits: Once per day when attempting a Swim check, you can treat the water as if it were one category calmer (as if stormy water were rough water or rough water were calm water).

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you can treat the water as if it were one category calmer (as if stormy water were rough water or rough water were calm water).

star struck (hell's rebels).name: Star Struck (Hell's Rebels)
star struck (hell's rebels).description:
Growing up in Kintargo, it’s hard not to become obsessed with one of the city’s celebrities. There are so many to choose from, and they’re all so glamorous, rich, and successful! Wouldn’t it be amazing to, perhaps some day, meet one of them? Or even better, to be a Kintargan celebrity yourself? Your interest in one of Kintargo’s celebrities could be completely benign, with the NPC acting as a muse, inspiration, or role model for you that you used to guide many life choices leading you to this day. Or perhaps your interest is more akin to an obsession, in which you hope to some day live a life like that you imagine your idol lives, or perhaps even to some day meet and become friends (or perhaps more than friends) with your idol. Keep in mind, though, that sometimes a celebrity’s public persona and real personality are two very different things, and if some day you were to meet your idol, you might be surprised at the truth! That said, the fact that, by all accounts, your idol has been missing since House Thrune instituted martial law has you worried; you hope he or she is all right! The fact that all five of the local legends listed below have been rumored to have ties to the Silver Ravens further concerns you, considering how that group seems to have been particularly targeted by Barzillai Thrune’s agents over the past week. The fate of all five of these Kintargan icons will be revealed at some point during the Hell’s Rebels Adventure Path, but keep in mind that some of those fates might not be pleasant discoveries for you!

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (local) checks. Pick one of the following celebrities with which to be obsessed. Each celebrity is associated with a particular ability score. Once per day, you can draw on your inspiration from your icon when you are about to attempt a skill check modified by that ability score. When you do so, roll the check twice and take the better of the two results as your actual result.

Jackdaw (sex and race unknown; Intelligence): A notorious folk hero or heroine (no one seems to know if Jackdaw is a man or woman) who helped defend Kintargo during the Chelish Civil War, and whom many believe still lives on today in the city’s shadows.

Jilia Bainilus (female human; Wisdom): Kintargo’s previous lord-mayor was well known for her cutting insights into the political world. Few have fought harder to maintain Kintargo’s independence.

Octavio Sabinus (male human; Strength): The Lictor of the Hellknight Order of the Torrent might seem an odd choice for admiration, but Octavio’s physical stature certainly lent him all the support he needed to command a room.

Shensen (female half-elf; Charisma): Shensen’s performances as an outspoken force against the diabolism of House Thrune have won her nearly as many admirers as have her memorable performances on stage in the Kintargo Opera House.

Strea Vestori (female tiefling; Dexterity): Strea is often regarded as the face of the slums known as the Devil’s Nursery. As Kintargo’s most outspoken and public tiefling citizen and leader of the Cloven Hoof Society, she has nearly as many admirers as she has political enemies in Cheliax.

Usage: Select one Celebrity described above when you take this trait.

On the details tab of this trait, edit the Use> Description to remove the 4 choices not selected.

Update the target of the Context note on the changes tab of this trait to reflect: skills> (Chosen ability) Skills.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you can roll a skill check modified by the chosen ability score twice and take the better result.

in tune with language (vampire; jiang-sh.name: In Tune with Language (Vampire; Jiang-Shi)
in tune with language (vampire; jiang-sh.description:
Requirements: Jiang-Shi Vampire Heritage

Benefits: Your keen ear for language grants you a +2 trait bonus on skill checks when decoding secret messages or finding hidden meaning in overheard conversations.

contextNotes.text:
+[[2]] Trait bonus 
 when decoding secret messages or finding hidden meaning in overheard conversations.

tropical upbringing.name: Tropical Upbringing
tropical upbringing.description:
You were raised in searing-hot deserts, plains, jungles, or other areas, and developed an impressive resistance to heat.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against fire effects and a +2 trait bonus on Fortitude saves against the effects of hot weather.

Suggested Characters

Garundi, Keleshites, Mwangi

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus vs fire effects
+[[2]] Trait bonus vs hot weather effects

swamp skulker (river kingdoms).name: Swamp Skulker (River Kingdoms)
swamp skulker (river kingdoms).description:
Terrible things prowl the moors of your homeland, and you are all too familiar with hiding or fleeing from them.

Requirements: River Kingdoms

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Stealth checks when you are in bog, marsh, or swamp terrain, and you treat shallow bogs and swamp undergrowth as normal terrain for the purposes of movement.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus when you are in bog, marsh, or swamp terrain.

nirmathi militia (nirmathas).name: Nirmathi Militia (Nirmathas)
nirmathi militia (nirmathas).description:
You have served in one of the militia bands of Nirmathas, and have learned valuable skills.

Requirements: Nirmathas

Benefits: Select one of the following skills: Profession (soldier), Ride, or Survival. You gain a +1 trait bonus on that skill, and it is always a class skill for you.

Usage: Select Profession (soldier), Ride, or Survival when you take this trait.

if you selected Profession (soldier), update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the chosen skill.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

stabilizing touch (pharasma).name: Stabilizing Touch (Pharasma)
stabilizing touch (pharasma).description:
You were born with the ability to stop the passage to death’s door.

Requirements: Pharasma

Benefits: Once per day, you can use @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.fipqx43u5blpjsvo]{stabilize} as a spell-like ability, but with a range of touch.

world traveler (human).name: World Traveler (Human)
world traveler (human).description:
Your family has taken the love of travel to an extreme, roaming the world extensively. You've seen dozens of cultures and have learned to appreciate the diversity of what the world has to offer.

Requirements: Human

Benefits: Select one of the following skills: Diplomacy, Knowledge (local), or Sense Motive. You gain a +1 trait bonus to that skill, and it is always a class skill for you.

Usage:

Select one of the following skills: Diplomacy, Knowledge (local), or Sense Motive, when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

unintentional linguist.name: Unintentional Linguist
unintentional linguist.description:
You can speak with outsiders.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on all Linguistics checks, and you begin play knowing one of the following languages (in addition to those granted by your race and Intelligence modifier): Abyssal, Aquan, Celestial, Ignan, Infernal, Protean, or Terran.

Usage: Add chosen language (one of Abyssal, Aquan, Celestial, Ignan, Infernal, Protean, or Terran) via details tab

wendifa apprentice.name: Wendifa Apprentice
wendifa apprentice.description:
For a time during your childhood, you were left in the care of a juju oracle or wendifa, who saw your potential and taught you simple juju magic to influence the weakminded for brief periods of time.

Benefits: You can use daze as a spell-like ability once per day, using your character level as your caster level (minimum 1). The save DC of this effect is Charisma-based. The dazed individual looks ashen and deathly for the duration of the effect.

aldori caution.name: Aldori Caution
aldori caution.description:
You learned defensive moves from Aldori trainers.

Benefits: You gain an additional +1 dodge bonus to Armor Class while you fight defensively or use the total defense action.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Dodge bonus to Armor Class while you fight defensively or use the total defense action.

ghoulish affinity (darklands).name: Ghoulish Affinity (Darklands)
ghoulish affinity (darklands).description:
Ever since you recovered from ghoul fever, the scars on your arms and hands itch whenever one of these vile creatures is nearby.

Requirements: Darklands

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Perception checks to notice undead disguised as living creatures, and a +2 trait bonus on Fortitude saves against diseases and poisons inflicted by undead.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to notice undead disguised as living creatures.
+[[2]] Trait bonus against diseases and poisons inflicted by undead.

lair snake (dragon’s lair).name: Lair Snake (Dragon’s lair)
lair snake (dragon’s lair).description:
You spent your formative years bowing and scraping in the presence of a dragon, witnessing the glory of the vast hoard... and squirreling away a few bits of coin for yourself.

Requirements: Dragon’s lair

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Appraise checks, and your starting wealth increases by 200 gp.

secret-keeper.name: Secret-Keeper
secret-keeper.description:
Growing up in a land where paranoia fills the air and any accusation of heresy or dissent can spell doom for whole families, you learned very quickly to keep a secret.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Bluff checks and a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against divinations, domination effects, and effects that would compel you to speak the truth.

Suggested Characters

Chelaxians, Galtans, Nidalese.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] trait bonus vs divinations, domination effects, effects that compel to speak the truth

vexed (kobold).name: Vexed (Kobold)
vexed (kobold).description:
You had multiple narrow escapes from gremlin saboteurs and learned to avoid their magic.

Requirements: Kobold

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against curses and fear effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against curses and fear effects.

underlying principals (nethys).name: Underlying Principals (Nethys)
underlying principals (nethys).description:
You've spent a large amount of time around magical items, and understand the similarities between many of them.

Requirements: Nethys

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Use Magic Device checks, and it becomes a class skill for you.

subjective truth.name: Subjective Truth
subjective truth.description:
