You are able to divorce your knowledge of the facts from your beliefs about the truth, and thus from your facial expressions and body language.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Bluff checks to lie, provided the person you’re lying to has never known you to lie to him. Failing this check counts as being caught in a lie by the target, negating any future use of this bonus against that person.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to lie, provided the person you’re lying to has never known you to lie to them previously.

amiable blunder.name: Amiable Blunder
amiable blunder.description:
Your easygoing demeanor allows you to sometimes recover from potentially awkward social situations or even condemnatory faux pas.

Benefits: Once per day when you fail a Diplomacy check by 5 or more and would cause an NPC’s attitude toward you to worsen, you can immediately attempt another Diplomacy check against the same DC as a free action. If you succeed at this second Diplomacy check, the character’s attitude doesn’t change, as though you had failed the original check by 4 or less.

contextNotes.text: If you fail by 5 or more, see @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.3D3H4PR2ONlGWJqh]{Amiable Blunder}

denial of fate (urgathoa).name: Denial of Fate (Urgathoa)
denial of fate (urgathoa).description:
The Pallid Princess has blessed you with the ability to avoid the final fate.

Requirements: Urgathoa

Benefits: Once per day while you are below 0 hit points, at the start of your turn you can choose to stabilize without needing to succeed at a Constitution check.

contextNotes.text: Once per day while you are below 0 hit points, at the start of your turn you can choose to stabilize without needing to succeed at a Constitution check.

forest senses (gnome).name: Forest Senses (Gnome)
forest senses (gnome).description:
Requirements: Gnome

Benefits: When in any unsettled, uncivilized terrain, you gain a +2 trait bonus on Perception checks to act during a surprise round, and take no penalty on Perception checks while sleeping.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus, when in any unsettled or uncivilized terrain,  to act during a surprise round. Take no penalty on checks while sleeping.

masterful demeanor (human; chelaxian).name: Masterful Demeanor (Human; Chelaxian)
masterful demeanor (human; chelaxian).description:
As a proud Chelaxian, you are confident that members of other, inferior races will obey you, and quickly.

Requirements: Chelaxian Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +3 trait bonus on Intimidate checks against members of non-human humanoid races.

contextNotes.text: +[[3]] Trait bonus against members of non-human humanoid races.

radiant healer.name: Radiant Healer
radiant healer.description:
You’ve learned how to infuse your healing magic with lingering traces of light.

Benefits: Whenever you cast a spell from the conjuration (healing) school that has a casting time of a standard action, you can extend its casting time to a full-round action to imbue the target with @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.ip9onjfp6nf1vlv9]{light}, as per light, with a duration of 1 round per caster level.

contextNotes.text: You may extend the casting time of a spell from the conjuration (healing) school from a standard action to a full-round action to imbue it with light as the spell for 1 round per caster level.

crusader road acolyte (thornkeep, echo w.name: Crusader Road Acolyte (Thornkeep, Echo Wood)
crusader road acolyte (thornkeep, echo w.description:
You trained with the Hellknights at Fort Inevitable or the paladins of Iomedae at Fort Riverwatch.

Requirements: Thornkeep or Echo Wood

Benefits: If you trained with the Hellknights, you gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against chaotic spells and the spell-like abilities of chaotic outsiders. If you trained with the Iomedaean Paladins, you gain this bonus against evil spells and evil outsiders instead.

Usage

Select Hellknights at Fort Inevitable (chaotic) or the paladins of Iomedae at Fort Riverwatch (evil) when you take this trait.

Edit the text of the note on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus  against  spells with the [choice of chaotic or evil] descriptor and the spell-like abilities of  [same as previous choice chaotic or evil] outsiders.

heart of two worlds.name: Heart of Two Worlds
heart of two worlds.description:
Born into a world split in twain, you are particularly skilled at understanding and empathizing with those around you.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy and Sense Motive checks involving creatures that belong to the same race (or, if human, ethnicity) as your mother or father.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on checks involving creatures that belong to the same race (or, if human, ethnicity) as your mother or father.
+[[2]] Trait bonus on checks involving creatures that belong to the same race (or, if human, ethnicity) as your mother or father.

numerian fluid indulger (palace of falle.name: Numerian Fluid Indulger (Palace of Fallen Stars)
numerian fluid indulger (palace of falle.description:
You have tasted Numerian fluids many times.

Requirements: Associated with the court of the Palace of Fallen Stars in Numeria

Benefits: Once per day, after rolling to determine the side effects of consuming a Numerian fluid (Numeria, Land of Fallen Stars page 29), you can roll on the table again but you must take the second result.

alchemical breath (kobold).name: Alchemical Breath (Kobold)
alchemical breath (kobold).description:
Your tribe, in an effort to emulate the green chromatic dragons they admire, goes to great lengths to secure alchemical coal.

Requirements: Kobold

Benefits: As a member of a green kobold tribe, you begin play with three pieces of the choking smoke variety of @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-items.Item.Zf3UVfrbf2UPJ9ZC]{alchemical coal}.

Eating one of these pellets allows you to exhale noxious vapors, damaging and sickening your enemies.

These pellets do not count against your starting character wealth.

stolen fury (wrath of the righteous).name: Stolen Fury (Wrath of the Righteous)
stolen fury (wrath of the righteous).description:
You were forced to take part in a demonic ritual as a youth after having been captured by cultists. Whatever the ritual’s purpose may have been, it didn’t work out the way your captors envisioned—rather than corrupting your soul, you absorbed the ritual’s energy and made it your own before you escaped to safety. Ever since, you’ve been haunted by strange nightmares about the ritual, and have long felt that the energies it bathed you in have changed you. Recently, those energies have changed — it’s as if you’ve finally managed to come to terms with your past and have turned the ritual’s aftereffects to your advantage, following the old adage of what doesn’t kill you makes you stronger. You’ve been unable to learn more about the ritual or what it was for, but the question lingers in the back of your head to this day. This nagging has instilled in you a fury against demonkind.

Benefits: Today, when you face demons in combat, those energies bolster your fury, granting you a +2 trait bonus on all combat maneuver checks against demons.

Associated Mythic Path

Champion

Multiple Characters

You and any other PC who takes this trait were all part of the same ritual, and it was only by working together that you managed to escape—further, the support of your fellow ritual survivors has played a key role in your coming to terms with it, and you retain a close bond of friendship (or perhaps a friendly rivalry) to this day.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on all combat maneuver checks against demons.

sharp eyes (tundra).name: Sharp Eyes (Tundra)
sharp eyes (tundra).description:
Requirements: Tundra

Benefits: You are immune to the dazzled condition. 

Usage: Update your Actor on the Attributes tab>Condition Immunities to add "dazzle" as an condition to which your actor is immune.

kobold faith (kobold).name: Kobold Faith (Kobold)
kobold faith (kobold).description:
You are one of the diehard kobold faithful, respected in your kobold community.

Requirements: Kobold

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks when dealing with other kobolds. Additionally, if your deity grants the Artifice, Earth, or Trickery domain, then your kobold faith unlocks a corresponding subdomain: Trap, Radiation, or Ambush.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus when dealing with other kobolds.

venom resistance.name: Venom Resistance
venom resistance.description:
You have spent considerable time in arid wastelands where girtablilus and other venomous creatures are common, and you have built up a resistance to poison.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Fortitude saving throws against poison, and once per day when attempting a saving throw to resist girtablilu poison, you can roll twice and take the higher result.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against poison.

talented.name: Talented
talented.description:
You are a virtuoso musician, actor, or storyteller.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on checks with a single Perform skill (your choice), and all Perform skills are always class skills for you.

Usage: Set corresponding Perform skill as target in Changes tab, once you've set up the skill in your actor.

commanding (human; keleshite).name: Commanding (Human; Keleshite)
commanding (human; keleshite).description:
Your family taught you that you always deserve respect.

Requirements: Keleshite Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Intimidate checks. Add a +1 trait bonus to the save DC of any language-dependent compulsion spells you cast.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to the save DC of any language-dependent compulsion spells you cast.

ever wary (tiefling).name: Ever Wary (Tiefling)
ever wary (tiefling).description:
Constant fear that your fiendish nature might provoke a sudden attack ensures that you never completely let down your guard.

Requirements: Tiefling

Benefits: During the surprise round and before your first action in combat, you can apply half your Dexterity bonus (if any) to your AC. You still count as flat-footed for the purposes of attacks and effects.

contextNotes.text: During the surprise round and before your first action in combat, you can apply half your Dexterity bonus (if any) to your AC. You still count as flat-footed for the purposes of attacks and effects.

the snoop (tyrant's grasp).name: The Snoop (Tyrant's Grasp)
the snoop (tyrant's grasp).description:
You know a little bit about most everything and everyone in Roslar’s Coffer. It may be your profession, as you could be an archivist, crier, or librarian—or you simply make a hobby of gossip. Either way, you have a gift for recall and reading others.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (history) and Knowledge (local) checks, and these both become class skills for you. Once each day you can reroll a single Bluff or Sense Motive check against a creature you have known for at least 24 hours, using the better result.

living bulwark (arqueros).name: Living Bulwark (Arqueros)
living bulwark (arqueros).description:
The Golden Bulwark inspires you to give your all for those you protect.

Requirements: Arqueros

Benefits: Once per day, when an adjacent ally is targeted by an attack but before the attack roll is attempted, you can interpose yourself as an immediate action. You become the target of the attack instead.

acclimatize alignment.name: Acclimatize Alignment
acclimatize alignment.description:
Like a climber ascending a mountain, you gradually grow acclimatized to an opposing planar alignment.

Benefits: For each consecutive day you remain on a mildly aligned plane that opposes your alignment, reduce your penalty on Charisma-based checks by 1, to a minimum of 0. This does not affect the penalty from a strongly aligned plane.

contextNotes.text: Reduce Charisma-based check penalties by 1 for each day on a mildly opposing plane, to a minimum of 0, excluding strongly opposing planes.

rider's bond (lastwall).name: Rider's Bond (Lastwall)
rider's bond (lastwall).description:
Your mount keenly understands your commands.

Requirements: Associated with the court of Castle Overwatch in Lastwall

Benefits: If you gain a horse as an animal companion or mount as one of your class abilities, it gains two bonus tricks that don’t count against the normal limit of tricks known by the animal.

empyreal focus (empyreal lords).name: Empyreal Focus (Empyreal Lords)
empyreal focus (empyreal lords).description:
Your dedication to the freedom espoused by the Empyreal Lords grants you an occasional boon.

Requirements: Empyreal Lords

Benefits: Once per day, before you make a skill check, you can use this boon to grant that skill check a +2 trait bonus.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to any one check. Usable once per day and must be used before you make the check.

fortune found (katapesh; vanaran).name: Fortune Found (Katapesh; Vanaran)
fortune found (katapesh; vanaran).description:
You have learned a great many tricks and techniques for bartering with humans.

Requirements: Vanaran native to Katapesh

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Appraise and Diplomacy checks, and one of these skills (your choice) is a class skill for you.

Usage

Select Appraise or Diplomacy when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

curator of mystic secrets.name: Curator of Mystic Secrets
curator of mystic secrets.description:
Your broad collection of lore grants unparalleled understanding of magic’s underlying principles.

Exemplar Type

Magic Exemplar

Benefits: Twice per day when spontaneously casting a spell altered by metamagic, you can do so without increasing the spell’s casting time. For each other magic trait you have, you can use this trait’s benefit an additional time each day.

Exemplar Traits

A character can have only one exemplar trait, and selecting an exemplar trait takes the place of two regular traits. Each exemplar trait is tied to a trait category, such as combat traits or regional traits; a character with an exemplar trait is no longer restricted to a single trait of that category and can select any number of such traits when gaining further traits. In addition, a character with an exemplar trait can select the Additional Traits feat any number of times and use the feat to gain an exemplar trait of a valid trait category for which they qualify.

Usage: Increase number of uses in details tab by one for every magic trait you have.

contextNotes.text: [[@resources.curatorOfMysticSecrets.max]]  times per day, when spontaneously casting a spell altered by metamagic, you can do so without increasing the  casting time. ([[@resources.curatorOfMysticSecrets.value]] remaining uses)

hardly a fool.name: Hardly a Fool
hardly a fool.description:
You have always been able to ferret out lies and deception. Maybe you worked as an investigator for a time, you came from a place rife with lies, or you’ve studied the human condition long enough to read a person’s face and get to the heart of his message.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks and a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against illusion effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against illusion effects.

transmuter of korada.name: Transmuter of Korada
transmuter of korada.description:
You learned the secrets of transmutation from a follower of the empyreal lord Korada.

Benefits: Whenever you cast a spell from the transmutation school, its effects manifest at +1 caster level. Additionally, select one of the following transmutation spells: @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.usdv1eqvibmxun6x]{bear's endurance}, @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.05i5rxwim12hwktu]{bull's strength}, @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.ns8jbp0ilbvbvuif]{cat's grace}, @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.d4oubr5bdoo8w1ev]{eagle's splendor}, @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.743anqr1ahefv8zd]{fox's cunning}, or @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.b9ggsagifzk4fwut]{owl's wisdom}. Once per day when you cast that spell, its duration is doubled. A spell affected by this trait cannot be modified further by the Extend Spell metamagic feat or similar abilities.

Usage: Increase caster level for each transmutation spell by 1 in Spell Details caster offset settings.

whip specialist (okeno).name: Whip Specialist (Okeno)
whip specialist (okeno).description:
Your time spent lashing slaves has taught you how to get the most out of a whip.

Requirements: Okeno

Benefits: You deal +1 point of damage on all attacks made with a @UUID[Compendium.pf1.weapons-and-ammo.Item.OPTlPwKg2NyQAjhe]{whip}.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] point of damage on all attacks made with a whip.

bounty hunter (isger).name: Bounty Hunter (Isger)
bounty hunter (isger).description:
You have made it your mission to help bring the bandits of Isger to justice.

Requirements: Isger

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Survival checks to find or follow tracks, and a +1 trait bonus on Perception checks to avoid being surprised.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus  to find or follow tracks.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to avoid being surprised.

earthbound (dwarf).name: Earthbound (Dwarf)
earthbound (dwarf).description:
Your druid spells use a handful of earth or sand as a divine focus in addition to the normal components.

Requirements: Dwarf

Benefits: While you are touching the ground, you add a +2 trait bonus to saving throw DCs and on caster level checks to overcome spell resistance for all spells you cast against creatures with the air subtype.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on saving throws and CL checks made to overcome spell resistance for all spells you cast against creatures with the air subtype.

augmented spell poisons (vishkanya).name: Augmented Spell Poisons (Vishkanya)
augmented spell poisons (vishkanya).description:
You can use your natural poison to augment the spells you cast.

Requirements: Vishkanya

Benefits: You can spend a use of your toxic racial trait and use a bit of your toxic saliva or blood as an additional material component for a spell with the poison descriptor to increase the spell’s save DC by 1.

contextNotes.text: You can spend a use of your toxic racial trait and use a bit of your toxic saliva or blood as an additional material component for a spell with the poison descriptor to increase the spell’s save DC by 1.

pirate duelist (ilizmagorti).name: Pirate Duelist (Ilizmagorti)
pirate duelist (ilizmagorti).description:
You’ve rubbed elbows with pirates of all stripes in Ilizmagorti’s taverns and convinced a few to show off their swordfighting skills.

Requirements: Ilizmagorti

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attacks of opportunity made with daggers, short swords, cutlasses, rapiers, scimitars, or hook hands.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus  on attacks of opportunity made with daggers, short swords, cutlasses, rapiers, scimitars, or hook hands.

beastkin.name: Beastkin
beastkin.description: Benefits: Select one specific type of animal (such as wolves or apes). You were raised from infancy by an animal or pack of animals of that type. You learned their ways and have more in common with your beastly kin than members of any humanoid race. You gain a +1 trait bonus on Survival checks and can use @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.ct9fhh8uawn8e4md]{speak with animals} as a spell-like ability at will to communicate with the type of animal that raised you.

relic hunter (human; garundi).name: Relic Hunter (Human; Garundi)
relic hunter (human; garundi).description:
You have made a study of the ancient lands and ruins of Garund and are familiar with the relics and artifacts their inhabitants left behind.

Requirements: Garundi Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Appraise and Knowledge (history) checks made to examine ruined buildings more than 1,000 years old as well as any ruined objects found in them. If such an item is magical, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Spellcraft checks to identify its properties and Use Magical Device checks to activate it.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on checks made to examine ruined buildings more than 1,000 years old as well as any ruined objects found in them.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks made to examine ruined buildings more than 1,000 years old as well as any ruined objects found in them.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks to identify  properties of  any magical item obtained from ruined buildings more than 1,000 years old.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks to activate any magical item obtained from ruined buildings more than 1,000 years old.

unearth secrets (tiefling; hellspawn).name: Unearth Secrets (Tiefling; Hellspawn)
unearth secrets (tiefling; hellspawn).description:
You have a gift for recognizing secret desires.

Requirements: Hellspawn Tiefling Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks when trying to get a hunch that might reveal a target's hidden vice, dark impulse, or any other craving it would not want publicly revealed.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus when trying to get a hunch that might reveal a target's hidden vice, dark impulse, or any other craving it would not want publicly revealed.

excitable (gnome).name: Excitable (Gnome)
excitable (gnome).description:
You demand that everything must happen right now, no matter what, and are too impatient to wait for everyone else to catch up. You finish people's sentences, blurt out the punch lines to slowly-told jokes, rarely make plans, and leap headlong into anything that looks like it might get interesting. While this has sometimes gotten you in trouble, it's also trained you to start moving a split second before everyone else, and that's saved your hide a few times as well.

Requirements: Gnome

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on all Initiative checks.

tainted spirit.name: Tainted Spirit
tainted spirit.description:
When you were a child, a parent or other person with authority over you made a minor pact with a fiend on your behalf, stealing some of your vitality in exchange for a minor boon.

Effects: At the end of any combat, you must attempt a Fortitude saving throw. The DC is 10 + the number of rounds you acted in combat. If you fail this save, you become fatigued for 10 minutes per round you acted during that encounter.

contextNotes.text: At the end of any combat, you must attempt a Fortitude saving throw. The DC is 10 + the number of rounds you acted in combat. If you fail this save, you become fatigued for 10 minutes per round you acted during that encounter.

reluctant apprentice.name: Reluctant Apprentice
reluctant apprentice.description:
Your early training grants you knowledge of the arcane.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (arcana) checks, and are considered trained in that skill even if you have no ranks in it.

contextNotes.text: Considered trained.

signaler (molthune).name: Signaler (Molthune)
signaler (molthune).description:
By watching Imperial troops, you know how to use flags, smoke signals, and other methods to send secret messages.

Requirements

Molthune

Benefits

You gain a +5 trait bonus on Bluff checks to receive secret messages and a +5 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks to intercept messages.

contextNotes.text: +[[5]] Trait bonus on Bluff checks to receive secret messages.
+[[5]] Trait bonus to intercept messages.

born to the water (human; ijo).name: Born to the Water (Human; Ijo)
born to the water (human; ijo).description:
You were born on the shore, and you easily live on and under the water.

Requirements: Ijo Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 bonus on Swim checks and only need to make Swim checks once every 2 hours to avoid fatigue, instead of once each hour.

contextNotes.text: You only need to make Swim checks once every 2 hours to avoid fatigue, instead of once each hour.

wary of danger (human; kellid).name: Wary of Danger (Human; Kellid)
wary of danger (human; kellid).description:
Your people are beset on all sides by threats, and you have developed a keen sense for danger.

Requirements: Kellid Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on initiative checks.

scion of goblinblood (goblin; isger).name: Scion of Goblinblood (Goblin; Isger)
scion of goblinblood (goblin; isger).description:
The fury of the Goblinblood Wars still courses through your veins.

Requirements: Goblin; Isger

Benefits: Whenever you're within 60 feet of an enemy hobgoblin, you gain a +1 trait bonus on all weapon damage rolls.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on all weapon damage rolls whenever you're within 60 feet of an enemy hobgoblin.

affable.name: Affable
affable.description:
You have a genial personality and make it a point to befriend and help people wherever you go. In your travels, you stop to aid others, tell interesting stories, and often buy rounds of drinks for patrons at the local taverns. You bring good cheer to those you encounter, and for this reason, you often find yourself attending important events or fruitful gatherings, and have even become an honorary member of many families. People find you trustworthy, and they are willing to share information with you.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks to gather information, and can do so in half the normal time. In addition, Diplomacy and Knowledge (local) are always class skills for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to gather information, and can do so in half the normal time

artist of battle in all forms.name: Artist of Battle in All Forms
artist of battle in all forms.description:
You employ complex combat tactics with ease.

Exemplar Type: Combat Exemplar

Benefits: Choose a type of combat maneuver. You gain a +1 trait bonus on combat maneuver checks to perform that maneuver, and you do not provoke an attack of opportunity from the target unless that creature has the appropriate Improved feat, such as Improved Grapple if you attempt a combat maneuver check to grapple. For every two other combat traits you have, choose an additional type of combat maneuver to gain these benefits.

Exemplar Traits: A character can have only one exemplar trait, and selecting an exemplar trait takes the place of two regular traits. Each exemplar trait is tied to a trait category, such as combat traits or regional traits; a character with an exemplar trait is no longer restricted to a single trait of that category and can select any number of such traits when gaining further traits. In addition, a character with an exemplar trait can select the Additional Traits feat any number of times and use the feat to gain an exemplar trait of a valid trait category for which they qualify.

Usage: Maintain selected combat maneuver(s) in context note.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on <selected Combat Maneuvers>; these don't provoke AoOs unless the target has "Improved <maneuver>" feat.

superstitious (human; kellid).name: Superstitious (Human; Kellid)
superstitious (human; kellid).description:
You have a healthy fear of sorcerers' speech and wizards' words that has helped you to survive their charms.

Requirements: Kellid Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against arcane spells.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against arcane spells.

lucky hound (riding dog).name: Lucky Hound (Riding dog)
lucky hound (riding dog).description:
Requirements: Riding dog

Benefits: Lucky hounds gain a +1 luck bonus on saves against spells or effects with the evil descriptor or which originate from evil outsiders or undead.

the daughter.name: The Daughter
the daughter.description:
You are filled with the light-hearted joy of the Daughter, who dances her way into springtime.

Requirements: 19 Gozran - 13 Desnus

Benefits: Every creature within 10 feet of you adds a +2 morale bonus to the DCs of checks to demoralize them.

jadwiga medicine (irrisen).name: Jadwiga Medicine (Irrisen)
jadwiga medicine (irrisen).description:
Requirements: Irrisen

Benefits: Once per day as an immediate action when you cast a spell of the healing subschool or a hex that produces the effect of such a spell, you can attempt an Intimidate check to demoralize your target or anyone who can see you within 30 feet. You gain a +2 trait bonus on this check.

contextNotes.text: Once per day as an immediate action, when you cast a spell of the healing subschool or a hex that produces the effect of such a spell, you can attempt to demoralize anyone who can see you within 30 feet. You gain a +2 trait bonus on this check. ([[@resources.jadwigaMedicineIrrisen.value]] remaining uses)

pragmatic activator.name: Pragmatic Activator
pragmatic activator.description:
While some figure out how to use magical devices with stubborn resolve, your approach is more pragmatic.

Benefits: You may use your Intelligence modifier when making Use Magic Device checks instead of your Charisma modifier.

aid allies (shadow lodge).name: Aid Allies (Shadow Lodge)
aid allies (shadow lodge).description:
Requirements: Shadow Lodge

Benefits: When using the aid another action, you give your ally a +3 bonus instead of a +2 bonus.

contextNotes.text: When you  aid another, you give your ally a +[[3]] bonus instead of a +2 bonus.
When you  aid another, you give your ally a +[[3]] bonus instead of a +2 bonus.

havoc of the society (sorcerer, pathfind.name: Havoc of the Society (Sorcerer, Pathfinder Society)
havoc of the society (sorcerer, pathfind.description:
Through countless hours studying vast libraries of arcane power, you have learned how to cause greater damage to your foes.

Requirements: Sorcerer, Pathfinder Society

Benefits: Whenever you cast a spell that deals damage, you gain a +1 trait bonus to the total damage dealt. This damage is considered force damage regardless of the type of damage dealt by the spell.

Usage: In your spell's use window, go to the action tab and add a new damage formula. In the formula type: 1[Havoc of the Society]. Change the type to force. Repeat for each spell capable of dealing damage.

barroom talespinner (skull and shackles).name: Barroom Talespinner (Skull and Shackles)
barroom talespinner (skull and shackles).description:
You grew up listening to tales of adventure and piracy on the high seas and the sea chanteys of old salts in dockside dives. You learned all about what it's like to be a pirate by talking with the sailors on shore leave, and learned how to tell a tale (or perform some other pirate entertainment) yourself. You went to a tavern called the Formidably Maid in Port Peril to earn a few coins with your tales of the sea. The patrons seemed pleased with your performance and bought you a few drinks. You had just remembered a tale of an unfortunate sailor who found himself press-ganged on a pirate ship after his drink was drugged when suddenly everything went black.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks and one category of Perform checks, and one of these skills is a class skill for you. In addition, once per week you can make a DC 15 Knowledge (local) or Intelligence check to see if you know a popular legendary pirate tale, the telling of which grants you a +1 trait bonus on checks made to influence NPCs on the Wormwood.

Usage

  1. Select one category of Perform when you take this trait.

  2. Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the chosen Perform skill.

  3. Select Diplomacy or the previously selected Perform type

  4. Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

restless hunger (venkelvore; goblin).name: Restless Hunger (Venkelvore; Goblin)
restless hunger (venkelvore; goblin).description:
Requirements: Venkelvore, Goblin

Benefits: If you have a piece of food (or something a goblin might consider food), you can quickly eat it as part of a charge attack once per day. Doing so increases your speed by 10 feet for 1 round.

born in the light (dhampir).name: Born in the Light (Dhampir)
born in the light (dhampir).description:
The circumstance of your birth and upbringing left little room to accommodate your weaknesses, and many hours toiling in broad daylight have decreased the severity of your innate hindrances.

Requirements: Dhampir

Benefits: You gain a +2 bonus on checks and saves affected by the Endurance feat (this bonus does not stack with the bonus granted by the @UUID[Compendium.pf1.feats.Item.ehqx8txNRGMaNOPt]{Endurance feat}).

When your light sensitivity causes you to become dazzled, you do not take the –1 penalty on sight-based Perception checks.

contextNotes.text: When your light sensitivity causes you to become dazzled, you do not take the –1 penalty on sight-based Perception checks.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to resist nonlethal damage from exhaustion.  This bonus does not stack with the bonus from the Endurance feat.

+[[2]] Trait bonus to continue running; or  to hold your breath; and  to avoid nonlethal damage from a forced march, or starvation or thirst. This bonus does not stack with the bonus from the Endurance feat.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to avoid nonlethal damage from hot or cold environments; and  to resist damage from suffocation.  This bonus does not stack with the bonus from the Endurance feat.

poisonous upbringing (drow).name: Poisonous Upbringing (Drow)
poisonous upbringing (drow).description:
There were a few touchy moments in your education in the use of poisons.

Requirements: Drow

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on all saves to resist poison effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to resist poison effects.

convincing liar.name: Convincing Liar
convincing liar.description:
You've spent your life perfecting the art of half-truths in order to accomplish what you need to.

Requirements: Bellflower Network

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Bluff or Intimidate checks and one of these skills (your choice) is a class skill for you.

Usage: Select Bluff or Intimidate when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

draconic lineage.name: Draconic Lineage
draconic lineage.description:
Somewhere in your family line, draconic power entered your bloodline.

Benefits: As a result of this lineage, you gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against fear and against any effect created by a creature of the dragon type. If you gain a bloodrager or sorcerer bloodline, you must choose draconic.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against fear and against any effect created by a creature of the dragon type.

law enforcer.name: Law Enforcer
law enforcer.description:
If you follow this code, you are always alert to those who transgress against the laws of civilization.

Requirement:

Code of Vigilance

Benefits: You receive a +2 bonus on Sense Motive checks to gain a hunch from social situations, and begin play with a pair of masterwork manacles.

Usage: to take this trait you must follow the Vigilance Knightly code of conduct.

If a knight violates their code of conduct, they are disgraced and must perform penance to atone for their transgressions. until the penance is completed, disgraced knights no longer receive benefits from their knightly traits.

Vigilance: Knights who follow the code of vigilance believe that people are prone to chaos and society will eventually devolve into anarchy if given the opportunity. In order for society to continue, these knights must constantly remind people of the law, and take it upon themselves to enforce it in the absence of other authority figures.

Their vows are as follows.

  • I will be familiar with local laws and adhere to them strictly.

  • If I witness a crime, I will attempt to capture the criminal and report his actions to the authorities.

  • If any I meet are known to be escaped criminals, I will attempt to apprehend them by any means necessary.

  • I shall mete out appropriate punishment for those who violate the law, but only if no acceptable authority is readily available.

  • I shall remember that none are above the law, and that law is absolute. There shall be no mitigating circumstances for any individual, regardless of station or position.

contextNotes.text:
+[[2]] Trait bonus to gain a hunch from social
situations.

lessons of chaldira (chaldira zuzaristan.name: Lessons of Chaldira (Chaldira Zuzaristan)
lessons of chaldira (chaldira zuzaristan.description:
Your studies of Chaldira Zuzaristan's exploits have given you a knack for avoiding trouble.

Requirements: Chaldira Zuzaristan

Benefits: Once per day, when you fail a saving throw, you can reroll the saving throw. You must take the second result even if it is worse.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, when you fail a saving throw, you can reroll the saving throw. You must take the second result even if it is worse.

speech of the wilds (aasimar; idyllkin).name: Speech Of The Wilds (Aasimar; Idyllkin)
speech of the wilds (aasimar; idyllkin).description:
Like your agathion ancestor, you have a knack for communicating with other creatures, and you are adept at breaking down language barriers.

Requirements: Idyllkin Aasimar Heritage

Benefits: You can speak one extra language.

Usage

Select one (1) language of your choice when you take this trait.

Update the Granted Proficiencies>Languages section of the details tab of this trait to reflect this choice.

varki landspeaker (human; varki).name: Varki Landspeaker (Human; Varki)
varki landspeaker (human; varki).description:
You are deeply attuned to the lands your people call home and the spirits that live there.

Requirements: Varki Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Survival checks in arctic or cold terrain. In addition, while in such terrain, you may query the spirits of the land once per day to gain a +5 trait bonus on a single skill check to follow tracks, forage, or find shelter.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus  in arctic or cold terrain.
+[[5]] Trait bonus, once per day on a single check, to follow tracks, forage, or find shelter  in arctic or cold terrain.

killer.name: Killer
killer.description:
You made your first kill at a very young age and found the task of war or murder to your liking. You either take particular pride in a well-placed blow, or vile pleasure in such a strike as you twist the blade to maximize the pain.

Benefits: You deal additional damage equal to your weapon's critical hit modifier when you score a successful critical hit with a weapon; this additional damage is added to the final total, and is not multiplied by the critical hit multiple itself. This extra damage is a trait bonus.

Usage: Add @action.ability.critMult[Trait] to weapon's Critical Damage Bonus Formula.

burst of speed (dort charger).name: Burst of Speed (Dort Charger)
burst of speed (dort charger).description:
Requirements: Horse (Dort Charger)

Benefits: Three times per day, while riding a Dort charger and making a charge attack, you can increase your mount’s speed by 15 feet as a free action.

black sheep (any campaign).name: Black Sheep (Any Campaign)
black sheep (any campaign).description:
You were born and raised in town. You know many of the region’s secrets and the locals already know who you are. You aren’t quite hated in town, but folk seem to think you’re a troublemaker and a bastard and not to be trusted. The town’s tougher folk respect you, but the law-abiding citizens don’t.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (local) checks, and Knowledge (local) is a class skill for you. In addition, you can choose any one notorious citizen from below as an ally; depending on the person you pick, you’ll get different benefits.

Notorious Citizens

Apothecary

Everyone suspects the sweaty, shifty-eyed apothecary sells poisons from his cluttered, acrid smelling wreck of a shop. You’re one of the few in town who know this for a fact.

You start the game with 400 gp worth of poison, and the apothecary will continue to sell you poison as long as you don’t spread the word.

Bitter Nobleman

A local unscrupulous family was hit hard recently, revealing criminal ties that damaged its reputation as well. One noble is eager to rebuild his presence in town, and you’re one of the ones he’s selected as an agent.

Choose one of the following skills: Bluff, Sleight of Hand, or Stealth. Your work for the family gives you a +1 trait bonus on that skill, and it is always a class skill for you.


Usage

  1. Select Apothecary or Bitter Nobleman when you take this trait.

  2. If you select Apothecary, you may add the permitted amount of poison to your inventory, then delete the untargeted change on the changes tab.

  3. If you select Bitter Nobleman, Choose one of the optional skills: Bluff, Sleight of Hand, or Stealth; Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice and Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect this same choice.

irritable diplomat (half-orc).name: Irritable Diplomat (Half-Orc)
irritable diplomat (half-orc).description:
Most people expect half-orcs to be savage and violent, and you’re used to working a little harder to overcome their assumptions. Unfortunately, you’re still working on keeping patient when they ignore your efforts.

Requirements: Half-Orc

Benefits: Once per day, you may choose to gain a +5 trait bonus on any Diplomacy check, but if it fails, you take a –2 penalty on all Charisma-based checks for the next 10 minutes.

contextNotes.text: +[[5]] Trait bonus, once per day on a single check. If that check fails, you take a -[[2]] penalty on all Charisma-based checks for the next 10 minutes.

linked surge.name: Linked Surge
linked surge.description: Benefits: Once per day, you may make a Strength- or Constitution-based ability check or skill check using you eidolon’s ability score in place of your own. Your eidolon must be summoned and with 30 feet for you to use this trait.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you may use your eidolon’s ability score for this check in place of your own. Your eidolon must be summoned and with 30 feet.
Once per day, you may use your eidolon’s ability score for this check in place of your own. Your eidolon must be summoned and with 30 feet.

stalwart of the society (paladin, pathfi.name: Stalwart of the Society (Paladin, Pathfinder Society)
stalwart of the society (paladin, pathfi.description:
The courageous powers of the ancient orders of Golarion's paladins are open to you thanks to the Society's records of heroic deeds.

Requirements: Paladin, Pathfinder Society

Benefits: Your aura of courage class ability grants an additional +1 trait bonus on saving throws against fear effects.

contextNotes.text: Aura of courage grants an additional +[[1]] trait bonus against fear effects.

arcane depth (nethys).name: Arcane Depth (Nethys)
arcane depth (nethys).description:
You have studied the great masters of spellcraft, and your knowledge is exceptional.

Requirements: Nethys

Benefits: You gain a bonus of either +1 on Spellcraft checks or +2 on Knowledge (arcana) checks. Which bonus you receive is chosen when you take the trait and may not be changed.

Usage: Choose Spellcraft or Knowledge (arcana) when this trait is taken and add that skill on the Changes tab. If you choose knowledge (arcana) increase the bonus to +2 instead of +1

impatient.name: Impatient
impatient.description:
You love leaping into battle at the earliest opportunity, and it frustrates you to wait for others to act.

Effects: You can’t delay or ready actions, and if you are the last of your allies to act in a round of combat, you take a –1 penalty on ability checks, attack rolls, saving throws, and skill checks.

contextNotes.text: You can’t delay or ready actions, and if you are the last of your allies to act in a round of combat, you take a -[[1]] penalty on ability checks, attack rolls, saving throws, and skill checks.
You can’t delay or ready actions, and if you are the last of your allies to act in a round of combat, you take a -[[1]] penalty on ability checks, attack rolls, saving throws, and skill checks.
You can’t delay or ready actions, and if you are the last of your allies to act in a round of combat, you take a -[[1]] penalty on ability checks, attack rolls, saving throws, and skill checks.
You can’t delay or ready actions, and if you are the last of your allies to act in a round of combat, you take a -[[1]] penalty on ability checks, attack rolls, saving throws, and skill checks.
You can’t delay or ready actions, and if you are the last of your allies to act in a round of combat, you take a -[[1]] penalty on ability checks, attack rolls, saving throws, and skill checks.

clockwork engineer.name: Clockwork Engineer
clockwork engineer.description:
You have experience with the ancient clockwork constructs that roam certain wastelands, and this has granted you an understanding of these guardians.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on damage rolls against creatures with the Clockwork subtype.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on damage rolls against creatures with the Clockwork subtype

omen.name: Omen
omen.description:
You are the harbinger of some future event. Whether this event bodes good or ill, you exude an ominous presence.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Intimidate checks, and Intimidate is always a class skill for you. Once per day, you may attempt to demoralize an opponent as a swift action.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you may attempt to demoralize an opponent as a swift action.

arcane temper.name: Arcane Temper
arcane temper.description: You have quick reactions and fierce concentration. You gain a +1 trait bonus on concentration and initiative checks.

influence.name: Influence
influence.description:
Your position in society grants you special insight into others, and special consideration or outright awe from others.

Benefits: Choose one of the following skills: Diplomacy, Intimidate, or Sense Motive. You gain a +1 trait bonus on that skill, and it is always a class skill for you.

Usage: Add one of the skill mentioned above in the class skills section of the Details tab of this trait.

Update the Target of the Change on the Changes tab to the selected skill.

twilight zeal (tiefling).name: Twilight Zeal (Tiefling)
twilight zeal (tiefling).description:
The gloom of night and of the places beyond the reach of the sun gives you comfort and courage.

Requirements: Tiefling

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Will saving throws when in dim light or darkness.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus when in dim light or darkness.

flotsam (the shackles).name: Flotsam (The Shackles)
flotsam (the shackles).description:
You were one of only a few that survived the destruction of the merchant vessel Vantage. Your crew was more than able to hold off the blundering pirate attackers, but their red dragon ally proved too much for your defenses. As you watched the sharks and eels feast on the bodies of your fallen comrades, you vowed to see Aashaq slain.

Requirements: The Shackles

Benefits: You gain a +2 bonus on Reflex saves against spells and effects with the fire descriptor.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus  against spells and effects with the fire descriptor.

deformed (lamashtu).name: Deformed (Lamashtu)
deformed (lamashtu).description:
You have monstrous deformities.

Requirements: Deformed

Benefits: Your deformities grant you a +1 trait bonus on Intimidate checks, and Intimidate becomes a class skill for you.

deathspeaker (urgathoa).name: Deathspeaker (Urgathoa)
deathspeaker (urgathoa).description:
You have experience in dealing with the undead.

Requirements: Urgathoa

Benefits: Your experience gives you a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks when dealing with undead, as well as a +2 trait bonus on Charisma checks made to influence undead (for example, as part of the command undead spell).

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus when dealing with undead.
+[[2]] Trait bonus on checks made to influence undead.

dungeon dweller (subterranean dungeon).name: Dungeon Dweller (Subterranean Dungeon)
dungeon dweller (subterranean dungeon).description:
Not only are you competent while deep underground, you thrive in these perilous environs. Whether you were born in one of the labyrinthine tunnels that riddle the islands of the Shackles or your mysterious parental figures brought you up in the sand-ridden corridors of some forgotten Osirian pyramid, your emergence on Golarion is somehow tied to one of the world’s infamous subterranean dungeons.

Requirements: Any Subterranean Dungeon

Benefits: You start with one additional language chosen from the following list: Aklo, Dark Folk, Draconic, Goblin, Necril, Orc, or Undercommon. In addition, whenever you must attempt a Survival check to avoid getting lost in a dungeon, you may roll the check twice and take the better result. Suggestion: Consider playing an uncommon race from the Pathfinder RPG Advanced Race Guide.

Usage

Select one (1) of Aklo, Dark Folk, Draconic, Goblin, Necril, Orc, or Undercommon when you take this trait.

Update the Granted Proficiencies>Languages section of the details tab of this trait to reflect this choice.

contextNotes.text: Whenever you must attempt a check to avoid getting lost in a dungeon, you may roll the check twice and take the better result

spiritual forester (forest of spirits).name: Spiritual Forester (Forest of Spirits)
spiritual forester (forest of spirits).description:
You grew up in a small settlement along the outskirts of the Forest of Spirits, and have learned much about the woods as well as about their supernatural inhabitants.

Requirements: Forest of Spirits

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (nature) and Knowledge (planes) checks, and one of these skills becomes a class skill for you. 

Usage: Select Knowledge (nature) or Knowledge (planes) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

spirit touched (return of the runelords).name: Spirit Touched (Return of the Runelords)
spirit touched (return of the runelords).description:
As a child, you were influenced by overwhelming emotional trauma associated with death or undeath, and that influence marked you for the rest of your life. You may have been possessed by a ghost, had strange dreams in a haunted house, or suffered from an affliction from an undead source. You might even have had a near-death experience, or actually died and been returned to life. Work with your GM to determine the details of how you became spirit touched; she may have suggestions for you to tie your experience more tightly into the themes of the Return of the Runelords Adventure Path.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on all saving throws made against supernatural attacks, spells, or spell-like abilities from undead creatures.

Once per adventure, you may automatically succeed at a Constitution check to stabilize or a saving throw to remove a negative level.

contextNotes.text: Once per adventure, you may automatically succeed at a Constitution check to stabilize or a saving throw to remove a negative level.
Once per adventure, you may automatically succeed at a Constitution check to stabilize or a saving throw to remove a negative level.
+[[2]] Trait bonus against supernatural attacks, spells, or spell-like abilities from undead creatures.

buried anxiety (tiefling; spitespawn).name: Buried Anxiety (Tiefling; Spitespawn)
buried anxiety (tiefling; spitespawn).description:
You laugh at what makes others tremble, but are secretly disturbed by a particular sort of mundane item.

Requirements: Spitespawn Tiefling Heritage

Benefits: Pick a specific sort of object, color, sound, or similar relatively common phenomena. You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws made to resist fear effects except when you can see, hear, smell, or taste that which unnerves you.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to resist fear effects except when you can see, hear, smell, or taste that which unnerves you.

dragon pact.name: Dragon Pact
dragon pact.description:
At some point your family served a dragon, and acted as its emissary to others. Some members of your family have benefited from this arrangement, gaining some small fraction of that dragon's noble bearing.

Benefits: Choose one category of true dragon (chromatic, metallic, primal, etc.). You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks against that category of dragon and a +1 trait bonus on Intimidate checks against all creatures of the dragon type that are not of the same category of true dragon.

Usage

Choose one category of true dragon (Chromatic, Esoteric, Imperial, Metallic, Outer, Planar, Primal) when you take this trait.

Update the text of the notes on the changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against true dragons of the <chosen> category.
+[[1]] Trait bonus against true dragons NOT of the <chosen> category.

brigh's insight (brigh).name: Brigh's Insight (Brigh)
brigh's insight (brigh).description:
You’re used to living with your own madness.

Requirements: Brigh

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against madness and confusion effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against madness and confusion effects.

big ears (goblin).name: Big Ears (Goblin)
big ears (goblin).description:
Your massive ears are your pride and joy, and other goblins claim you can hear a flea scream as it falls off a goblin dog.

Requirements: Goblin

Benefits: While this might not quite be the case, you gain a +2 bonus on all Perception checks made to hear noises. Additionally, you can hear noises that normally only dogs or other animals can hear, like the sound of a dog whistle.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] bonus to hear noises. Additionally, you can hear noises that normally only dogs or other animals can hear.

born under the green star (castrovel).name: Born under the Green Star (Castrovel)
born under the green star (castrovel).description:
You were born on Castrovel and touched by the planet’s profound psychic bounty.

Requirements: Castrovel

Benefits: Treat your caster level as 1 higher when casting spells and spell-like abilities with the mind-affecting descriptor.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]]  to caster level when casting spells and spell-like abilities with the mind-affecting descriptor.

planar escapee.name: Planar Escapee
planar escapee.description:
You were a prisoner or slave in a city on the Elemental Planes, such as the cruel efreet’s City of Brass on the Plane of Fire or the dwarven city of Urgashaz on the Plane of Earth. When you escaped that city, you had to use your captors’ weapons and magic against them in addition to your sheer wits, and you learned how to find weaknesses in their natural resistances.

Benefits: At character creation, select acid, cold, electricity, or fire (once chosen, this selection can’t be changed). Once per day as a swift action, you can choose a single creature that is within 30 feet of you and within your line of sight; treat that creature’s resistance against your chosen energy as 5 lower than its normal energy resistance for 1 round. This ability has no effect if the creature has no resistance to the chosen energy or if the creature has immunity to the chosen energy.

student of philosophy.name: Student of Philosophy
student of philosophy.description:
You were trained in a now defunct philosophical tradition—such as that of the now destroyed Galtan universities or Lirgeni astrologers—and learned to use logic and reason to persuade others.

Benefits: You can use your Intelligence modifier in place of your Charisma modifier on Diplomacy checks to persuade others and on Bluff checks to convince others that a lie is true. (This trait does not affect Diplomacy checks to gather information or Bluff checks to feint in combat.)

Suggested Characters

Arodenites, Galtans, Sodden Lands natives.

contextNotes.text: You can use your Intelligence modifier in place of your Charisma modifier to persuade others, but not on checks to gather information.
You can use your Intelligence modifier in place of your Charisma modifier  to convince others that a lie is true, but not on checks to feint in combat.

king in waiting.name: King in Waiting
king in waiting.description:
You’ve been raised with tales of the great linnorms, and taught that the death curses of these vile dragons cannot stop the truly mighty.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saves to resist curses and spells with the curse descriptor and a +3 trait bonus on saves to resist curses originating from a linnorm.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to resist curses and spells with the curse descriptor.
+[[3]] Trait bonus on saves to resist curses originating from a linnorm.

monster stalker.name: Monster Stalker
monster stalker.description:
You’ve been raised to hunt certain beasts.

Benefits: Choose either aberrations, animals, dragons, fey, magical beasts, oozes, or vermin. You gain a limited version of the scent ability (15 feet normal, 30 feet upwind, 5 feet downwind) that detects only your chosen creature type.

spirit animal (realm of the mammoth lord.name: Spirit Animal (Realm of the Mammoth Lords)
spirit animal (realm of the mammoth lord.description:
A spirit animal watches over you.

Requirements: Realm of the Mammoth Lords

Benefits: Choose one of the following spirit animals. The benefit you receive depends on which of the following spirit animals you choose.

Mammoth: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Fortitude saves.

Smilodon: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Reflex saves.

Woolly Rhinoceros: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Will saves.

Usage: Select a spirit animal when you take this trait.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect the save type corresponding to your choice.

self-sustaining.name: Self-Sustaining
self-sustaining.description:
As a follower of Razmir, the Prophecies of Kalistrade, or some other tradition that preaches self-reliance, you make a point of not seeking divine aid for minor matters.

Benefits: When you gain this trait, choose one of the following 0-level spells: create water, mending, purify food and drink, or stabilize. You can cast this spell once per day as a spell-like ability, using your character level as your caster level. Once chosen, this selection can’t change.

Usage: When you add the spell to the spellbook, you can adjust the "0/0" display to be "1/1", change the "Casts Per Day Used" formula to 1, then change the spellbook's spell slots to match.

venom-drenched (norgorber; halfling).name: Venom-Drenched (Norgorber; Halfling)
venom-drenched (norgorber; halfling).description:
You have nothing to fear from one sort of poison. In fact, you can actually drink the toxic brew in order to make your blood dangerous.

Requirements: Norgorber; Halfling

Benefits: You are immune to one specific non-magical poison. If you ingest a dose of that poison (even if it is a contact, inhaled, or injury poison), it remains in your system for 24 hours; any creature that bites you during that time is subject to the poison’s effects.

numerologist (osirion).name: Numerologist (Osirion)
numerologist (osirion).description:
Having studied the sciences and other formal academics in your formative years, you swiftly became interested in the esoteric numerological practices of the ancient Osirian mathematicians, and know how to apply this science to dungeons.

Requirements: Osirion

Benefits: Once per day, you can attempt a Perception check while studying a trap (a standard action). The DC of this check is equal to the Disable Device DC of the trap – 5. If you succeed, you identify the DC necessary to disable the trap, as well as the DC of the Reflex save (if any) to avoid its effects.

elven polytheist (any elven deity).name: Elven Polytheist (any elven deity)
elven polytheist (any elven deity).description:
Your knowledge of the diverse elven gods has given you a broad perspective.

Requirements: Any elven deity

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (religion) skill checks, and Knowledge (religion) is a class skill for you.

beast bully (tiefling).name: Beast Bully (Tiefling)
beast bully (tiefling).description:
You have learned how to exploit the fear felt by creatures of the natural world when they sense the shadow in your soul.

Requirements: Tiefling

Benefits: You can make an Intimidate check instead of a Handle Animal check when trying to handle or push an animal.

contextNotes.text: You can make an Intimidate check instead of a Handle Animal check when trying to handle or push an animal.
You can make an Intimidate check instead of a Handle Animal check when trying to handle or push an animal.

desert child (desert).name: Desert Child (Desert)
desert child (desert).description:
You were born and raised in rocky deserts and are accustomed to high temperatures.

Requirements: Desert

Benefits: You gain a +4 trait bonus on any saving throws made to resist the effects of being in hot conditions and a +1 trait bonus on all saving throws against fire effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus vs fire effects.
+[[4]] Trait bonus to resist the effects of being in hot conditions.

bauble fascination (ratfolk).name: Bauble Fascination (Ratfolk)
bauble fascination (ratfolk).description:
You are fond of eldritch baubles and are skilled at activating their wondrous powers.

Requirements: Ratfolk

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Use Magic Device checks and Use Magic Device is always a class skill for you.

mentored.name: Mentored
mentored.description:
A tutor or private instructor guided you in learning your art, profession, or trade, and through your education, you became capable of teaching and guiding others.

Benefits: Choose a single Craft, Perform, or Profession skill. You gain a +1 trait bonus on checks with that skill. You also gain a +1 trait bonus when you aid another’s skill check with any skill.

Usage: Choose a single Craft, Perform, or Profession skill when taking this trait. Update the Target of the change on the changes Tab of this trait.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] to aid another with any skill.

brute (orc).name: Brute (Orc)
brute (orc).description:
You are an especially large and hulking individual, and when you strike, you aim to deliver the maximum amount of pain.

Requirements: Half-Orc, Orc

Benefits: Whenever you score a critical threat, you add (but not multiply, in the case of a confirmed critical hit) an amount of damage equal to your weapon’s critical modifier.

Usage: For each weapon your actor wields, modify as follows:

  • Edit the weapon.

  • On the weapon's details tab, edit the attack.

  • On the attack's Action tab, scroll down to the Damage section and click the "+ Add Critical Damage Bonus Formula" button

  • In the damage formula, enter the weapon's Critical Multiplier.

  • The type (B/P/S) should match that of the weapon.

The above steps will add damage automatically to confirmed criticals but, as this trait applies to all threats, this bonus damage must still be added manually to regular damage on threats which are not confirmed.

contextNotes.text: Whenever you score a critical threat, you add (but not multiply, in the case of a confirmed critical hit) an amount of damage equal to your weapon’s critical modifier.

corpse cannibal (urgathoa).name: Corpse Cannibal (Urgathoa)
corpse cannibal (urgathoa).description:
Your faith encourages you to indulge a variety of perverse hungers, leading you to shrug at even some of the darkest taboos.

Requirements: Urgathoa

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Fortitude saves against diseases and can gain sustenance from rotting flesh with no negative side effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against diseases and can gain sustenance from rotting flesh with no negative side effects.

plainsman (m'neri plains).name: Plainsman (M'neri Plains)
plainsman (m'neri plains).description:
You live and work on the M'neri Plains.

Requirements: M'neri Plains

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on all Knowledge (geography) and Survival checks regarding the M'neri Plains.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on all checks regarding the M'neri Plains.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on all checks regarding the M'neri Plains.

imperial soldier (molthune).name: Imperial Soldier (Molthune)
imperial soldier (molthune).description:
You've served in the Imperial Army of Molthune.

Requirements: Molthune

Benefits: Choose one of the following skills: Heal, Intimidate, or Ride. You gain a +1 trait bonus on that skill, and it is always a class skill for you. 

Usage: Select Heal, Intimidate, or Ride when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

silvertongued eunuch (quain).name: Silvertongued Eunuch (Quain)
silvertongued eunuch (quain).description:
You were brought up as part of Quain's strictly eunuch bureaucracy, trading your sexual potency for education and tact.

Requirements: Quain

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (nobility) and Diplomacy checks.

betrayal of trust.name: Betrayal of Trust
betrayal of trust.description:
Benefits: If you have the trait @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.7P4hpXJPrQz1BXW1]{Tight Bonds}, you swap it for this trait at the moment you decide to betray the relationship. (You need not wait until you have actually committed an act of betrayal.) You gain a +2 trait bonus on Bluff checks, increasing to +4 on checks specifically made to conceal or deny your infidelity.

It is possible to regain the Tight Bonds trait, for either the same or a different relationship, but it requires substantial in-character work. The GM decides when this trait reverts back to the original one.

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Trait bonus on checks specifically made to conceal or deny your infidelity.

dunewalker (osirion faction).name: Dunewalker (Osirion Faction)
dunewalker (osirion faction).description:
Your people have long roamed the shifting and treacherous sands of Osirion, and you do not fear the harsh wrath of the unforgiving desert.

Requirements: Osirion Faction

Benefits: You gain a +4 trait bonus on Fortitude saves to resist nonlethal damage from hot conditions, and you can always move through nonmagical sand as if it were normal terrain.

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Trait bonus  to resist nonlethal damage from hot conditions.

red mantis bleeding.name: Red Mantis Bleeding
red mantis bleeding.description:
You witnessed the training of Red Mantis assassins, either as a child on Mediogalti Island or as an aspirant, and learned to leave bleeding wounds.

Benefits: Whenever you confirm a critical hit with a slashing weapon, you also deal 1 point of bleed damage.

contextNotes.text: You deal 1 point of bleed damage whenever you confirm a critical hit with a slashing weapon.

indomitable (liberty's edge).name: Indomitable (Liberty's Edge)
indomitable (liberty's edge).description:
Your strong, self-reliant swagger has made you more resistant to domination and control.

Requirements: Liberty's Edge

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws versus enchantment spells and effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] trait bonus vs enchantments

distance aptitude.name: Distance Aptitude
distance aptitude.description:
You were exposed to the magic of an elf gate, portal, or some other form of distance-spanning magic when you were young. This contact with ancient world-spanning magic has granted you insight into means to extend the range of your own spells.

Benefits: Treat your caster level as 1 higher for the purposes of determining the range of spells and spell-like abilities.

Suggested Characters

Elves, Numerians, Sarkorians.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] to CL to determine the range of spells and spell-like abilities.

caravan nomad (varisia; kitsune).name: Caravan Nomad (Varisia; Kitsune)
caravan nomad (varisia; kitsune).description:
Your family has lived on the open roads of Varisia for untold generations, impersonating nomadic Varisians.

Requirements: Kitsune native to Varisia

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Bluff and Perform checks, and one of these skills is a class skill for you.

Usage

Select Bluff or Perform when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

poison connoisseur (taldor).name: Poison Connoisseur (Taldor)
poison connoisseur (taldor).description:
When the ceaseless bickering and elaborate posturing of Taldan politics proves ineffective, it is not unheard of for the nobility of Taldor to turn to poison to solve their problems, and therefore many are taught to identify the telltale signs of commonly used toxins.

Requirements: Taldor

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against ingested poisons. Additionally, you can cast detect poison as a spell-like ability once per day, using your character level as your caster level.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against ingested poisons.

meticulous artisan (lantern lodge).name: Meticulous Artisan (Lantern Lodge)
meticulous artisan (lantern lodge).description:
A well-carved statuette, the smooth stitching on a fine coat, the keen edge on a blade—you know how to craft masterpieces.

Requirements: Lantern Lodge

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Day Job checks using the Craft skill.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on Day Job checks.

faithful artist (desna).name: Faithful Artist (Desna)
faithful artist (desna).description:
With Desna's blessing, you have pursued an artistic path.

Requirements: Desna

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus to one Perform skill and Perform is always a class skill for you.

Usage: Select one Perform skill when you take this trait.

Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the chosen skill.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

demon eradicator (mendevian crusader).name: Demon Eradicator (Mendevian Crusader)
demon eradicator (mendevian crusader).description:
Requirements: Mendevian Crusader

Benefits: Anytime an outsider with the demon subtype must make a Will save against one of your spells, increase the DC of that save by +1.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] to the  Will DC of your spells versus outsiders with demon subtype.

eldritch smith (dwarf).name: Eldritch Smith (Dwarf)
eldritch smith (dwarf).description:
You are learned in the secret lore of the forge, rituals handed down for generations that some say come from Torag himself.

Requirements: Dwarf

Benefits: Whenever you use the Craft skill or a crafting feat to make a stone or metal item, you reduce the cost of making the item by 5%. This includes metal-headed weapons with nonmetal parts, such as axes and spears.

contextNotes.text: Making a stone or metal item costs 5% less.

industrious (gnome).name: Industrious (Gnome)
industrious (gnome).description:
You are helpful and hardworking.

Requirements: Gnome

Benefits: When you successfully use the aid another action to aid an ally’s Craft or Profession check, that character gains an additional +1 trait bonus on the check (in addition to the +2 bonus normally granted for successfully aiding another). If you have a racial bonus on that skill, your ally gains a +2 trait bonus instead.

contextNotes.text: When you  aid another  the aided character gains an additional +[[1]] Trait bonus on their check (in addition to the +2 bonus normally granted for successfully aiding another). If you also have a racial bonus on that skill, your ally gains a +[[2]] Trait bonus instead.

When you  aid another  the aided character gains an additional +[[1]] Trait bonus on their check (in addition to the +2 bonus normally granted for successfully aiding another). If you also have a racial bonus on that skill, your ally gains a +[[2]] Trait bonus instead.

failed apprentice (half-elf).name: Failed Apprentice (Half-Elf)
failed apprentice (half-elf).description:
As a child, your parents sent you to a distant wizard's tower as an apprentice so that you might learn the arcane arts. Unfortunately, you had no arcane talent whatsoever, though you did learn a great deal about the workings of magic and how to resist them.

Requirements: Half-Elf

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saves against arcane spells.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against arcane spells.

irrepressible.name: Irrepressible
irrepressible.description:
Your hope for a brighter future blessed by the gods, combined with your powerful personality, allows you to force your way free of spells that cloud the mind.

Benefits: You can use your Charisma modifier in place of your Wisdom modifier when attempting Will saving throws against charm and compulsion effects.

Suggested Characters

Gnomes, Keleshites, Shelyn worshipers.

contextNotes.text: You can use your Charisma modifier in place of your Wisdom modifier when attempting Will saving throws against charm and compulsion effects.

follower of the stars (desna).name: Follower of the Stars (Desna)
follower of the stars (desna).description:
Requirements: Desna

Benefits: You gain a +4 trait bonus when using Survival to navigate under clear skies.

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Trait bonus to navigate under clear skies.

oagan diver (the sodden lands).name: Oagan Diver (the Sodden Lands)
oagan diver (the sodden lands).description:
You know how to operate the diving bells of Oagon at the mouth of the Black Flow. You've dived the streets of sunken Lirgen yourself, and you know what to expect.

Requirements: Sodden Lands

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Swim checks, and Swim is always a class skill for you.

purple scales (kobold).name: Purple Scales (Kobold)
purple scales (kobold).description:
Your outlandish purple scales made you an outcast from your tribe and forced you to survive in isolation at the edges of kobold society. While wandering alone through the Darklands, you contacted several other underground races and expanded your linguistic repertoire.

Requirements: Kobold

Benefits: In addition to speaking Draconic, you begin play with the ability to speak one of the following: Aklo, Dwarven, Goblin, or Undercommon.

Usage: Select one (1) of Aklo, Dwarven, Goblin, or Undercommon when you take this trait.

Update the Granted Proficiencies>Languages section of the details tab of this trait to reflect this choice.

triaxus-trained.name: Triaxus-Trained
triaxus-trained.description:
You have been trained in intricate dragon-handling techniques that originated on Triaxus.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Perception checks, and a +1 trait bonus on skill checks attempted as part of trying to influence a dragon.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on all attempts to influence a dragon.

captain's blade (liberty's edge).name: Captain's Blade (Liberty's Edge)
captain's blade (liberty's edge).description:
You were born on board a ship and learned to fight beside the sailing men and women of the Andoran fleet.

Requirements: Liberty's Edge

Benefits: While on board any vessel afloat on water, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Acrobatics and Climb checks. One of these skills (your choice) becomes a class skill for you.

Usage: Choose either Acrobatics or Climb when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus when on a vessel in water
+[[1]] Trait bonus when on a vessel in water

mwangi herbal tradition (mwangi expanse).name: Mwangi Herbal Tradition (Mwangi Expanse)
mwangi herbal tradition (mwangi expanse).description:
You are trained in a Mwangi tradition of herbalism dating back to the Age of Darkness and therefore have great insight into how to use the natural ingredients at your disposal to nurse an ailing body.

Requirements: Must hail from the Mwangi Expanse or have lived there for at least 1 year

Benefits: You gain a +4 trait bonus on Heal checks when providing long-term care, and Heal is a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Trait bonus when providing long-term care.

light in the darkness.name: Light in the Darkness
light in the darkness.description:
You’re destined to bring light to the darkness and expunge the faithless from the deep.

Benefits: Whenever you cast a spell granted by the sun domain (or associated subdomains) while in the Darklands, the spell functions at 1 caster level higher than your actual caster level.

contextNotes.text: Whenever you cast a spell granted by the sun domain (or associated subdomains) while in the Darklands, the spell functions at 1 caster level higher than your actual caster level.

elemental pupil (qadira).name: Elemental Pupil (Qadira)
elemental pupil (qadira).description:
You have studied at the feet of the masters of Katheer's colleges of magic, and so increased your own mastery of the elements.

Requirements: Qadira

Benefits: Choose one of the following energy types: acid, cold, electricity, or fire. When casting a spell of 1st level or higher that deals damage of your chosen energy type, the spell deals 1 extra point of damage of this type.

Usage

Select one (1) energy type: acid, cold, electricity, or fire when you take this trait.

Update the text of the context note on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your selection.

contextNotes.text: When casting a spell of 1st level or higher that deals damage of your chosen energy type, the spell deals 1 extra point of damage of this type.

true devotion (strange aeons).name: True Devotion (Strange Aeons)
true devotion (strange aeons).description:
You wake from your recent ordeal with a sense of shame, as if you had sinned deeply against your morals and beliefs. However, you feel a bit of relief as well, as if your god had granted you a second chance. You not only have a drive to figure out what happened to you, but an urge to fight back against the unspeakable forces of evil. If, during the course of your investigation, you discover that you took part in unsavory activities that would normally be opposed to your faith or alignment, this event no longer has any impact with your standing in the eyes of your god due to your renewed righteous convictions.

Benefits: Once per day when casting a divine spell, you can attempt to cast the spell without losing the spell from its spell slot or using one of your allotted spells per day. There is a 20% chance that this attempt is successful. In addition, select one domain or inquisition you have. You can use the first granted power of that domain or inquisition one additional time per day.

You must have a good alignment and be class capable of casting divine spells in order to select this trait.

Usage: Select one domain or inquisition you have when you take this trait.

Update the chosen domain or inquisition on the features tab of your Actor, edit the class feature and on the details tab of the feature> Limited Uses add "+1" to the formula calculating the total uses.

grounded (dwarf).name: Grounded (Dwarf)
grounded (dwarf).description:
You are well balanced, both physically and mentally.

Requirements: Dwarf

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on balance-related Acrobatics checks, and a +1 trait bonus on Reflex saves.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on balance-related checks.

stigmatized.name: Stigmatized
stigmatized.description:
You were kept at the periphery of society for a long period of time, so that even when you are among strangers in a new place, you feel the weight of your missing socialization.

Effects: You take a –3 penalty on Diplomacy checks to gather information or improve a creature’s attitude.
