contextNotes.text: -[[3]] penalty to checks to gather information or to improve a creature's attitude.

dogged.name: Dogged
dogged.description:
Drawing inspiration from the Cult of the Failed, you refuse to let setbacks stop you, no matter how disastrous.

Benefits: Once per day when you roll a natural 1 on an attack roll, saving throw, skill check, or ability check, you gain a +1 trait bonus on any one roll you attempt before the end of the next round.

contextNotes.text: Once per day when you roll a natural 1, you gain a +[[1]] trait bonus on any one roll you attempt before the end of the next round.
Once per day when you roll a natural 1, you gain a +[[1]] trait bonus on any one roll you attempt before the end of the next round.
Once per day when you roll a natural 1, you gain a +[[1]] trait bonus on any one roll you attempt before the end of the next round.
Once per day when you roll a natural 1, you gain a +[[1]] trait bonus on any one roll you attempt before the end of the next round.

noble born - garress (kingmaker).name: Noble Born - Garress (Kingmaker)
noble born - garress (kingmaker).description:
You claim a tangential but legitimate connection to one of Brevoy’s noble families. If you aren’t human, you were likely adopted by one of Brevoy’s nobles or were instead a favored servant or even a childhood friend of a noble scion. Whatever the cause, you’ve had a comfortable life, but one far from the dignity and decadence your distant cousins know. Although you are associated with an esteemed name, your immediate family is hardly well to do, and you’ve found your name to be more of a burden to you than a boon in many social situations. You’ve recently decided to test yourself, to see if you can face the world without the aegis of a name you have little real claim or care for. An expedition into the storied Stolen Lands seems like just the test to see if you really are worth the title “noble.”

Benefits: Choose one of the following noble families and associated benefits:

Garress

Your family’s long association with the dwarves of the Golushkin Mountains has left its mark. You ignore the movement penalty for the first 5 feet of rocky difficult terrain you move through per round. This applies only to terrain made difficult by rocks or ruins. In addition, you gain a +2 trait bonus on Appraise checks to assess the value of natural stones or metals. Your family motto is “Strong as the Mountains.”

Special Note

This is one of six versions of this trait. Other versions can be found here:

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.nWlZ25PwQDz2zhoH]{Noble Born - Lebeda (Kingmaker)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.stEGVAsBCoaY5Ifk]{Noble Born - Lodovka (Kingmaker)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.c8eiTJJmkH6EyxnY]{Noble Born - Medvyed (Kingmaker)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.BMzDJlUF3aWiDEJF]{Noble Born - Orlovsky (Kingmaker)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.9RUX6RB5UQYnNIxR]{Noble Born - Surtova (Kingmaker)}

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to assess the value of natural stones or metals.

deep faith.name: Deep Faith
deep faith.description:
Your deity was born of the depths, and you’re a child of such realms.

Benefits: Whenever you cast a spell granted by the earth domain (or associated subdomains) while in the Darklands, that spell functions at 1 caster level higher than your actual caster level.

contextNotes.text: Whenever you cast a spell granted by the earth domain (or associated subdomains) while in the Darklands, that spell functions at 1 caster level higher than your actual caster level.

seasoned hunter (ruins of azlant).name: Seasoned Hunter (Ruins of Azlant)
seasoned hunter (ruins of azlant).description:
You grew up hunting every season, any season. Maybe you grew up in a rural area and hunted to put food on the table, or perhaps you come from a comfortable lifestyle where hunting for sport is an excellent excuse to camp in the wilderness and hone your skills. After sharing stories of your most exciting hunts and proving to them that you can hit a bull’s eye at 100 feet, the Bountiful Venture Company selected you for the expedition assured that you can provide food and security to the young colony.

Benefits: Pick one of the following creature types: aberration, animal, magical beast, or vermin. You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls or damage rolls (choose one; once chosen it can’t be changed) against creatures of that type.

Usage: Select one of creature types permitted when you take this trait.

Select attack or damage rolls when you take this trait.

Update the text of the note on the changes tab of this trait to reflect your choices.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on <choice of attack  or damage> rolls against <choice of  aberration, animal, magical beast, or vermin> type.

purchased loyalty (druma).name: Purchased Loyalty (Druma)
purchased loyalty (druma).description:
As a member of Druma's Mercenary League, you are utterly loyal to the holder of your contract,at least until it expires.

Requirements: Druma

Benefits: Once per day, when protecting someone who has bought your services, you may roll twice when making a Will save against charm effects and take the better result.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, when protecting someone who has bought your services, you may roll twice when making a Will save against charm effects and take the better result.

mordant heritage (the steaming sea).name: Mordant Heritage (the Steaming Sea)
mordant heritage (the steaming sea).description:
You have lived among the Mordant Spire elves and know their strange ways.

Requirements: The Steaming Sea

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Swim checks and a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against enchantment effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against enchantment effects.

amiable briber (absalom).name: Amiable Briber (Absalom)
amiable briber (absalom).description:
Your attempts to bribe others usually come off as playful or as a gag.

Requirements: Absalom

Benefits: The first time someone refuses a bribe you offer, that person’s attitude toward you doesn’t worsen, even if the offer would normally offend the person (as though you had failed a Diplomacy check by 5 or more).

contextNotes.text: The first time someone refuses a bribe you offer, that person’s attitude toward you doesn’t worsen

black blood heritage.name: Black Blood Heritage
black blood heritage.description:
One of your ancestors came in contact with the mystical black blood (Pathfinder Campaign Setting: Into the Darklands 51), granting you an affinity for the substance.

Benefits: You are immune to the negative effects of black blood. Once per day as a swift action, you can channel the essence of black blood, increasing your effective caster level by 1 for any 1st-or 2nd-level necromancy spell you cast. Your blood is pitch black as a result of this trait.

contextNotes.text: Immune to the negative effects of black blood.

bully.name: Bully
bully.description:
You grew up in an environment where the meek were ignored and you often had to resort to threats or violence to be heard.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Intimidate checks, and Intimidate is always a class skill for you.

magic survivor (trox).name: Magic Survivor (Trox)
magic survivor (trox).description:
You fought off devious magic in order to escape duergar captors.

Requirements: Trox

Benefits: Choose two of Fortitude, Reflex, or Will saves. You gain a +1 trait bonus on saves of the types you chose against spells and spell-like abilities, and you take a –1 penalty on all saves of the remaining type.

Usage: Select which 2 save types will receive a bonus and which 1 type which will receive a penalty when you take this trait.

Update the targets of all 3 changes on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choices.

superstitious (realm of the mammoth lord.name: Superstitious (Realm of the Mammoth Lords)
superstitious (realm of the mammoth lord.description:
You are distrustful of any magic that goes beyond your native shamanic ways.

Requirements: Realm of the Mammoth Lords

Benefits: As long as you do not possess any levels in a class that grants arcane spellcasting power, you gain a +1 trait bonus on all saving throws against arcane spells.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against arcane spells as long as you do not possess any levels in a class that grants arcane spellcasting power,

trifler (human; varisian wanderers, vari.name: Trifler (Human; Varisian Wanderers, Varisian human)
trifler (human; varisian wanderers, vari.description:
A childhood spent at your mystically gifted grandmother's side has taught you the basics of real magic.

Requirements: Varisian Wanderers or Varisian Human Heritage

Benefits: You may cast @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.a6isom7wx0nmhwfp]{Prestidigitation} three times per day as a spell-like ability. This spell-like ability is cast at your highest caster level; if you have no caster level, it functions at CL 1st.

the artisan (tyrant's grasp).name: The Artisan (Tyrant's Grasp)
the artisan (tyrant's grasp).description:
Precision is important in getting exactly what you want, and you give careful consideration in building everything from homes and vases to roads and empires. Your own attention to detail has proven vital in rebuilding Roslar’s Coffer from the orc occupation a decade ago, whether you are an accountant, architect, engineer, or herbalist.

Benefits: Your attention to detail provides you a +1 trait bonus on Appraise and Heal checks, and one of these skills becomes a class skill for you.

Once per day, you can spend a full round casting a spell you can cast that has a normal casting time of 1 standard action to increase your effective caster level for that spell by 1. This ability has no affect on spells with a casting time other than 1 standard action.

Usage: Select Appraise or Heal when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, as a full round action, you can cast with a casting time of 1 standard action to increase your effective caster level for that spell by 1. This ability has no affect on spells with a casting time other than 1 standard action. ([[@resources.theArtisanTyrantsGrasp.value]] remaining uses)

tight bonds.name: Tight Bonds
tight bonds.description:
Whether siblings, spouses, or dedicated members of the same clan, characters who take this trait are an inseparable pair committed to each other. 

Benefits: Your partner or partners must also have this trait. You gain a +1 trait bonus on Will saves against charm and compulsion effects if a partner is within 60 feet, increasing to +3 against a command to compromise your relationship or harm a partner.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against charm and compulsion effects if a partner is within 60 feet. Increasing to a +[[3]] Trait bonus  against a command to compromise your relationship or harm a partner.

jistkan scholar.name: Jistkan Scholar
jistkan scholar.description:
You have specialized in studying Jistkan history and culture.

Benefits: You gain Jistka as a bonus language and a +1 trait bonus on all Knowledge checks related to the Jistka Imperium.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on all checks related to the Jistka Imperium.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on all checks related to the Jistka Imperium.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on all checks related to the Jistka Imperium.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on all checks related to the Jistka Imperium.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on all checks related to the Jistka Imperium.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on all checks related to the Jistka Imperium.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on all checks related to the Jistka Imperium.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on all checks related to the Jistka Imperium.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on all checks related to the Jistka Imperium.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on all checks related to the Jistka Imperium.

gregarious.name: Gregarious
gregarious.description:
At least once in your childhood, your family moved for a reason you didn’t understand and you were forced to leave behind your old friends and make new ones. Ever since, you have always been the first to meet strangers, make new friends, and welcome travelers. You learned that first impressions are the most important, and have a knack for making a good one.

Benefits: Once per day, when you attempt a Diplomacy check to improve a creature’s attitude toward you, you may choose to reroll the check after the first roll is made but before the results are revealed. You must take the second result, even if it is worse.

Suggested Characters

Eagle Knights, Garundi, Pathfinders.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, when you attempt to improve a creature’s attitude toward you, you may choose to re-roll the check after the first roll is made but before the results are revealed. You must take the second result, even if it is worse.

caretaker.name: Caretaker
caretaker.description:
As the child of an herbalist or an assistant in a temple infirmary, you often had to assist in tending to the sick and wounded.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Heal checks, and Heal is always a class skill for you.

shadow diplomat (shadow lodge).name: Shadow Diplomat (Shadow Lodge)
shadow diplomat (shadow lodge).description:
Requirements: Shadow Lodge

Benefits: Balancing the political infighting of the Society’s factions grants you a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks, and Diplomacy becomes a class skill for you.

resilient.name: Resilient
resilient.description:
Growing up in a poor neighborhood or in the unforgiving wilds often forced you to subsist on food and water from doubtful sources. You've built up your mettle as a result.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Fortitude saves.

magaambyan arcana (human; mwangi).name: Magaambyan Arcana (Human; Mwangi)
magaambyan arcana (human; mwangi).description:
Requirements: Mwangi Human Heritage

Benefits: Your study of Magaambyan magic traditions allows you to add one spell with the good descriptor from the druid or cleric spell list to the spell list of an arcane spellcasting class of your choice, at the same spell level as it appears on the cleric or druid list. Pick the spell when you choose this trait.

child of the crusades (wrath of the righ.name: Child of the Crusades (Wrath of the Righteous)
child of the crusades (wrath of the righ.description:
Your parents were members of the crusade, as were their parents before them. (If you are an elf, gnome, or other long-lived race, these could be brothers or cousins instead, since it’s possible that the Worldwound simply didn’t exist at a time before you were born.) The righteousness of the crusades sometimes feels as if it runs in your very blood, and it bolsters you against demonic inf luence. Your parents may be alive still, or they may have perished on a mission—that choice is up to you. You grew up knowing them, though, and their zeal and devotion to the crusade is the primary reason you feel the same way. They’ve told you of other family members who have also been involved in the crusade, and it’s not uncommon for you to meet a distant cousin or long-lost aunt, uncle, or other family member while traveling among the border towns of Mendev. This strong family tie bolsters your mind and sense of belonging to the crusade.

Benefits: Once per day when you fail a saving throw against an effect created by a demon that would possess or incapacitate you mentally, you may immediately reroll that saving throw as a free action. You must take the second result, even if it is worse.

Associated Mythic Path

Marshal

Multiple Characters

If other characters take this trait, you should all be related—you can be siblings, cousins, or even more distant relations, but you should all be aware of this shared lineage.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, when you fail a saving throw against an effect created by a demon that would possess or incapacitate you mentally, you may immediately reroll that saving throw as a free action. You must take the second result, even if it is worse.

adrenaline rush (gnome).name: Adrenaline Rush (Gnome)
adrenaline rush (gnome).description:
You are determined to ward off the Bleaching, and get a certain thrill out of being forced to experience negative emotions.

Requirements: Gnome

Benefits: Once per day when you fail a saving throw against a spell with the emotion descriptor (see Pathfinder RPG Ultimate Magic), you can gain 1d6 temporary hit points. These temporary hit points last for 1 minute.

citizen of kalabuto (kalabuto).name: Citizen of Kalabuto (Kalabuto)
citizen of kalabuto (kalabuto).description:
You live and work in Kalabuto.

Requirements: Kalabuto

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on all Knowledge (local) and Knowledge (history) checks regarding Kalabuto.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on checks regarding Kalabuto.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks regarding Kalabuto.

mind trapper (dhampir; svetocher).name: Mind Trapper (Dhampir; Svetocher)
mind trapper (dhampir; svetocher).description:
Your honeyed words are nearly impossible for your enchanted subjects to ignore.

Requirements: Svetocher Dhampir Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Charisma checks to convince an ally enchanted by one of your charm spells (such as @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.tjog6bufg5b08lvq]{charm person} or @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.smbkd2yobhshbpqf]{charm monster}) to do things it wouldn’t normally do.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to convince an ally enchanted by one of your charm spells  to do things it wouldn’t normally do.

blood of pharaohs (mummy's mask).name: Blood of Pharaohs (Mummy's Mask)
blood of pharaohs (mummy's mask).description:
Long ago, one of your ancestors ruled over the lands of Osirion. Although you are many generations removed and the line of descent is hard to prove, his or her blood still runs in your veins. Perhaps you may find some proof of your lineage in the tombs of Wati’s necropolis.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Will saves. You also gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (nobility) checks and that skill is always a class skill for you. In addition, you may choose Ancient Osiriani as one of your bonus languages.

outsider ties.name: Outsider Ties
outsider ties.description:
An outsider visited you at a young age, and you have had a significant affinity with that type of outsider ever since.

Benefits: Choose a subtype of outsider, such as angel or elemental. When you summon an outsider of that subtype via summon monster, planar ally, planar binding, or similar spells and abilities, the summoned creature gains a number of temporary hit points equal to the level of spell used to summon it.

contextNotes.text: When you summon an outsider of your chosen subtype, the summoned creature gains a number of temporary hit points equal to the level of the spell.

battlefield surgeon (zon-kuthon).name: Battlefield Surgeon (Zon-Kuthon)
battlefield surgeon (zon-kuthon).description:
You're skilled at both dealing and repairing wounds.

Requirements: Zon-Kuthon

Benefits: Heal becomes a class skill for you, and you can use the treat deadly wounds aspect of the skill 1 additional time per creature per day.

contextNotes.text: You can treat deadly wounds 1 additional time per creature per day.

chosen child (po li).name: Chosen Child (Po Li)
chosen child (po li).description:
The oracles of Po Li smiled upon you when your parents claimed you were of divine right, and you were raised with privileges few Po Lians can fathom.

Requirements: Po Li

Benefits: Your starting money increases by 900 gp.

ancient explorer (skull and shackles).name: Ancient Explorer (Skull and Shackles)
ancient explorer (skull and shackles).description:
You are a student of the ancient history of Golarion, and you've come to the Shackles to explore the crumbling and vine-choked ruins of the cyclops empire of Ghol-Gan, which have lain abandoned among the isles of the Shackles for thousands of years. You went to a tavern called the Formidably Maid in Port Peril last night to meet a contact who supposedly had an old map of lost cyclops ruins to sell, but the drink was stronger than you had expected and you passed out before making the deal.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (history) and Knowledge (local) checks, and one of these skills is a class skill for you. In addition, you gain Cyclops or Polyglot as a bonus language.

Usage: Select Knowledge (History) or Knowledge (Local) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

Select Cyclops or Polyglot when you take this trait.

Update the Language skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

historian of the rebellion (hell's rebel.name: Historian of the Rebellion (Hell's Rebels)
historian of the rebellion (hell's rebel.description:
You’ve long been interested in the legacy of the Silver Ravens—a group of freedom fighters that rose to prominence in Kintargo during the Chelish Civil War. There’s frustratingly little information today about the group, and you suspect that most of what was recorded about the Silver Ravens has long since been redacted or destroyed by government agents, but you’ve managed to pick up a tidbit here and there. Most of your knowledge isn’t so much about the Silver Ravens specifically, but more about general histories of rebel groups and freedom fighters who have fought against oppressive governments throughout history, both in Cheliax and beyond.

Benefits: Your familiarity with rebel groups allows you to grant a +2 bonus on an Organization check of your choice once the party reestablishes the Silver Ravens during the first adventure. You can change which check you assign this bonus to once at the start of the rebellion’s Upkeep phase. Your time preparing for joining the Silver Ravens has also honed your skill at remaining unseen. You gain a +1 trait bonus on Stealth checks, and Stealth is always a class skill for you.

monster hunter (rise of the runelords).name: Monster Hunter (Rise of the Runelords)
monster hunter (rise of the runelords).description:
Perhaps you came to the Varisian Gulf in search of the Sandpoint Devil, or maybe you followed fisherman’s tales of Old Murdermaw— regardless, you’ve ventured through Varisia to hunt down famous monsters. While they have all eluded you so far, you made it to Sandpoint to research and restock before heading back out into the wilderness.

Benefits: Because of your training, you gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls and weapon damage rolls against aberrations and magical beasts.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attack rolls and weapon damage rolls against aberrations and magical beasts.

thrune informant (hell's vengeance).name: Thrune Informant (Hell's Vengeance)
thrune informant (hell's vengeance).description:
The Thrice-Damned House of Thrune rules Cheliax with autocratic power, and its representatives can be found throughout the empire, drawn from all walks of life. Most of these agents do not work directly as government employees, but they still serve House Thrune in whatever ways they can. You are a true patriot of Cheliax, and have long worked for House Thrune as a paid informant. You report what you see to the authorities, and occasionally carry out specific tasks suited to your skill set and abilities when asked. People like you help prevent potential traitors and other “undesirables” from undermining Thrune’s authority and keep Chelish society running smoothly. You hope that with continued faithful service, you will eventually be rewarded with greater authority and autonomy as a sworn agent of House Thrune.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks to gather information, Disguise checks, and Knowledge (local) checks, and one of these skills (your choice) is a class skill for you.

Usage

Select Diplomacy, Disguise, or Knowledge (local) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to gather information.

trunau native (giantslayer).name: Trunau Native (Giantslayer)
trunau native (giantslayer).description:
You were born and raised in the town of Trunau, one of the few human settlements in the orc-dominated Hold of Belkzen. Orc attacks are an ever-present threat, and like all Trunauans, you have made the Standing Vow: to hold Trunau against all attackers, orc or otherwise, to stand your ground, and to live free or die trying. Upon coming of age, you were given a hopeknife—a small, sheathed dagger, usually worn on a chain under your clothes—and taught how to use it to take your own life to avoid capture by orcs, or to grant the mercy of a quick death to the wounded. As a native, you have served in Trunau’s militia, and fought alongside Patrol Captains Kurst and Rodrik Grath.

Benefits: You begin play with a hopeknife (a masterwork dagger), and your tenacity in the face of adversity grants you a +1 trait bonus on Will saves.

prankster (gnome).name: Prankster (Gnome)
prankster (gnome).description:
You always have a great idea for a prank.

Requirements: Gnome

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on dirty trick combat maneuver checks. You also gain a +1 trait bonus to your CMD when an opponent attempts a dirty trick combat maneuver check against you. If you succeed at a dirty trick combat maneuver check against a flatfooted opponent, increase the duration of the condition caused by 1 round.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on dirty trick combat maneuver checks.  If you succeed at a dirty trick combat maneuver check against a flatfooted opponent, increase the duration of the condition caused by 1 round.
+[[1]] Trait bonus  to your CMD when an opponent attempts a dirty trick combat maneuver check against you.

kobold herbalist (kobold).name: Kobold Herbalist (Kobold)
kobold herbalist (kobold).description:
You are familiar with the flora of your native forest and have rudimentary skill as an herbalist.

Requirements: Kobold

Benefits: Once per day, you can attempt a DC 20 Survival check instead of a Craft (alchemy) check to create a single dose of @UUID[Compendium.pf1.items.Item.avdskczyslzefify]{scent cloak} without paying any cost for raw materials. Any scent cloak created using this method must be used within 24 hours, or it becomes inert.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you can attempt a DC 20 Survival check instead of a Craft (alchemy) check to create a single dose of @UUID[Compendium.pf1.items.Item.avdskczyslzefify]{scent cloak} without paying any cost for raw materials. Any scent cloak created using this method must be used within 24 hours, or it becomes inert.

Once per day, you can attempt a DC 20 Survival check instead of a Craft (alchemy) check to create a single dose of @UUID[Compendium.pf1.items.Item.avdskczyslzefify]{scent cloak} without paying any cost for raw materials. Any scent cloak created using this method must be used within 24 hours, or it becomes inert.

tar-baphon's whisper (whispering way).name: Tar-Baphon's Whisper (Whispering Way)
tar-baphon's whisper (whispering way).description:
You have learned the secrets of sotto voce, the eerie, dry whispering voice used to pass on cult secrets.

Requirements: Whispering Way

Benefits: You may cast @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.5s8jqu87susjxarc]{sotto voce} as a spell-like ability three times per day as a spell-like ability. This spell-like ability is cast at your highest caster level; if you have no caster level, it functions at CL 1st.

Usage: In your character sheet, go to the settings tab and check "Spell-like" under spellbook usage. Then navigate to the spells tab, go to your spell-like spellbook, and add the sotto voce spell. In the spell-like spellbook configuration, set the spellcasting class to the same spellcasting class you have if any. If you have no caster level, open your new sotto voce spell and go to the details tab, under "Offsets", type 1 inside the "Caster Level Offset". Then, open the use properties, in the use window go to the usage tab, and at the bottom, below "Action" choose "Per Day" and type 3 in the formula.

allies in the deep.name: Allies in the Deep
allies in the deep.description:
You’ve learned to deal amicably with individuals of questionable morals when necessary.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Charisma-based skill checks to influence evil creatures. You must have a nonevil alignment in order to take this trait.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to influence evil creatures

eye of the falcon (eagle knight).name: Eye of the Falcon (Eagle Knight)
eye of the falcon (eagle knight).description:
You have a way of seeing right to the heart of things.

Requirements: Eagle Knight

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against illusion (phantasm) spells and a +1 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks to know when you are being lied to, so long as you are face to face with the speaker in question.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against illusion (phantasm) spells.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to know when you are being lied to, so long as you are face to face.

tar taargadth trained.name: Tar Taargadth Trained
tar taargadth trained.description:
You know the ancient fighting techniques of the Tar Taargadth Empire.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on damage rolls when using any weapon with the word “dwarven” in its name.

Usage: In Combat tab, add `+ 1[Trait Bonus - Dwarven Weapon]` to Damage Formula on any weapon with the word "dwarven" in its name.

relic-proof.name: Relic-Proof
relic-proof.description:
Your experiments—both your successes and failures—have inured you to the effects of stored magic.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against effects produced by spell completion and spell trigger items.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against effects produced by spell completion and spell trigger items.

dragonfoe (giantslayer).name: Dragonfoe (Giantslayer)
dragonfoe (giantslayer).description:
You’ve always hated dragons. Perhaps one of your ancestors was a well-known dragonslayer, or a rampaging dragon killed your family or friends. Maybe the stories you grew up hearing about knights rescuing captive princes and princesses from the clutches of evil dragons inspired you to slay dragons. Whatever the reason for your obsession, it has driven you to study how to fight dragons and defend yourself against their fearsome attacks.

Benefits: You gain a +1 dodge bonus to AC against creatures with the dragon type and a +2 trait bonus on Reflex saves against breath weapon attacks.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Dodge bonus to AC against creatures with the dragon type.
+[[2]] Trait bonus against breath weapon attacks.

fruits of your labor (jaidi).name: Fruits of Your Labor (Jaidi)
fruits of your labor (jaidi).description:
You take pride in providing for your community.

Requirements: Jaidi

Benefits: Once per day you can serve a meal comprising food you have produced, purchased, or foraged to yourself and up to six other creatures.

Serving and consuming this meal takes 10 minutes, during which the other participants can also partake in light activity such as conversation, reading, and keeping watch.

Each creature regains 1d3 hit points at the end of the meal, and gains a +1 trait bonus on its next d20 roll using the aid another action to assist other creatures who consumed the meal.

The +1 trait bonus lasts 24 hours, and a creature can gain that bonus on its next d20 roll to assist other creatures from this trait only once per day.

covered sniper (kasatha).name: Covered Sniper (Kasatha)
covered sniper (kasatha).description:
You were attacked by strangers that threatened to expose your existence to dangerous forces and you defended yourself using cover.

Requirements: Kasatha

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on ranged weapon damage rolls as long as you have cover from your opponent.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus  on ranged weapon damage rolls as long as you have cover from your opponent.

child of wati (osirion).name: Child of Wati (Osirion)
child of wati (osirion).description:
You were raised near the infamous necropolis of Wati, and even managed to sneak into these hallowed grounds on occasion. Entering the clergy of the Voices of the Spire in Wati was a natural choice as you grew older, and your past experiences made you a particularly well-informed disciple of the church.

Requirements: Osirion

Benefits: Your effective cleric level is 1 higher for the purposes of channeling positive energy to damage undead. Your effective cleric level cannot exceed your character level.

Usage

Cleric level affects channel energy in 2 ways, the damage and save dc formulae.

edit your actor's Channel energy ability, for harming undead, as follows:

1) the damage formula should be:

(ceil(min(@attributes.hd.total, @classes.cleric.level +1 )/ 2))d6

2) the DC formula should be:

10 + floor(min(@attributes.hd.total, @classes.cleric.level +1 )/ 2)+ @abilities.cha.mod

(replace cleric with the class that grants your actor the channel energy ability if not cleric for both formulae above)

humble beginnings (varisia).name: Humble Beginnings (Varisia)
humble beginnings (varisia).description:
You didn’t have any intention of becoming an adventurer, but a single, unexpected confrontation changed everything.

Requirements: Varisia

Benefits: Choose one of the following: boot, bucket, frying pan, mug, rolling pin, spade, or stool. You are treated as having the Catch Off- Guard feat when wielding the chosen item. (These items should all be considered improvised melee weapons that deal 1d4 points of bludgeoning damage.)

improvisational equipment.name: Improvisational Equipment
improvisational equipment.description:
You have an uncanny knack for turning equipment to new and unexpected uses.

Benefits: When using an item for anything other than its intended purpose—such as using a crowbar as a grappling hook or an old shirt to bandage a deadly wound—reduce the improvisation penalty by 2. This does not apply to improvised weapon penalties. Your GM may rule that some things are just not suitable for use in certain ways; for example, gluing a dead orc’s face to your own face won’t help you disguise yourself as an orc.

fight for liberty (andoran).name: Fight for Liberty (Andoran)
fight for liberty (andoran).description:
You inspire slaves to fight for freedom with a rousing battle cry.

Requirements: Andoran

Benefits: As a standard action, you may make a rallying cry with a DC 15 Perform (oratory) check. On a successful check, any unarmed ally within 30 feet who is able to hear you gains a +2 trait bonus on attack rolls with improvised weapons and unarmed strikes. This bonus lasts for 1 round per level.

generous (human; taldan).name: Generous (Human; Taldan)
generous (human; taldan).description:
Your neighbors gained your respect with their generosity, and you emulate their example.

Requirements: Taldan Human Heritage

Benefits: Whenever you give to a creature a gift or service worth at least 25 gold pieces × the creature’s CR, you gain a +2 trait bonus on Bluff and Diplomacy checks against that creature for the next 24 hours.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on checks against any  creature, to whom you have given a gift or service worth at least 25 gold pieces × the creature’s CR,  for the next 24 hours.
+[[2]] Trait bonus on checks against any  creature, to whom you have given a gift or service worth at least 25 gold pieces × the creature’s CR,  for the next 24 hours.

warthog (boar).name: Warthog (Boar)
warthog (boar).description:
Requirements: Boar

Benefits: Warthogs are quicker than they look, with a base speed of 45 feet instead of the boar’s 40 feet.

obnoxious.name: Obnoxious
obnoxious.description:
You have long since forgotten to care what people think about you— though your interactions are always memorable, at the very least.

Benefits: Choose Perform (act), Perform (comedy), Perform (oratory), or Perform (sing); you gain a +1 trait bonus on checks with the chosen skill and gain it as a class skill.

Usage: Choose one Perform type from the 4 listed types when taking this trait.

On the details tab select this Perform skill to add it as a class skill and on the changes tab change the missing change target to the selected Perform skill.

cowardly.name: Cowardly
cowardly.description:
You might face dangerous situations with bravado, but you are constantly afraid.

Effects: Your base speed when frightened and fleeing increases by 5 feet, and the penalties you take from having the cowering, frightened, panicked, or shaken conditions increase by 1. If you would normally be immune to fear, you do not take these penalties but instead lose your immunity to fear (regardless of its source).

dueling cloak adept.name: Dueling Cloak Adept
dueling cloak adept.description:
You are trained in using your cloak to disguise your body and deceive your enemies.

Benefits: While wearing a cloak and using an @UUID[Compendium.pf1.weapons-and-ammo.Item.ioPvNyyv6L4hv0aW]{Aldori dueling sword}, you gain a +2 trait bonus on Bluff checks made to feint.

Additionally, every time you successfully feint while wearing a cloak, you gain a +1 dodge bonus to your Armor Class until your next turn.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to feint, while wearing a cloak and using an Aldori dueling sword.
+[[1]] Dodge bonus to your Armor Class until your next turn every time you successfully feint while wearing a cloak.

relentless fortitude (qadiran dromedary).name: Relentless Fortitude (Qadiran Dromedary)
relentless fortitude (qadiran dromedary).description:
Requirements: Camel (Qadiran Dromedary)

Benefits: Qadiran dromedaries heal nonlethal damage at a rate of 2 hit points per hour per Hit Die. This trait does not stack with other effects that increase a creature’s rate of healing nonlethal damage.

finish the fight (half-orc, orc-raised).name: Finish the Fight (Half-Orc, Orc-Raised)
finish the fight (half-orc, orc-raised).description:
You’re at the bottom of the pecking order, so when you challenge that order, your victory must be absolute.

Requirements: Half-Orc, Orc-Raised

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls against opponents you already injured in the past 24 hours.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus attack rolls against opponents you already injured in the past 24 hours.

egorian school apprentice (egorian).name: Egorian School Apprentice (Egorian)
egorian school apprentice (egorian).description:
You studied under one of the masters of the Egorian School style of architecture.

Requirements: Egorian

Benefits: You gain a +3 trait bonus on Knowledge (engineering) checks and a +2 trait bonus on Perception checks to find secret doors or traps in buildings built in the Egorian School style.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to find secret doors or traps in buildings built in the Egorian School style.

ingratiating (human; chelaxian).name: Ingratiating (Human; Chelaxian)
ingratiating (human; chelaxian).description:
Someone close to you was removed from her position and replaced after losing the government’s favor, and you had to quickly prove your loyalty to her replacement or face your own disgrace.

Requirements: Chelaxian Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Bluff and Diplomacy checks when interacting with people in positions of authority over you.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus when interacting with people in positions of authority over you.
+[[2]] Trait bonus when interacting with people in positions of authority over you.

misbegotten.name: Misbegotten
misbegotten.description:
Whether due to the influence of malign magic, disease, or the scorn of the gods, you were born with a troublesome deformity that interferes with your movement.

Effects: You take a –2 penalty on all Dexterity-based skill checks.

blood potion brewer (belkzen).name: Blood Potion Brewer (Belkzen)
blood potion brewer (belkzen).description:
The orcs of Belkzen routinely mix the blood of their enemies with potions they brew to gain some of their foe’s strength.

Requirements: Belkzen

Benefits: Twice per day when you brew a potion, you can add some of an enemy’s blood to the mixture. When you do, the potion’s caster level is treated as 1 higher when you drink it for the purpose of determining the duration of the potion’s effects. This ability has no effect if another creature drinks the potion. This trait does not grant the ability to brew potions.

durable change (skinwalker; werebear-kin.name: Durable Change (Skinwalker; Werebear-Kin)
durable change (skinwalker; werebear-kin.description:
Requirements: Werebear-Kin Skinwalker Heritage

Benefits: When you use your change shape ability, you can choose to gain the benefits of the @UUID[Compendium.pf1.feats.Item.ehqx8txNRGMaNOPt]{Endurance} feat rather than gaining a special ability.

avowed inspiration (ragathiel).name: Avowed Inspiration (Ragathiel)
avowed inspiration (ragathiel).description:
In emulation of the empyreal lord’s intensely channeled rage, you are an inspiration on the battlefield.

Requirements: Ragathiel

Benefits: Once per day, when you attack a foe who injured you during the same combat, you can inspire allies who can see you as a move action. Each other ally within 30 feet who can see you gains a +2 morale bonus on her next attack roll.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, when you attack a foe who injured you during the same combat, you can inspire allies who can see you as a move action.

vagabond child (urban).name: Vagabond Child (Urban)
vagabond child (urban).description:
You grew up among the outcasts and outlaws of your society, learning to forage and survive in an urban environment.

Requirements: Urban

Benefits: Select one of the following skills: Disable Device, Escape Artist, or Sleight of Hand. You gain a +1 trait bonus on checks with that skill, and it is always a class kill for you.

Usage: Select Disable Device, Escape Artist, or Sleight of Hand when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

local know-it-all (varisia).name: Local Know-it-All (Varisia)
local know-it-all (varisia).description:
Requirements: Varisia

Benefits: Choose a community that you’ve lived in for at least 5 years. You gain a +2 trait bonus on Knowledge (local) checks related to that community.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on checks related to your chosen community.

trailblazer (human; mwangi).name: Trailblazer (Human; Mwangi)
trailblazer (human; mwangi).description:
Your ability to endure in the darkest jungles has earned you a reputation as a novice guide.

Requirements: Mwangi Human Heritage

Benefits: While traversing any wilderness, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Survival checks, and gain a +1 trait bonus on Fortitude saving throws against diseases.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus  while traversing any wilderness.
+[[1]] Trait bonus against diseases while traversing any wilderness.

ex-sczarni (shattered star).name: Ex-Sczarni (Shattered Star)
ex-sczarni (shattered star).description:
Though you were raised as a member of one of Magnimar's various Sczarni gangs, you never fit in with the rest of your settled, metropolitan brethren. The Pathfinder Society in particular recognized your wanderlust and potential as an agent, and on more than one occasion you impressed or bamboozled their agents with your street smarts and finesse. Over the years, you fell out of favor with the Sczarni, and eventually left to pursue interests outside of simple banditry, though you still remember the lessons your criminal relations taught you.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Bluff checks to pass hidden messages. Also, anytime you deliver a coup de grace, you deal an additional 1d6 points of damage.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to pass hidden messages.
Anytime you deliver a coup de grace, you deal an additional [[1d6]] points of damage.

failed winter witch apprentice (reign of.name: Failed Winter Witch Apprentice (Reign of Winter)
failed winter witch apprentice (reign of.description:
As a child, you were apprenticed as a winter witch in the frozen land of Irrisen, but you did not complete your training. Perhaps you disagreed with the politics of Irrisen’s White Witches, or you had an altercation with one of your teachers, or maybe you were just ill-suited to the practice of witchcraft. Whatever the reason, you left the ranks of the winter witches and left Irrisen. Whether or not you have continued your training on your own, you still retain some small knowledge of witchery and the magic of the icy north.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (arcana) and Spellcraft checks to identify spells or magical effects with the cold descriptor, and one of these skills (your choice) is a class skill for you. In addition, you gain Hallit or Skald (this does not count toward your number of languages).

Usage

  1. Select Knowledge (arcana) or Spellcraft when you take this trait.

  2. Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

  3. Select Hallit or Skald when you take this trait.

  4. Update the Granted Proficiencies>Languages section of the details tab of this trait to reflect this choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to identify spells or magical effects with the cold descriptor.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to identify spells or magical effects with the cold descriptor.

infernal colleague (imperial palace in c.name: Infernal Colleague (Imperial Palace in Cheliax)
infernal colleague (imperial palace in c.description:
Your experience working with devils has given you nerves of steel.

Requirements: Associated with the court of the Imperial Palace in Cheliax

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Will saves to resist fear effects created by outsiders.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against fear effects created by outsiders.

absalom bouncer.name: Absalom Bouncer
absalom bouncer.description:
You learned to fight while serving as a bodyguard in the taverns of Absalom, where keeping the patrons alive through bloody bar brawls was just part of your job.

Benefits: When you make an attack that deals nonlethal damage with an unarmed strike, you gain a +1 bonus on your damage roll.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Bonus when dealing nonlethal damage with an unarmed strike.

covert channeler.name: Covert Channeler
covert channeler.description:
Your mentor in the Wagons of Light taught you to hide holy symbols quickly.

Benefits: You can draw or stow a holy symbol as a swift action.

merciful scimitar.name: Merciful Scimitar
merciful scimitar.description:
You learned to cut foes with a scimitar without killing them.

Benefits: You can deal nonlethal damage with a scimitar without taking a penalty on your attack rolls.

scrapper (half-orc).name: Scrapper (Half-Orc)
scrapper (half-orc).description:
A childhood spent scrabbling for every possession and scrap of food has made you fierce and wary.

Requirements: Half-Orc

Benefits: You receive a +1 trait bonus on all Intimidate checks made to demoralize opponents and a +1 trait bonus on all Perception checks to avoid being surprised.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to demoralize opponents.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to avoid being surprised.

slave runner.name: Slave Runner
slave runner.description:
You witnessed, or were party to, the rescue of a halfling from Chelish slavery by the Bellflower Network or Eagle Knights.

Benefits: Once per day when attempting to directly free a slave, you gain a +2 trait bonus on your choice of an Acrobatics check, an Escape Artist check, a Stealth check, or a skill check attempted as part of a heist (Ultimate Intrigue 118) or pursuit (Ultimate Intrigue 142).

giant-harried (wastelands).name: Giant-Harried (Wastelands)
giant-harried (wastelands).description:
You were robbed by giants or nearly killed by a giant’s prank, and the experience gave you a keen instinct for outmaneuvering bigger creatures.

Requirements: Wastelands

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on combat maneuver checks against creatures larger than you and a +1 trait bonus to CMD against such creatures.

Likely Regions

Geb, Mana Wastes, Nex, Nidal, Numeria

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against creatures larger than you.
+[[1]] Trait bonus  against creatures larger than you.

blighted physiology (numeria).name: Blighted Physiology (Numeria)
blighted physiology (numeria).description:
Exposure to the corruption that seeps through every drop of water and grain of dirt in Numeria has altered your body.

Requirements: Numeria

Benefits: Horrific growths beneath your skin provide you a +1 natural armor bonus to AC, but your body does not work as a normal creature’s would. You become sickened for 1 round anytime you receive magical healing.

deep cover.name: Deep Cover
deep cover.description:
You have lived a double life since your youth, perhaps to avoid persecution for your true identity or in service to an enemy of the people or group associated with your cover identity.

Benefits: You can always take 10 on Bluff and Disguise checks to assume and maintain your cover identity. Bluff or Disguise (your choice) is a class skill for you.

Usage: Select Bluff or Disguise when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: You can always take 10  to assume and maintain your cover identity.
You can always take 10  to assume and maintain your cover identity.

noble born - surtova (kingmaker).name: Noble Born - Surtova (Kingmaker)
noble born - surtova (kingmaker).description:
You claim a tangential but legitimate connection to one of Brevoy’s noble families. If you aren’t human, you were likely adopted by one of Brevoy’s nobles or were instead a favored servant or even a childhood friend of a noble scion. Whatever the cause, you’ve had a comfortable life, but one far from the dignity and decadence your distant cousins know. Although you are associated with an esteemed name, your immediate family is hardly well to do, and you’ve found your name to be more of a burden to you than a boon in many social situations. You’ve recently decided to test yourself, to see if you can face the world without the aegis of a name you have little real claim or care for. An expedition into the storied Stolen Lands seems like just the test to see if you really are worth the title “noble.”

Benefits: Choose one of the following noble families and associated benefits:

Surtova

Your family is well known for their political agility and scheming natures. You deal +2 damage when attacking a flat-footed opponent while wielding a light or one-handed weapon. Your family motto is “Ours is the Right.”

Special Note

This is one of six versions of this trait. Other versions can be found here:

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.79pUkpcI6MYSTShf]{Noble Born - Garress (Kingmaker)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.nWlZ25PwQDz2zhoH]{Noble Born - Lebeda (Kingmaker)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.stEGVAsBCoaY5Ifk]{Noble Born - Lodovka (Kingmaker)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.c8eiTJJmkH6EyxnY]{Noble Born - Medvyed (Kingmaker)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.BMzDJlUF3aWiDEJF]{Noble Born - Orlovsky (Kingmaker)}

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] damage when attacking a flat-footed opponent while wielding a light or one-handed weapon.

azlanti scholar (ironbound archipelago,.name: Azlanti Scholar (Ironbound Archipelago, non-elf )
azlanti scholar (ironbound archipelago,.description:
Your interactions with the elves of the Mordant Spire have taught you much about Azlanti artifacts, and you now share the Mordant Spire elves’ fascination with these relics.

Requirements: Ironbound Archipelago, non-elf 

Benefits: You gain Azlanti as a bonus language, and you gain a +2 trait bonus on Appraise and Use Magic Device checks when attempting to identify or use authentic Azlanti artifacts.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus when attempting to identify or use authentic Azlanti artifacts.
+[[2]] Trait bonus when attempting to identify or use authentic Azlanti artifacts.

outsider enemy.name: Outsider Enemy
outsider enemy.description:
One of your ancestors was a conjurer who specialized in binding outsiders of a particular type. You have inherited some of his skills, but also some of his enemies.

Benefits: Choose a single outsider subtype. The DC of your spells increases by 1 against outsiders of the chosen subtype. Outsiders of the chosen subtype gain a +1 morale bonus on attack and damage rolls made against you, and their starting attitude toward you is reduced by one step.

Usage: choose a single outsider subtype when taking this trait. Add the chosen outsider type to the notes on the changes tab.

contextNotes.text: <Chosen subtype> Outsiders gain a +1 morale bonus to hit and damage against you
<Chosen subtype> Outsiders' starting attitude is one step worse than normal toward you

secret of the impossible kingdom (jalmer.name: Secret of the Impossible Kingdom (Jalmeray)
secret of the impossible kingdom (jalmer.description:
You have studied the ancient lore of Vudra at a monastery in Jalmeray, and have learned a mystical secret that empowers your spellcasting.

Requirements: Jalmeray

Benefits: Pick one spell when you choose this trait - from this point on, whenever you cast that spell, you do so at +1 caster level.

Usage: Select one spell when you take this trait.

Edit the chosen spell. In the Offsets Section>Caster Level Offset box enter a 1

ruthless (dwarf).name: Ruthless (Dwarf)
ruthless (dwarf).description:
You never hesitate to strike a killing blow. 

Requirements: Dwarf

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls to confirm critical hits.

curse in the blood (skinwalker).name: Curse in the Blood (Skinwalker)
curse in the blood (skinwalker).description:
Requirements: Skinwalker

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against curses. You treat your caster level as 1 higher when casting spells and spell-like abilities with the curse descriptor.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus  against curses.
You treat your caster level as 1 higher when casting spells and spell-like abilities with the curse descriptor.

helpful (halfling).name: Helpful (Halfling)
helpful (halfling).description:
You see nothing wrong with letting others achieve greatness so long as the job gets done.

Requirements: Halfling

Benefits: Whenever you successfully perform an aid another action, you grant your ally a +4 bonus instead of the normal +2.

lover of the law (abadar).name: Lover of the Law (Abadar)
lover of the law (abadar).description:
You have a strong belief in the righteousness of law and justice.

Requirements: Abadar

Benefits: You receive a +1 trait bonus on saves vs. charm and compulsion spells, and may make a new save with an additional +1 bonus (for a total of +2) if directed to act against the law.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus vs. charm and compulsion spells and you may make a new save (with  a total of +[[2]] Trait bonus) if directed to act against the law.

xa hoi soldier (xa hoi).name: Xa Hoi Soldier (Xa Hoi)
xa hoi soldier (xa hoi).description:
You were a soldier in one of Xa Hoi's extensive armies, trained under the oversight of one of Pham Duc Quan's draconic brethren.

Requirements: Xa Hoi

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Reflex saves.

surefooted ascent (hill or mountain).name: Surefooted Ascent (Hill or Mountain)
surefooted ascent (hill or mountain).description:
Requirements: Hill or Mountain

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Acrobatics and Climb checks.

rousing courage (angradd).name: Rousing Courage (Angradd)
rousing courage (angradd).description:
A dwarven warrior’s simple act of courage in Angradd’s name inspired you during one of your first battles. Now, you strive to be equally inspiring to others.

Requirements: Angradd

Benefits: Once per day, when you generate an effect that grants a morale bonus, increase that bonus by 1 for all creatures affected.

gravelwalker (darklands).name: Gravelwalker (Darklands)
gravelwalker (darklands).description:
Requirements: Darklands, usually under warm mountains

Benefits: Having grown up navigating rockslides and picking your way through partially collapsed tunnels, you are able to determine the most stable path across difficult terrain caused by rubble or other debris. You can move normally through such obstructed spaces, and can run or charge though them with a successful Acrobatics check.

contextNotes.text: You can move normally through difficult terrain caused by rubble or other debris and can run or charge though them with a successful Acrobatics check.

ilizmagorti native (skull and shackles).name: Ilizmagorti Native (Skull and Shackles)
ilizmagorti native (skull and shackles).description:
You grew up in the city of Ilizmagorti on Mediogalti Island, a pirate port infamous for both the scoundrels who visit it and the feared Red Mantis assassins who run it. You've been around pirates all your life, but you've learned to be wary in your dealings with people, as there's no telling who might be a Red Mantis in disguise. Looking to make your fortune, you hopped aboard a pirate ship in Ilizmagorti, and decided to celebrate your safe arrival in Port Peril with a few drinks at a tavern called the Formidably Maid. There were some suspicious-looking characters there, to be sure, and you decided to give them a wide berth, but as you left, feeling a bit woozy from drink, you were set upon by thugs in a dark alley and knocked unconscious.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks, and Sense Motive is a class skill for you. In addition, your familiarity with the pirate's life allows you to make untrained Knowledge (local) checks regarding pirates or the region of the Shackles.

contextNotes.text: You can make checks regarding pirates, or the region of the Shackles, untrained.

easy way or the hard way.name: Easy Way or the Hard Way
easy way or the hard way.description:
You are skilled at subduing your enemies.

Benefits: Whenever you attack one of your @UUID[Compendium.pf1.class-abilities.Item.XmALhWqXdN9yOmtd]{favored enemies} with a weapon that deals lethal damage and choose to deal nonlethal damage, you take only a –2 penalty on the attack roll instead of the normal –4.

contextNotes.text: -[[2]] Penalty on attack rolls (instead of the normal -4) made to deal non-lethal damage with a lethal weapon, when attacking one of your favored enemies.

treerazer's bane (kyonin).name: Treerazer's Bane (Kyonin)
treerazer's bane (kyonin).description:
Having fought in many battles against the demon Treerazer, you have learned the best ways to kill the various creatures under his control.

Requirements: Kyonin

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on weapon damage against demons, evil fey, and plants and animals corrupted by evil.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on weapon damage against demons, evil fey, and plants and animals corrupted by evil.

forlorn (elf).name: Forlorn (Elf)
forlorn (elf).description:
Having lived outside of traditional elf society for much or all of your life, you know the world can be cruel, dangerous, and unforgiving of the weak.

Requirements: Elf

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Fortitude saving throws.

caravan guard (jade regent).name: Caravan Guard (Jade Regent)
caravan guard (jade regent).description:
You recently hired on to Sandru's caravan to serve as a guard, but the job, while it paid well, gave you little chance to scratch the adventuring itch, since the caravan never really ventured into dangerous territory. You understand why Sandru avoided the more dangerous routes, of course, but that didn't help assuage your urge to seek adventure. You've just told Sandru that you're quitting the job in order to try your hand at adventuring,he seemed to understand, and even gave you some good tips on how best to survive potential challenges.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Survival checks, and Survival becomes a class skill for you.

NPC Choices Sandru

seasoned climber (wall of heaven).name: Seasoned Climber (Wall of Heaven)
seasoned climber (wall of heaven).description:
Your time on the slopes of the Wall of Heaven has improved your ability to surmount great peaks.

Requirements: Wall of Heaven

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Climb checks, and Climb becomes a class skill for you.

spidery climber (kasatha).name: Spidery Climber (Kasatha)
spidery climber (kasatha).description:
More hands means more handholds.

Requirements: Kasatha

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Climb checks. In addition, as long as you have two hands free, the DC to catch yourself on a wall while falling is equal to the wall’s DC + 10, and the DC to catch yourself on a slope is equal to the slope’s DC + 5.

contextNotes.text: As long as you have two hands free, the DC to catch yourself on a wall while falling is equal to the wall’s DC + 10, and the DC to catch yourself on a slope is equal to the slope’s DC + 5.

focused burn.name: Focused Burn
focused burn.description:
You know the secret of making punishing fire bombs.

Benefits: Any bombs you have that deal fire damage deal 1 additional point of fire damage on a direct hit per 2d6 of fire damage normally dealt (minimum 1 point). This additional damage does not apply to the bomb’s splash damage.

Usage: Modify damage formula of the bomb class feature in your actor.

regal presence (iomedae).name: Regal Presence (Iomedae)
regal presence (iomedae).description:
You’ve always had a presence that people respect.

Requirements: Iomedae

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks, and Diplomacy is a class skill for you.

gemstone collector (yuelral).name: Gemstone Collector (Yuelral)
gemstone collector (yuelral).description:
You have long found focus and inspiration in the natural beauty of rare gemstones.

Requirements: Yuelral

Benefits: As long as you carry bejeweled objects worth a total combined value of 200 gp x your character level, you gain a +1 trait bonus on all Will saving throws and a +2 trait bonus on a single Charisma-based skill check of your choice. 

Usage: Select any one skill that is normally Charisma-based when you take this trait.

Update the target of the note on the changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus as long as you carry bejeweled objects worth a total combined value of [[200 * @attributes.hd.total]] gp
+[[2]] Trait bonus as long as you carry bejeweled objects worth a total combined value of [[200 * @attributes.hd.total]] gp

fearless defiance.name: Fearless Defiance
fearless defiance.description:
Upon overcoming your fear, you become a scourge to your enemies.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on all saving throws against fear effects. In addition, if you successfully save against such an effect, you receive a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls against your favored enemies for 1 round.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against fear effects.
If you successfully save against a fear effect, you receive a +[[1]] trait bonus on attack rolls against your favored enemies for 1 round.

pragmatic polytheist (human; kellid).name: Pragmatic Polytheist (Human; Kellid)
pragmatic polytheist (human; kellid).description:
You pray to whatever local deity will answer your prayers—and if they won’t, to hell with them.

Requirements: Kellid Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (religion) checks and can make Knowledge (religion) checks as if trained. Once per day, you can cast @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.g8j278xcu2s1zlwy]{Resistance} on yourself as a spell-like ability cast at your highest caster level (CL 1st if you have no caster level).

contextNotes.text: You can make checks as if trained.

call of the longships (lands of the linn.name: Call of the Longships (Lands of the Linnorm Kings)
call of the longships (lands of the linn.description:
Your blood sings with longing to relive the adventures of one of your ancestors, and you are inexorably drawn to the sea.

Requirements: Lands of the Linnorm Kings

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Profession (sailor) checks and a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls made onboard ships.

Usage

Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the Profession (sailor) skill.

Update the target of the changes on the Changes tab of this trait to Profession (sailor).

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attack rolls made onboard ships.

coincunning (varisia; janderhoff).name: Coincunning (Varisia; Janderhoff)
coincunning (varisia; janderhoff).description:
Whether from working with dwarven merchants or being one yourself, you’ve developed a distinctly dwarven business sense.

Requirements: Janderhoff in Varisia

Benefits: You receive a +2 trait bonus on Perception checks to notice coins, gems, jewelry, and valuable trade goods. You gain a chance to notice such goods whenever you pass within 10 feet of them, whether or not you are actively looking.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to notice coins, gems, jewelry, and valuable trade goods. You gain a chance to notice such goods whenever you pass within 10 feet of them, whether or not you are actively looking.

fey-taken.name: Fey-Taken
fey-taken.description:
As a child, you were whisked away to the First World by mischievous fey for a time. When you returned, you were ever after considered odd and distant. You long to return there, and find the mortal world dull and at times revolting, so you do not eat as you should and do not question strange visions.

Effects: You take a –2 penalty on saving throws against disease, illusions, and poison of all kinds, as well as against the spells, spell-like abilities, and supernatural abilities of fey.

contextNotes.text: -[[2]] Penalty against disease, illusions, and poisons
-[[2]] Penalty against spells, spell-like abilities, and supernatural abilities of fey

chilled by brutality (tundra).name: Chilled by Brutality (Tundra)
chilled by brutality (tundra).description:
You were part of a group that was mercilessly attacked by giants of the tundra, who may have gone so far as to eat the dead and dying. After that, little can faze you, but you strongly prefer to finish fights with giants quickly to prevent anything like that from happening again.

Requirements: Tundra

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on attack rolls to confirm critical hits against giants and on saving throws against fear effects.

Likely Regions

Brevoy, Irrisen, Lands of the Linnorm Kings, Mendev, Realm of the Mammoth Lords.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to  confirm critical hits against giants.
+[[2]] Trait bonus against fear effects.

uskwood hunter (nidal).name: Uskwood Hunter (Nidal)
uskwood hunter (nidal).description:
You have served the Umbral Court, discreetly following interlopers through the twilit city streets of Pangolais to pinpoint their locations for the authorities.

Requirements: Nidal

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Stealth checks, and Stealth is always a class skill for you.

iron liver.name: Iron Liver
iron liver.description:
Due to a lucky constitution or frequent exposure, your body is resistant to poison, including alcohol and drugs.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Fortitude saves against poison and drugs, and a +4 trait bonus on Fortitude saves to avoid the effects of alcohol.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] trait bonus vs poison and drugs
+[[4]] trait bonus vs alcohol

planar savant.name: Planar Savant
planar savant.description:
You have always had an innate sense of the workings of the planes and their denizens.

Benefits: You may use your Charisma modifier when making Knowledge (planes) checks instead of your Intelligence modifier.

Usage: On your character's sheet, go to the Skills tab and click on the lock icon to unlock the skill configurations. Find the Knowledge (planes) skill and change the ability modifier from Int to Cha.

scion of legend (return of the runelords.name: Scion of Legend (Return of the Runelords)
scion of legend (return of the runelords.description:
Your character has a personal link or association with one of Varisia’s established heroes. This hero should be a PC you played in a previous campaign (and could be, but need not be, one of the Sihedron Heroes). Your character could be a younger sibling, younger cousin, or even an apprentice of the hero. If enough time has passed, you might be the hero’s child, or your character could have reincarnated from the soul of a hero that died before your character was born.

Benefits: Choose two skills for which your associated hero has skill ranks. You gain a +1 trait bonus on checks with both of those skills, and those two skills are class skills for you.

Once per adventure as a free action, you can call upon a previously forgotten bit of advice or lore imparted to you by the hero to gain an insight bonus equal to your class level (minimum +10) to a skill check. You may choose to apply this bonus after you roll the skill check, but must do so before the result of the roll is known.

Usage

Select two skills for which your associated hero has skill ranks when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect these two choices.

Update the target of the changes on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect these two choices.

contextNotes.text: Once per adventure, as a free action, you can gain a (+[[max(10, @attributes.hd.total)]] Insight bonus). You may use this bonus after you roll, but before the result of the roll is known.

criminally connected.name: Criminally Connected
criminally connected.description:
You have spent a lot of time dealing with the criminal underworld—so much that it might be hard for observers to see you as distinct from it. However, this familiarity gives you a leg up on heroes who refuse to muddy their reputation.

Benefits: While in settlements, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (local) checks and on Diplomacy checks to gather information.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus while in settlements.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to gather information, while in settlements.

bellis honey harvester (andoran; bellis).name: Bellis Honey Harvester (Andoran; Bellis)
bellis honey harvester (andoran; bellis).description:
Your frequent exposure to bees—and their stings—makes all such creatures seem more like nuisances than actual threats.

Requirements: Bellis [in Andoran]

Benefits: You gain DR 3/- against damage dealt by swarms and a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against distraction and poison from swarms.

contextNotes.text: DR 3/- against damage dealt by swarms.
+[[1]] Trait bonus  against distraction and poison from swarms.

empty mask.name: Empty Mask
empty mask.description:
You have spent so long hiding your true identity to escape political enemies that you have lost much of your sense of self.

Effects: You take a -1 penalty on Will saving throws against compulsions. This penalty increases to -2 against foes who know your true identity.

contextNotes.text: -[[1]] Penalty against compulsions. This penalty increases to -[[2]] against foes who know your true identity.

i know a guy (sczarni faction).name: I Know a Guy (Sczarni Faction)
i know a guy (sczarni faction).description:
The Sczarni are often related to each other, and no matter how distant the kinship, you know that you can rely upon your extended family for information.

Requirements: Sczarni Faction

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks made to gather information and a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (local) checks.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to gather information checks.

strong-willed (cayden cailean).name: Strong-Willed (Cayden Cailean)
strong-willed (cayden cailean).description:
Your fervent desire to choose your own path gives you strong willpower.

Requirements: Cayden Cailean

Benefits: You receive a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against charm and compulsion effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus charm and compulsion effects.

lepidstadt scar (ustalav).name: Lepidstadt Scar (Ustalav)
lepidstadt scar (ustalav).description:
You engaged in nonlethal duels at Fifthstones Hill so often that you gained a visible (though minor) scar on your face. Because of this experience, you’re unflinching in the heat of combat, calmly analyzing how to lessen the impact of a foe’s best attacks.

Requirements: Ustalav

Benefits: You gain a +1 dodge bonus to AC against attack rolls made to confirm a critical threat against you.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Dodge bonus to AC against attack rolls made to confirm a critical threat against you.

quain martial artist (quain).name: Quain Martial Artist (Quain)
quain martial artist (quain).description:
Having grown up in Quain, you were taught under various schools of martial arts, and have used all you have learned to hone your fighting prowess.

Requirements: Quain

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on damage rolls when using unarmed strikes.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on damage rolls when using unarmed strikes.

desert nomad (thuvia).name: Desert Nomad (Thuvia)
desert nomad (thuvia).description:
You were born and raised in the burning sands of Thuvia's desert interior.

Requirements: Thuvia

Benefits: You gain a +4 trait bonus on any saving throws made to resist the effects of being in hot conditions, and a +1 trait bonus on all saving throws against fire effects.

contextNotes.text:
+[[1]] Trait bonus 
vs fire effects.

+[[4]] Trait bonus 

 to resist the effects of being in hot conditions.

cruel child (changeling).name: Cruel Child (Changeling)
cruel child (changeling).description:
Your wicked bloodline shines through, manifesting as a sadistic bloodlust in battle.

Requirements: Changeling

Benefits: When you make a full-round attack with your claws, you gain a +1 trait bonus on the attack rolls.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to attack when you make a full-round attack with your claws.

shadow stalker (fetchling).name: Shadow Stalker (Fetchling)
shadow stalker (fetchling).description:
You are dedicated to mastering the art of hiding in the shadows.

Requirements: Fetchling

Benefits: Increase the miss chance gained from your @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-racial-traits.Item.bTQhfYlU0IgTdMMX]{shadow blending} ability by 5%.

warsmith (dwarf).name: Warsmith (Dwarf)
warsmith (dwarf).description:
You have an intuitive grasp of the weak points of metal and stone.

Requirements: Dwarf

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus to damage rolls against creatures and objects made primarily of clay, crystal, earth, metal, or stone. Knowledge (engineering) is a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to damage rolls against creatures and objects made primarily of clay, crystal, earth, metal, or stone.

clever mount.name: Clever Mount
clever mount.description:
Requirements: Any animal can be a clever mount.

Benefits: This mount can learn four tricks per point of Intelligence, instead of the typical three. Commanding the mount with the Handle an Animal use of the Handle Animal skill requires a successful DC 15 skill check instead of the usual DC 10, however, as it is willful and easily bored.

contextNotes.text: When handling your clever mount, Handle an Animal requires a successful DC 15 skill check instead of the usual DC 10.

ascendant recollection.name: Ascendant Recollection
ascendant recollection.description:
Your first bloodline power is particularly strong.

Benefits: Your sorcerer level is considered to be 1 level higher when determining the effects of your 1st-level bloodline power.

well bred (hermea).name: Well Bred (Hermea)
well bred (hermea).description:
You are the product of generations of selective breeding, organized by the gold dragon Mengkare. Your education is top-notch, and you have a broad base of knowledge. Unfortunately, the isolation of Hermea has also made you slightly naive.

Requirements: Hermea

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (history) and Knowledge (religion) checks but take a –1 penalty on all Sense Motive checks.

necrotoxin resistance (geb).name: Necrotoxin Resistance (Geb)
necrotoxin resistance (geb).description:
As a resident of the undead lands of Geb, you know well that the walking dead often carry deadly poisons and diseases within them.

Requirements: Geb

Benefits: You gain a +3 trait bonus on saving throws against disease and poison abilities and effects inflicted by creatures of the undead type (such as ghoul fever or a vargouille’s poison).

contextNotes.text:
+[[3]] Trait bonus 
 against disease and poison abilities and effects inflicted by creatures of the undead type.

student survivalist (jade regent).name: Student Survivalist (Jade Regent)
student survivalist (jade regent).description:
Although she is seen as something of a mystery to most of Sandpoint's citizens, Shalelu has never really seemed all that mysterious to you. Of course, that's probably because she helped raise you. You have never quite figured out why Shalelu decided to treat you as a younger sibling, but you certainly appreciated it - and you eagerly absorbed all of the survivalist tricks that she showed you over the years.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on all Survival checks, and Survival is a class skill for you. In addition, you gain a +1 trait bonus on all attack rolls against foes that threaten your mentor.

NPC Choices: Shalelu

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on all attack rolls against foes that threaten Shalelu, your mentor.

secret scrolls.name: Secret Scrolls
secret scrolls.description:
You were inducted into a secret magical tradition such as the Esoteric Order of the Palatine Eye.

Benefits: You can cast @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.j0bc0iii8zp1jiwr]{read magic} once per day as a spell-like ability, and gain a +1 trait bonus on Use Magic Device checks to activate scrolls you cannot normally use.

Usage: When you add the spell to the spellbook, you can adjust the "0/0" display to be "3/3", change the "Casts Per Day Used" formula to 1, then change the spellbook's spell slots to match.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on Use Magic Device checks to activate scrolls you cannot normally use.

colonial sympathizer (human; mwangi).name: Colonial Sympathizer (Human; Mwangi)
colonial sympathizer (human; mwangi).description:
Your behavior is heavily influenced by the Sargavan colonials.

Requirements: Mwangi Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Bluff and Diplomacy checks involving Sargavan colonials and others with this trait.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus checks involving Sargavan colonials and others with the Colonial Sympathizer trait.
+[[1]] Trait bonus checks involving Sargavan colonials and others with the Colonial Sympathizer trait.

protective faith (torag).name: Protective Faith (Torag)
protective faith (torag).description:
Your faith in the Father of Creation strengthens your ability to shield others.

Requirements: Torag

Benefits: When you use the aid another action to grant your ally a bonus to AC, you grant a +3 bonus to AC instead of the normal +2.

contextNotes.text: When you  aid another  to grant  a bonus to AC, you grant a +3 bonus to AC instead of the normal +2.

rapscallion.name: Rapscallion
rapscallion.description:
