You’ve spent your entire life thumbing your nose at the establishment, and you take pride in your run-ins with the law. Somehow, despite all the mischievous behavior in your life, you’ve never been caught—you always manage to stay one step ahead of your pursuers.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Escape Artist checks and a +1 trait bonus on Initiative checks.

inured to disease (skinwalker; wererat-k.name: Inured to Disease (Skinwalker; Wererat-Kin)
inured to disease (skinwalker; wererat-k.description:
The first sign of your wererat heritage was your seeming imperviousness to disease.

Requirements: Wererat-Kin Skinwalker Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Fortitude saving throws against diseases.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against diseases.

colonial entitlement (human; sargavan).name: Colonial Entitlement (Human; Sargavan)
colonial entitlement (human; sargavan).description:
You are a firm believer in the superiority of your Chelish heritage, and you cling to your racial and cultural pride.

Requirements: Human Heritage (Chelish or colonial Sargavan )

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Intimidate checks against Mwangi.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus  against Mwangi.

quantium university graduate (nex).name: Quantium University Graduate (Nex)
quantium university graduate (nex).description:
You graduated from one of Quantium's justly famous arcane academies, and the rigorous program of study has honed your mind.

Requirements: Nex

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on concentration checks when casting arcane spells.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on concentration checks when casting arcane spells.

demonbane summoner (worldwound).name: Demonbane Summoner (Worldwound)
demonbane summoner (worldwound).description:
Your line is derived directly from the god callers of Sarkoris. You adamantly oppose the demonic forces of the Worldwound in hopes of reclaiming your lost lands.

Requirements: The Worldwound

Benefits: The attacks of creatures you summon are treated as cold iron for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction.

contextNotes.text: The attacks of creatures you summon are treated as cold iron for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction.

giant ambivalence (temperate highlands).name: Giant Ambivalence (Temperate Highlands)
giant ambivalence (temperate highlands).description:
You witnessed or were otherwise strongly influenced by two separate acts by giants, one of great beneficence and one of great violence. You reflexively gauge the intentions of powerful creatures in case they turn to violence.

Requirements: Temperate Highlands

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on initiative checks. You also gain a +1 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks, and a +2 trait bonus on Perception checks to not be surprised in a surprise round of combat.

Likely Regions

Isger, Molthune, Numeria, Realm of the Mammoth Lords, Varisia.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus  to not be surprised in a surprise round of combat.

kwanlai believer (kwanlai).name: Kwanlai Believer (Kwanlai)
kwanlai believer (kwanlai).description:
You were raised in Kwanlai in one of the devout tengu churches, and your dedication to your cause makes your divine abilities particularly potent.

Requirements: Kwanlai

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on concentration checks made while casting divine spells.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus while casting divine spells.

shadowsight (nidal).name: Shadowsight (Nidal)
shadowsight (nidal).description:
Your people’s ancient compact with Zon-Kuthon manifests strongly in you. While the darkness remains fearsome, you can at least see what lurks there.

Requirements: Nidal

Benefits: As a swift action once per day, your eyes can pierce the surrounding darkness—you gain the benefit of low-light vision for a number of rounds equal to your level.

fast talker.name: Fast Talker
fast talker.description:
You had a knack at getting yourself into trouble as a child, and as a result developed a silver tongue at an early age.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Bluff checks, and Bluff is always a class skill for you.

planar voyager (the concordance).name: Planar Voyager (The Concordance)
planar voyager (the concordance).description:
Your experience with planar travel gives you an edge on your foes when you travel beyond the Material Plane.

Requirements: The Concordance

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on initiative checks and saving throws when you are not on the Material Plane.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus when you are not on the Material Plane.
+[[1]] Trait bonus when you are not on the Material Plane.

sea-souled (coastline or island).name: Sea-Souled (Coastline or Island)
sea-souled (coastline or island).description:
You are at home at sea.

Requirements: Coastline or Island

Benefits: You receive a +1 trait bonus on Swim checks, and you can always take 10 while Swimming.

contextNotes.text: You can always take 10 while Swimming.

corpse dodger (ustalav).name: Corpse Dodger (Ustalav)
corpse dodger (ustalav).description:
You spent your formative years in the town of Ravengro, where your proximity to both a prominent graveyard and an infamous prison left you no stranger to the occasional zombie or skeleton. As such, you developed unique methods of dodging these pests.

Requirements: Ustalav

Benefits: Once per day, you can cast @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.rekl0ikgnorz60jc]{hide from undead} as a spell-like ability. The casting time is a swift action and the caster level for this effect is always 1st; this ability is otherwise identical to the spell.

merchant family (rise of the runelords).name: Merchant Family (Rise of the Runelords)
merchant family (rise of the runelords).description:
You are related to one of the four noble families from Magnimar who founded the Mercantile League of Sandpoint. You either grew up in Magnimar as a cousin in the Valdemar or Deverin family or were born and raised in Sandpoint. Education in running a business and years of looking after the family enterprise have given you a knack for trade.

Benefits: You increase the gp limit of any settlement by 20% and can resell items at an additional 10% over the amount of gp you normally would get from selling off treasure.

riverfolk (river kingdoms).name: Riverfolk (River Kingdoms)
riverfolk (river kingdoms).description:
You've grown up on the tributaries of the Sellen River system, and are as comfortable on boats as on land.

Requirements: River Kingdoms

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Profession (sailor) checks and any skill checks involving rope use.

Usage: Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the Profession (sailor) skill.

Update the target of the Change on the Changes tab of this trait to Profession (sailor).

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on checks involving rope use.

blood of giants (reign of winter).name: Blood of Giants (Reign of Winter)
blood of giants (reign of winter).description:
You’re a big person, and people have always said you’ve got some giant blood in you. Even as a child, you towered over your friends, and as you grew older, you grew even taller and stronger. Maybe your hair has a tint of blue as well, or your skin is as pale as snow. Perhaps someday you’ll get the opportunity to travel to the North and meet some real giants, and see whether the rumors about you are true.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on combat maneuver checks to sunder, and a +1 trait bonus to your CMD against bull rush and overrun combat maneuvers.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on checks to sunder,
+[[1]] Trait bonus against bull rush and overrun combat maneuvers.

bent body (tiefling; beastbrood).name: Bent Body (Tiefling; Beastbrood)
bent body (tiefling; beastbrood).description:
While nothing is actually reversed, your bones and limbs are slightly out of alignment.

Requirements: Beastbrood Tielfling Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus to CMD when resisting grapple attempts.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus when resisting grapple attempts.

jungle fighter (sargavan).name: Jungle Fighter (Sargavan)
jungle fighter (sargavan).description:
You are sure-footed in jungle environments.

Requirements: Sargavan

Benefits: Your speed may not be reduced to less than 10 feet by difficult terrain in jungles (meaning you may take a 5-foot step in difficult terrain and not provoke an attack of opportunity).

practiced impression (suli).name: Practiced Impression (Suli)
practiced impression (suli).description:
You traveled constantly in your youth, and were forced to practice making a good first impression until you could do so effortlessly.

Requirements: Suli

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks made against NPCs whom you have known for less than 1 hour.

contextNotes.text:
+[[2]] Trait bonus 
 on checks made against NPCs whom you have known for less than 1 hour.

starchild (desna).name: Starchild (Desna)
starchild (desna).description:
Desna sensed your love of travel and promised you would always be able to find your way home. You can automatically determine where true north is.

Requirements: Desna

Benefits: You can automatically determine where true north is. You gain a +4 trait bonus on Survival checks to avoid becoming lost.

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Trait bonus to avoid becoming lost. You can automatically determine where true north is.

regional recluse - urglin (varisia).name: Regional Recluse - Urglin (Varisia)
regional recluse - urglin (varisia).description:
Your home community prides itself on remaining independent from Varisia’s city-states.

Requirements: Varisia

Benefits: Choose one of Varisia’s unaffiliated settlements from this list below. You gain a +1 trait bonus on Survival checks and one of the following benefits.

Urglin

Gain Orc as a bonus language. If you already know this language, gain a +1 trait bonus on Intimidate checks.

Usage:

  1. If you already have the Orc language, Update the target of the untargeted Change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect Stealth.

  2. if this trait is your only source of the Orc language, delete the untargeted Change .


Special Note

This is one of five versions of this trait. Other versions can be found here:

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.QezjzIjvbfIxTLPD]{Regional Recluse - Arsmeril or Crying Leaf (Varisia)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.SUxxga9Juql4kY3J]{Regional Recluse - Ilsurian (Varisia)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.qDxSXompaz69Ceqj]{Regional Recluse - Sanos Forest or Whistledown (Varisia)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.EtUNYMr9ykH2XhZA]{Regional Recluse - Turtleback Ferry (Varisia)}

unspeakable bond (any elder god).name: Unspeakable Bond (any Elder God)
unspeakable bond (any elder god).description:
You are an ally of the unclean creatures of the world.

Requirements: Any Elder God (Great Old Ones or Outer Gods)

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks when dealing with creatures of the aberration type.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus when dealing with creatures of the aberration type

arcane scholar.name: Arcane Scholar
arcane scholar.description:
Study of the “Prime Archmage” chapter has broadened your magical knowledge.

Benefits: Choose either Knowledge (arcana) or Spellcraft; you gain a +1 trait bonus on that skill and it is a class skill for you.

Usage: Go to the Details tab on this trait and select either Knowledge (arcana) or Spellcraft. Select corresponding skill in Changes tab afterwards.

iron control (half-orc; human-raised).name: Iron Control (Half-Orc; Human-Raised)
iron control (half-orc; human-raised).description:
You learned the hard way that expressing the rage you inherited from your orc blood exacerbates humans’ fearful and hostile reactions to you; after years of effort, you’ve mastered self-control and buried your anger deep.

Requirements: Half-Orc, Human-Raised

Benefits: Whenever you’re confused and roll to determine behavior in a given round, subtract 10 from the result.

witch-tongued (irrisen).name: Witch-Tongued (Irrisen)
witch-tongued (irrisen).description:
Hailing from frozen Irrisen, where witches rule the land, you deal with others in a cool, calculated way.

Requirements: Irrisen

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks, and you gain a +1 trait bonus on Intimidate checks against non-witches.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against non-witches.

noble born - orlovsky (kingmaker).name: Noble Born - Orlovsky (Kingmaker)
noble born - orlovsky (kingmaker).description:
You claim a tangential but legitimate connection to one of Brevoy’s noble families. If you aren’t human, you were likely adopted by one of Brevoy’s nobles or were instead a favored servant or even a childhood friend of a noble scion. Whatever the cause, you’ve had a comfortable life, but one far from the dignity and decadence your distant cousins know. Although you are associated with an esteemed name, your immediate family is hardly well to do, and you’ve found your name to be more of a burden to you than a boon in many social situations. You’ve recently decided to test yourself, to see if you can face the world without the aegis of a name you have little real claim or care for. An expedition into the storied Stolen Lands seems like just the test to see if you really are worth the title “noble.”

Benefits: Choose one of the following noble families and associated benefits:

Orlovsky

Your family has a reputation for avoiding conflicts. You gain a +1 trait bonus on your CMD. In addition, choose Acrobatics, Diplomacy, or Stealth—you gain a +1 trait bonus on this skill. Your family motto is “High Above.”

Usage: Select Acrobatics, Diplomacy, or Stealth when you take this trait.

Update the target of the untargeted change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

Special Note

This is one of six versions of this trait. Other versions can be found here:

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.79pUkpcI6MYSTShf]{Noble Born - Garress (Kingmaker)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.nWlZ25PwQDz2zhoH]{Noble Born - Lebeda (Kingmaker)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.stEGVAsBCoaY5Ifk]{Noble Born - Lodovka (Kingmaker)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.c8eiTJJmkH6EyxnY]{Noble Born - Medvyed (Kingmaker)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.9RUX6RB5UQYnNIxR]{Noble Born - Surtova (Kingmaker)}

boarded in varisia (serpent's skull).name: Boarded in Varisia (Serpent's Skull)
boarded in varisia (serpent's skull).description:
Traveling from lands far to the north, you likely have little experience with the jungle, its denizens, or its strange forces. Just as the lands of Garund are wild and exotic to you, so are you to them.

Benefits: This unusualness manifests in one of two ways. Choose to gain either a +2 trait bonus on all Bluff checks made against inhabitants of the jungle, or treat your spells with the mind-affecting descriptor as being +1 caster level higher when cast against creatures and natives of the jungle.

Usage: Select either Bluff checks or mind-affecting descriptor spells when you take this trait.

Update the target of the note on the changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice and delete the change relating to the other choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on all Bluff checks made against inhabitants of the jungle.
+[[1]] caster level  to your spells with the mind-affecting descriptor when cast against creatures and natives of the jungle.

pioneer (kingmaker).name: Pioneer (Kingmaker)
pioneer (kingmaker).description:
You have long lived along the southern border of Brevoy, in the shadow of wilderness known as the Stolen Lands. Life has been hard, but through hunting, trapping, trading, and coaxing crops from the freezing earth, you've learned how to survive on the rugged frontier. With the wilderness ever at your door, you've also learned much about its denizens and the wild creatures that lurk in that unwholesome land. Your family might even claim holdings in the Stolen Lands, with elders telling stories of being driven from or robbed of a lost ancestral homestead, fertile farmlands, bountiful orchards, or a hidden mining claim. Whether because of your personal expertise and familiarity with the borderlands or in order to reclaim your family's land, you've joined the expedition into the Stolen Lands.

Benefits: You begin play with a horse. Also, choose one of the following skills: Climb, Handle Animal, Knowledge (nature), Perception, Ride, Survival, or Swim - you gain a +1 trait bonus on this skill.

Usage: Select Climb, Handle Animal, Knowledge (nature), Perception, Ride, Survival, or Swim when you take this trait.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

sevenarches seeker (sevenarches).name: Sevenarches Seeker (Sevenarches)
sevenarches seeker (sevenarches).description:
You’ve long heard rumors about the mysterious archways of Sevenarches and have spent much of your life trying to unravel the mysteries behind the portals.

Requirements: Sevenarches

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (history) checks and Perception checks to notice secret doors, and Knowledge (history) becomes a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to notice secret doors.

proxy of many.name: Proxy of Many
proxy of many.description:
As a pantheist, you are accustomed to calling on aid from a variety of sources, and have a pragmatic sense for how to best make use of any ally.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks made to request a favor from a creature that has not performed a favor for you in the past week. This bonus increases to +4 if you have performed a favor for that creature since it last did a favor for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus  to request a favor from a creature that has not performed a favor for you in the past week. This increases to a +[[4]] Trait bonus  if you have performed a favor for that creature since it last did a favor for you.

devotee of kalistrade (druma).name: Devotee of Kalistrade (Druma)
devotee of kalistrade (druma).description:
As an adherent of the Prophecies of Kalistrade, you understand the importance of a good deal.

Requirements: Druma

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Appraise checks, and a +1 trait bonus on Bluff checks when trying to make a deal with nonbelievers.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus when trying to make a deal with nonbelievers [in the Prophecies of Kalistrade].

superstitious (qc).name: Superstitious (QC)
superstitious (qc).description:
Raised around those who regarded most magic as innately dangerous, no matter the source, you cannot bring yourself to submit to magic willingly even for your own good.

Effects: Whenever you are affected with a harmless spell, there is a 50% chance that you must roll a saving throw to prevent that effect, even if it would benefit you. This does not apply to harmless spells cast by you upon yourself.

contextNotes.text: Whenever you are affected with a harmless spell, there is a 50% chance that you must roll a saving throw to prevent that effect, even if it would benefit you. This does not apply to harmless spells cast by you upon yourself.

spirit sense.name: Spirit Sense
spirit sense.description:
You are so attuned to the spiritual world that it is hard to get the jump on you.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Perception checks to avoid being surprised and to detect invisible or incorporeal creatures.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to avoid being surprised and to detect invisible or incorporeal creatures.

ogre avoidance.name: Ogre Avoidance
ogre avoidance.description:
You have practiced defensive maneuvers to avoid being caught by ogres, whose grasps you know are tight and unforgiving.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus to CMD against bull rush and grapple combat maneuvers.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against bull rush
+[[2]] Trait bonus against grapple

free people (realm of the mammoth lords;.name: Free People (Realm of the Mammoth Lords; Kellid)
free people (realm of the mammoth lords;.description:
The independent tribes from the Realm of the Mammoth Lords hold little regard for the control others wield over free people. Their willful, independent streak makes them suspicious of any attempt to guide their behavior.

Requirements: Kellid; Realm of the Mammoth Lords native

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks and on Will saves against mind-affecting effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus

chosen of iomedae.name: Chosen of Iomedae
chosen of iomedae.description:
At your birth, your parents dedicated your soul to Iomedae to mold into a sword of her light. The goddess blessed you, granting you a gift of light to brighten your path through darkness and a fine sword with which to spread her will.

Benefits: You may cast @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.ip9onjfp6nf1vlv9]{light} once per day as a spell-like ability (caster level 1st), and you begin play with a masterwork longsword. In addition, whenever light is cast upon this sword, the radius of light and its duration is doubled.

Usage: When you add the spell to the spellbook, you can adjust the "0/0" display to be "1/1", change the "Casts Per Day Used" formula to 1, then change the spellbook's spell slots to match.
Add a longsword to your inventory and mark it masterwork.

healthy (ruins of azlant).name: Healthy (Ruins of Azlant)
healthy (ruins of azlant).description:
You come from a long line of hale and long-lived people. Growing up, you rarely got sick and had enough energy to run all day if it suited you. You don’t get as tired as other people and repeated physical activities don’t seem to bother you much. You were selected as part of the expedition because the Bountiful Venture Company saw great importance and promise in your robust health.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Fortitude saves, Swim checks to prevent nonlethal damage from fatigue after swimming for more than an hour at a time, and Constitution checks to prevent fatigue. In addition, you can hold your breath for a number of rounds equal to 3 times your Constitution score.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on checks to prevent nonlethal damage from fatigue after swimming for more than an hour at a time.  You can hold your breath for a number of rounds equal to [[3 * @abilities.con.total]].
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks to prevent fatigue. You can hold your breath for a number of rounds equal to [[3 * @abilities.con.total]].

aberration hunter (shenmen).name: Aberration Hunter (Shenmen)
aberration hunter (shenmen).description:
You refuse to give in to the monsters that feast on your people, and hunt them instead.

Requirements: Shenmen

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attacks of opportunity against aberrations.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus  on attacks of opportunity against aberrations.

wild ferocity (half-orc).name: Wild Ferocity (Half-Orc)
wild ferocity (half-orc).description:
Requirements: Half-Orc

Benefits: Select one terrain type from the ranger’s @UUID[Compendium.pf1.class-abilities.Item.U2YBSx1s8cT8iDq1]{favored terrain} list. While in this terrain type, taking a standard action while disabled does not cause you to take the normal 1 point of damage. You can use this trait when acting as if disabled despite having a negative hit point total as a result of @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-racial-traits.Item.OyIvyI3x7V5HIzZL]{orc ferocity} or the @UUID[Compendium.pf1.feats.Item.O0e0UCim27GPKFuW]{Diehard }feat.

darklands scholar.name: Darklands Scholar
darklands scholar.description:
Unsatisfied with mere stories, you have spent years researching journals and firsthand accounts of those who explored the Darklands before you.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Knowledge checks to identify the weaknesses of Darklands creatures.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to identify the weaknesses of Darklands creatures.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to identify the weaknesses of Darklands creatures.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to identify the weaknesses of Darklands creatures.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to identify the weaknesses of Darklands creatures.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to identify the weaknesses of Darklands creatures.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to identify the weaknesses of Darklands creatures.

brave words (ninshaburian).name: Brave Words (Ninshaburian)
brave words (ninshaburian).description:
You inherit your forebears’ ability to fight off terror with bragging or insults.

Requirements: Ninshaburian

Benefits: As a swift action, when you have the @UUID[Compendium.pf1.pf1e-rules.JournalEntry.NSqfXaj4MevUR2uJ.JournalEntryPage.uqpJ7ZMnWF5qjOTl]{Frightened} condition, you can function as if you only have the @UUID[Compendium.pf1.pf1e-rules.JournalEntry.NSqfXaj4MevUR2uJ.JournalEntryPage.XxLUswkCuXnXmA5T]{Shaken} condition for 1 round by shouting at the source of your fear. You must be able to see the source of the fear condition. You can use this trait only once per fear effect. This trait grants no benefits against the shaken or @UUID[Compendium.pf1.pf1e-rules.JournalEntry.NSqfXaj4MevUR2uJ.JournalEntryPage.nGTsTfpWcJTTU3rk]{Panicked} conditions.

stalwart elk (chernasardo riding elk).name: Stalwart Elk (Chernasardo Riding Elk)
stalwart elk (chernasardo riding elk).description:
Requirements: Elk - Chernasardo Riding Elk

Benefits: While riding a Chernasardo riding elk, you gain a +4 trait bonus on Handle Animal, Ride, and wild empathy checks to resist another creature’s unnatural aura.

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Trait bonus on to resist another creature’s unnatural aura.
+[[4]] Trait bonus on to resist another creature’s unnatural aura.
+[[4]] Trait bonus on to resist another creature’s unnatural aura.

wild shadow (halfling).name: Wild Shadow (Halfling)
wild shadow (halfling).description:
Requirements: Halfling

Benefits: Select one terrain type from the ranger’s @UUID[Compendium.pf1.class-abilities.Item.U2YBSx1s8cT8iDq1]{favored terrain} list. You can attempt Stealth checks in this terrain in normal light without having cover or being invisible. You still can’t attempt Stealth checks in areas of bright light without invisibility or cover.

contextNotes.text: You can attempt checks in your favored  terrain in normal light without having cover or being invisible. You still can’t attempt Stealth checks in areas of bright light without invisibility or cover.

keleshite princess (qadiran keleshite fe.name: Keleshite Princess (Qadiran Keleshite female)
keleshite princess (qadiran keleshite fe.description:
You have been raised to expect obedience from those around you, regardless of their station, and are not shy about demanding what you want.

Requirements: Qadiran Keleshite female

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus to Diplomacy and Intimidate checks, and one of these skills (your choice) is always a class skill for you.

Usage: Choose one of the skills mentioned above and add it in the Class Skill section of the Details tab of this trait.

wardbreaker (worldwound).name: Wardbreaker (Worldwound)
wardbreaker (worldwound).description:
You have studied techniques designed to pierce magical protections, with the goal of assisting your Abyssal masters in bringing down the wardstones that guard the borders of Mendev.

Requirements: Worldwound

Benefits: Once per day, you can attempt a melee touch attack against an opponent that you believe has a deflection bonus to AC. If your attack is successful, your foe’s deflection bonus to AC is halved (to a minimum of +0) for a number of rounds equal to your Charisma modifier. You can use this ability twice per day at 10th level and three times per day at 20th level.

pearl diver (xidao).name: Pearl Diver (Xidao)
pearl diver (xidao).description:
You were a pearl harvester for a nation bordering Xidao, and you know just what clues to look for when searching for a worthwhile haul in the briny shallows.

Requirements: Xidao

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Appraise and Swim checks.

monster survivor.name: Monster Survivor
monster survivor.description:
You’ve escaped with your life after a terrible monster attack.

Benefits: You gain a +4 dodge bonus to AC against attacks of opportunity made against you by Large or larger opponents when you’re making a withdraw action.

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Dodge bonus against attacks of opportunity made against you by Large or larger opponents when you’re making a withdraw action.

dockside brawler (skull and shackles).name: Dockside Brawler (Skull and Shackles)
dockside brawler (skull and shackles).description:
You grew up on the dangerous docks of one of the Shackles' numerous ports or anchorages, and quickly learned that fists often make a better point than fancy words, especially when used the right way. You've always preferred action to talk, anyway. While drinking at a tavern called the Formidably Maid in Port Peril last night, you got into a brawl with some of the other patrons. You handled yourself well, but a group of rough-looking characters ganged up on you and overwhelmed you, knocking you unconscious. Fortunately, you were able to conceal your brass knuckles when you were press-ganged, and you begin the campaign with them, regardless of your starting circumstances.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on damage rolls with @UUID[Compendium.pf1.weapons-and-ammo.Item.b5FfLdVhbcxQrNdU]{brass knuckles} and improvised weapons.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on damage rolls with brass knuckles and improvised weapons.

cleansing light (sarenrae).name: Cleansing Light (Sarenrae)
cleansing light (sarenrae).description:
Your faith is pure and strong, and your positive energy purges undead.

Requirements: Sarenrae

Benefits: When dealing damage to undead with your channel energy, you can reroll any damage die that shows a natural 1.

contextNotes.text: When dealing damage to undead with your channel energy, you can reroll any damage die that shows a natural 1.

mizu ki hikari rebel (chu ye).name: Mizu Ki Hikari Rebel (Chu Ye)
mizu ki hikari rebel (chu ye).description:
source: Dragon Empires Primer pg. 9
You have trained in martial arts under the banner of Mizu Ki Hikari rebels.

Requirements: Chu Ye

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on damage rolls made with unarmed attacks.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on damage rolls made with unarmed attacks.

abyssal eavesdropper (sarkoris, worldwou.name: Abyssal Eavesdropper (Sarkoris, Worldwound)
abyssal eavesdropper (sarkoris, worldwou.description:
Having grown up near the Worldwound, you have a rough understanding of the demons’ guttural babble.

Requirements: Sarkoris or the Worldwound

Benefits: You add Abyssal to your list of languages known, though you take a –2 penalty on Diplomacy, Intimidate, and Bluff checks made while speaking Abyssal, as well as a –2 penalty on Linguistics checks made to read Abyssal text. If you gain Abyssal as a language again at a later time (such as by taking a rank in Linguistics and selecting Abyssal as a learned language), these skill check penalties become +2 trait bonuses instead.

contextNotes.text: -[[2]] penalty on checks made while speaking Abyssal. Becomes +[[2]] Trait bonus once you learn Abyssal.
-[[2]] penalty on checks made while speaking Abyssal. Becomes +[[2]] Trait bonus once you learn Abyssal.
-[[2]] penalty on checks made while speaking Abyssal. Becomes +[[2]] Trait bonus once you learn Abyssal.
-[[2]] penalty  to read Abyssal text. Becomes +[[2]] Trait bonus once you learn Abyssal.

carnation scales (kobold).name: Carnation Scales (Kobold)
carnation scales (kobold).description:
Though your light pink scales made you the subject of ridicule by your tribe, you can adapt to more environments than they can.

Requirements: Kobold

Benefits: You gain a +1 bonus on Survival checks, and Survival is always a class skill for you. Most kobolds with carnation scales also have the @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-racial-traits.Item.VIBm0jH0Xerz1xxp]{day raider} alternate racial trait (see page 7).

lillend's harp (aasimar; musetouched).name: Lillend's Harp (Aasimar; Musetouched)
lillend's harp (aasimar; musetouched).description:
Your ancestor was a lillend, a being of exceptional grace and singular musical talent.

Requirements: Musetouched Aasimar Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Perform (string) checks. This bonus increases to +2 when you make a Perform check as part of a bardic performance.

Usage: Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the Perform (string) skill.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to Perform (string).

Update the target of the note on the Changes tab of this trait to Perform (string).

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus when you make a check as part of a bardic performance. (This trait already provides a +1 trait bonus to all checks, simply add 1))

fey hex (irrisen).name: Fey Hex (Irrisen)
fey hex (irrisen).description:
You have learned to incorporate fey enchantments into your hexes to snare victims that shake off your curses.

Requirements: Irrisen

Benefits: Once per day as a free action, when a creature succeeds at its saving throw against your hex that cannot target the same creature more than once per day, you can declare that creature to be subject to your fey hex. You can attempt the failed hex against the creature a second time, so long as you do so within 1 minute.

the word (tyrant's grasp).name: The Word (Tyrant's Grasp)
the word (tyrant's grasp).description:
In a nation overcrowded with faith, you often worry that your message is drowned out, but nonetheless important. The souls in Roslar’s Coffer needed comfort and charity once upon a time, but have bounced back so fully—in part thanks to your hard work—that you worry you’ve made yourself unnecessary.

Benefits: You are accustomed to long hours, granting you a +1 trait bonus on Fortitude saving throws. Your faith is invigorating, and once per day you can lay on hands as a paladin of half your character level (minimum 1). If you gain the lay on hands ability from a class, you instead gain one additional use of lay on hands each day.

Usage:

  1. If you do not have the lay on hands ability from a class:

    a) Add that feature to your actor

    b) Edit the Feature and update the Details tab> Limited Uses to

    floor(@attributes.hd.total/ 4) + @abilities.cha.mod

  2. If you do have the lay on hands ability from a class:

    a) Edit the Feature and update the Details tab> Limited Uses to

    floor(@classes.paladin.level / 2) + @abilities.cha.mod +1

*replace paladin in step 2a with the class tag for the class that granted the feature if you gained LoH from a different class

skillful (ruins of azlant).name: Skillful (Ruins of Azlant)
skillful (ruins of azlant).description:
You’ve always been interested in a wide array of things, and growing up you’ve spent time honing some of these talents, which has helped you in life. Some have attributed your successes to luck, but others have recognized what you truly have— skill. This interest in and practice of a diverse array of skills and abilities has served you well in a number of different jobs, and following in your varied interests you learned of the expedition to the ruins of Azlant. After your application, the Bountiful Venture Company selected you for the expedition because your skillful approach to matters would be helpful for a growing colony.

Benefits: Once per day when you fail an Acrobatics, Climb, Spellcraft, Stealth, or Use Magic Device check, you can immediately reroll that check as a free action. You must take the second result, even if it’s worse.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, when you fail a check, you can immediately reroll that check as a free action. You must take the second result, even if it’s worse. ([[@resources.skillfulRuinsOfAzlant.value]] remaining uses)

Once per day, when you fail a check, you can immediately reroll that check as a free action. You must take the second result, even if it’s worse. ([[@resources.skillfulRuinsOfAzlant.value]] remaining uses)

Once per day, when you fail a check, you can immediately reroll that check as a free action. You must take the second result, even if it’s worse. ([[@resources.skillfulRuinsOfAzlant.value]] remaining uses)

Once per day, when you fail a check, you can immediately reroll that check as a free action. You must take the second result, even if it’s worse. ([[@resources.skillfulRuinsOfAzlant.value]] remaining uses)

Once per day, when you fail a check, you can immediately reroll that check as a free action. You must take the second result, even if it’s worse. ([[@resources.skillfulRuinsOfAzlant.value]] remaining uses)

robot slayer (iron gods).name: Robot Slayer (Iron Gods)
robot slayer (iron gods).description:
The strange automatons that plague the wilds of Numeria are a blight upon the world, and the idea that there could be countless more of these creatures lying in wait in the unknown ruins scattered throughout the region chills your blood. Khonnir’s recovery of a deactivated robot from the caves below Torch worries you—not only could that thing wake up and run amok in town, but there may well be more lurking below!

Benefits: You want to explore the caves under Torch to determine if there is indeed a lurking robot threat below town. You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls against robots and a +1 dodge bonus to AC against attacks made by robots.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait against robots.
+[[1]] Dodge bonus against attacks of robots.

friends in high places.name: Friends in High Places
friends in high places.description:
You have often seen lawbreakers go free when they demonstrate they have friends in high places, and so you put special effort into befriending powerful people yourself.

Benefits: Whenever you are in an area in which you have used the Diplomacy skill to gather information, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy and Intimidate checks. This bonus increases to +2 when dealing with government officials.

Suggested Characters

Absalom natives, Aspis Consortium agents, Taldans.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus in areas in which you have used the Diplomacy skill to gather information. This bonus increases to +[[2]] when dealing with government officials.
+[[1]] Trait bonus in areas in which you have used the Diplomacy skill to gather information. This bonus increases to +[[2]] when dealing with government officials.

magic is life (nethys).name: Magic is Life (Nethys)
magic is life (nethys).description:
Your faith in magic allows you to reflexively use the energy of any spell effect on you to save you from death.

Requirements: Nethys

Benefits: As long as you are under the effects of any spell, you gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against death effects. If you are reduced to negative hit points while you are under the effects of any spell, you automatically confirm stabilization checks to stop bleeding.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against death effects while  you are under the effects of any spell.
If you are reduced to negative hit points, while you are under the effects of any spell, you automatically confirm stabilization checks to stop bleeding.

diva in training (hell's rebels).name: Diva in Training (Hell's Rebels)
diva in training (hell's rebels).description:
The opera is perhaps the most important and prestigious form of entertainment offered in Kintargo, and the Kintargo Opera House is among the most famous of its kind. You’re hardly a superstar among the performers who’ve graced the stage there, but you have performed several times before in small parts. Just recently, you got your big break—you’d won a role in the infamous opera Huntress of Heroes, and had been studying for the role furiously before the onset of martial law dashed your plans to the dirt. Now that the Kintargo Opera House has been claimed by Barzillai Thrune, all shows have been canceled and your chance at success seems to have been lost. Yet you can’t lose hope. You still practice your skills, and some day, perhaps the Kintargo Opera House will open its doors again, at which point you intend to be center stage!

Benefits: Choose one type of Perform skill. You gain a +1 trait bonus in that specific Perform skill, and all Perform skills are class skills for you. You also increase the save DCs of all language-dependent spells and effects you create by 1.

Usage: Select one Perform skill when you take this trait.

Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the chosen skill.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] to the save DCs of all language-dependent spells and effects you create.

never stop shooting.name: Never Stop Shooting
never stop shooting.description:
Even when the chips are down, you keep on shooting.

Benefits: If your hit point total drops to 0 or lower but you are not dead, you may act as if disabled rather than dying (as if you possessed the Diehard feat). However, you can use your actions only to draw a firearm, reload a firearm, or attack with a firearm. If you have the Diehard feat, this trait also allows you to substitute your Wisdom score for your Constitution score for the purpose of determining the negative hit point total at which you die.

natural philosopher (gozreh).name: Natural Philosopher (Gozreh)
natural philosopher (gozreh).description:
You study the outdoors, and leave books for dusty libraries.

Requirements: Gozreh

Benefits: You can attempt Knowledge (geography) and Knowledge (nature) checks as if you were trained.

contextNotes.text: You can attempt checks as if you were trained.
You can attempt checks as if you were trained.

freed slave (bellflower network, halflin.name: Freed Slave (Bellflower Network, Halfling)
freed slave (bellflower network, halflin.description:
You grew up as a slave in Cheliax and know the ins and outs of Chelish nobility better than most.

Requirements: Bellflower Network, Halfling

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy and Knowledge (nobility) checks and Knowledge (nobility) is a class skill for you.

cliff strider (arcadia native).name: Cliff Strider (Arcadia native)
cliff strider (arcadia native).description:
You grew up along the Grinding Coast of Arcadia, scaling perilous cliffs and dealing with the dangerous creatures that live on these rocky surfaces.

Requirements: Arcadia native

Benefits: As a move action while climbing, you can climb 5 feet without provoking an attack of opportunity, even if you don’t have a climb speed.

contextNotes.text: As a move action you can climb 5 feet without provoking an attack of opportunity, even without a climb speed.

inspiring rush.name: Inspiring Rush
inspiring rush.description:
source: Knights of the Inner Sea pg. 0 (front inside cover)

Your charge inspires nearby companions to new heights of daring.

Benefits: Once per day when you make a charge attack, you gain a +1 trait bonus on both your attack and damage rolls, as do any allies who make a charge attack before the beginning of your next turn.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on both your attack and damage rolls, once per day when you make a charge attack. Any allies who make a charge attack before the beginning of your next turn gain the same benefit.

telekinetic dependence (vampire; nosfera.name: Telekinetic Dependence (Vampire; Nosferatu)
telekinetic dependence (vampire; nosfera.description:
Your body is so ancient you fear damaging it with exertion.

Requirements: Nosferatu Vampire Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus to your caster level when casting (or using as a spell-like ability) @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.7d6sv5ecvi7kho3m]{Fly}, @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.plou8h168bfn5hq6]{Levitate}, @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.p8lm7khq3ynyif21]{Mage Hand}, @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.3lfx1ccxo2hdqrf3]{Telekinesis}, and similar spells that move objects through sheer will.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus  to your caster level when casting (or using as a spell-like ability)  spells that move objects through sheer will.

enemy of delusion (andoletta).name: Enemy of Delusion (Andoletta)
enemy of delusion (andoletta).description:
You were once faced with a grave injustice committed by an ignorant or delusional person, and righteous indignation moved you to correct the mistaken party.

Requirements: Andoletta

Benefits: Once per day, when you confirm a critical hit against, successfully use a smite attack on, or use lay on hands on a creature that is affected by a charm or compulsion effect, you can invoke Andoletta to open the target’s eyes. If the effect originally allowed a saving throw to resist it, the target can attempt a new saving throw against the charm or compulsion; if she’s successful, the effect ends immediately.

fury of the red.name: Fury of the Red
fury of the red.description:
Somewhere in your family line, the mystic power of red dragons was infused into one of your relatives.

Benefits: If you ever take a class that requires you to select a draconic heritage, you must select a red dragon as your type. After successfully completing a charge attack, you gain a +2 trait bonus on Will saves for a full round.

contextNotes.text: After successfully completing a charge attack, you gain a +[[2]] Trait bonus on Will saves for a full round.

hellknight inquisitor.name: Hellknight Inquisitor
hellknight inquisitor.description: Benefits: If you are an inquisitor, you can select any inquisition associated with your preferred Hellknight order (see below), as long as you and your patron deity are of a lawful alignment. Additionally, you gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against spells and effects with the chaos descriptor.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against spells and effects with the chaos descriptor.

wisdom in the flesh (irori).name: Wisdom in the Flesh (Irori)
wisdom in the flesh (irori).description:
Your meditation on the nature of strength and speed allows you to focus your thoughts to achieve things your body might not be able to do on its own.

Requirements: Irori

Benefits: Select any Strength, Constitution, or Dexterity-based skill. You make checks with that skill using your Wisdom modifier instead of its normal ability score. That skill is always a class skill for you.

Usage: Select any one skill that is normally Strength, Constitution, or Dexterity-based when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect your choice. Update the Skills tab of the actor to reflect wisdom rather than the default ability for the chosen skill.

rostlander (kingmaker).name: Rostlander (Kingmaker)
rostlander (kingmaker).description:
You were raised in the south of Brevoy, a land of dense forests and rolling plains, of crystalline rivers and endless sapphire skies. You come from hearty stock and were raised with simple sensibilities of hard work winning well-deserved gains, the importance of charity and compassion, and the value of personal and familial honor. Yours is the country of the Aldori swordlords and the heroes who refused to bend before the armies of a violent conqueror. You care little for matters of politics and nobles or of deception and schemes. As you are thoroughly Brevic, the call for champions willing to expand your land's influence into the Stolen Lands has inflamed your sense of patriotism and honor, and so you have joined an expedition to quest southward.

Benefits: Your hardy nature grants you a +1 trait bonus on all Fortitude saves.

dragon friend (triaxian).name: Dragon Friend (Triaxian)
dragon friend (triaxian).description:
You know how dragons move and how they perceive body language.

Requirements: Triaxian

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus to your CMD against dragons, and whenever you encounter a particular dragon for the first time, you can make a Diplomacy check to improve its attitude toward you by a maximum of one step as a full-round action as long as you have not acted in a hostile manner toward it.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus   against dragons.

When you encounter a dragon for the first time, you can attempt to improve its attitude toward you, by a maximum of one step, as a full-round action as long as you have not acted in a hostile manner toward it.

exiled scholar (nex).name: Exiled Scholar (Nex)
exiled scholar (nex).description:
Since you were young, you’ve always been interested in the process of lichdom and what drives people to desire such a fate. Nexian officials marked you as a traitor and exiled you when they discovered your morbid fascination with the consummate undead, but you still benefit from your forbidden studies.

Requirements: Nex

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (arcana) and (religion) checks. In addition, choose one Knowledge skill; you can attempt untrained checks with this skill as long as the DC is lower than 15.

Usage: Select one Knowledge skill when you take this trait.

Update the target of the context note on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: You can attempt untrained checks with this skill as long as the DC is lower than 15.

boarded in mediogalti (serpent's skull).name: Boarded in Mediogalti (Serpent's Skull)
boarded in mediogalti (serpent's skull).description:
Your travels have brought you to the Jenivere through the dangerous port of Ilizmagorti, home to both pirates and assassins. Perhaps you're fleeing a price on your head, perhaps you've jumped ship from another vessel, or perhaps you're merely looking for a new start in a land where the dangers make themselves more obvious.

Benefits: Your familiarity with subtle slayings and toxins grants you a +2 trait bonus on all saves against poison. In addition, you can pick one type of poison listed below to be immune to, having undergone a long and painful process of building up an immunity to its effects: blue whinnis, giant wasp poison, medium spider venom, small centipede poison, or spider vine poison (a poison derived from a toxic plant native to the Mwangi Expanse).

Usage: Select one type of poison (blue whinnis, giant wasp poison, medium spider venom, small centipede poison, or spider vine poison) when you take this trait.

Update the text of the note on the changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against poison.  In addition, you are immune to <chosen poison>.

riftwarden orphan (wrath of the righteou.name: Riftwarden Orphan (Wrath of the Righteous)
riftwarden orphan (wrath of the righteou.description:
You bear a strange birthmark on your body—something you’ve learned is the Sign of the Seeker’s Spiral, a rune associated with the secret society known as the Riftwardens. You have researched this rune, and have learned that the mark sometimes appears on the children of Riftwardens who have been exposed to particularly strange planar energies. Unfortunately, you never knew your parents, for you were raised by a foster family in Kenabres. Your foster family has conf irmed that both of your parents were Riftwardens, and has further conf irmed that your parents went missing on a secret mission into the Worldwound less than a month after you were born. You’re not sure what happened to them, but you’re certain they’re dead—and your gut tells you that the one who murdered them yet lives! In any event, you’ve long felt magic in your blood, and casting spells comes easily to you.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on all concentration checks.

Associated Mythic Path

Archmage

Multiple Characters

You should be siblings with any other character that takes this trait, so that you share the same missing parents. Your parents could even be foster parents.

blood algorithm (android).name: Blood Algorithm (Android)
blood algorithm (android).description:
Your inhumanity extends to your very core.

Requirements: Android

Benefits: Pain receptors were omitted during your construction, so you gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against spells with the pain descriptor.

Additionally, once per day when you are healed by a spell that targets constructs (such as a make whole spell), you heal 1 extra hit point per level of the spell.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against spells with the pain descriptor.

alkenstar defender (mana wastes).name: Alkenstar Defender (Mana Wastes)
alkenstar defender (mana wastes).description:
You have lived in Alkenstar and served in its militia. You know just where to aim to do the most damage.

Requirements: Mana Wastes

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on all rolls to confirm critical hits with ranged weapons.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on all rolls to confirm critical hits with ranged weapons.

loner.name: Loner
loner.description:
Raised by pirates, bandits, or other troublemakers unwilling to stick out their necks for you, you are accustomed to operating on your own. The presence of allies can easily become a distraction to you, and you do your best to keep them out of your space.

Effects: You take a –1 penalty to AC and on attack rolls while adjacent to allies or when taking the aid another action.

contextNotes.text: -[[1]] penalty to AC while adjacent to allies or when taking the aid another action.
-[[1]] penalty on attack rolls while adjacent to allies or when taking the aid another action.

brute (half-orc).name: Brute (Half-Orc)
brute (half-orc).description:
You have worked for a crime lord, either as a low- level enforcer or as a guard, and are adept at frightening away people.    

Requirements: Half-Orc

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Intimidate checks and Intimidate is always a class skill for you.

hatred of the gods (rovagug).name: Hatred of the Gods (Rovagug)
hatred of the gods (rovagug).description:
Your god's own hatred of other deities is reflected in your ability to withstand the attacks of their followers.

Requirements: Rovagug

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on all saves against divine spells.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against divine spells.

technic tinkerer (numeria).name: Technic Tinkerer (Numeria)
technic tinkerer (numeria).description:
You managed to uncover some lesser secrets of the Technic League, and have mastered a minor bit of magic.

Requirements: Numeria

Benefits: Choose a 0-level spell. You may cast that spell once per day as a spell-like ability. This is cast at your highest caster level gained; if you have no caster level, it functions at CL 1st.

destructive blows (rovagug).name: Destructive Blows (Rovagug)
destructive blows (rovagug).description:
You call on your faith to destroy.

Requirements: Rovagug

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Strength checks made to break things, as well as on combat maneuver checks to sunder.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on checks made to break things.
+[[2]] Trait bonus on checks made to sunder.

noble savage (ulfen guard).name: Noble Savage (Ulfen Guard)
noble savage (ulfen guard).description:
You hail from the fringes of civilization or the bottommost strata of society, but something in your character makes that seem endearing.

Requirements: Ulfen Guard

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy and Perform checks when dealing with people of high social standing.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus when dealing with people of high social standing.
+[[2]] Trait bonus when dealing with people of high social standing.

fate's favored.name: Fate's Favored
fate's favored.description:
The fates watch over you.

Benefits: Whenever you are under the effect of a luck bonus of any kind, that bonus increases by 1.

contextNotes.text: Whenever you are under the effect of a luck bonus of any kind, that bonus increases by [[1]].

winter witchcraft survivor.name: Winter Witchcraft Survivor
winter witchcraft survivor.description:
You narrowly escaped death when a Winter Witch punished you for supporting the Heralds.

Benefits: You gain cold resistance 5 against the first cold damage you take each day, and gain a +2 bonus on saving throws against the fear effects of creatures with the cold subtype.

contextNotes.text: You are considered to have 5 cold resistance when taking damage for the first time each day.
+[[2]] Bonus against fear effects of creatures with the cold subtype.

backstabber (thamir gixx).name: Backstabber (Thamir Gixx)
backstabber (thamir gixx).description:
Pledging your faith to Norgorber's lethal shadow gives you the skill to make a brutal attack against an opponent who cannot fully fend you off.

Requirements: Thamir Gixx

Benefits: When you hit a foe you are flanking, you deal an additional 1 point of damage (this damage is added to your base damage, and is multiplied on a critical hit). This additional damage is a trait bonus.

contextNotes.text:
+[[1]] Trait bonus to damage when flanking. 
 (This damage is multiplied on a critical hit).

undead crusader (mummy's mask).name: Undead Crusader (Mummy's Mask)
undead crusader (mummy's mask).description:
You have dedicated your life to eradicating the scourge of the undead from Golarion. You have spent countless hours studying the different types of undead and have trained endlessly to learn the best ways to defeat them. If any undead creatures come out of the tombs of Wati’s necropolis, you’ll be ready for them!

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on damage rolls against undead creatures. In addition, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (religion) checks, and that skill is always a class skill for you.

Usage: add the damage bonus as a Conditional Modifier in all your attacks.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on damage rolls against undead creatures.

vengeful (ah).name: Vengeful (AH)
vengeful (ah).description:
You were brutally betrayed, and you have dedicated yourself to pursuing revenge.

Benefits: Choose a humanoid subtype that is not your own from the following list: dwarf, elf, gnome, halfling, or human. You gain a +2 trait bonus on Intimidate checks against creatures with that subtype.

Usage: Update context note according to choosen subtype

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against chosen humanoid subtype.

dirty fighter.name: Dirty Fighter
dirty fighter.description:
You wouldn't have lived to make it out of childhood without the aid of a sibling, friend, or companion on whom you could always count to distract your enemies long enough to do a little bit more damage than normal. That companion may be another PC or an NPC (who may even be recently departed from your side).

Benefits: When you hit a foe you are flanking, you deal an additional 1 point of damage (this damage is added to your base damage, and is multiplied on a critical hit). This additional damage is a trait bonus.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus damage when flanking (it is multiplied on a critical hit).

dreadful forethought (ustalav).name: Dreadful Forethought (Ustalav)
dreadful forethought (ustalav).description:
As a minor Ustalavic noble, you have friends linked to Karcau’s fiendish cults. Your observations give you a keen insight into the blasphemies of the idle rich.

Requirements: Ustalav

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (nobility) and Knowledge (planes) checks.

weathered emissary.name: Weathered Emissary
weathered emissary.description:
Your travels in the wildlands gave you insight into how to survive their dangers and communicate with their inhabitants.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on all Linguistics and Survival checks, and Linguistics is always a class skill for you.

wicked leader.name: Wicked Leader
wicked leader.description: Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Charisma checks against evil creatures. If you select the @UUID[Compendium.pf1.feats.Item.R3a1OV18RwDJftvq]{Leadership} feat or the Vile Leadership feat at any point when you meet the prerequisites for that feat, you can recruit a cohort who is up to 1 level lower than you (instead of the normal requirement that your cohort must be 2 or more levels lower than you are) as long as your cohort is evil.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus vs evil creatures

redeemer.name: Redeemer
redeemer.description:
You’ve always held the strong belief that morality is everyone’s choice, and that those who act in wicked ways have simply never been shown how their actions truly affect others. If they could be shown their errors, then they would accept a more positive course of action—and you have just enough patience to see this through.

Benefits: When acting as a sponsor for an evil creature seeking redemption, your patience and kindness grant the creature a +3 bonus on its save rather than a +1 bonus.

memory mastery (gillman).name: Memory Mastery (Gillman)
memory mastery (gillman).description:
You can suppress your own memories to help tell more convincing lies.

Requirements: Gillman

Benefits: Once per day as an immediate action, you can cause yourself to forget a single event or fact for a period of 10 minutes. Enemies reading your mind cannot learn the information. Until the memory returns, you gain a +10 trait bonus on Bluff checks to convince others that you do not know the information.

contextNotes.text: Once per day as an immediate action, you can cause yourself to forget a single event or fact for a period of 10 minutes. Enemies reading your mind cannot learn the information. Until the memory returns, you gain a +[[10]] Trait bonus on Bluff checks to convince others that you do not know the information.

senatorial hopeful (war for the crown).name: Senatorial Hopeful (War for the Crown)
senatorial hopeful (war for the crown).description:
While you belong to a noble family, hailing from the outlying prefectures means you lack the pull and wealth needed to rub shoulders with the stars of Oppara’s social scene. Your life has been quiet, isolated from the thrum of fashion and pop culture, but you see this as a source of insight and aim to help people by someday joining the senate. It’ll be a long journey, but you’ve already begun learning which hands to shake and how to listen carefully when others tell you what they want.

Benefits: You gain a +1 bonus on Diplomacy and Knowledge (local) checks, and both of these skills are always class skills for you. Once each week you can call on family connections to obtain a single piece of equipment or consumable magic item (such as a potion or scroll) worth up to your character level × 25 gp. Delivery of the goods you request takes 1d4 days. At 10th level, the potential value of a magical item increases to your character level × 50 gp, and your requests can also include spellcasting services and wands.

harrow chosen (human; varisian).name: Harrow Chosen (Human; Varisian)
harrow chosen (human; varisian).description:
All your life you’ve been drawn to the mysteries surrounding your family’s harrow deck.

Requirements: Varisian Human Heritage

Benefits: You begin play with your family’s heirloom harrow deck. If you use this specific harrow deck as an additional focus component when you cast a divination spell, your caster level is treated as being 2 levels higher.

Additionally, twice per week, you can spend 10 minutes consulting this harrow deck on a particular action to gain the effects of the spell @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.d1wxnax51cecplm4]{Augury} as a spellcaster of your character level. No one other than you gains these benefits from this harrow deck.

If your harrow deck is destroyed, it cannot be replaced. However, by returning to your family, paying 300 gp on materials, and spending 1 week immersed in the songs and stories of your people, you can create a new harrow deck that grants half the benefits of the lost heirloom.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] to caster level when you cast a divination spell,  If you use your heirloom harrow deck as an additional focus component.

patient calm.name: Patient Calm
patient calm.description:
As a child, you struggled to suppress a sharp temper, but the care and patient respect of a trusted community leader rubbed off on you. As you grew older, you developed a remarkable ability to keep calm and collected when performing a task you are familiar with.

Benefits: Choose one Craft or Profession skill. Whenever you take 10 with that skill, determine the result as if you had rolled a 12 instead of a 10.

Suggested Characters

Asmodeans, Drumans, Irorans.

Usage: This trait is set to the Craft skill by default. If you choose the Profession skill, change the target in the changes tab from Craft to Profession.

contextNotes.text: If you take 10, you gain an additional +[[2]] bonus.

birthmark.name: Birthmark
birthmark.description:
You were born with a strange birthmark that looks very similar to the holy symbol of the god you chose to worship later in life.

Benefits: This birthmark can serve you as a divine focus for casting spells, and you gain a +2 trait bonus on all saving throws against charm and compulsion effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against charm and compulsion effects.

cruel master (okeno).name: Cruel Master (Okeno)
cruel master (okeno).description:
You’re a master of dominating slaves through body language and inflection, and this ability carries over into other lines of work as well.

Requirements: Okeno

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Intimidate checks, and it becomes a class skill for you.

balanced education (anuli).name: Balanced Education (Anuli)
balanced education (anuli).description:
Your upbringing focused on strengthening mind and body in equal measure.

Requirements: Anuli

Benefits: Once per day, you can draw on this training to apply a physical ability modifier to a skill check instead of its usual mental ability modifier, or apply a mental ability modifier to a skill check instead of its usual physical ability modifier. You can only exchange ability modifiers between the following pairings: Strength with Intelligence, Dexterity with Wisdom, and Constitution with Charisma.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you can  apply an alternate ability modifier to a skill check instead of its usual ability modifier,  exchanging between the following pairings: Strength with Intelligence, Dexterity with Wisdom, and Constitution with Charisma. ([[@resources.balancedEducationAnuli.value]] remaining uses)

vigilante witch hunter (reign of winter).name: Vigilante Witch Hunter (Reign of Winter)
vigilante witch hunter (reign of winter).description:
You don’t trust witches. They deal with otherworldly beings, consort with beasts, and brew vile poisons in their cauldrons. As a child, perhaps you barely escaped some horrid fate at the hands of an evil witch, or maybe a loved one was stolen from you by a witch’s charms. Perhaps you wanted to be a witch yourself, but the local witch refused to take you on as an apprentice. Whatever the reason, you now hate witches, and have dedicated your life to ferreting them out and exposing their wickedness for all to see. You know that the North is full of winter witches, and should you ever find yourself there, you’ll relish bringing your justice to them as well.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks, and Sense Motive is a class skill for you. In addition, you begin the campaign with 1d4 @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-wondrous.Item.Z3o9VAPaIvzT4nzf]{Hex Nails}.

ratfolk avenger (ratfolk).name: Ratfolk Avenger (Ratfolk)
ratfolk avenger (ratfolk).description:
You protect your own.

Requirements: Ratfolk

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on weapon damage rolls against opponents you have witnessed dealing damage to one of your allies.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus  on weapon damage rolls against opponents you have witnessed dealing damage to one of your allies.

eye for quality (abadar).name: Eye for Quality (Abadar)
eye for quality (abadar).description:
Your deity has granted you an uncanny ability to find the true worth of items.

Requirements: Abadar

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Appraise checks, you don’t suffer the effects of failing such checks by 5 or more, and Appraise is a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: You don’t suffer the effects of failing checks by 5 or more.

underbridge dweller (varisia; magnimar).name: Underbridge Dweller (Varisia; Magnimar)
underbridge dweller (varisia; magnimar).description:
You were raised in the Shadow district of Magnimar beneath the Irespan, a veiled place the light of the sun touches for only 3 hours per day.

Requirements: Magnimar in Varisia

Benefits: Your well-founded suspicions grant you a +2 trait bonus on Perception checks in dim light, and Perception is always a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus in dim light.

commercial savvy (katapesh).name: Commercial Savvy (Katapesh)
commercial savvy (katapesh).description:
You excel at maximizing mercantile contacts and convincing merchants to dig just a bit deeper into their pockets for you.

Requirements: Katapesh

Benefits: Whenever you buy from or sell to a merchant, you treat a settlement’s Base Value and Purchase Limit (Pathfinder RPG GameMastery Guide 204) as 10% higher.

sworn enemy.name: Sworn Enemy
sworn enemy.description:
You were raised with defiance, fear, or hatred of a particular sort of foe.

Benefits: To make you ready to resist such foes, you were trained for long hours in the proper ways to defeat them. Choose a foe from the ranger’s list of favored enemies. You gain a +1 trait bonus on attacks of opportunity against the chosen foe.

Suggested Characters

Dwarves, Isgeri, Mendevian crusaders.

Usage: Add conditional modifier to weapons / attacks against selected enemy.

strip the veils (sivanah).name: Strip the Veils (Sivanah)
strip the veils (sivanah).description:
You are unusually perceptive when dealing with others.

Requirements: Sivanah

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks, and Sense Motive becomes a class skill for you.

mana wastes medic (mana wastes; alchemis.name: Mana Wastes Medic (Mana Wastes; Alchemist)
mana wastes medic (mana wastes; alchemis.description:
After witnessing a terrible disaster result from an attempt at magical healing in the Mana Wastes, you are careful and methodical when crafting your own healing tinctures.

Requirements: Alchemist healers who hail from the Mana Wastes or who have lived there for at least 1 year

Benefits: The amount of hit points restored by your Heal checks to treat deadly wounds and your extracts of the healing subschool is increased by 1.

contextNotes.text: The amount of hit points restored by your Heal checks to treat deadly wounds is increased by 1.
The amount of hit points restored by your extracts of the healing subschool is increased by 1.

justiciar (rahadoum).name: Justiciar (Rahadoum)
justiciar (rahadoum).description:
You are experienced in interpreting and applying legal doctrine, notably the Laws of Man, independent of any divine mandates.

Requirements: Rahadoum

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy and Knowledge (local) checks to settle disputes or arbitrate laws. Once per day if you succeed at an Intimidate check to demoralize a foe who has repudiated or ignored a pronounced judgment you’ve made, you may choose to cause the creature to be sickened instead of shaken. The duration of the effect remains the same.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to settle disputes or arbitrate laws.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to settle disputes or arbitrate laws.

Once per day if you succeed at an Intimidate check to demoralize a foe who has repudiated or ignored a pronounced judgment you’ve made, you may choose to cause the creature to be sickened instead of shaken. ([[@resources.justiciarRahadoum.value]] remaining uses)

librarian (dark archive).name: Librarian (Dark Archive)
librarian (dark archive).description:
You are trained to manage and maintain books, and you find it easy to procure information from tomes.

Requirements: Dark Archive

Benefits: You gain a +1 bonus on Linguistics or Profession (librarian) checks, and one of these skills (your choice) becomes a class skill for you. Once per day when you would gain a bonus on a skill check from reading a book, you can increase that bonus by 1.

Usage: Select Linguistics or Profession (librarian) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

(If you have selected Profession (librarian), update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the Profession (librarian) skill.)

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, when you would gain a bonus on a skill check from reading a book, you can increase that bonus by 1. ([[@resources.librarianDarkArchive.value]] remaining uses)

master of pentacles (dark archive).name: Master of Pentacles (Dark Archive)
master of pentacles (dark archive).description:
Your many years spent studying the art of summoning have given you a unique knowledge of this subtle and complicated discipline.

Requirements: Dark Archive

Benefits: Once per day, when casting a spell of the Conjuration school, treat your caster level as two higher when determining the spell’s duration.

contextNotes.text: Once per day +[[2]] to Caster level when determining a  Conjuration school spell’s duration.  ([[@resources.masterOfPentaclesDarkArchive.value]] remaining uses)

lasting ferociousness (half-orc).name: Lasting Ferociousness (Half-Orc)
lasting ferociousness (half-orc).description:
Requirements: Half-Orc

Benefits: Increase the number of rounds you can act as disabled from your orc ferocity racial trait to 2 rounds.

Usage: You must have the orc ferocity racial trait for this trait to provide any benefit.

Increase the duration of this feature to 2 rounds.

headstrong.name: Headstrong
headstrong.description:
You feel compelled to correct every action and argument that contradicts your worldview.

Effects: Whenever you witness an action or hear an argument that contradicts your alignment, you must attempt to stop or correct that action or argument. If you either don’t try to stop it or fail in your attempt to stop it (as adjudicated by the GM), you are shaken for 1 hour.

freed slave (andoran).name: Freed Slave (Andoran)
freed slave (andoran).description:
You were either born or sold into slavery, but were freed by Andoren abolitionists. Your strong will helped you persevere in captivity, and gave you strength to start again from nothing in your new life in Andoran.

Requirements: Andoran

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Will saves.

pristine reputation (shizuru).name: Pristine Reputation (Shizuru)
pristine reputation (shizuru).description:
You come from an especially esteemed family, religious order, school, or other group that worships Shizuru, and draw upon that tradition to maintain your identity.

Requirements: Shizuru

Benefits: The first time each day that you fail a saving throw against a charm or compulsion effect, you can reroll that saving throw. If you do so, you gain a +2 trait bonus on this roll and use this result as the actual result for that saving throw.

contextNotes.text: The first time each day that you fail a saving throw against a charm or compulsion effect, you can reroll that saving throw. If you do so, you gain a +2 trait bonus on this roll and use this result as the actual result for that saving throw.

contract master (asmodeus).name: Contract Master (Asmodeus)
contract master (asmodeus).description:
Your facility with contracts makes you good at understanding dense text and obfuscating your intent.

Requirements: Asmodeus

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Linguistics checks, and it becomes a class skill for you.

mammoth master (realm of the mammoth lor.name: Mammoth Master (Realm of the Mammoth Lords)
mammoth master (realm of the mammoth lor.description:
You have experience trapping and training large creatures such as mammoths, mastodons, woolly rhinoceroses, and other megafauna.

Requirements: Realm of the Mammoth Lords

Benefits: You gain a +4 trait bonus on Handle Animal checks related to these creatures.

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Trait bonus on checks related to megafauna.

statuesque (oread).name: Statuesque (Oread)
statuesque (oread).description:
You inherited the statuesque physical features of your shaitan parent.

Requirements: Oread

Benefits: Disguise becomes a class skill for you, and you gain a +2 trait bonus on Disguise checks to disguise yourself as a statue made of a specific type of stone, such as marble, sandstone, or alabaster. Furthermore, you take none of the penalties normally associated with disguising yourself as a different type of creature. You must choose the type of stone you resemble when you take this trait; once chosen, it cannot be changed.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus  to disguise yourself as a statue made of your chosen specific type of stone.

vengeful (qc).name: Vengeful (QC)
vengeful (qc).description:
During your childhood, you suffered occasional or frequent abuse from those too powerful for you to resist. Now you thrill at the chance to get back at those who wronged you.

Benefits: Whenever you strike the last creature that damaged you in the past 24 hours, you gain a +1 trait bonus on damage rolls against that creature.

Suggested Characters

Calistrians, Galtan refugees, Gyronna worshipers

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on damage rolls against the last creature that damaged you in the past 24 hours.

mighty protector (trudd).name: Mighty Protector (Trudd)
mighty protector (trudd).description:
When you were too young to fight, you witnessed a great tragedy befall an innocent and you swore to Trudd you’d become strong enough to prevent something like that from happening again.

Requirements: Trudd

Benefits: Once per day when you hit a foe with a melee attack, you can attempt an aid another action as a free action to increase an adjacent ally’s AC against that foe. If you succeed, you grant the ally a +4 bonus to AC instead of a +2 bonus for that round.

contextNotes.text:
Once per day when you hit a foe with a melee attack, you can attempt an aid another action as a free action to increase an adjacent ally’s AC against that foe.
 If you succeed, you grant the ally a +4 bonus to AC instead of a +2 bonus for that round.

balanced offensive (the concordance).name: Balanced Offensive (The Concordance)
balanced offensive (the concordance).description:
You can call forth a surge of elemental energy to strike against your foes.

Requirements: The Concordance

Benefits: Once per day, you can target a distant foe with an elemental attack. This functions as one of the following cleric domain powers, chosen whenever you activate this ability: @UUID[Compendium.pf1.class-abilities.Item.L5jrgNDA43dMH1wm]{acid dart}, @UUID[Compendium.pf1.class-abilities.Item.V57hEfKt1MroRI91]{fire bolt}, @UUID[Compendium.pf1.class-abilities.Item.ructcpEg6rejcpYQ]{icicle}, @UUID[Compendium.pf1.class-abilities.Item.o8rxNsQ7P7Yjlvvu]{lightning arc}, or @UUID[Compendium.pf1.class-abilities.Item.Kgcm3ye8CYGipsg3]{storm burst}. Your effective cleric level for this ability is equal to your character level. You can use this ability one additional time per day at 5th level and every 5 levels thereafter.

Usage

Select one (1) of five (5) available options when you take this trait.

Add this class ability to your actor and edit it as follows:

in the Limited Uses formula, replace existing text with: 1 + floor(@attributes.hd.total / 5)

in the Damage Formula, replace existing text with: 1d6 + (floor(@attributes.hd.total / 2))

ice walker (human; kellid).name: Ice Walker (Human; Kellid)
ice walker (human; kellid).description:
