You spent years roaming the frozen tundra of the frigid north, and have adapted to its numbing effects.

Requirements: Kellid Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against attacks that deal cold damage. Furthermore, you may ignore the Acrobatics penalty for ice and can move across ice at normal speed.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against attacks that deal cold damage.
You may ignore the penalty for ice and can move across ice at normal speed.

sheltered.name: Sheltered
sheltered.description:
Brought up in courtly environments and knowing little of violence, you have trouble getting accustomed to it.

Effects: Whenever you have less than half your maximum hit points, you are @UUID[Compendium.pf1.pf1e-rules.JournalEntry.NSqfXaj4MevUR2uJ.JournalEntryPage.XxLUswkCuXnXmA5T]{Shaken}.

bastard (kingmaker).name: Bastard (Kingmaker)
bastard (kingmaker).description:
One of your parents was a member of one of the great families of Brevoy, perhaps even of the line of Rogarvia itself. Yet you have no substantive proof of your nobility, and you've learned that claiming nobility without evidence makes you as good as a liar. While you might own a piece of jewelry, a scrap of once-rich fabric, or an aged confession of love, none of this directly supports your claim. Thus, you've lived your life in the shadow of nobility, knowing that you deserve the comforts and esteem of the elite, even though the contempt of fate brings you nothing but their scorn. Whether a recent attempt to prove your heritage has brought down the wrath of a noble family's henchmen or you merely seek to prove the worth of the blood in your veins, you've joined an expedition into the Stolen Lands, hoping to make a name all your own.

Requirements: Human

Benefits: You take a –1 penalty on all Charisma-based skill checks made when dealing with members of Brevic nobility but gain a +1 trait bonus on Will saves as a result of your stubbornness and individuality. (The penalty aspect of this trait is removed if you ever manage to establish yourself as a true noble.)

contextNotes.text: -[[1]] penalty on all Charisma-based skill checks made when dealing with members of Brevic nobility. (The penalty aspect of this trait is removed if you ever manage to establish yourself as a true noble.)

illusion obsession (gnome).name: Illusion Obsession (Gnome)
illusion obsession (gnome).description:
After realizing the great potential of illusions—particularly figments—for use in pranks, you became obsessed with studying their properties and better methods to cast them.

Requirements: Gnome

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus to your caster level when casting illusions in the figment subschool.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus  to Caster level when casting illusions in the figment subschool.

shift the blame (tiefling; spitespawn).name: Shift the Blame (Tiefling; Spitespawn)
shift the blame (tiefling; spitespawn).description:
You have learned many tricks to keep yourself from facing the consequences of your misdeeds.

Requirements: Spitespawn Tiefling Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Bluff and Diplomacy checks made to convince someone that another person is actually at fault for your actions.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to convince someone that another person is actually at fault for your actions.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to convince someone that another person is actually at fault for your actions.

resourceful alchemy (thuvia).name: Resourceful Alchemy (Thuvia)
resourceful alchemy (thuvia).description:
Alchemy is a point of national pride in Thuvia, home of the famous sun orchid elixir. But resources are often hard to come by in the blasted desert landscape, so you have found creative ways to create many of the tools you need to survive.

Requirements: Thuvia

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Craft (alchemy) skill checks. This bonus increases to +3 when you are using Craft (alchemy) to craft an alchemical item.

Usage: Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the Craft (alchemy) skill.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect the Craft (alchemy) skill.

Update the target of the context note on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect the Craft (alchemy) skill.

contextNotes.text: +[[3]] Trait bonus when you are using Craft (alchemy) to craft an alchemical item. (this trait provides +2 Trait bonus to Craft (alchemy) at all times)

outer dragon blood.name: Outer Dragon Blood
outer dragon blood.description:
The blood of outer dragons courses through your veins, and you’ve inherited a portion of their magic.

Benefits: You gain Draconic as a bonus language. In addition, if you are a sorcerer with the draconic bloodline, you can select one of the outer dragon types for your bloodline powers; this affects your resistances and the type of energy damage dealt by a number of your abilities.

Dragon Type

Energy Type

Breath Shape

Lunar

Cold

60-foot line

Solar

Fire

60-foot line

Time

Electricity

30-foot cone

Void

Cold

30-foot cone

Vortex

Fire

30-foot cone

loreseeker (lissala).name: Loreseeker (Lissala)
loreseeker (lissala).description:
Requirements: Lissala

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Spellcraft checks made to identify magical items.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to identify magical items.

warded against witchery (reign of winter.name: Warded against Witchery (Reign of Winter)
warded against witchery (reign of winter.description:
Sometime in your youth, you encountered a location, object, or being steeped in the power of evil witchcraft. Whether you were the victim of this force, were a conduit for it, or merely witnessed its effects, the event changed your life. You have tried to put the strange incident behind you and forget it, but nebulous premonitions of danger and eerie feelings of deja vu have dogged your steps ever since. For some inexplicable reason, you feel drawn to the lands of the North, though you fear another encounter with the evil witchcraft that touched you once before. Whether through your purity, the blessing of goodly spirits, an innate determination, or an intuitive and inexplicable familiarity with the ways of black magic, you have acquired a resilience against the power of the dark arts.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against the spells, spell-like abilities, and supernatural abilities of evil arcane spellcasters, and a +1 trait bonus on Spellcraft checks to identify spells cast by evil arcane spellcasters.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against the spells, spell-like abilities, and supernatural abilities of evil arcane spellcasters.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to identify spells cast by evil arcane spellcasters.

tiger's claw (skinwalker; weretiger-kin).name: Tiger's Claw (Skinwalker; Weretiger-Kin)
tiger's claw (skinwalker; weretiger-kin).description:
Your feline agility helps you inflict a devastating blow at the end of a charge.

Requirements: Weretiger-Kin Skinwalker Heritage

Benefits: Whenever you use the charge action, you gain a +1 trait bonus on your weapon damage roll.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on your weapon damage roll whenever you use the charge action.

power-hungry.name: Power-Hungry
power-hungry.description:
You’re addicted to power.

Effects: You take a –2 penalty on Will saving throws against charm and compulsion effects if the creature creating the effect promises wealth or power.

contextNotes.text: -[[2]] penalty  vs charm and compulsion effects  if promised wealth or power by the source of the effect.

malleable magic.name: Malleable Magic
malleable magic.description: Benefits: Once per day as a swift action, you can lose a single 1st-, 2nd- or 3rd-level magus spell to regain 1 arcane pool point. You can’t exceed the maximum number of arcane pool points in your arcane pool by using this trait.

strong arm, supple wrist.name: Strong Arm, Supple Wrist
strong arm, supple wrist.description:
You have been trained in the javelin and the spear, and know how to make the best use of your momentum.

Benefits: Whenever you move at least 10 feet before making an attack with a thrown weapon, you add 10 feet to the range increment of the weapon thrown.

Restrictions

You can only gain this benefit once per round.

contextNotes.text: Whenever you move at least 10 feet before making an attack with a thrown weapon, you add 10 feet to the range increment of the weapon thrown. You can only gain this benefit once per round.

murky allegiance (fetchling).name: Murky Allegiance (Fetchling)
murky allegiance (fetchling).description:
You came of age in a place where fetchlings serve dark forces.

Requirements: Fetchling

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Bluff checks to send secret messages and to imply you serve a powerful sinister conspiracy, evil authority figure, or shadow government. You also gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks when interacting with such entities.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to send secret messages and to imply you serve a powerful sinister conspiracy, evil authority figure, or shadow government.
+[[2]] Trait bonus  when interacting with a powerful sinister conspiracy, evil authority figure, or shadow government.

shoreline skirmisher (skinwalker; weresh.name: Shoreline Skirmisher (Skinwalker; Wereshark-Kin)
shoreline skirmisher (skinwalker; weresh.description:
You were raised without many toys or possessions, but you made do exploring every detail of the nearby seashore, and know how to spot potential tools or obstacles.

Requirements: Wereshark-Kin Skwinwalker Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on combat maneuver checks as long as you or your opponent is on sand or in water at least 6 inches deep.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on combat maneuver checks as long as you or your opponent is on sand or in water at least 6 inches deep.
+[[2]] Trait bonus on combat maneuver checks as long as you or your opponent is on sand or in water at least 6 inches deep.

cyphermage backer (riddleport).name: Cyphermage Backer (Riddleport)
cyphermage backer (riddleport).description:
You’ve sold relics to a Cyphermage in the past, and she’s agreed to assist you with future findings.

Requirements: Riddleport

Benefits: When in Riddleport, you can use your contact to gain information about artifacts and history. Treat this ability as a single Knowledge (arcana) or Knowledge (history) check with a skill bonus of +15. You may make this check once per day that you spend in Riddleport. You can make a new check every day. Each time you do so, you can choose whether to use Knowledge (arcana) or Knowledge (history).

contextNotes.text: Once per day that you spend in Riddleport you can make a single  check with a skill bonus of +15. ([[@resources.cyphermageBackerRiddleport.value]] remaining uses)
Once per day that you spend in Riddleport you can make a single  check with a skill bonus of +15. ([[@resources.cyphermageBackerRiddleport.value]] remaining uses)

signature moves.name: Signature Moves
signature moves.description:
You’re known for some unique item that has become your trademark.

Benefits: Your starting equipment includes a single masterwork item worth less than 900 gp, and you gain a +1 trait bonus on Bluff and Intimidate checks while wielding this item in one or both hands.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus while wielding signature masterwork item in starting equipment in one or both hands.
+[[1]] Trait bonus while wielding signature masterwork item in starting equipment in one or both hands.

undaunted.name: Undaunted
undaunted.description:
Following the path of freedom has resulted in you being threatened by ruffians and bullies throughout your childhood, and it now takes a lot to rattle you.

Benefits: You increase the DC to demoralize you with an Intimidate check by 2.

monster challenger.name: Monster Challenger
monster challenger.description:
You have made it your mission to bring down a fearsome beast.

Benefits: Choose two of the following: aberrations, animals, dragons, fey, magical beasts, oozes, and vermin. You can move at full speed without penalty while using Survival to follow tracks left by your chosen creature types.

Usage: In Changes tab, replace [creatureType] with your chosen creatures types.

contextNotes.text: You can move at full speed without penalty while following tracks left by [creatureType] and [creatureType].

erastil's speaker (erastil).name: Erastil's Speaker (Erastil)
erastil's speaker (erastil).description:
You understand the importance of keeping the peace in your community and you have learned how to speak to the faithful in ways that they understand.

Requirements: Erastil

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks and Diplomacy is always a class skill for you.

regional recluse - turtleback ferry (var.name: Regional Recluse - Turtleback Ferry (Varisia)
regional recluse - turtleback ferry (var.description:
Your home community prides itself on remaining independent from Varisia’s city-states.

Requirements: Varisia

Benefits: Choose one of Varisia’s unaffiliated settlements from this list below. You gain a +1 trait bonus on Survival checks and one of the following benefits.

Turtleback Ferry

Gain a +1 bonus to damage on all attacks against creatures of the giant type.


Special Note

This is one of five versions of this trait. Other versions can be found here:

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.QezjzIjvbfIxTLPD]{Regional Recluse - Arsmeril or Crying Leaf (Varisia)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.SUxxga9Juql4kY3J]{Regional Recluse - Ilsurian (Varisia)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.qDxSXompaz69Ceqj]{Regional Recluse - Sanos Forest or Whistledown (Varisia)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.BDByEeeSK08bn8gp]{Regional Recluse - Urglin (Varisia)}

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] bonus to damage on all attacks against creatures of the giant type.

militia (amanandar).name: Militia (Amanandar)
militia (amanandar).description:
As part of Amanandar's militia, you have trained extensively with groups.

Requirements: Amanandar

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attacks made while flanking an opponent.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attacks made while flanking an opponent.

deathkeeper (dwarf; pahmet).name: Deathkeeper (Dwarf; Pahmet)
deathkeeper (dwarf; pahmet).description:
You have lived for a time deep inside the necropolises of the pharaohs. Your exposure to undead creatures and their necromantic energies has strengthened your ability to resist the powers of the unliving.

Requirements: Pahmet Dwarf Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against the supernatural abilities of undead creatures.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against the supernatural abilities of undead creatures.

godclaw disciple (lawful).name: Godclaw Disciple (Lawful)
godclaw disciple (lawful).description:
You worship the amalgamation of lawful deities known as the Godclaw.

Requirements: Lawful alignment

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Knowledge (religion) checks related to lawful deities, their clergy, their mythology, and their tenets. Additionally, Knowledge (religion) is a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on checks related to lawful deities, their clergy, their mythology, and their tenets.

patron's boon (rahadoum).name: Patron's Boon (Rahadoum)
patron's boon (rahadoum).description:
In your youth, a Rahadoumi witch granted you a boon promoting your continued health and long life.

Requirements: Must hail from Rahadoum or have lived there for at least 1 year

Benefits: The DCs of any Heal checks to stop bleed damage you are suffering are reduced by 4.

contextNotes.text: The DCs of any Heal checks to stop bleed damage you are suffering are reduced by 4.

old before your time (dhampir; ancient-b.name: Old Before Your Time (Dhampir; Ancient-Born)
old before your time (dhampir; ancient-b.description:
Your fragile flesh shows signs of years not yet lived.

Requirements: Ancient-Born Dhampir Heritage

Benefits: Any creature trying to discern your true age must make a Perception or Sense Motive check opposed by your Bluff check; you gain a +5 trait bonus on this check. You gain a +2 trait bonus on Disguise checks if your disguise makes you look at least one age category older, and ignore the check penalty for disguising yourself as a different age category while doing so.

contextNotes.text: +[[5]] Trait bonus on opposed checks versus any creature trying to discern your true age using Perception or Sense Motive.
+[[2]] Trait bonus  if your disguise makes you look at least one age category older.  You also ignore the check penalty for disguising yourself as a different age category while doing so.

master of the sudden strike (human; tian.name: Master of the Sudden Strike (Human; Tian)
master of the sudden strike (human; tian.description:
You have mastered the deadly fighting arts of your people.

Requirements: Tian Human Heritage

Benefits: When you wield a masterwork version of any sword if you act before your opponent during the surprise round, you gain a +2 trait bonus on weapon damage rolls for the surprise round only. This additional damage is precision damage.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on weapon damage rolls, for the surprise round only, when you wield a masterwork version of any sword  and if you act before your opponent during the surprise round. This additional damage is precision damage.

feline instinct (skinwalker; weretiger-k.name: Feline Instinct (Skinwalker; Weretiger-Kin)
feline instinct (skinwalker; weretiger-k.description:
When your weretiger heritage first manifested, you spent a period of time living off what you could catch, developing a sharp instinct to act when your foes were most vulnerable.

Requirements: Weretiger-Kin Skinwalker Heritage

Benefits: In any combat in which you act during the surprise round, you gain a +3 trait bonus on your initiative check.

contextNotes.text: In any combat in which you act during the surprise round, you gain a +[[3]] Trait bonus on your initiative check.

angradd's valor (angradd).name: Angradd's Valor (Angradd)
angradd's valor (angradd).description:
Requirements: Angradd

Benefits: You hope to earn a place at Angradd’s side after death and are inspired to charge with all your might into any righteous battle. You gain a +1 trait bonus on all melee attack rolls when you charge.

contextNotes.text: +[[1] Trait bonus on melee attacks when charging.

kalistocratic prophecy.name: Kalistocratic Prophecy
kalistocratic prophecy.description:
You were raised under the Prophecies of Kalistrade, and were inspired to seize upon an untapped business opportunity.

Benefits: Up to once per week, when you work to advance this opportunity with at least 1 day of effort, you can attempt an appropriate Profession check to earn money or gain capital using the downtime rules (Ultimate Campaign 76) with a +2 trait bonus. In the event of a failed check, your opportunity is discovered by rivals and you cannot employ this trait for 1 month while you look for a new opportunity.

contextNotes.text: With at least 1 day of effort, you can attempt an appropriate Profession check to earn money or gain capital using the downtime rules (Ultimate Campaign 76) with a +[[2]] trait bonus. In the event of a failed check, your opportunity is discovered by rivals and you cannot employ this trait for 1 month while you look for a new opportunity.

destined pioneer (human; taldan).name: Destined Pioneer (Human; Taldan)
destined pioneer (human; taldan).description:
Your forebears served in an Army of Exploration, and you retain that expansionist spirit.

Requirements: Taldan Human Heritage

Benefits: Select a terrain from the ranger’s list of @UUID[Compendium.pf1.class-abilities.Item.U2YBSx1s8cT8iDq1]{favored terrain}s. Once selected, this choice cannot be changed. Once per day while in the selected terrain, you may move across nonmagical difficult terrain as though it were not difficult terrain until the end of your turn.

avarice.name: Avarice
avarice.description:
Deep, compulsive greed gnaws at you.

Effects: Whenever monetary treasure is divided, you must end up with a greater share of that treasure than your companions or you’re wracked with feelings of jealousy and ill will. When treasure is divided, if you do not end up with at least 10% more treasure than any other individual companion does, you have a hard time being helpful to your allies. You become irritable, and can’t take the aid another action for the next week.

lastwall cavalry rider (lastwall).name: Lastwall Cavalry Rider (Lastwall)
lastwall cavalry rider (lastwall).description:
From the moment you were old enough to walk, you learned how to ride the mighty warhorses of Lastwall.

Requirements: Lastwall

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Ride checks, and Ride is always a class skill for you.

charming smile, cunning soul.name: Charming Smile, Cunning Soul
charming smile, cunning soul.description:
You know how to guilefully weave words, sway hearts and minds, verbally destroy rivals, and pierce cunning lies.

Exemplar Type: Social Exemplar

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Bluff, Diplomacy, Intimidate, and Sense Motive checks. This bonus increases by 1 for every two other social traits you have.

Exemplar Traits: A character can have only one exemplar trait, and selecting an exemplar trait takes the place of two regular traits. Each exemplar trait is tied to a trait category, such as combat traits or regional traits; a character with an exemplar trait is no longer restricted to a single trait of that category and can select any number of such traits when gaining further traits. In addition, a character with an exemplar trait can select the Additional Traits feat any number of times and use the feat to gain an exemplar trait of a valid trait category for which they qualify.

Usage: Increase bonus in changes tab by one for every two social traits you have.

shadow stabber (tiefling).name: Shadow Stabber (Tiefling)
shadow stabber (tiefling).description:
An instinct for dishonorable conduct serves you well when fighting opponents who are blind, oblivious, or blundering around in the dark.

Requirements: Tiefling

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on melee weapon damage rolls made against foes that cannot see you.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on melee weapon damage rolls made against foes that cannot see you.

bellis axe master (bellis).name: Bellis Axe Master (Bellis)
bellis axe master (bellis).description:
No one knows how to swing an axe better than a Bellisian!

Requirements: Bellis [in Andoran]

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on all rolls made to confirm critical hits with axes (battle axe, hand axe, or greataxe).

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus  to confirm critical hits with axes.

footsteps of aganhei (crown of the world.name: Footsteps of Aganhei (Crown of the World)
footsteps of aganhei (crown of the world.description:
You are one of the brave travelers who have traversed the Path of Aganhei, a lucrative trade route from Tian Xia to Avistan that crosses the frigid Crown of the World.

Requirements: Crown of the World

Benefits: These voyages have left you hardier, granting you a +1 trait bonus on Fortitude saving throws to resist the effects of cold weather. Survival is a class skill for you, and you gain Hallit, Hon-La, Minkaian, Skald, or Tien as a bonus language.

Usage

Select one (1) of Hallit, Hon-La, Minkaian, Skald, or Tien when you take this trait.

Update the Granted Proficiencies>Languages section of the details tab of this trait to reflect this choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to resist the effects of cold weather.

reverent wielder (scarab sages).name: Reverent Wielder (Scarab Sages)
reverent wielder (scarab sages).description:
Some of the most powerful weapons are priceless and ancient, and you are vigilant in protecting your equipment as much as you protect yourself.

Requirements: Scarab Sages

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus to CMD against disarm, steal, and sunder combat maneuvers, and your equipment gains a +1 trait bonus on all saving throws.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus vs disarm, steal, and sunder.
+[[1]] Trait bonus for your equipment  only.

wrecking wrath (rovagug).name: Wrecking Wrath (Rovagug)
wrecking wrath (rovagug).description:
Your ferocity is devastating, sometimes even to your own weapons.

Requirements: Rovagug

Benefits: Once per day, after successful hitting a foe with a melee weapon, you can add your Strength modifier to the damage roll a second time (your Strength Modifier is not doubled if you are using a two-handed weapon). Doing so has a 25% chance of giving your weapon the broken condition.

unnatural revenge.name: Unnatural Revenge
unnatural revenge.description: Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on all Intimidate checks against creatures of the animal, fey, or plant type, and Intimidate is always a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against creatures of animal, fey, or plant types.

contagious mettle.name: Contagious Mettle
contagious mettle.description:
Requirements: Code of Valor

Benefits: If you follow this code, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Will saves against fear. In addition, any allies adjacent to you receive a +1 morale bonus on Will saves against fear.

Usage: to take this trait you must follow the Valor Knightly code of conduct.

If a knight violates their code of conduct, they are disgraced and must perform penance to atone for their transgressions. until the penance is completed, disgraced knights no longer receive benefits from their knightly traits.

Valor: Knights who follow the code of valor believe that nothing is more important than courage in battle. The strength of knights is determined by their willingness to stand in the face of overwhelming odds. Without valor, knighthood is merely a title.

Their vows are as follows.

  • I will accept any challenge to combat if given regardless of my opponent.

  • If I know combat to be the best course of action, I shall not let an enemy discourage me from it with a threat of overwhelming force.

  • Once battle is a forgone conclusion, I will be among the first of my allies to charge and engage our foes.

  • I shall not willingly succumb to fear. If retreat is a necessity, I will be among the last of my allies to leave the field of battle.

  • I will not leave any of my allies on the field, nor will I abandon them while they are in immediate danger.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on saves against fear. In addition, any allies adjacent to you receive a +1 morale bonus on Will saves against fear.

freedom fighter (liberty's edge).name: Freedom Fighter (Liberty's Edge)
freedom fighter (liberty's edge).description:
Your family has long waged war against tyranny, and you learned a great deal about guerilla warfare in your youth.

Requirements: Liberty's Edge

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Stealth checks and a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls made during the surprise round.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus during the surprise round.

greenskin stalker (isger, river kingdoms.name: Greenskin Stalker (Isger, River Kingdoms, Varisia)
greenskin stalker (isger, river kingdoms.description:
You’re a member of the Greenskin Stalkers. Due to your affiliation with this brotherhood of goblin slayers, you’ve learned to think and act like a goblin, as disturbing as that sounds.

Requirements: Isger, River Kingdoms, or Varisia

Benefits: You receive a +1 trait bonus on Bluff, Knowledge, Perception, Sense Motive, and Survival checks against or concerning goblinoids. You can also attempt Knowledge checks concerning goblinoids untrained.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on checks against or concerning goblinoids.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks against or concerning goblinoids.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks against or concerning goblinoids.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks against or concerning goblinoids.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks against or concerning goblinoids and such checks may be untrained.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks against or concerning goblinoids and such checks may be untrained.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks against or concerning goblinoids and such checks may be untrained.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks against or concerning goblinoids and such checks may be untrained.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks against or concerning goblinoids and such checks may be untrained.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks against or concerning goblinoids and such checks may be untrained.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks against or concerning goblinoids and such checks may be untrained.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks against or concerning goblinoids and such checks may be untrained.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks against or concerning goblinoids and such checks may be untrained.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks against or concerning goblinoids and such checks may be untrained.

triaxian tradition (triaxus).name: Triaxian Tradition (Triaxus)
triaxian tradition (triaxus).description:
Your experience with one of the long Triaxian seasons has taught you much about the dangers of a long summer or winter.

Requirements: Triaxus

Benefits: Choose disease or cold. You gain a +2 trait bonus on saves against spells and spell-like abilities with the chosen descriptor. If you choose disease, you gain the bonus on Fortitude saves against nonmagical diseases as well.

Usage: Select disease or cold when you take this trait.

Update the text of the first context note on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

If you chose disease, update the target of the second context note to target fortitude saves

if you chose cold, delete the second context note.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against spells and spell-like abilities with the <choose from Disease or Cold when taking this trait> descriptor.

excellent penmanship.name: Excellent Penmanship
excellent penmanship.description:
You are a master of calligraphy, an art once highly valued in Lung Wa and still held in high esteem in Lingshen.

Benefits: The DC to detect forgeries you create increases by 2. If you attempt to persuade someone via a written message, you gain a +2 bonus on any Bluff, Diplomacy, or Intimidate check attempted for that communication. Linguistics is always a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: The DC to detect forgeries you create increases by 2.
+[[2]] Bonus if you attempt to persuade someone via a written message.
+[[2]] Bonus if you attempt to persuade someone via a written message.
+[[2]] Bonus if you attempt to persuade someone via a written message.

mechanical expertise (alkenstar).name: Mechanical Expertise (Alkenstar)
mechanical expertise (alkenstar).description:
Growing up bordering the Mana Wastes and living within the Grand Duchy of Alkenstar has made you familiar with tools and practical objects. You rely on mechanics to solve problems and are familiar with basic engineering concepts.

Requirements: Alkenstar native

Benefits: Select one of the following skills: Craft (any), Disable Device, or Knowledge (engineering). You gain a +1 trait bonus on checks with that skill, and it is always a class skill for you.

Usage: Select one of skills permitted [Craft (any one), Disable Device, or Knowledge (engineering)] when you take this trait.

Update the target of the change on the changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

mind over matter (lantern lodge).name: Mind Over Matter (Lantern Lodge)
mind over matter (lantern lodge).description:
Whether from the pages of a dusty scroll or at the feet of a master, you have learned to set your mind to adapt to the physical realm.

Requirements: Lantern Lodge

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Will saving throws.

gruff watcher (human; ulfen).name: Gruff Watcher (Human; Ulfen)
gruff watcher (human; ulfen).description:
Having grown up among headstrong sailors and bloodthirsty raiders, you learned to keep quiet and pay attention.

Requirements: Ulfen Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on initiative checks and Perception checks.

focused disciple.name: Focused Disciple
focused disciple.description:
When weaker wills falter, you keep a clear mind.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against charm and compulsion effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against charm and compulsion effects.

exchange agent (shattered star).name: Exchange Agent (Shattered Star)
exchange agent (shattered star).description:
Although you're a relative newcomer to Magnimar, you're not some naive stranger. You've been sent by the Pathfinder Society lodge in your home country to learn more about Varisia by experiencing the frontier firsthand. After arriving, you reported to the Magnimar lodge and placed your expertise at the venture-captain's disposal.

Benefits: To represent your experience in distant lands, choose a country other than Varisia as your homeland and gain one of the languages spoken in that country as a bonus language. Additionally, choose one of the following skills: Handle Animal, Knowledge (geography), Linguistics, or Survival. You gain a +1 trait bonus on checks made with this skill and it is always a class skill for you.

Usage

  1. Select a country other than Varisia and a language spoken therein when you take this trait.

  2. Update the Granted Proficiencies>Languages section of the details tab of this trait to reflect this choice.

  3. Select one (1) of: Handle Animal, Knowledge (geography), Linguistics, or Survival when you take this trait.

  4. Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

  5. Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

mountain guide (five kings mountains).name: Mountain Guide (Five Kings Mountains)
mountain guide (five kings mountains).description:
You are skilled at getting along in the mountains, enabling you to find hidden trails and secret entrances into the caverns beneath mountains.

Requirements: Five Kings Mountains

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (geography) and Survival checks when in mountainous areas, and Knowledge (geography) is always a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus when in mountainous areas.

spark of creation.name: Spark of Creation
spark of creation.description:
You have always had a knack for making useful things, and your talent as an artisan was evident even at an early age.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Craft checks, and the cost of creating magic items is reduced by 5%.

contextNotes.text: The cost of creating magic items is reduced by 5%.

rough and ready.name: Rough and Ready
rough and ready.description:
Your intense familiarity with the tools of your trade allows you to use them in combat as if they were actual weapons and makes them more effective for that purpose than they would normally be.

Benefits: When you use a tool of your trade (requiring at least 1 rank in the appropriate Craft or Profession skill) as a weapon, you do not take the improvised weapon penalty and instead receive a +1 trait bonus on your attack. This trait is commonly used with shovels, picks, blacksmith hammers, and other sturdy tools—lutes and brooms make terribly fragile weapons.

get the cargo through (serpent's skull).name: Get the Cargo Through (Serpent's Skull)
get the cargo through (serpent's skull).description:
Sea trade is never safe, even on the highly trafficked waters of the Inner Sea, but the way to Eleder is more perilous still. The ever-present hurricane known as the Eye of Abendego blows hundreds of ships each year off course, into jagged rocks, or directly to the sea f loor. Pirates operating out of the Shackles Isles wait on the other side to take cargo from ships lucky enough to traverse or bypass the storm. Your job is to ensure a precious cargo aboard the Jenivere arrives safely in Eleder—what form this cargo takes and the nature of your relationship to it are up to you and your GM.

Benefits: Your character’s history as a merchant or mercenary has earned her a respectable living, and you begin the campaign with an additional 300 gp in starting wealth.

Characters with this trait likely boarded in Magnimar, Corentyn, Ilizmagorti, Port Peril, or Bloodcove.

sodden child (sodden lands).name: Sodden Child (Sodden Lands)
sodden child (sodden lands).description:
You spent most of your life moving through murky bogs and have grown acclimated to the terrain.

Requirements: Sodden Lands

Benefits: You move normally through shallow bogs (as long as the terrain hasn’t been magically manipulated) but are still affected by deep bogs as normal.

extended toil (droskar).name: Extended Toil (Droskar)
extended toil (droskar).description:
Your faith teaches you to push magical servants to the limit.

Requirements: Droskar

Benefits: When you cast a spell that creates a loyal servant to fulfill a specific or mindless task (such as unseen servant, mount, or spiritual weapon, but not summon monster spells), you may choose to increase its duration by 50%. This does not stack with the Extend Spell feat. The effect weakens the spell, however; dispel checks against spells you cast in this manner gain a +4 bonus.

contextNotes.text: When you cast a spell that creates a loyal servant to fulfill a specific or mindless task (such as unseen servant, mount, or spiritual weapon, but not summon monster spells), you may choose to increase its duration by 50%. This does not stack with the Extend Spell feat. The effect weakens the spell, however; dispel checks against spells you cast in this manner gain a +[[4]] bonus.

debt to a witch (irrisen).name: Debt to a Witch (Irrisen)
debt to a witch (irrisen).description:
Your life was spared in youth by the magic of a White Witch healer who was using your survival to perpetuate her agenda. Her magic was painful, but it made your body stronger.

Requirements: Must hail from Irrisen or have lived there for at least 1 year

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against spells with the cold descriptor and against the dangers of cold weather.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus  against spells with the cold descriptor and against the dangers of cold weather.

monkey goblin (goblin; mediogalti).name: Monkey Goblin (Goblin; Mediogalti)
monkey goblin (goblin; mediogalti).description:
You are adept at arboreal activity, and are more at home in the branches of a tree than you are on the ground.

Requirements: Goblin; Mediogalti

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Acrobatics and Climb checks while climbing trees.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus while climbing trees.
+[[2]] Trait bonus while climbing trees.

deck fighter (besmara).name: Deck Fighter (Besmara)
deck fighter (besmara).description:
You are accustomed to compensating for the pitch and yaw of ocean-going ships.

Requirements: Besmara

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attacks of opportunity when fighting aboard a ship.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attacks of opportunity when fighting aboard a ship.

cynical.name: Cynical
cynical.description:
You have seen many heroes in your time, but you see only their capacity for failure and the ways they fall short of being truly good. This practice in looking past facades has proven a useful skill.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against illusions and charm effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against illusions and charm effects.

liar's tongue (asmodeus).name: Liar's Tongue (Asmodeus)
liar's tongue (asmodeus).description:
Thanks to your friendly mien and sly tongue, your lies are often believed.

Requirements: Asmodeus

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Bluff checks, and Bluff is a class skill for you.

eyes of the sun (human; garundi).name: Eyes of the Sun (Human; Garundi)
eyes of the sun (human; garundi).description:
You are inured to the bright glare of the hot desert sun, and its dazzling rays pose little danger to you.

Requirements: Garundi Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against effects that would cause you to become dazzled or blinded, and once per day when you would be dazzled or blinded by an effect, you can reduce the duration of that effect by half (minimum 1 round). You cannot use this ability if you are already dazzled or blinded or if you have the light sensitivity or light blindness special quality.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against effects that would cause you to become dazzled or blinded. Once per day when you would be dazzled or blinded by an effect, you can reduce the duration of that effect by half (minimum 1 round). You cannot use this ability if you are already dazzled or blinded or if you have the light sensitivity or light blindness special quality.

pact servant.name: Pact Servant
pact servant.description:
The faith of Holomog focuses on finding the good in unusual places and appreciating the nuances of virtue in the world.

Benefits: You may treat Asmodeus as if he were a lawful neutral deity for the purposes of determining your own alignment as a cleric, inquisitor, or other divine spellcaster. You may not select the evil domain unless your own alignment also contains an evil aspect.

slave smuggler (halfling).name: Slave Smuggler (Halfling)
slave smuggler (halfling).description:
You know how to get contraband across borders.

Requirements: Halfling

Benefits: When hiding a Medium or smaller creature from an opponent actively searching you or your cargo, both you and the creature to be smuggled can attempt Stealth checks and select the higher of the two results for your smuggled ally to use while hiding.

contextNotes.text: When hiding a Medium or smaller creature from an opponent actively searching you or your cargo, both you and the creature to be smuggled can attempt Stealth checks and select the higher of the two results for your smuggled ally to use while hiding.

mutabi-qi explorer (shanguang).name: Mutabi-qi Explorer (Shanguang)
mutabi-qi explorer (shanguang).description:
You belong to one of the nomadic Mutabi-qi tribes that wander the wastes of Shaguang, and you are adept at navigating the seemingly endless desert wastes.

Requirements: Shanguang

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (geography) checks and Survival checks made in the desert.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus in the desert.

covenant of abaddon (any four horsemen).name: Covenant of Abaddon (any Four Horsemen)
covenant of abaddon (any four horsemen).description:
Your soul is pledged to one of the Horsemen.

Requirements: Any of the Four Horsemen

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Fortitude saving throws against level-draining effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against level-draining effects.

natural born leader (hell's rebels).name: Natural Born Leader (Hell's Rebels)
natural born leader (hell's rebels).description:
Whenever you found yourself involved in a group effort in the past, be it working with siblings to handle a family emergency, conspiring with friends to orchestrate a prank, or throwing in with coworkers to take care of an unanticipated complication at work, you tended to end up in a position of leadership. It might be unclear to you why this is the case, or you might deliberately seek out such positions, knowing you can organize any group to be something greater than the sum of its parts, but your knack for managing groups has always pushed you to the front of any operation you’ve found yourself a part of.

Benefits: You excel in the role of manager. You treat your Charisma score as if it were 14 (or 2 points higher than its actual score if your actual Charisma is already 14 or higher) for the purposes of determining how many teams you can manage in the rebellion, and for the purposes of determining the bonus you add to your managed teams’ actions. In addition, you gain a +1 trait bonus to your Leadership score if you take the Leadership feat.

planetar's visions (aasimar; angelkin).name: Planetar's Visions (Aasimar; Angelkin)
planetar's visions (aasimar; angelkin).description:
Ever since you were a child, you've experienced vivid dreams of cutting down hordes of shrieking, twisted demons.

Requirements: Angelkin Aasimar Heritage

Benefits: Whenever you succeed at a critical hit with a melee weapon against an evil outsider, you ignore an amount of the outsider's damage reduction (if any) equal to the critical multiplier of your weapon (this cannot reduce a creature's damage reduction to below 0).

contextNotes.text: Whenever you succeed at a critical hit with a melee weapon against an evil outsider, you ignore an amount of the outsider's damage reduction (if any) equal to the critical multiplier of your weapon (this cannot reduce a creature's damage reduction to below 0).

well-prepared (angradd).name: Well-Prepared (Angradd)
well-prepared (angradd).description:
Angradd teaches that a good offense can be the best defense.

Requirements: Angradd

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls made as part of a readied action, and a +1 trait bonus on caster level checks when casting a spell as a readied action.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus when part of a readied action.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on caster level checks when casting a spell as a readied action.

magical knack.name: Magical Knack
magical knack.description:
You were raised, either wholly or in part, by a magical creature, either after it found you abandoned in the woods or because your parents often left you in the care of a magical minion. This constant exposure to magic has made its mysteries easy for you to understand, even when you turn your mind to other devotions and tasks.

Benefits: Pick a class when you gain this trait—your caster level in that class gains a +2 trait bonus as long as this bonus doesn't increase your caster level higher than your current Hit Dice.

Usage: Change the class in the drop down menu in the details tab on this trait to the class you pick.

defender of the society (fighter, pathfi.name: Defender of the Society (Fighter, Pathfinder Society)
defender of the society (fighter, pathfi.description:
Your time spent fighting and studying the greatest warriors of the Society has taught you new defensive skills while wearing armor.

Requirements: Fighter, Pathfinder Society

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus to Armor Class when wearing medium or heavy armor.

corpse hunter (pharasma).name: Corpse Hunter (Pharasma)
corpse hunter (pharasma).description:
You have dedicated yourself to the destruction of undead.

Requirements: Pharasma

Benefits: You receive a +1 trait bonus on all attacks made against undead.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on all attacks made against undead.

elemental apprentice.name: Elemental Apprentice
elemental apprentice.description:
You spent your childhood in a region where the use of elemental magic is commonplace or the environment is heavily infused with elemental energies. Your time near raw elemental energies has inured you to taking damage from them.

Benefits: At character creation, select acid, cold, electricity, or fire (once chosen, this damage type can’t be changed).

Once per day as a free action, when you hit a creature with a melee weapon, you can convert an amount of damage dealt by your attack equal to your character level into the selected energy type.

You cannot convert this damage if any damage from the attack would normally deal a different type of energy damage (such as that of a flaming weapon).

contextNotes.text:
Once per day as a free action, when you hit a creature with a melee weapon, you can convert an amount of damage dealt by your attack equal to your character level into the selected energy type.

You cannot convert this damage if any damage from the attack would normally deal a different type of energy damage (such as that of a flaming weapon).

arodenite historian.name: Arodenite Historian
arodenite historian.description:
You believe there is much useful information to glean from the mark Aroden left on history, and learn all you can about his exploits and teachings. This has given you significant insights on how different kinds of information are interconnected.

Benefits: If you fail a Knowledge (arcana, history, or religion) check, you can reroll the check 1 round later using your bonus for one of the other two skills.

contextNotes.text: If you fail, you can reroll the check 1 round later using your bonus for Knowledge (history, or religion).
If you fail, you can reroll the check 1 round later using your bonus for Knowledge (arcana, or religion).
If you fail, you can reroll the check 1 round later using your bonus for Knowledge (history, or religion).

know the land (green faith).name: Know the Land (Green Faith)
know the land (green faith).description:
Your have a deep familiarity with the plants and animals of a variety of environments.

Requirements: Green Faith

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (nature) and Survival checks, and one of these skills becomes a class skill for you.

Usage: Select Knowledge (nature) or Survival when you take this trait. Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

structural knowledge (findeladlara).name: Structural Knowledge (Findeladlara)
structural knowledge (findeladlara).description:
You are blessed with an insight into architecture and artifice.

Requirements: Findeladlara

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (engineering) checks, and Knowledge (engineering) is a class skill for you.

slippery (bellflower network).name: Slippery (Bellflower Network)
slippery (bellflower network).description:
You have escaped from so many dangerous situations in your life that you've gotten quite good at not getting caught.

Requirements: Bellflower Network

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Stealth checks and Stealth is a class skill for you.

ancient historian (scarab sages).name: Ancient Historian (Scarab Sages)
ancient historian (scarab sages).description:
You are well acquainted with the lore of fallen empires.

Requirements: Scarab Sages

Benefits: Choose either Knowledge (history) or Linguistics. That skill becomes a class skill for you, and you begin play able to speak and read one of the following languages: Ancient Osiriani, Azlanti, Cyclops, Jistkan, Tekritanin, or Thassilonian.

Usage: Choose either Knowledge (history) or Linguistics when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

Select one (1) of Ancient Osiriani, Azlanti, Cyclops, Jistkan, Tekritanin, or Thassilonian when you take this trait.

Update the Granted Proficiencies>Languages section of the details tab of this trait to reflect this choice.

friend of the family (jade regent).name: Friend of the Family (Jade Regent)
friend of the family (jade regent).description:
Your family has been close friends with the Mvashtis for as long as you've been alive. Old Niska Mvashti's recent death wasn't all that unexpected - she must have been over a hundred years old, as far as you know,but it was a sobering moment nonetheless. What no one else in your family knows, though, is that in the weeks before her death, Niska must have known that her days were numbered, because she called you to her house one day and made you make a promise: If her daughter Koya ever went on a long trip, you would go with her to ensure her safety. Although the chances of Koya going on a long trip seem remote (she must be at least 60 years old, after all), you've made a promise to Niska to accompany her daughter if such an event comes to pass. Ever since you made this promise, you've felt a strange sense of destiny looming in your future,you might even think that Niska is now watching over your shoulder, just as she asked you to watch over her daughter. As a result of this eerie sense of being watched over, you've been able to react to dangerous situations more quickly than ever.

Benefits: As long as Koya remains alive, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Perception checks, and Perception becomes a class skill for you. In addition, you gain a +1 trait bonus on all attack rolls against foes that threaten Koya.

NPC Choices Koya

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on all attack rolls against foes that threaten Koya.
+[[1]] Trait bonus as long as Koya remains alive.

snowstride (darklands).name: Snowstride (Darklands)
snowstride (darklands).description:
Requirements: Darklands, usually under cold mountains

Benefits: The wide stance and sure footing you developed while traipsing over ice-covered rock and frozen earth provides you with a +1 trait bonus on Acrobatics checks made on slippery surfaces and enables you to trip or bull rush opponents two size categories larger than you instead of just one.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on slippery surfaces.
You may trip or bull rush opponents two size categories larger than you.

azlanti inheritor (gillman).name: Azlanti Inheritor (Gillman)
azlanti inheritor (gillman).description:
You have prominent Azlanti features, which you can use to inspire confidence in your allies.

Requirements

Gillman

Benefits

Whenever you use the aid another action to aid an adjacent ally, increase the bonus you grant your ally by 1. This is a trait bonus.

contextNotes.text: Whenever you use the aid another action to aid an adjacent ally, increase the bonus you grant your ally by 1. This is a trait bonus.
Whenever you use the aid another action to aid an adjacent ally, increase the bonus you grant your ally by 1. This is a trait bonus.

upstanding (the exchange).name: Upstanding (The Exchange)
upstanding (the exchange).description:
Your preferred means of doing business involves giving your clients a fair deal and winning their continued business and good will.

Requirements: The Exchange

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy and Sense Motive checks, and one of these skills (your choice) becomes a class skill for you.

Usage: Choose either Diplomacy or Sense Motive when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

a sure thing (silver crusade).name: A Sure Thing (Silver Crusade)
a sure thing (silver crusade).description:
Requirements: Silver Crusade

Benefits: Once per day, you gain a +2 bonus on a single attack roll against an evil-aligned creature. If the creature is not evil-aligned, this ability is wasted with no benefit.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you gain a +[[2]] bonus on a single attack roll against an evil-aligned creature. If the creature is not evil-aligned, this ability is wasted with no benefit.  ([[@resources.aSureThingSilverCrusade.value]] remaining uses)

righteous indignation.name: Righteous Indignation
righteous indignation.description:
You have difficulty controlling your temper after living in inhuman conditions in Cheliax, Geb, Irrisen, Katapesh, or Qadira.

Effects: Whenever a foe provokes an attack of opportunity from you, you must take it unless you succeed at a Will save against a DC equal to 10 + your level. You take a -1 penalty on Will saving throws against spells with the emotion descriptor that do not also have the fear descriptor.

contextNotes.text: -[[1]] penalty against spells with the emotion descriptor that do not also have the fear descriptor.

kin bond.name: Kin Bond
kin bond.description:
The bond between you and a close sibling is nearly mystical.

Benefits: Choose a single sibling with whom to share this bond. Once per day when you fail a saving throw while your sibling is within 30 feet, you may reroll that saving throw using your sibling’s saving throw modifier. If you and the sibling are twins or otherwise part of a multiple birth, you gain a +2 trait bonus on the rerolled saving throw. Your sibling must be willing to grant you the reroll. If you fail the saving throw, you and your sibling are dazed for 1 round.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, if you fail a saving throw with your sibling within 30 feet, reroll using their modifier; twins receive a +2 bonus. Sibling's consent is required, and if the reroll fails, both are dazed for 1 round.

adopted.name: Adopted
adopted.description:
You were adopted and raised by someone not of your actual race, and raised in a society not your own.

Benefits: You picked up a race trait from your adoptive parents and society, and may immediately select a race trait from your adoptive parents' race.

social slaver (cheliax).name: Social Slaver (Cheliax)
social slaver (cheliax).description:
Your upbringing has accustomed you to commanding those you deem inferior.

Requirements: Cheliax

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Intimidate checks against creatures one or more size categories smaller than yourself. The DC of compulsion spells cast by you against smaller creatures increases by 1.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against creatures one or more size categories smaller than yourself.
+[[1]] to DC of compulsion spells cast by you against creatures one or more size categories smaller than yourself.

coven casting.name: Coven Casting
coven casting.description:
You have belonged to a coven or magical cabal and know how to coordinate your spellcasting with others.

Benefits: When within 30 feet of an allied spellcaster, you both gain a +2 trait bonus on concentration checks.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] trait bonus on concentration checks when within an allied spellcaster.

dusk market bribery.name: Dusk Market Bribery
dusk market bribery.description:
You learned how to bribe by watching Dusk Market merchants interact with Westcrown’s dottari guards.

Benefits: Reduce the amount of gold you must spend to bribe a guard or official or compensate a conjured creature by 5%.

inexorable authority.name: Inexorable Authority
inexorable authority.description:
When you speak with the full weight of law, your conviction lends you a terrible presence.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Intimidate checks when enforcing or pronouncing a lawful judgment that you’re legitimately deputed to enforce or pronounce.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus when enforcing or pronouncing a lawful judgment that you’re legitimately deputed to enforce or pronounce.

outlander - exile (any campaign).name: Outlander - Exile (Any Campaign)
outlander - exile (any campaign).description:
You are not from town; you’ve recently come from somewhere else and are hoping to make your fortune here. Pick one of the following reasons to be a newcomer to the area.

Benefits: Pick one of the following reasons to be a newcomer to the area:

Exile

For whatever reason, you were forced to flee your homeland. Chance or fate has brought you to town, and it’s here that your money ran out, leaving you stranded in this small town. You are also being pursued by enemies from your homeland, and that has made you paranoid and quick to react to danger.

You gain a +2 trait bonus on Initiative checks.

Special Note

This is one of three versions of this trait. Other versions can be found here:

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.KVrNf7eTgJbtA8gl]{Outlander - Lore Seeker (Any Campaign)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.0mlO02qRdJH6656V]{Outlander - Missionary (Any Campaign)}

vigilant battler.name: Vigilant Battler
vigilant battler.description:
You are hard to fool and harder to trick with a feint.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on all Sense Motive checks. This trait bonus increases to +2 when used to counter a feint in combat.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] to counter a feint in combat.

undergrowth specialist.name: Undergrowth Specialist
undergrowth specialist.description:
You’ve trained yourself to move in and out of undergrowth with ease.

Benefits: The DCs of Acrobatics checks don’t increase for you when you’re moving through light undergrowth, and the DCs of Acrobatics checks you attempt while you’re moving through heavy undergrowth increase only by 2.

contextNotes.text: The DCs of Acrobatics checks don’t increase for you when you’re moving through light undergrowth, and the DCs of Acrobatics checks you attempt while you’re moving through heavy undergrowth increase only by 2.

teaching mistake (grand lodge).name: Teaching Mistake (Grand Lodge)
teaching mistake (grand lodge).description:
You know the consequences of failure and strive never to make the same mistake twice.

Requirements: Grand Lodge

Benefits: Once per scenario, when you roll a natural 1 on any saving throw, you gain a +1 trait bonus on your next saving throw, which must be used before the end of the scenario.

contextNotes.text: Once per scenario, when you roll a natural 1 on any saving throw, you gain a +[[1]] Trait bonus on your next saving throw, which must be used before the end of the scenario.

good slip (hell's vengeance; halfling).name: Good Slip (Hell's Vengeance; Halfling)
good slip (hell's vengeance; halfling).description:
Halflings are a common sight in Cheliax and make up the majority of that nation’s slave population. Known derisively as “slips,” halflings are almost universally treated with scorn and contempt, while paradoxically valued as the most precious slaves. You are a Chelish halfling, but unlike most of your race, you have embraced your role in Chelish society, and used it to your own advantage. People constantly overlook you or discount you as an inconsequential slip, but you know the truth—halflings are just as vital to the empire’s health as its nobles, priests, and warriors. You may have been born a free halfling who has never known the chains of slavery, or a slave who was later freed after a period of loyal servitude. Or perhaps you are still a slave, happy (or at least content) in your enslavement, and hoping for some eventual recognition of your contribution, however small, to Cheliax’s great and orderly society.

Requirements: Halfling

Benefits: You are skilled at prevaricating and dissembling, both to deflect blame away from yourself and to mislead others as to your true abilities and role. You gain a +1 trait bonus on Bluff checks, and Bluff is a class skill for you. In addition, your tacit support of Cheliax’s social structure, and the clear knowledge of your own place in society, strengthens your resolve. Once per day, when you are adjacent to a human ally of Chelish descent, you can reroll a Will saving throw before success or failure is known. You must take the results of the second roll, even if it is worse.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, when you are adjacent to a human ally of Chelish descent, you can reroll a Will saving throw before success or failure is known. You must take the results of the second roll, even if it is worse.

ancestral weapon (numeria).name: Ancestral Weapon (Numeria)
ancestral weapon (numeria).description:
You have inherited a sacred tribal weapon wielded by your forebears since the days before the Rain of Stars, and you were trained in its use from a young age.

Requirements: Numeria

Benefits: Select either cold iron or silver. You begin play with a masterwork melee weapon made of the material of your choice. You must be proficient with this weapon, and its combined cost cannot exceed 500 gp. You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls with weapons made of the selected material.

Usage

Select cold iron or silver when you take this trait.

Update the text of the note on the changes tab of this trait to reflect your chosen material.

For each weapon of the chosen material your actor wields, modify as follows:

  • Edit the weapon.
  • On the weapon's details tab, edit the attack.
  • On the attack's Action tab, in the Attack section in the Attack Roll Bonus field add "1[Ancestral Weapon Material]"

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attack rolls with weapons made of the <chosen material>.

zest for battle (dwarf).name: Zest for Battle (Dwarf)
zest for battle (dwarf).description:
Your greatest joy is being in the thick of battle, and smiting your enemies for a righteous or even dastardly cause.

Requirements: Dwarf

Benefits: Whenever you have a morale bonus to weapon attack rolls, you also receive a +1 trait bonus on weapon damage rolls.

contextNotes.text: Whenever you have a morale bonus to weapon attack rolls, you also receive a +[[1]] Trait bonus on weapon damage rolls.

rebel leader (shokuro).name: Rebel Leader (Shokuro)
rebel leader (shokuro).description:
A close relative was an important player in the rebellions that led to the independence of Shokuro, and passed on her passion to you.

Requirements: Shokuro

Benefits: If you take the Leadership feat, you receive a +1 trait bonus to your Leadership score.

shoanti steed (human; shoanti).name: Shoanti Steed (Human; Shoanti)
shoanti steed (human; shoanti).description:
Your nomadic culture gives you a profound knowledge of and respect for the horses that roam your homelands.

Requirements: Shoanti Human Heritage

Benefits: You begin play with a combat trained light @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-goods-services.Item.NFafBpbIMEXHQyt0]{horse}. While riding this horse bareback, you are treated as though you were riding it with a riding @UUID[Compendium.pf1.items.Item.dn6yBOrH8vUpXahk]{saddle} (placing any actual saddle upon the horse negates this benefit). The horse can fend for itself outside of settlements in all but the most hostile environments. If whistled for outside of a community, the horse typically arrives within [[1d6]] minutes. If your horse is slain, you may receive a new horse by returning to your clan and performing a vigil for its spirit (a rite that costs 100 gp in rare herbs and incense).

bruising intellect.name: Bruising Intellect
bruising intellect.description:
Your sharp intellect and rapier-like wit bruise egos.

Benefits: Intimidate is always a class skill for you, and you may use your Intelligence modifier when making Intimidate checks instead of your Charisma modifier.

Usage: Change ability modifier of intimidate skill to Intelligence.

meticulous concoction.name: Meticulous Concoction
meticulous concoction.description:
Your meticulous carefulness makes your bombs and extracts more potent.

Benefits: Once per day, you can either add a +2 trait bonus to the save DC for one bomb you throw or extend the duration of one extract you imbibe by 2 rounds. Extracts with an instantaneous duration are not affected by this trait.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, gain a +[[2]] trait bonus to the save DC of one bomb you throw or extend the duration of one extract you imbibe by 2 rounds.

charau-ka slayer (mwangi expanse).name: Charau-Ka Slayer (Mwangi Expanse)
charau-ka slayer (mwangi expanse).description:
As a native of the Mwangi Jungle, you’ve had frequent encounters with the Charau-Ka and learned how to fight against these coldly calculating demon worshipers.

Requirements: Mwangi Expanse

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus to AC against attacks made with thrown weapons.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to AC against attacks made with thrown weapons.

peg leg (skull and shackles).name: Peg Leg (Skull and Shackles)
peg leg (skull and shackles).description:
One of your legs was bitten off below the knee by a shark when you were just a child, and was replaced with a wooden peg leg. You've long since gotten used to your prosthesis, however, and take none of the normal penalties from having a peg leg. Your balance always gets a bit shaky after a few drinks, and last night at the Formidably Maid a tavern in Port Peril,was no exception. That must be why you fell to the floor as soon as you tried to stand up. What happened next is hazy, but you're pretty sure you were dragged out of the bar and onto a ship at the docks. At least they didn't throw you to the sharks...

Benefits: You've had to learn to deal with the pain of your injury as well, and you gain a +1 trait bonus on Fortitude saves. Ever since your accident you've hated sharks, and you also gain a +1 trait bonus on damage rolls against sharks and other animals with the aquatic subtype.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on damage rolls against sharks and other animals with the aquatic subtype.

mock gladiator.name: Mock Gladiator
mock gladiator.description:
You have learned how to work a crowd in Magnimar’s mock-gladiator fights.

Benefits: Choose one weapon. You take no penalty on attack rolls when dealing nonlethal damage with that weapon, and once per day if you score a critical hit with the weapon, you can immediately attempt an Intimidate check to demoralize your target.

magical klutz.name: Magical Klutz
magical klutz.description:
You were born in a place with a plethora of strangely interacting magic, such as Geb, the Mana Wastes, or Nex, and magic is dangerously eager to surge into action around you.

Effects: You roll twice and take the lower result on Use Magic Device checks to activate items blindly and on Reflex saving throws against effects produced by magic items.

contextNotes.text: You roll twice and take the lower result  to activate items blindly.
You roll twice and take the lower result against effects produced by magic items.

strong heart (gorum).name: Strong Heart (Gorum)
strong heart (gorum).description:
You can stand strong against even the most terrifying foes.

Requirements: Gorum

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saves against fear effects, and the DC of Intimidate checks made against you increases by +2.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus vs fear effects.

chernasardo hopeful (ironfang invasion).name: Chernasardo Hopeful (Ironfang Invasion)
chernasardo hopeful (ironfang invasion).description:
You’ve pledged your skills and your life to the Chernasardo rangers, studying to protect your homeland from foreign invaders. You currently remain a neophyte in this secretive guerrilla army. You have yet to be entrusted with many of their secrets, and spend a great deal of time training with the old ranger Aubrin in Phaendar to hone your skills of hunting and tracking.

Benefits: In forest or plains terrain, you may reduce a single target’s effective level of concealment against you (from total concealment to concealment to not concealed) by studying your natural surroundings as a move action. This reduced concealment ends immediately once the target moves from its current location. At 10th level, you may study your terrain as a swift action instead.

contextNotes.text: In forest or plains terrain, you may reduce a single target’s effective level of concealment against you (from total concealment to concealment to not concealed) by studying your natural surroundings as a move action. This reduced concealment ends immediately once the target moves from its current location. At 10th level, you may study your terrain as a swift action instead.

ugly swine (goblin).name: Ugly Swine (Goblin)
ugly swine (goblin).description:
You are a repulsive little freak whose origins are unclear. Your head is small for a goblin and your mouth oddly narrow, allowing you to pass in urban environments as an unidentifiable mongrel, providing you keep a low profile. However, if examined closely, your goblin origins are revealed.

Requirements: Goblin

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Disguise checks, and Disguise is a class skill for you.

bloody vengeance (vildeis).name: Bloody Vengeance (Vildeis)
bloody vengeance (vildeis).description:
You were slain by evil forces as a child, but a follower of Vildeis restored you to life. Now you live on in service to the Cardinal Martyr, battling the forces of evil so no other child will know the icy grip of death.

Requirements: Vildeis

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on attack rolls to confirm critical hits against any creature that has at least a faint evil aura (see detect evil).

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to confirm critical hits against any creature that has at least a faint evil aura.

superstitious ward (ustalav).name: Superstitious Ward (Ustalav)
superstitious ward (ustalav).description:
You're not frightened of the old wives' tales of vampires and ghosts, and though you try to avoid the undead, you've learned how to deal with them.

Requirements: Ustalav

Benefits: You may cast @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.oyu8efso7sio8arn]{disrupt undead} once per day as a spell-like ability. This is cast at your highest caster level gained; if you have no caster level, it functions at CL 1st.

amazingly ugly (half-orc, orc).name: Amazingly Ugly (Half-Orc, Orc)
amazingly ugly (half-orc, orc).description:
Your skull is misshapen in a grotesque way.

Requirements: Half-Orc, Orc

Benefits: You receive a +1 trait bonus on Intimidate skill checks and it becomes a class skill.

mystery initiate.name: Mystery Initiate
mystery initiate.description:
You were initiated into a mystery cult in your youth, and learned secrets that turned your understanding of your faith and the world on its head. This experience has given you a knack for lateral thinking.

Benefits: Once per day, you may reroll any Knowledge skill check. You must decide to use this ability after the first roll is made but before the results are revealed. You must take the second result, even if it is worse.

Suggested Characters

Mystery cultists, Nethys worshipers, Norgorber worshipers.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you may reroll. You must decide to use this ability after the first roll is made but before the results are revealed. You must take the second result, even if it is worse.
Once per day, you may reroll. You must decide to use this ability after the first roll is made but before the results are revealed. You must take the second result, even if it is worse.
Once per day, you may reroll. You must decide to use this ability after the first roll is made but before the results are revealed. You must take the second result, even if it is worse.

Once per day, you may reroll. You must decide to use this ability after the first roll is made but before the results are revealed. You must take the second result, even if it is worse.
Once per day, you may reroll. You must decide to use this ability after the first roll is made but before the results are revealed. You must take the second result, even if it is worse.
Once per day, you may reroll. You must decide to use this ability after the first roll is made but before the results are revealed. You must take the second result, even if it is worse.
Once per day, you may reroll. You must decide to use this ability after the first roll is made but before the results are revealed. You must take the second result, even if it is worse.
Once per day, you may reroll. You must decide to use this ability after the first roll is made but before the results are revealed. You must take the second result, even if it is worse.

eternal understanding (irori).name: Eternal Understanding (Irori)
eternal understanding (irori).description:
Though you haven’t been traditionally educated, you’re blessed with potent observation and discernment.

Requirements: Irori

Benefits: You can attempt Knowledge checks with a DC of 15 or lower untrained.

contextNotes.text: You can attempt checks with a DC of 15 or lower untrained.
You can attempt checks with a DC of 15 or lower untrained.
You can attempt checks with a DC of 15 or lower untrained.
You can attempt checks with a DC of 15 or lower untrained.
You can attempt checks with a DC of 15 or lower untrained.
You can attempt checks with a DC of 15 or lower untrained.
You can attempt checks with a DC of 15 or lower untrained.
You can attempt checks with a DC of 15 or lower untrained.
You can attempt checks with a DC of 15 or lower untrained.
You can attempt checks with a DC of 15 or lower untrained.

trench navigator (xidao).name: Trench Navigator (Xidao)
trench navigator (xidao).description:
You've sailed the treacherous waters of the Xidao Gulf in your past, and you can navigate their dangers adroitly.

Requirements: Xidao

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (geography) checks, and a +1 trait bonus on Profession (sailor) checks. One of these skills becomes a class skill for you.

Usage: Select Knowledge (geography) or Profession (sailor) when you take this trait.

Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the Profession (sailor) skill.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

hermean paragon (the steaming sea).name: Hermean Paragon (the Steaming Sea)
hermean paragon (the steaming sea).description:
You are a product of Hermea's breeding programs - either your parents were chosen to be citizens, or you were, but later failed to live up to the island's high standards.

Requirements: The Steaming Sea

Benefits: Whatever the case, you are quicker than normal members of your race, and gain a +2 trait bonus on Initiative checks.

mana wastes survivalist (mana wastes).name: Mana Wastes Survivalist (Mana Wastes)
mana wastes survivalist (mana wastes).description:
You grew up in the blasted badlands of the Mana Wastes, or have spent a lot of time there, and the harsh land has toughened your body.

Requirements: Mana Wastes

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Fortitude saves.

dedicated defender.name: Dedicated Defender
dedicated defender.description:
When a companion is down, you fight harder.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls and on checks made to overcome spell resistance while you are adjacent to a dying or disabled ally, animal companion, eidolon, familiar, or mount.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attack rolls while you are adjacent to a dying or disabled ally, animal companion, eidolon, familiar, or mount.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to overcome spell resistance while you are adjacent to a dying or disabled ally, animal companion, eidolon, familiar, or mount.

divine confidante.name: Divine Confidante
divine confidante.description:
You were inspired with the reverence offered by worshipers to priests and other spiritual leaders, so you assisted them.

Benefits: You gain a +3 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks to get hunches about those who discuss matters of faith, mythology, morality, religion, or the Outer Planes with you. Sense Motive is a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[3]] Trait bonus to get hunches about those who discuss matters of faith, mythology, morality, religion, or the Outer Planes with you.

alchemical prodigy (thuvia).name: Alchemical Prodigy (Thuvia)
alchemical prodigy (thuvia).description:
Early on in your life, you showed significant promise in the sciences and alchemical arts, and received extensive tutoring from those seeking to maximize your potential.

Requirements: Thuvia

Benefits: If you have the alchemy class feature, your alchemist level is considered 1 higher when calculating the duration of your extracts.

contextNotes.text: If you have the alchemy class feature, your alchemist level is considered 1 higher when calculating the duration of your extracts.

wiscrani ear.name: Wiscrani Ear
wiscrani ear.description:
You have a keen ear for the movements of thieves and monsters.

Benefits: You can always take 10 on Perception checks, even when stress and distractions would normally prevent you from doing so.

contextNotes.text: You can always take 10.

promising peacemaker (half-orc).name: Promising Peacemaker (Half-Orc)
promising peacemaker (half-orc).description:
