You were raised by a mix of human and orc family members, or by accommodating half-orc parents, and many people expected you to act as a flawless peacemaker. Whether these hopes saw fruition or met with frustration, you learned a great deal about both cultures.

Requirements: Half-Orc

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Knowledge (local) checks involving orcs, half-orcs, and humans, and a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks against members of those races.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on checks involving orcs, half-orcs, and humans.
+[[2]] Trait bonus on checks involving orcs, half-orcs, and humans.

giant investigator (coast).name: Giant Investigator (Coast)
giant investigator (coast).description:
As a result of your attempts to understand a rare encounter with the unearthly divine magic or secretive movements of coast-dwelling giants, you have learned to carefully consider all facts related to a mystery and form deductions after taking time to mull over an unanswered question.

Requirements: Coast

Benefits: Whenever you fail a Knowledge (local) or Knowledge (religion) check, you can attempt the check again in 24 hours with a +2 bonus to represent your careful formation of a new deduction. Additionally, you can attempt such skill checks untrained when the checks involve giants.

Likely Regions

Katapesh, Lands of the Linnorm Kings, Mwangi Expanse, Rahadoum, the Shackles, Varisia

contextNotes.text: Whenever you fail a check, you can attempt the check again in 24 hours with a +[[2]] bonus. Additionally, you can attempt such checks untrained when they involve giants.
Whenever you fail a check, you can attempt the check again in 24 hours with a +[[2]] bonus. Additionally, you can attempt such checks untrained when they involve giants.

sense of order (abadar; halfling).name: Sense of Order (Abadar; Halfling)
sense of order (abadar; halfling).description:
Your ability to notice even the slightest change, and the discomfort it causes, made service to Abadar a logical choice.

Requirements: Abadar; Halfling

Benefits: You gain a +3 trait bonus on any Perception check made to identify any changes to a location that have occurred since you last visited it.

contextNotes.text: +[[3]] Trait bonus to identify any changes to a location that have occurred since you last visited it.

inhabitant of illusion (kyonin).name: Inhabitant of Illusion (Kyonin)
inhabitant of illusion (kyonin).description:
Your experience with illusions allows you to discern them easily.

Requirements: Associated with the court of Kyonin

Benefits: When you first encounter an illusion, you can attempt a Will saving throw with a –2 penalty to disbelieve it, even if you don’t suspect the illusion. The GM rolls this saving throw in secret.

contextNotes.text: When you first encounter an illusion, you can attempt a Will saving throw with a –[[2]] penalty to disbelieve it. The GM rolls this saving throw in secret.

callous.name: Callous
callous.description:
You have endured many hardships throughout your life, and this has hardened you to the suffering of others to the point that you are not easily swayed by a sad story.

Benefits: You gain a +4 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks that oppose the Bluff checks of creatures that attempt to deceive you by winning your sympathy.

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Trait bonus on checks that oppose the Bluff checks of creatures that attempt to deceive you by winning your sympathy.

ambitious.name: Ambitious
ambitious.description:
You exude confidence in the presence of those more powerful than you—sometimes unreasonably so.

Benefits: You gain a +4 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks made to influence creatures with at least 5 Hit Dice more than you possess.

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Trait bonus to influence creatures with at least 5 Hit Dice more than you possess

mischievous smite (chaldira zuzaristan).name: Mischievous Smite (Chaldira Zuzaristan)
mischievous smite (chaldira zuzaristan).description:
You consider mischief a sacred rite in humbling enemies.

Requirements: Chaldira Zuzaristan

Benefits: You do not provoke attacks of opportunity when performing a dirty trick combat maneuver while your smite evil bonus applies to combat maneuver checks. If you already do not provoke such attacks of opportunity (such as by having Improved Dirty Trick), you instead gain a +2 trait bonus on dirty trick combat maneuver checks against creatures that are the targets of your smite.

contextNotes.text: You do not provoke attacks of opportunity when performing a dirty trick combat maneuver while your smite evil bonus applies to combat maneuver checks. If you already do not provoke such attacks of opportunity (such as by having Improved Dirty Trick), you instead gain a +2 trait bonus on dirty trick combat maneuver checks against creatures that are the targets of your smite.

cynic (mendev).name: Cynic (Mendev)
cynic (mendev).description:
As a Mendevian native, you have seen all manner of people claiming holy motives, and you have learned to judge them by their deeds, rather than their words.

Requirements: Mendev

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks, and Sense Motive is always a class skill for you.

dragon armor.name: Dragon Armor
dragon armor.description:
Xa Hoi’s elite warriors are trained to deflect energy attacks. 

Benefits: Choose an energy type (acid, cold, electricity, fire, or sonic) when you acquire this trait.

When you are wearing medium or heavy armor, you gain a +2 trait bonus on Reflex saves against spells and effects of the chosen energy type. 

Usage: Select one energy type (acid, cold, electricity, fire, or sonic) when you take this trait.

Update the note on the changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against spells and effects of <the chosen energy> type when you are wearing medium or heavy armor.

nearly blinded (human; tian).name: Nearly Blinded (Human; Tian)
nearly blinded (human; tian).description:
After a close call with snow blindness on the Crown of the World or the scouring winds of Hongal, you honed your instincts to protect your eyes.

Requirements: Tian Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws to avoid being blinded or dazzled.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to avoid being blinded or dazzled.

cheat death (besmara).name: Cheat Death (Besmara)
cheat death (besmara).description:
Even Besmara's most faithful worshipers usually call upon her only in times of greatest need. Sometimes Besmara intervenes on behalf of her faithful; other times, she turns her face away as they sink beneath the waves.

Requirements: Besmara

Benefits: Once per day, when you would be reduced to 0 or fewer hit points as a result of a failed ability check, skill check, or saving throw on your part, you may invoke this ability in order to reroll the failed check. You must take the result of the second roll, even if it is worse than the original.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, when you would be reduced to 0 or fewer hit points as a result of a failed check on your part, you may invoke your Cheat Death ability to reroll the failed check. You must take the result of the second roll, even if it is worse.

Once per day, when you would be reduced to 0 or fewer hit points as a result of a failed check on your part, you may invoke your Cheat Death ability to reroll the failed check. You must take the result of the second roll, even if it is worse.

Once per day, when you would be reduced to 0 or fewer hit points as a result of a failed check on your part, you may invoke your Cheat Death ability to reroll the failed check. You must take the result of the second roll, even if it is worse.

blessing of darkness (tiefling).name: Blessing of Darkness (Tiefling)
blessing of darkness (tiefling).description:
Your innate connection with the powers of darkness serves you well when evil zealots pray on your behalf.

Requirements: Tielfing

Benefits: Whenever a spellcaster capable of channeling negative energy casts a beneficial spell on you, she acts as if she were one level higher for the purpose of determining that spell's effects.

rapid drain (vampire; nosferatu).name: Rapid Drain (Vampire; Nosferatu)
rapid drain (vampire; nosferatu).description:
The ferocity of your hunger grants you the ability to draw life from your victims faster.

Requirements: Nosferatu Vampire Heritage

Benefits: When you use your blood drain ability, you gain 7 temporary hit points instead of 5.

Usage: Increase the temporary hit points your blood drain ability grants from 5 to 7.

contextNotes.text: When you use your blood drain ability, you gain 7 temporary hit points instead of 5.

st. clydwell's ward (mendev crusaders).name: St. Clydwell's Ward (Mendev Crusaders)
st. clydwell's ward (mendev crusaders).description:
You have learned the secrets of an ancient prayer that wards off the attacks of demons.

Requirements: Mendev Crusaders

Benefits: As a standard action, you can recite a holy chant that affects demons as if you were under the effect of a sanctuary spell (caster level equal to 1/2 your Hit Dice + your Charisma modifier).

lightning blessed (kobold).name: Lightning Blessed (Kobold)
lightning blessed (kobold).description:
You’re favored by the lightning shaman of your tribe, and he gifted you several sacred items as a reminder of your origins.

Requirements: Kobold

Benefits: You begin play with three vials of @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-items.Item.wLfyb5GkHZ3eOBNc]{bottled lighting}. These don’t count against your starting character wealth.

lastwall defender.name: Lastwall Defender
lastwall defender.description:
Your military service in Lastwall taught you how to identify undead creatures, changing them in your mind from mysterious terrors to concrete foes you know how to deal with.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack and damage rolls against undead creatures that you have personally identified using the Knowledge (religion) skill.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attack and damage rolls against undead creatures that you have personally identified using the Knowledge (religion) skill.

watching taldor (qadira).name: Watching Taldor (Qadira)
watching taldor (qadira).description:
You have spent all of your adult life with eyes turned northward, waiting for the Taldan weaklings to give you an excuse to fight, ready to act at a moment's notice.

Requirements: Character from Qadira who have had family members, neighbors, or friends affected by Taldan aggression or who have fallen victim to this hatred themselves.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus to Initiative checks, and if you are able to act during the surprise round of an encounter, you can draw a weapon (but not a potion or magic item) as a free action during that round.

contextNotes.text: If you are able to act during the surprise round, you can draw a weapon (but not a potion or magic item) as a free action during that round.

the newlyweds.name: The Newlyweds
the newlyweds.description:
Your sign is of two lovers, intertwined as one.

Requirements: 17 Calistril - 11 Pharast

Benefits: You gain a +2 bonus on Diplomacy checks to influence those who might be romantically attracted to you, and Diplomacy becomes a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] bonus to influence those who might be romantically attracted to you

almost human (half-orc).name: Almost Human (Half-Orc)
almost human (half-orc).description:
You have enough human features that it’s easy for you to pass for a pureblooded human.

Requirements: Half-orc

Benefits: You gain a +4 trait bonus on Disguise checks to pass as human and Disguise is always a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Trait bonus to pass as human.

thunder and fang performer.name: Thunder and Fang Performer
thunder and fang performer.description:
You learned from Shoanti champions to take advantage of the dramatic swings required by heavy weapons like the earth breaker.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Intimidate checks while wielding a two-handed melee weapon.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus while wielding a two-handed melee weapon.

perpetual companion.name: Perpetual Companion
perpetual companion.description:
You are bolstered against fear when your eidolon is near.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on all saving throws against fear effects whenever your eidolon is within 30 feet.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus vs fear effects whenever your eidolon is within 30 feet.

volatile fuse.name: Volatile Fuse
volatile fuse.description:
Firearms you wield are especially dangerous to you and everyone nearby.

Benefits: Whenever a firearm you wield misfires or explodes, roll a d%. On a result of 1–25, the misfire or explosion is delayed; reroll the attack with a –5 penalty (penalties from the broken condition are not applied to this reroll, and a firearm that was already broken does not explode). After the reroll is resolved, apply the effects of the misfire or explosion as normal. (If the d% roll result is 26 or higher, the misfire or explosion happens as normal.) Whenever a firearm you wield explodes due to a misfire, increase the explosion’s damage by 1d6 plus 1d6 for every 4 character levels you have.

Usage: Add context note to firearms with misfire chance.

vile kiss (tiefling; the motherless).name: Vile Kiss (Tiefling; The Motherless)
vile kiss (tiefling; the motherless).description:
You have learned how to exploit the natural disgust you provoke when in close proximity to others.

Requirements: The Motherless Tiefling Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on combat maneuver checks to perform a dirty trick combat maneuver for the purpose of making your opponent sickened.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to perform a dirty trick combat maneuver for the purpose of making your opponent sickened.

unorthodox strategy (silver crusade).name: Unorthodox Strategy (Silver Crusade)
unorthodox strategy (silver crusade).description:
You are particularly quick on your feet.

Requirements: Silver Crusade

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Acrobatics checks made to move through an enemy’s threatened squares.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to move through threatened squares.

asmodean demon hunter (asmodeus).name: Asmodean Demon Hunter (Asmodeus)
asmodean demon hunter (asmodeus).description:
Raised in the church of Asmodeus (whether or not you are currently a follower), you've focused your indoctrinated fervor primarily on the elimination of demons.

Requirements: Asmodeus

Benefits: You gain a +3 trait bonus on Knowledge (planes) check about demons and a +2 trait bonus on Will saves against mind-affecting spells and effects from demons.

contextNotes.text: +[[3]] Trait bonus on checks about demons.
+[[2]] Trait bonus against mind-affecting spells and effects from demons.

river acumen.name: River Acumen
river acumen.description:
Your connection with the river and its tributaries has blended with your reverence for the gods of the river passages so much so that you seem divinely blessed while plying river waters.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Swim checks in rivers or streams, and a +2 trait bonus on Survival checks to forage for food in or near a river or stream.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to forage for food in or near a river or stream.
+[[2]] Trait bonus on Swim checks in rivers or streams.

suck in your gut (darklands).name: Suck in Your Gut (Darklands)
suck in your gut (darklands).description:
Requirements

Darklands

Benefits: Because of the frequent need to move through the tight tunnels and other narrow spaces surrounding your tribe’s lair, you’re able to move normally when squeezing and take only a –2 penalty on attack rolls and to AC.

contextNotes.text: Take only a -[[2]] penalty on attack rolls when squeezing.
Take only a -[[2]] penalty to AC.when squeezing.

sharp wits (human; vudrani).name: Sharp Wits (Human; Vudrani)
sharp wits (human; vudrani).description:
You were raised with a daily regimen to keep your mind sharp and your words fluid.

Requirements: Vudrani Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on concentration checks and on Bluff checks.

cavern survivor (nar-voth).name: Cavern Survivor (Nar-Voth)
cavern survivor (nar-voth).description:
You’ve regularly traversed Nar-Voth and know how to subsist in that lightless realm better than most surface dwellers.

Requirements: Nar-Voth

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Survival checks to survive in a subterranean wilderness, and Survival is a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to survive in a subterranean wilderness.

fey mediator (grungir forest).name: Fey Mediator (Grungir Forest)
fey mediator (grungir forest).description:
Requirements: Grungir Forest

Benefits: You gain Sylvan as a bonus language. Additionally, choose Bluff, Diplomacy, or Knowledge (nature). That skill is a class skill for you.

Usage

Select Bluff, Diplomacy, or Knowledge (nature) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

abolitionist hunter (okeno).name: Abolitionist Hunter (Okeno)
abolitionist hunter (okeno).description:
You hunt those who hunt slavers, whether as one of Captain Bhey’s special task forces, or independently.

Requirements: Okeno

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls when fighting abolitionists or those you have good reason to believe targeted you because of your slaver activities.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attack rolls when fighting abolitionists or those you have good reason to believe targeted you because of your slaver activities.

doublespeak.name: Doublespeak
doublespeak.description:
It isn’t what you say—it’s how you say it.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Bluff checks to pass along secret messages.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to pass along secret messages.

unseen but not undone.name: Unseen but Not Undone
unseen but not undone.description:
Without a single gesture, you can unleash a bloodline spell.

Benefits: Once per day, you can cast a bloodline spell as if you had the Still Spell feat. Using this trait does not modify the spell’s level.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you can cast a bloodline spell as if you had the Still Spell feat without increasing its level.

scarred descendant.name: Scarred Descendant
scarred descendant.description:
Your own people are your favored enemy.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on rolls to confirm critical hits against favored enemies of your own subtype and on Survival checks made to track such creatures.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on rolls to confirm critical hits against favored enemies of your own subtype.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to track against favored enemies of your own subtype.

insatiable.name: Insatiable
insatiable.description:
You have become so accustomed to binging on the finer things in life that you find going without such excess particularly strenuous.

Effects: Goods and services cost you 10% more (and can’t be paid for by allies), and you need twice as much food and liquid as normal for the purposes of preventing starvation and thirst.

contextNotes.text: You need twice as much food and liquid as normal for the purposes of preventing starvation and thirst.

clumsy slave (cheliax, katapesh; halflin.name: Clumsy Slave (Cheliax, Katapesh; Halfling)
clumsy slave (cheliax, katapesh; halflin.description:
As a former slave, you learned how to conceal your malice toward your owner behind a facade of clumsiness.

Requirements: Cheliax, Katapesh; Halfling

Benefits: The first time you attack someone with an attack that deals nonlethal damage, your target must make a Sense Motive opposed by your Bluff check minus the damage dealt to realize you actually intended to harm him; failure means he believe the injury was an accident on your part instead (though he may still want to punish you for it).

detect disobedience.name: Detect Disobedience
detect disobedience.description:
You have an uncanny ability to spot a mutiny brewing.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks to detect when an underling is trying to hide something from you, and can attempt such checks instead of Perception checks to notice and react to a subordinate’s surprise attack against you.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to detect when an underling is trying to hide something from you, and can attempt such checks instead of Perception checks to notice and react to a subordinate’s surprise attack against you.

envoy of healing (sarenrae).name: Envoy of Healing (Sarenrae)
envoy of healing (sarenrae).description:
You strive to spread the Dawnflower’s message of kindness and compassion throughout the land, no matter how dangerous the road may be.

Requirements: Sarenrae

Benefits: Whenever you use @UUID[Compendium.pf1.class-abilities.Item.kXXz1lO4i7CibHJ9]{channel energy}, @UUID[Compendium.pf1.class-abilities.Item.Rzexvt4z8taA8dCu]{fervor}, or @UUID[Compendium.pf1.class-abilities.Item.WhE4j0Jc82npdMCP]{lay on hand}s to heal a living creature, you can reroll any healing die roll that results in a natural 1 (you must use the new result).

Usage: Add the following text to the Effect Notes of any of the 3 class features your actor may have:

"You can reroll any healing die roll that results in a natural 1 but you must use the new result. "

serpent runner (shattered star).name: Serpent Runner (Shattered Star)
serpent runner (shattered star).description:
You've participated in the mock gladiatorial battles and athletic feats at the Serpent's Run, Magnimar's grand hippodrome, but the safe nature of those games leaves you bored. Seeking greater thrills and tests of your abilities, you've pledged your arms and skill to the local Pathfinder lodge.

Benefits: Choose one of the following benefits to represent the techniques you learned from your time at the Serpent's Run:

  • Choose a specific type of armor. When wearing armor of that type, you treat its armor check penalty as if it were –1 lower.
  • When fighting with two weapons, the penalty imposed on your primary weapon is reduced by 1.
  • You gain a +2 trait bonus on Intimidate checks to demoralize opponents.

acadamae neophyte.name: Acadamae Neophyte
acadamae neophyte.description:
You have shown a significant talent for the magical arts, gaining the attention of the famed Acadamae of Korvosa.

Benefits: Whenever you succeed at a Knowledge (planes) check to identify a creature and its special powers or vulnerabilities, you gain one additional piece of useful information (as if your skill check result had been 5 higher).

contextNotes.text: One additional piece of useful information, when creature is identified.

slithering stride (kobold).name: Slithering Stride (Kobold)
slithering stride (kobold).description:
You were raised as a forest trapper, and are adept at moving quickly through underbrush.

Requirements: Kobold

Benefits: For 3 rounds per day, you can move through light or heavy undergrowth at full speed. This ability doesn’t work in terrain that has been magically manipulated to impede motion.

practiced disruptor.name: Practiced Disruptor
practiced disruptor.description:
You are experienced at facing off against spellcasters.

Benefits: You can attempt Knowledge (arcana) checks untrained; if you have ranks in that skill, you instead gain a +1 trait bonus on those checks. The concentration DC to cast a spell defensively in your threatened area increases by 1.

Usage: If you become trained in Knowledge (arcana), edit the target on the changes tab to Knowledge (arcana).

contextNotes.text: You can attempt checks untrained.

ooze defense (kobold).name: Ooze Defense (Kobold)
ooze defense (kobold).description:
The murky tunnels of your tribal home were infested with voracious oozes, which were a constant threat to your tribe. To protect yourselves, you and your tribespeople never strayed far without anti-ooze acid.

Requirements: Kobold

Benefits: You begin play with three @UUID[Compendium.pf1.items.Item.jplmkdpgjrlouwzu]{alkali flasks}. These don’t count against your starting character wealth.

color thief (goblin).name: Color Thief (Goblin)
color thief (goblin).description:
Your skin is an odd tint that somehow absorbs background colors. Maybe because your mother drank a lot of potions when you were still inside her belly. Maybe because you've been blessed by the barghests. Maybe because you're just better than the other boring goblins.

Requirements: Goblin

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Stealth checks, providing you wear only light armor or no armor.

passionate believer (po li).name: Passionate Believer (Po Li)
passionate believer (po li).description:
Your faith was stifled while you lived in the religiously oppressive slums of Changdo, and the persecutions against you only spurred your own beliefs and made your convictions stronger.

Requirements: Po Li

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against divine spells.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against divine spells.

sleepy.name: Sleepy
sleepy.description: Effects: You must sleep or rest for at least 12 hours each night to get the benefits of a full night’s rest. You take a –2 penalty on saving throws against sleep effects.

contextNotes.text: -[[2]] penalty  vs sleep effects.

expert distractor (ifrit).name: Expert Distractor (Ifrit)
expert distractor (ifrit).description:
Requirements: Ifrit

Benefits: Whenever you damage an opponent that is casting a spell with a melee attack that deals fire damage (such as a flaming weapon or a torch), the DC of the concentration check for your opponent to cast the spell is equal to 10 + the non-fire damage dealt + double the fire damage dealt + the spell’s level (instead of just 10 + the damage dealt + the spell’s level).

contextNotes.text: Whenever you damage an opponent that is casting a spell with a melee attack that deals fire damage (such as a flaming weapon or a torch), the DC of the concentration check for your opponent to cast the spell is equal to 10 + the non-fire damage dealt + double the fire damage dealt + the spell’s level (instead of just 10 + the damage dealt + the spell’s level).

home port (the shackles).name: Home Port (The Shackles)
home port (the shackles).description:
You have a fondness for a particular port, and the people who live and do business there return the sentiment.

Requirements: The Shackles

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks to gather information in your chosen home port.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus  to gather information in your chosen home port.

clan artisan (xa hoi).name: Clan Artisan (Xa Hoi)
clan artisan (xa hoi).description:
You were raised in one of the numerous clans of Xa Hoi, and learned a valuable trade in your early years.

Requirements: Xa Hoi

Benefits: Pick one Craft skill. You gain a +2 trait bonus on checks with that skill, and it becomes a class skill for you.

Usage

Select one Craft skill when you take this trait.

Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the chosen skill.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

crusader tactician (lastwall).name: Crusader Tactician (Lastwall)
crusader tactician (lastwall).description:
You have studied at Vigil's Crusader War College, and are skilled in both heraldry and siege tactics.

Requirements: Lastwall

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (engineering) and Knowledge (nobility) checks, and one of these skills is always a class skill for you.

Usage

Select Knowledge (engineering) or Knowledge (nobility) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

foreign-tongued (jade regent).name: Foreign-Tongued (Jade Regent)
foreign-tongued (jade regent).description:
Intrigued by the travels of one of your long-time acquaintances, you’ve convinced her to teach you one of the exotic languages she picked up in her journeys.

Benefits: Choose one of the following characters. You may select one of that character’s languages as a bonus language. Additionally, you gain a +2 trait bonus on Bluff checks made to pass secret messages to the chosen character (see the description of the Bluff skill).

NPC Choices

Ameiko: Tien, Varisian

Koya or Sandru: Varisian

Shalelu: Elven, Goblin

Usage

  1. Select one character (Ameiko, Koya, Sandru, or Shalelu) when you take this trait.

  2. Update the text of the note on the changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice

  3. Select one of the languages permitted by your first choice (at step 1) as described above.

  4. Update the Granted Proficiencies>Languages section of the details tab of this trait to reflect this choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to pass secret messages to <the chosen character>.

winter's soul (irrisen).name: Winter's Soul (Irrisen)
winter's soul (irrisen).description:
The eternal winter of Irrisen is as much a part of you as it is the land.

Requirements: Irrisen

Benefits: You may cast @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.n879x6fw79jiofew]{ray of frost} once per day as a spell-like ability. This is cast at your highest caster level gained; if you have no caster level, it functions at CL 1st.

exalted of the society (cleric, pathfind.name: Exalted of the Society (Cleric, Pathfinder Society)
exalted of the society (cleric, pathfind.description:
The vaults of the Grand Lodge in Absalom contain many secrets of the divine powers of the gods, and you have studied your god extensively.

Requirements: Cleric, Pathfinder Society

Benefits: You may channel energy 1 additional time per day.

Usage: Increase the number of uses per day on your channel energy.

guerrilla mender (nirmathas).name: Guerrilla Mender (Nirmathas)
guerrilla mender (nirmathas).description:
With the help of a druidic healer, you narrowly escaped death by disease or poisoning while on the run from Molthuni militias, whether contracting the ailment was an accident or you were the victim of more sinister forces.

Requirements: Must hail from Nirmathas or have lived there for at least 1 year

Benefits: Whenever you successfully use the Heal skill to treat an ally’s poison or disease, you grant the ally an additional +1 trait bonus on the saving throw against the disease or poison. Heal is a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: When you successfully use the Heal skill to treat an ally’s poison or disease, you grant the ally an additional +1 trait bonus on their saving throw against the disease or poison.

magical talent.name: Magical Talent
magical talent.description:
Either from inborn talent, whimsy of the gods, or obsessive study of strange tomes, you have mastered the use of a cantrip.

Benefits: Choose a 0-level spell. You may cast that spell once per day as a spell-like ability. This spell-like ability is cast at your highest caster level gained; if you have no caster level, it functions at CL 1st. The spell-like ability's save DC is Charisma-based.

Usage: If you don't have the spells tab, navigate to your sheet's Settings tab and check "Spell-like" under Spellbook Usage, then, go to the newly added spells tab and add your chosen 0-level spell. In the spell's tab configuration, if your caster level is 0, type 1 in the "Class Level Modification" formula.

seafaring slayer (tengu).name: Seafaring Slayer (Tengu)
seafaring slayer (tengu).description:
At a vulnerable age, you survived an encounter with a monstrous sea creature.

Requirements: Tengu

Benefits: You reduce any foe’s cover bonus to AC from water by 2.

contextNotes.text: You reduce any foe’s cover bonus to AC from water by 2.

propitiation (any dwarven deity).name: Propitiation (any dwarven deity)
propitiation (any dwarven deity).description:
Your knowledge of the dwarven pantheon tells you precisely which gods have jurisdiction over which aspects of your life, and you can call upon their help even if they are not your patron.

Requirements: Any dwarven deity

Benefits: At the start of each day, pick one of the following skills: Appraise, Bluff, Craft (pick one craft skill), Diplomacy, Intimidate, or Knowledge (local). You gain a +2 trait bonus on that skill until the start of the next day.

contextNotes.text: At the start of each day, pick one of the following skills: Appraise, Bluff, Craft (pick one craft skill), Diplomacy, Intimidate, or Knowledge (local). You gain a +2 trait bonus on that skill until the start of the next day.

At the start of each day, pick one of the following skills: Appraise, Bluff, Craft (pick one craft skill), Diplomacy, Intimidate, or Knowledge (local). You gain a +2 trait bonus on that skill until the start of the next day.

At the start of each day, pick one of the following skills: Appraise, Bluff, Craft (pick one craft skill), Diplomacy, Intimidate, or Knowledge (local). You gain a +2 trait bonus on that skill until the start of the next day.

At the start of each day, pick one of the following skills: Appraise, Bluff, Craft (pick one craft skill), Diplomacy, Intimidate, or Knowledge (local). You gain a +2 trait bonus on that skill until the start of the next day.

At the start of each day, pick one of the following skills: Appraise, Bluff, Craft (pick one craft skill), Diplomacy, Intimidate, or Knowledge (local). You gain a +2 trait bonus on that skill until the start of the next day.

At the start of each day, pick one of the following skills: Appraise, Bluff, Craft (pick one craft skill), Diplomacy, Intimidate, or Knowledge (local). You gain a +2 trait bonus on that skill until the start of the next day.

alkenstar engineer.name: Alkenstar Engineer
alkenstar engineer.description:
Your extensive experience with firearms allows you to get the most out of those unique weapons.

Benefits: When you’re wielding a broken early firearm, the amount your misfire value increases by is reduced by 1.

contextNotes.text: When you’re wielding a broken early firearm, the amount your misfire value increases by is reduced by 1.

pathfinder's focus.name: Pathfinder's Focus
pathfinder's focus.description: Benefits: You can select a magnetic compass for your arcane bond class feature; this compass is nonmagical, but you can later spend 250 gp to upgrade it into a wayfinder. Choosing this trait allows you to upgrade this wayfinder to a variant or unique wayfinder per the normal rules for improving magic items on page 553 of the Pathfinder RPG Core Rulebook. A bonded wayfinder must be held in your hand to grant the benefits of being a bonded object.

boarded in cheliax (serpent's skull).name: Boarded in Cheliax (Serpent's Skull)
boarded in cheliax (serpent's skull).description:
While you aren't necessarily a native of Cheliax, you boarded the Jenivere at one of that country's port cities. As the people of Cheliax generally know the Mwangi Expanse as a near legendary land home to strange beasts and exotic treasures, your views of the region have a distinctly romantic tinge, and the desire for fame, wealth, and adventure likely motivate your travels.

Benefits: You begin the campaign with a detailed map of the Mwangi Expanse (this grants +2 competence bonus on all Knowledge (geography) checks made regarding this region) and 200 gp worth of mundane equipment to aid your exploration of the jungle.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Competence bonus on check made regarding the Mwangi Expanse, as long as you remain able to consult the detailed map of the Mwangi Expanse you began play with.

armed grit.name: Armed Grit
armed grit.description:
You make an impression during a standoff.

Benefits: As long as you have a firearm drawn, you gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against fear and the DCs of Intimidate checks to demoralize you increase by 2.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on saving throws against fear, when wielding a firearm

shield-trained (gorum).name: Shield-Trained (Gorum)
shield-trained (gorum).description:
You were trained to use shields as weapons.

Requirements: Gorum

Benefits: Heavy and light shields are considered simple weapons rather than martial weapons for you. Heavy shields are considered light weapons for you.

Usage:

Modify the Category (from Martial to Simple) on the Details tab of any light or heavy shields in your actor's inventory.

Any heavy shield should also be modified to change their Type (from One-handed to Light).

traditionalist (divine anthology).name: Traditionalist (Divine Anthology)
traditionalist (divine anthology).description:
You attempt to maintain older traditions, despite changing social norms, and find great value in the trappings and codes of the aristocracy.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (nobility) checks, and Knowledge (nobility) is a class skill for you.

frost spitter (kobold).name: Frost Spitter (Kobold)
frost spitter (kobold).description:
The icy breath of a white dragon fills your lungs.

Requirements: Kobold

Benefits: Once per day, you can quickly exhale to produce a frigid cloud of dense fog that quickly sinks to the ground. This freezing mist covers a 10-foot-square area with slippery ice that’s as difficult to traverse as an ice sheet (Core Rulebook 430).

fire-tongued (kobold).name: Fire-Tongued (Kobold)
fire-tongued (kobold).description:
The sorcerers of your tribe are adept at summoning and binding outsiders from the Elemental Plane of Fire. In order to communicate with their thralls, many in your tribe learned the rudiments of the outsiders’ language.

Requirements: Kobold

Benefits: In addition to speaking Draconic, you begin play with the ability to speak Ignan.

urban acolyte.name: Urban Acolyte
urban acolyte.description:
Growing up in a city has broadened your philosophy, and helped you interpret your patron’s divine will in a novel way.

Benefits: You gain Knowledge (local) as a class skill.

Additionally, you can select one of the following subdomains using the normal rules for subdomains so long as your deity grants access to its associated domain: Competition, Duels, Espionage, Fortifications, Industry, Legislation, Plague, or Riot.

necrotic aura (whispering way).name: Necrotic Aura (Whispering Way)
necrotic aura (whispering way).description:
Your exposure to the necromantic arts has strengthened your defenses against its vile rot.

Requirements: Whispering Way

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against spells from the necromancy school or any effect that deals negative energy damage.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against spells from the necromancy school or any effect that deals negative energy damage.

outlander - lore seeker (any campaign).name: Outlander - Lore Seeker (Any Campaign)
outlander - lore seeker (any campaign).description:
You are not from town; you’ve recently come from somewhere else and are hoping to make your fortune here. Pick one of the following reasons to be a newcomer to the area.

Benefits: Pick one of the following reasons to be a newcomer to the area:

Lore Seeker

The secrets of an ancient fallen civilization intrigue you, particularly the magical traditions of its highly mystical culture. You’ve studied magic intensely, and hope to increase that knowledge by adding ancient lore. You’ve come to the region to pursue that study, and chose the town as your base because it was out of the way of bigger cities—meaning less competition to study the ancient monuments in the region, you hope!

You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (arcana) checks, and Knowledge (arcana) is a class skill for you.

If you cast arcane spells, pick three spells on your spell list. You are particularly adept at casting these spells, so they function at +1 caster level when you cast them, and their save DCs (if any) gain a +1 bonus.

Usage: If you cast arcane spells, you must pick 3 from your spell list when you select this trait.

You must edit these three spells independently once you have added them to your actor.

Special Note

This is one of three versions of this trait. Other versions can be found here:

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.HSzL4QzbRr2BtTjJ]{Outlander - Exile (Any Campaign)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.0mlO02qRdJH6656V]{Outlander - Missionary (Any

animalistic affliction (human; ulfen).name: Animalistic Affliction (Human; Ulfen)
animalistic affliction (human; ulfen).description:
You grew up with an affinity for predatory animals, and whispers amid your clan suggest somewhere in the past, lycanthropy afflicted an ancestor. Whether or not you share this affliction, you often have animalistic urges.

Requirements: Ulfen Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Handle Animal and Wild Empathy checks.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on Wild Empathy checks.

contemptuous.name: Contemptuous
contemptuous.description:
Whether you are a commoner who has come to despise the haughty upper crust or a holier-than-thou noble who can’t stand being around the unwashed masses, your contempt drives you to study carefully, making sure you always have the upper hand in social exchanges with them.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (local) checks and Knowledge (nobility) checks, and one of these skills becomes a class skill for you.

Usage: Select Knowledge (local) or Knowledge (nobility) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

hero worship (jade regent).name: Hero Worship (Jade Regent)
hero worship (jade regent).description:
Everyone in town seems to admire Ameiko and Shalelu,they're heroes, after all, and played key roles in the recent troubles that plagued Sandpoint. You, though, take this admiration to a new level,you practically worship one of these two. This could be because you saw one of them perform a particularly impressive act of bravery at some point in the past, or maybe they just happened to give you the exact right words of encouragement or a kind gift at some point a few years ago when you were young and impressionable. In any case, you've done your best to emulate your hero's talents at spellcasting or combat.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on all attack rolls against foes that threaten your hero as well as one of the following:

If your hero is Ameiko, you gain a +2 bonus on concentration checks. If your hero is Shalelu, you gain a +1 bonus to your AC against attacks of opportunity.

NPC Choices Ameiko, Shalelu

Usage

  1. Select Ameiko (concentration) or Shalelu (AC) when you take this trait.

  2. If you selected Ameiko, Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect Concentration and delete the untargeted context note.

  3. If you selected Shalelu, Update the target of the 1st context note to AC, then delete the untargeted change

  4. Update the text of the context note on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice. (Ameiko or Shalelu)

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] bonus to your AC against attacks of opportunity
+[[1]] Trait bonus on all attack rolls against foes that threaten, (Ameiko or Shalelu),  your hero.

creative reality (ganzi).name: Creative Reality (Ganzi)
creative reality (ganzi).description:
Straightforward answers bore you, for they only encourage people to depend on others rather than find out for themselves. You have grown adept over the years at convincing people of falsehoods and couching your honest answers in so much doublespeak they may as well be lies.

Requirements: Ganzi

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Bluff checks, which increases to +5 when attempting to convince someone an honest answer is a falsehood.

contextNotes.text: +[[5]] Trait bonus when attempting to convince someone an honest answer is a falsehood. (This trait already provides +1)

transitional (triaxian).name: Transitional (Triaxian)
transitional (triaxian).description:
As a Transitional Triaxian, you are attuned to the weather no matter what world you stand upon.

Requirements: Triaxian

Benefits: Once per day when you attempt a Survival check to predict the weather, you can add a trait bonus equal to 3 + 1/2 your character level. In addition, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Fortitude saves against the effects of cold and hot weather.

contextNotes.text: +[[3+floor(@attributes.hd.total /2)]] Trait bonus, once per day, when you attempt  to predict the weather.
+[[1]] Trait bonus against the effects of cold and hot weather.

born under the cradle.name: Born under the Cradle
born under the cradle.description:
Your birth sign aligned significantly with Bretheda on the day you were born, granting you a knack for cooperation in the heat of battle.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on attack rolls to perform the aid another action.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to perform the Aid Another action
+[[2]] Trait bonus to perform the Aid Another action

flair for destruction (tiefling; pitborn.name: Flair for Destruction (Tiefling; Pitborn)
flair for destruction (tiefling; pitborn.description:
You have a talent for striking objects at their weakest points.

Requirements: Pitborn Tielfling Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on weapon damage rolls made against objects and constructs.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on weapon damage rolls made against objects and constructs.

berserker of the society (barbarian, pat.name: Berserker of the Society (Barbarian, Pathfinder Society)
berserker of the society (barbarian, pat.description:
Your time spent as a Pathfinder has taught you new truths about the origins of the your rage ability.

Requirement(s) Barbarian, Pathfinder Society

Benefits: You may use your rage ability for 3 additional rounds per day.

Usage: Add @resource.BerserkeroftheSociety(Barbarian, PathfinderSociety).max:3 on Rage Use Formula.

guilty fraud.name: Guilty Fraud
guilty fraud.description:
You received something through trickery that you did not deserve, and your guilt for the misdeed distracts you from dangers around you.

Effects: You take a -4 penalty on Bluff checks against creatures with an attitude toward you of indifferent or better.

contextNotes.text: -[[4]] Penalty against creatures with an attitude toward you of indifferent or better

snake hater (aasimar; plumekith).name: Snake Hater (Aasimar; Plumekith)
snake hater (aasimar; plumekith).description:
You've always loathed serpents and other slithering monsters, ever since you can remember, and have studied them extensively.

Requirements: Plumekith Aasimar Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Knowledge (dungeoneering) checks and Knowledge (dungeoneering) is always a class skill for you.

icy mementos (kobold).name: Icy Mementos (Kobold)
icy mementos (kobold).description:
As a parting gift, the shaman of your tribe gave you an alchemical concoction as a reminder of your icy home.

Requirements: Kobold

Benefits: You begin play with three bottles of @UUID[Compendium.pf1.items.Item.mbhdwlzmycxkuobo]{liquid ice}. These don’t count against your starting character wealth.

inner beauty (shelyn).name: Inner Beauty (Shelyn)
inner beauty (shelyn).description:
Requirements: Shelyn

Benefits: Once per day when you manifest your faith in your goddess, you gain a +4 trait bonus on a single Bluff, Craft, Diplomacy, or Perform skill check. You must choose to use this trait immediately after rolling the skill check but before the results are revealed by the GM.

alluring (bolka).name: Alluring (Bolka)
alluring (bolka).description:
You have the art of turning a passing glance into love.

Requirements: Bolka

Benefits: You receive a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks with those who find you attractive. You can use daze once per day as a spell-like ability, with a caster Level equal to your character level.

Usage: In your character sheet, go to the settings tab and check "Spell-like" under spellbook usage. Then navigate to the spells tab, go to your spell-like spellbook, and add the Daze spell. In the spell-like spellbook configuration, set the spellcasting class to "Hit Die". Open your new Daze spell and go to the details tab, open the use properties, in the use window go to the usage tab, and at the bottom, below "Action" choose "Per Day" and type 1 in the formula.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus with those who find you attractive.

feral speech.name: Feral Speech
feral speech.description:
You were raised in a community that included numerous individuals who spoke with creatures of the wild.

Benefits: You gain one of the following languages of your choice as a bonus language: Aklo, Aquan, Auran, Giant, Ignan, or Sylvan.

With your GM’s permission, you can instead select Druidic, but druids are protective of their language—you are likely to find yourself hunted by druids wishing to eliminate you as a threat to their sole ownership of their secrets.

Usage: Choose one of the permitted language options Aklo, Aquan, Auran, Giant, Ignan, or Sylvan (or Druidic).

Add the chosen language to the bonus language area of the "Granted Proficiencies" section of the Details tab of this trait

the reclaimer (tyrant's grasp).name: The Reclaimer (Tyrant's Grasp)
the reclaimer (tyrant's grasp).description:
You lost something precious in the destruction of Roslar’s Coffer 12 years ago—a home, a legacy, perhaps even family—but returned because a soul shouldn’t run from pain, but heal from it. Rebuilding your life and preventing that same hurt from dragging down others drives you to serve and protect, perhaps as a politician, charity worker, healer, or soldier, but that same loss fills you with an uncharitable anger that is often difficult to reconcile.

Benefits: Your wary eyes grant you a +1 trait bonus on Perception checks. You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack and damage rolls against any opponent that dealt hit point damage to one of your allies in the previous round.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attack and damage rolls against any opponent that dealt hit point damage to one of your allies in the previous round.

unbreakable survivor (ironfang invasion).name: Unbreakable Survivor (Ironfang Invasion)
unbreakable survivor (ironfang invasion).description:
Over a decade ago, bandits took everything you valued in life and left you barely alive. You managed to rebuild your life in the years since, and your tenacity has made you a local legend.

Benefits: Once per day as a full round action, you may shrug off some of your injuries and immediately heal a number of hit points equal to your Constitution modifier + 1 per Hit Die. Your reputation for tenacity inspires your neighbors, and you gain a +1 trait bonus whenever you attempt to influence residents of Phaendar with Diplomacy or Intimidate checks. At 6th level, your reputation spreads further across the nation, and you may apply your trait bonus on Diplomacy and Intimidate checks to influence all humanoids in Nirmathas.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus whenever you attempt to influence residents of Phaendar. At 6th level, you may apply your trait bonus on checks to influence all humanoids in Nirmathas.
+[[1]] Trait bonus whenever you attempt to influence residents of Phaendar. At 6th level, you may apply your trait bonus on checks to influence all humanoids in Nirmathas.

brastlewark businessman (gnome).name: Brastlewark Businessman (Gnome)
brastlewark businessman (gnome).description:
Your time among the gnome elite of Brastlewark in Cheliax has led to an extensive knowledge of alchemy and ready connections in the business.

Requirements: Gnome

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on all Craft (alchemy) checks.

Usage

Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the Craft (alchemy) skill.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect Craft (alchemy).

fleet of foot (chiardmar).name: Fleet of Foot (Chiardmar)
fleet of foot (chiardmar).description:
Requirements: Horse (Chiardmar)

Benefits: While riding a chiardmar, you treat your mount’s base speed as +10 feet faster for the purpose of using the run or withdraw actions.

liquid-tongued (kobold).name: Liquid-Tongued (Kobold)
liquid-tongued (kobold).description:
The magic-users in your tribe pride themselves on their ability to control the forces of water. Integral to this practice is the ability to study and communicate with visitors from the Elemental Plane of Water.

Requirements: Kobold

Benefits: In addition to speaking Draconic, you begin play with the ability to speak Aquan.

holy tattoo (varisia).name: Holy Tattoo (Varisia)
holy tattoo (varisia).description:
When you had your deity’s holy symbol tattooed on your body by a Sczarni tattooist years ago, you never imagined it would save your life.

Requirements: Varisia

Benefits: Your holy symbol tattoo functions in all ways as a holy symbol (Pathfinder RPG Ultimate Equipment 57). When you use your holy symbol tattoo to keep a vampire at bay, the DC for the vampire to overcome its revulsion is 28 (rather than the normal 25).

nine-tailed scion (kitsune).name: Nine-Tailed Scion (Kitsune)
nine-tailed scion (kitsune).description:
You are talented with kitsune magic.

Requirements: Kitsune

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on caster level checks for your racial spell-like abilities as well as those from the Magical Tail feat. Additionally, you can select Magical Tail as a bonus feat whenever your favored class grants you a bonus bloodline feat, combat feat, or metamagic feat instead of the normal type of feat granted by that class. You cannot exchange specific feats granted by a class or race for Magical Tail in this manner; for instance, a monk cannot exchange his Stunning Fist feat for Magical Tail.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on caster level checks for your racial spell-like abilities as well as those from the Magical Tail feat.

burnished skin (aasimar; emberkin).name: Burnished Skin (Aasimar; Emberkin)
burnished skin (aasimar; emberkin).description:
Sometime during your adolescence, you suffered severe burns in a devastating fire. Along with your scars, you developed the uncanny ability to tell what is and isn't real.

Requirements: Emberskin Aasimar Hertiage

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws made to disbelieve illusions.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to disbelieve illusions.

devout visionary.name: Devout Visionary
devout visionary.description:
As a child, you had a strange vision while suffering from a terrible illness, and only found your way back to the mundane world by following the call of a spiritual leader—perhaps a shaman’s drum or a druid’s lilting poetry. Since then, you have studied symbolism and dreams in the hope that you might understand your own vision, though your lessons prove just as helpful to others.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Will saving throws against spells and effects that grant the confused or dazed condition. Once per day as a full-round action, you may grant an adjacent ally a new Will saving throw to end an effect that grants the confused or dazed condition.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against spells and effects that grant the confused or dazed condition. Once per day as a full-round action, you may grant an adjacent ally a new Will saving throw to end an effect that grants the confused or dazed condition.

canter.name: Canter
canter.description:
You grew up among thieves and scoundrels, and their unusual speech patterns and turns of phrase don't faze you in the slightest today as a result.

Benefits: Anyone who attempts to use Bluff to deliver a secret message to you gains a +5 bonus on his Bluff check. When you attempt to intercept a secret message using Sense Motive, you gain a +5 trait bonus on the attempt.

contextNotes.text: +[[5]] Trait bonus to intercept a secret message

preternatural awareness.name: Preternatural Awareness
preternatural awareness.description:
Multiple encounters with fell magic have left you wary.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Perception and Sense Motive checks to notice cursed objects, haunts, and magically disguised creatures.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on checks to notice cursed objects, haunts, and magically disguised creatures.
+[[2]] Trait bonus on checks to notice cursed objects, haunts, and magically disguised creatures.

the outsider (tyrant's grasp).name: The Outsider (Tyrant's Grasp)
the outsider (tyrant's grasp).description:
You don’t have a place, but the frontier seems comfortable enough for now. Maybe you’re too informal for city life, too selfish for Lastwall’s general culture of camaraderie, or too scarred by things you’ve seen to entirely trust another person.

Benefits: You are accustomed to moving without drawing much attention to yourself, gaining a +1 trait bonus on Stealth and Survival checks, and one of these skills is always a class skill for you.

Accustomed to working alone, you gain only half the benefit (+1) from aid another actions, but you’re accustomed to doing the job right the first time and so increase the bonus you provide by 1 when aiding another character.

Usage

Select Stealth or Survival when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: You gain only half the benefit from aid another actions, but increase the bonus you provide by 1 when aiding another character.
You gain only half the benefit from aid another actions, but increase the bonus you provide by 1 when aiding another character.

orphaned.name: Orphaned
orphaned.description:
You grew up separated from your birth parents, and had to learn to watch out for yourself.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Survival checks, and Survival is always a class skill for you.

scion of the nine circles (hell's vengea.name: Scion of the Nine Circles (Hell's Vengeance)
scion of the nine circles (hell's vengea.description:
While the worship of Asmodeus is widespread throughout Cheliax, you follow a different path: that of the philosophy of diabolism. Asmodeus and the other archdevils are worthy of respect and emulation, certainly, but rather than worship one Lord of Hell above all others, your faith is instead based on the veneration of the order and laws of Hell itself as a model for a perfect society. Free will must be suppressed to prevent rebellion, slavery is required to keep the unworthy in their place, and evil and cruelty are necessary for good and kindness to exist.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (planes) checks, and Knowledge (planes) is a class skill for you. You also know the Infernal tongue (this does not count toward your number of languages). In addition, your studies of Hell’s underlying structure give strength to your determination. You gain a +2 trait bonus on Will saves against the mind-affecting effects of outsiders with the good subtype.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against the mind-affecting effects of outsiders with the good subtype.

sprint (gnome).name: Sprint (Gnome)
sprint (gnome).description:
You were taught from a young age that the world is full of big, fast, dangerous foes, and that sometimes the best course of action is to put your head down and run. Your parents taught you how to make the most of a solid mid-length sprint and how to squeeze just a little extra speed out of yourself.

Requirements: Gnome

Benefits: If you do nothing but move in a turn (that is, if you run or use both of your actions in a round to move your speed) and you are in no armor or light armor, you may move an extra 5 feet.

winner's luck (riddleport).name: Winner's Luck (Riddleport)
winner's luck (riddleport).description:
Winning at the tables in Riddleport’s gambling houses can be as devastating as losing. Years of dodging the jealous blows of card table patrons have given you a sixth sense for when your luck is about to run out.

Requirements: Riddleport

Benefits: Whenever you succeed at a Reflex saving throw, you may draw a weapon as an immediate action, or a hidden weapon as a move action.

contextNotes.text: Whenever you succeed at a Reflex saving throw, you may draw a weapon as an immediate action or a hidden weapon as a move action.

hagfish hopeful (rise of the runelords).name: Hagfish Hopeful (Rise of the Runelords)
hagfish hopeful (rise of the runelords).description:
Ever since passing through Sandpoint when you were a child and hearing about the contest at the popular tavern known as the Hagfish, you wanted to take that coin purse as your own and carve your name on the ceiling beam above the bar. Training yourself to choke down indigestible food and drink water a pig would refuse, you’ve built up quite a strong resistance to all things putrid and gross.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Fortitude saves against disease and poison.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against disease and poison.

abject belief.name: Abject Belief
abject belief.description:
You cling tenaciously to a faith that is doubted by many, perhaps based around a god who has vanished, or was never a god in the first place. You refuse to forsake your beliefs despite all evidence, perhaps due to a need to belong, respect for tradition, or a religious experience. As a result, it is nearly impossible to convince you of your deity’s absence.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Will saving throws against fear effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against fear effects.

youthful infiltrator.name: Youthful Infiltrator
youthful infiltrator.description:
You were trained from a young age to ambush the enemies of your order or organization, which makes you hard to trust for most outside your organization if they learn your affiliation.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls against flat-footed foes, as long as they have not seen you make an attack with this bonus before.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against flat-footed foes, as long as they have not seen you make an attack with this bonus before.

martial manuscript.name: Martial Manuscript
martial manuscript.description:
You either carry or have memorized a sacred text from your school, temple, or monastery, and its wisdom gives you insight that makes your attacks more devastating.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on rolls to confirm critical hits when you’re using an unarmed strike or monk weapon.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on rolls to confirm critical hits when you’re using an unarmed strike or monk weapon.

heat fortitude (kobold).name: Heat Fortitude (Kobold)
heat fortitude (kobold).description:
You grew up close to sulfurous volcanic vents, which steeled you against heat dangers.

Requirements: Kobold

Benefits: You gain a +4 trait bonus on Fortitude saving throws against nonlethal damage from heat.

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Trait bonus against nonlethal damage from heat.

andoren freedom fighter (andoran).name: Andoren Freedom Fighter (Andoran)
andoren freedom fighter (andoran).description:
You've dedicated your life to fighting against oppression, tyranny, and slavery.

Requirements: Andoran

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack and damage rolls against slavers or any creature holding someone against their will.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attack and damage rolls against slavers or any creature holding someone against their will.

expert smuggler (erages).name: Expert Smuggler (Erages)
expert smuggler (erages).description:
Years of smuggling and other unsavory work have trained you to hide your assets even when being actively searched.

Requirements: Erages

Benefits: Other creatures never gain the +4 bonus on their Perception checks (opposed by your Sleight of Hand check) while frisking you.

contextNotes.text: Other creatures never gain the +4 bonus on their Perception checks  while frisking you.

urban sleuth (hell's rebels).name: Urban Sleuth (Hell's Rebels)
urban sleuth (hell's rebels).description:
Much of Kintargo’s history is lost or hidden. You know because you’ve made a point of seeking out those secrets. Local urban legends and bits of strange historical rumors have long fascinated you—who knows what amazing truths about Kintargo’s past have been lost forever to the redactors of House Thrune? What caused Professor Mangvhune of the Alabaster Academy to become the city’s most infamous serial killer? Who were the dragons Adrakash, Ithanothaur, and Rivozair, and what were their ties to Kintargo? Why do the Devil’s Bells of the Temple of Asmodeus seem to ring at random times, and is there a pattern to the peals? Who were the Silver Ravens who defended Kintargo during the Chelish Civil War, and why did they vanish so soon after that war’s resolution? So many mysteries, and who better than you to find the answers?

Benefits: Pick one of the questions above as your focus. Answers to these questions can be discovered at different points during Hell’s Rebels, but don’t expect to learn these answers any time soon! More important, your choice of focus grants you a +1 trait bonus in a particular Knowledge skill check associated with that focus (pick one of the two options provided below for your question). That Knowledge skill is always a class skill for you. Once per day, when you attempt a Knowledge skill check in either of the types associated with your focus, you can roll twice and take the better result as your actual result.

Devil’s Bells: Knowledge (arcana) or Knowledge (planes)

Local Dragons: Knowledge (arcana) or Knowledge (history)

Professor Mangvhune: Knowledge (local) or Knowledge (planes)

Silver Ravens: Knowledge (local) or Knowledge (history). 

Usage

Select both a Focus and one of the 2 associated skills when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

Update the target of both Context notes on the changes tab of this trait to reflect the 2 Knowledge skills associated with your focus.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you can roll twice and take the better result.
Once per day, you can roll twice and take the better result.

sneaky swimmer (kobold).name: Sneaky Swimmer (Kobold)
sneaky swimmer (kobold).description:
Swimming comes naturally to you, as does hunting marsh vipers and other aquatic creatures.

Requirements: Kobold

Benefits: Increase the number of rounds you can hold your breath underwater by an amount equal to your Constitution score. You gain a +2 bonus on Stealth checks in swamps and in underwater environments.

contextNotes.text:
+[[2]] Trait bonus 
 in swamps and in underwater environments.
Increase the number of rounds you can hold your breath underwater by an amount equal to your Constitution score [[@abilities.con.total]]

isgeri orphan (isger).name: Isgeri Orphan (Isger)
isgeri orphan (isger).description:
You grew up in one of the many Asmodean orphanages that dot the Isgeri countryside. Whether or not you took to their teachings, your strict indoctrination at the hands of the devil nuns has toughened your body to punishment.

Requirements: Isger

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Fortitude saves.

relentless duty (kols).name: Relentless Duty (Kols)
relentless duty (kols).description:
You were raised with the mantra that fulfilling your duty must always come first. Now living up to a promise comes as easily as breathing.

Requirements: Kols

Benefits: Once per day, you can take 10 on a skill check attempted as part of fulfilling a prior promise even if you are distracted or in combat.

born free.name: Born Free
born free.description:
You were introduced to an individualistic belief as a youth. Thriving under the promise of control of your own destiny, you soon began to demonstrate the truth of this ideal. Though others may mock you for your unwillingness to go along with a group or accept the easy security and comfort promised by more ecclesiastical faiths, you know that the revelations and destiny you seek can only be brought about through your own actions and individual search for meaning.

Benefits: Once per day, after you roll a saving throw against a compulsion effect, you can choose to roll again as an immediate action before the result of the saving throw is announced. You must keep the new roll, even if it is worse.

contextNotes.text: Against a compulsion effect, you can choose to roll again as an immediate action before the result is announced. You must keep the new roll, even if it is worse. ([[@resources.bornFree.value]] remaining uses)

devotee of the green.name: Devotee of the Green
devotee of the green.description:
Your faith in the natural world or one of the gods of nature makes it easy for you to pick up on related concepts.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (geography) and Knowledge (nature) checks, and one of these skills (your choice) is always a class skill for you.

Usage: Choose which skill is a class skill and mark it in the Details tab.

alabaster outcast (shattered star).name: Alabaster Outcast (Shattered Star)
alabaster outcast (shattered star).description:
You are a member of one of Magnimar’s wealthiest families… or rather, you used to be. A falling-out left you disowned and disinherited. Now you hope to get away from the city and find fortune enough to shame your former kinsfolk—goals which led you to the Pathfinder Society.

Benefits: Despite your exile, your name still carries considerable weight in Magnimar, and you gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy and Intimidate checks made within the city. Additionally, you start play with a noble’s outfit, a signet ring, and a single additional nonmagical item worth no more than 200 gp. Your last name is probably Derexhi, Kaddren, Mindurian, Niroden, Scarnetti, Valdemar, Vanderale, or Versade (but may be another of your choosing; see Magnimar, City of Monuments).

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus within the city of Magnimar.
+[[1]] Trait bonus within the city of Magnimar.

snake bleeder (nagajor).name: Snake Bleeder (Nagajor)
snake bleeder (nagajor).description:
You were a nagaji vassal of knowledgeable nagas, and they taught you much regarding how they concoct their various poisons.

Requirements: Nagajor

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Craft (alchemy) checks when brewing poisons, and Craft (alchemy) becomes a class skill for you.

Usage: Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the Craft (alchemy) skill.

Update the target of the context note on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect Craft (alchemy).

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on Craft (alchemy) checks when brewing poisons.

savage breaker (varisia; korvosa).name: Savage Breaker (Varisia; Korvosa)
savage breaker (varisia; korvosa).description:
You openly despise the crude Shoanti and their backward ways.

Requirements: Korvosa in Varisia

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on combat maneuver checks made to sunder clubs, earth breakers, klars, hide armor, primitive armor and weapons (see Ultimate Combat), and any other equipment the GM deems “barbarian-made.”

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] trait bonus on checks made to sunder clubs, earth breakers, klars, hide armor, primitive armor and weapons (see Ultimate Combat), and any other equipment the GM deems “barbarian-made.”

cruel rager (half-orc).name: Cruel Rager (Half-Orc)
cruel rager (half-orc).description:
Requirements: Half-Orc

Benefits: If you successfully confirm a critical hit against an enemy while raging, you regain 1 round of rage. This can occur only once per rage.

contextNotes.text: If you successfully confirm a critical hit against an enemy while raging, you regain 1 round of rage. This can occur only once per rage.

tomb raider (scarab sages).name: Tomb Raider (Scarab Sages)
tomb raider (scarab sages).description:
You’ve spent most of your life exploring the ancient tombs and catacombs of Osirion.

Requirements: Scarab Sages

Benefits: You gain a +1 bonus on Perception and Knowledge (dungeoneering) checks, and one of these skills (your choice) becomes a class skill for you.

Usage: Choose either Perception or Knowledge (dungeoneering) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

weed resistance (kyonin).name: Weed Resistance (Kyonin)
weed resistance (kyonin).description:
You have learned how to keep your flesh out of the way of the toxic thorns and vines that are often prevalent in verdant lands.

Requirements: Kyonin

Benefits: You gain a +3 trait bonus on saving throws against poison and disease abilities and effects inflicted by creatures of the plant type (such as a basidirond’s spores).

contextNotes.text: +[[3]] Trait bonus against poison and disease abilities and effects inflicted by creatures of the plant type.

fiendish sniper (tiefling).name: Fiendish Sniper (Tiefling)
fiendish sniper (tiefling).description:
You instinctively hide after you shoot your opponents, waiting for an opportune moment to strike again.

Requirements: Tiefling

Benefits: You gain a +5 trait bonus on Stealth checks following a sniping attack.

contextNotes.text: +[[5]] Trait bonus following a sniping attack.

insular (elf).name: Insular (Elf)
insular (elf).description:
After living among non-elves for a time, you developed a dismissive attitude toward rash, shorter-lived races.

Requirements: Elf

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Will saving throws against charm, compulsion, and fear effects created by humanoids other than elves.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus vs charm, compulsion, and fear effects created by humanoids other than elves.

starlit hexer (any outer god).name: Starlit Hexer (any Outer God)
starlit hexer (any outer god).description:
Your spellcasting powers are infused with the powers of the occult Outer Gods.

Requirements: Any Outer God

Benefits: Whenever you cast a spell or spell-like ability with the curse descriptor, increase the DC of the spell’s saving throw by 1. If you cast such a spell or spell-like ability at night, as long as the sky is clear and the stars are visible, treat your caster level as 1 higher when determining the spell’s duration.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] to the save DC of any spell or spell-like ability with the curse descriptor you cast.
Treat your caster level as 1 higher when determining the spell’s duration of any spell or spell-like ability with the curse descriptor you cast as long as the sky is clear and the stars are visible.,

river freedom (hanspur).name: River Freedom (Hanspur)
river freedom (hanspur).description:
While touching flowing water you are more resistant to effects that affect your movement.

Requirements: Hanspur

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on all saves against effects that would hamper your movement.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against effects that would hamper your movement.

splash attack.name: Splash Attack
splash attack.description:
You catch others off-guard when a confrontation escalates.

Benefits: As long as you are standing in or adjacent to water, or holding at least a pint of liquid (including insoluble liquids like oil or alchemist’s fire), you can perform a dirty trick combat maneuver to blind a foe within reach without provoking an attack of opportunity. If you select the Improved Dirty Trick feat, you gain a +2 trait bonus on dirty trick combat maneuvers to blind foes as long as you are standing in or adjacent to water or holding a pint of liquid.

contextNotes.text: You do not provoke an attack of opportunity while performing Dirty Trick as long as you are standing in or adjacent to water, or holding at least a pint of liquid (including insoluble liquids like oil or alchemist’s fire).

+[[2]] Trait bonus on Dirty Trick combat manuevers to blind foes as long as you are standing in or adjacent to water or holding a pint of liquid if you have @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-feats.Item.JcCSN3DHARBBGHmZ]{Improved Dirty Trick} feat.

elven bitterness (kyonin).name: Elven Bitterness (Kyonin)
elven bitterness (kyonin).description:
Only the most cunning scouts of Kyonin are selected to serve as guards along the border between the elven nation and the dreaded Tanglebriar. Most who are sent to patrol those blighted reaches go with the aid of potent magical items and under the cloak of arcane disguise. You are either close with some who have guarded the elven homeland or know of the magical defenses used by the elves against their demonic foes.

Requirements: Kyonin

Benefits: When you cast an enchantment spell on a chaotic evil outsider, the DC of that spell is increased by 2.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] to the DC of  your enchantment spells  versus a chaotic evil outsider.

shiv (sczarni faction).name: Shiv (Sczarni Faction)
shiv (sczarni faction).description:
Sometimes smooth talking just doesn’t work.

Requirements: Sczarni Faction

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on damage rolls made with one-handed piercing or slashing weapons in a surprise round.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on damage rolls made with one-handed piercing or slashing weapons in a surprise round.

beast bond.name: Beast Bond
beast bond.description:
You share a close bond with animals.

Benefits: You gain a +1 bonus on Handle Animal checks and Ride checks. One of these skills (your choice) is always a class skill for you.

Usage: Add class skill flag in Details tab.

wanderlust.name: Wanderlust
wanderlust.description:
Your childhood was brightened by the new places you constantly saw as you traveled with your parents, who were merchants. Still excited by travel, you gain great energy when traveling overland.

Benefits: Treat your base land speed as 10 feet higher when determining your overland speed.

Suggested Characters

Desnans, halflings, Varisians.

keeper of the secret shame (elf).name: Keeper of the Secret Shame (Elf)
keeper of the secret shame (elf).description:
You are a member of the Lantern Bearers or an agent of the Winter Council, tasked with ensuring that knowledge of the elves’ secret shame—the drow—never reaches the surface.

Requirements: Elf

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Knowledge checks pertaining to drow and drow settlements.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus  on checks pertaining to drow and drow settlements.
+[[2]] Trait bonus  on checks pertaining to drow and drow settlements.
+[[2]] Trait bonus  on checks pertaining to drow and drow settlements.
+[[2]] Trait bonus  on checks pertaining to drow and drow settlements.
+[[2]] Trait bonus  on checks pertaining to drow and drow settlements.
+[[2]] Trait bonus  on checks pertaining to drow and drow settlements.
+[[2]] Trait bonus  on checks pertaining to drow and drow settlements.
+[[2]] Trait bonus  on checks pertaining to drow and drow settlements.
+[[2]] Trait bonus  on checks pertaining to drow and drow settlements.
+[[2]] Trait bonus  on checks pertaining to drow and drow settlements.

indelible ire.name: Indelible Ire
indelible ire.description:
When you are gravely wounded, you’re stirred to greater combat focus.

Benefits: Each time a critical hit is confirmed against you, you gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls for 1 round.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attack rolls for 1 round each time a critical hit is confirmed against you.

naturally gifted (gnome).name: Naturally Gifted (Gnome)
naturally gifted (gnome).description:
For unknown reasons, you have deeper reserves of magical energy than those around you. Perhaps you are descended from a family of powerful arcane casters, or maybe you believe this trait marks you as destined for magical greatness.

Requirements: Gnome
