Benefits: Whenever you are affected by a transmutation spell of the polymorph subschool, your new form is always in some way horrific, grotesque, or otherwise marked by the corruption of the Great Old Ones. While polymorphed, you gain a +2 trait bonus on Intimidate checks and increase the DC of spells you cast with the fear descriptor by 1. However, you also take a –2 penalty on Diplomacy checks while polymorphed.

contextNotes.text: -[[2]] Penalty while polymorphed.
+[[2]] Trait bonus while polymorphed.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to the DC of spells you cast with the fear descriptor.

honor-bound.name: Honor-Bound
honor-bound.description:
The family’s honor is tied to a shared goal binding you all together.

Benefits: With the aid of the GM, choose a task that your family is bound to accomplish. Once per day, when you are within 60 feet of another character with this trait, you can gain a +1 trait bonus on a single saving throw or skill check when progress toward the goal is directly at stake (at the GM’s discretion).

desperate resolve.name: Desperate Resolve
desperate resolve.description:
You are adept at casting spells even in the most precarious situations.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on concentration checks. This trait bonus increases to +4 when you are grappled, pinned, in violent weather, or entangled.

contextNotes.text: +[[3]] trait bonus when you are grappled, pinned, in violent weather, or entangled.

the bridge.name: The Bridge
the bridge.description:
The Bridge spans the rushing waters that separate the darkness of winter from the warmth of spring. 

Requirements: 12 Pharast - 18 Gozran

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against spells and effects with the cold or darkness descriptors.

contextNotes.text: +1 Trait bonus against spells and effects with the cold or darkness descriptors.

serpentine squeeze (ydersius).name: Serpentine Squeeze (Ydersius)
serpentine squeeze (ydersius).description:
Requirements: Ydersius

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on combat maneuver checks to grapple a foe, as well as a +1 trait bonus to your CMD whenever an opponent tries to grapple you.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to resist being grappled.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to grapple.

dragonslayer (dahak).name: Dragonslayer (Dahak)
dragonslayer (dahak).description:
You’re greatly skilled in hunting and slaying those who resemble your lord’s favored prey.

Requirements: Dahak

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls against creatures of the dragon type.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attack rolls against creatures of the dragon type.

regional recluse - arsmeril or crying le.name: Regional Recluse - Arsmeril or Crying Leaf (Varisia)
regional recluse - arsmeril or crying le.description:
Your home community prides itself on remaining independent from Varisia’s city-states.

Requirements: Varisia

Benefits: Choose one of Varisia’s unaffiliated settlements from this list below. You gain a +1 trait bonus on Survival checks and one of the following benefits.

Arsmeril or Crying Leaf

Gain a +1 trait bonus on Perception checks made at night.

Special Note

This is one of five versions of this trait. Other versions can be found here:

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.SUxxga9Juql4kY3J]{Regional Recluse - Ilsurian (Varisia)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.qDxSXompaz69Ceqj]{Regional Recluse - Sanos Forest or Whistledown (Varisia)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.EtUNYMr9ykH2XhZA]{Regional Recluse - Turtleback Ferry (Varisia)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.BDByEeeSK08bn8gp]{Regional Recluse - Urglin (Varisia)}

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on checks made at night.

careful combatant.name: Careful Combatant
careful combatant.description:
You have a strong sense of self-preservation, believing it is more important to safely extract yourself from a fight that has turned hopeless than to stubbornly stand your ground and risk death—for when you’re dead, you can’t protect the innocent.

Benefits: When using the Withdraw action, both the first and second squares of your movement are not considered threatened by any opponents you can see, rather than just the first square.

forbidden knowledge (geb).name: Forbidden Knowledge (Geb)
forbidden knowledge (geb).description:
As a child, you somehow got your hands on a lurid, forbidden text written by a now-forgotten Blood Lord of ages past. You studied the details within intently, learning much about extraplanar undead from beyond this reality, and you developed a strange, almost macabre way of thinking because of this book’s influence.

Requirements: Geb

Benefits: Anytime you would roll a Knowledge (planes) check, you may roll a Knowledge (history) or Knowledge (religion) check instead.

contextNotes.text: Anytime you would roll a Knowledge (planes) check, you may roll a Knowledge (history) or Knowledge (religion) check instead.

undead slayer (pharasma).name: Undead Slayer (Pharasma)
undead slayer (pharasma).description:
Instructed at a young age in the tenets of the faith of Pharasma, you view the undead as abominations that must be destroyed so their souls can journey beyond to be judged.

Requirements: Pharasma

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on weapon damage against undead.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on weapon damage against undead.

criminal.name: Criminal
criminal.description:
You spent your early life robbing and stealing to get by.

Benefits: Select one of the following skills: Disable Device, Intimidate, or Sleight of Hand. You gain a +1 trait bonus on that skill, and it is always a class skill for you.

Usage: Select Disable Device, Intimidate, or Sleight of Hand when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

good dreams (desna; halfling).name: Good Dreams (Desna; Halfling)
good dreams (desna; halfling).description:
Your dreams often give you a hint of possible dangers you might face during your day, such as a fall, a blast of fire, or a poisonous snake, and this hint sometimes makes all the difference.

Requirements: Desna; Halfling, [Can not have the @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-racial-traits.Item.D7JILr96IKHWN4vQ]{Halfling jinx} racial trait]

Benefits: Each time you sleep, the GM tells you of a dangerous dream you had.

Once per day, if you have to make a saving throw against a threat related or similar to that dream, you can roll twice and take the more favorable result.

If you do not encounter circumstances similar to the threat or situation you dreamed, this trait has no effect that day.

If you have the @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-racial-traits.Item.D7JILr96IKHWN4vQ]{Halfling jinx} trait, you cannot select this trait and vice versa.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, if you have to make a saving throw against a threat related, or similar, to your dangerous dream, you can roll twice and take the more favorable result.

friends and enemies (rise of the runelor.name: Friends and Enemies (Rise of the Runelords)
friends and enemies (rise of the runelor.description:
One of your family members, perhaps a parent, cousin, aunt, or uncle, helped Daviren Hosk put down a group of goblins near Sandpoint. Since then, your family member passed away, but not before telling you about that day and the offer Daviren made her should she ever be in need.

Benefits: Once you make it to Sandpoint and meet up with Daviren Hosk at the Goblin Squash Stables, he gives you one of his best steeds and all the necessary accoutrements as gratitude for your family member’s help: a @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-goods-services.Item.JZdn5vUoAMGkBC3h]{heavy combat trained horse}, a @UUID[Compendium.pf1.items.Item.CIAbIcLjLUC7FtYP]{military saddle}, @UUID[Compendium.pf1.items.Item.gW1bPqOvVfpmqEqd]{saddlebags}, @UUID[Compendium.pf1.items.Item.zkfnrahkeufabend]{bit and bridle}, a month’s worth of @UUID[Compendium.pf1.items.Item.hwrgbauvnnirgqdy]{feed}, and lifetime @UUID[Compendium.pf1.items.Item.mioexvfndsvhtkgn]{stabling} at the Goblin Squash Stables.

repel sin (tiefling; the motherless).name: Repel Sin (Tiefling; The Motherless)
repel sin (tiefling; the motherless).description:
You have an instinctive revulsion for the sins that led to the rise of demons and subsequent downfall of qlippoth.

Requirements: The Motherless Tiefling Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws made against spells and effects with the [evil] descriptor.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against spells and effects with the [evil] descriptor.

naturalist (yamasa).name: Naturalist (Yamasa)
naturalist (yamasa).description:
Your ancestors taught you the importance of using what nature provides.

Requirements: Yamasa

Benefits: You gain a +3 trait bonus on Survival checks when attempting to survive in the wilderness.

contextNotes.text: +[[3]] Trait bonus to survive in the wilderness.

honey-tongued (abadar).name: Honey-Tongued (Abadar)
honey-tongued (abadar).description:
You have a deep understanding of human nature and social philosophy.

Requirements: Abadar

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy or Bluff checks when dealing with agents of the law, whether judges, guards, or paladins.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus when dealing with agents of the law, whether judges, guards, or paladins.
+[[1]] Trait bonus when dealing with agents of the law, whether judges, guards, or paladins.

jungle opportunist (catfolk).name: Jungle Opportunist (Catfolk)
jungle opportunist (catfolk).description:
You trained with your clan to hunt large prey such as the dinosaurs of the Valashmai Jungle.

Requirements: Catfolk

Benefits: When you grant an ally a bonus on an attack roll against a foe of Large or larger size due to flanking or the aid another action, that ally gains a +1 trait bonus on weapon damage rolls against that creature as well.

contextNotes.text: When you grant an ally a bonus on an attack roll against a foe of Large or larger size due to flanking or the aid another action, that ally gains a +[[1]] Trait bonus on weapon damage rolls against that creature as well.

athletic champion (war for the crown).name: Athletic Champion (War for the Crown)
athletic champion (war for the crown).description:
Your physique and skill brought you into the public eye, and a winning smile helps you stay there. Taldor honors its extraordinary athletes, celebrating them as cultural heroes. Whether you were a gladiator, a runner, a wrestler, or any other competitor, your most recent victory caused someone important to sit up and take notice. You may never have a political career in front of you, but for now your name is helping to bring a little extra money in, and maybe that’s good enough.

Benefits: Select two of the following skills: Climb, Diplomacy, Perception, and Swim. You gain a +1 trait bonus on checks with those skills, and they are always class skills for you. In addition, you are accustomed to maneuvering through crowds; you gain a +2 trait bonus on checks to navigate through a crowd or resist being moved against your will, including spells and bull rush, drag, and reposition combat maneuvers.

Usage

Select two of: Climb, Diplomacy, Perception, or Swim when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect both of your choices.

Update the targets of the 2 changes on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choices.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on checks to navigate through a crowd or resist being moved against your will, including spells and bull rush, drag, and reposition combat maneuvers.
+[[2]] Trait bonus on checks to navigate through a crowd or resist being moved against your will, including spells and bull rush, drag, and reposition combat maneuvers.

acrobat.name: Acrobat
acrobat.description:
Having trained from a young age, you’re capable of amazing feats of daring.

Benefits: You gain a +1 bonus on Acrobatics checks, and you take only a –2 penalty instead of the normal –5 penalty when using the Climb skill to attempt an accelerated climb.

contextNotes.text: –[[2]] Penalty instead of the normal –[[5]] Penalty to attempt an accelerated climb

eldritch auditor.name: Eldritch Auditor
eldritch auditor.description:
You are a magically adept merchant who has learned tricks to protect your own goods, or you serve a city as an impartial auditor who guards against arcane trickery in the bazaars.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Spellcraft checks and Will saves to identify an item’s true properties, such as against spells like magic aura.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to identify an item’s true properties.
+[[2]] Trait bonus when identifying an item’s true properties.

altruistic diplomat (hwanggot).name: Altruistic Diplomat (Hwanggot)
altruistic diplomat (hwanggot).description:
Your upbringing in Hwanggot has influenced you to be both peaceful and wise, and your arguments are consistently sound.

Requirements: Hwanggot, Good Alignment

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks. You must be Lawful Good, Neutral Good, or Chaotic Good to take this trait.

free agent (river kingdoms).name: Free Agent (River Kingdoms)
free agent (river kingdoms).description:
You embody the independent spirit of the River Kingdoms and do best when sailing under your own flag, in every sense.

Requirements: River Kingdoms

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Will saves to avoid charm and compulsion effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to avoid charm and compulsion effects.

auspicious tattoo (human; shoanti).name: Auspicious Tattoo (Human; Shoanti)
auspicious tattoo (human; shoanti).description:
You bear a tattoo depicting one of the totems listed for your quah (tribe) that favors you with good fortune.

Requirements: Shoanti Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Will saving throws.

fires of hell (cheliax faction).name: Fires of Hell (Cheliax Faction)
fires of hell (cheliax faction).description:
Your parents signed a pact with a fiend of Hell before you were born, and as a result you can summon fire to your aid.

Requirements: Cheliax Faction

Benefits: Once per day, as a swift action, you can summon fire, imbuing a single weapon you hold with a nimbus of fire that deals an extra 1 point of fire damage for a number of rounds equal to your Charisma bonus. This fire gives off light equivalent to a torch. This is a supernatural ability.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, as a swift action, you can  imbue a single weapon you hold with a nimbus that deals an extra 1 point of fire damage for [[@abilities.cha.mod]] rounds. ([[@resources.firesOfHellCheliaxFaction.value]] remaining uses)

sensual graces (dhampir; svetocher).name: Sensual Graces (Dhampir; Svetocher)
sensual graces (dhampir; svetocher).description:
Your unearthly beauty enchants those who court you.

Requirements: Svetocher Dhampir Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Bluff checks made against humanoids who would be attracted to you.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against humanoids who would be attracted to you.

beast of the society (druid, pathfinder.name: Beast of the Society (Druid, Pathfinder Society)
beast of the society (druid, pathfinder.description:
A master druid at the Grand Lodge revealed to you greater secrets of concentration when changing your form into that of an animal.

Requirements: Druid, Pathfinder Society

Benefits: Whenever you use your wild shape class ability to turn yourself into a Small or Medium-sized animal, the effect lasts for 2 hours per druid level instead of 1 hour per druid level.

permanent scars.name: Permanent Scars
permanent scars.description:
You carry the physical and emotional scars of your Gray Maiden initiation as mental armor.

Benefits: You receive a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against emotion and pain spells and effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against emotion and pain spells and effects.

heavenly touch (tianjing).name: Heavenly Touch (Tianjing)
heavenly touch (tianjing).description:
Growing up, you were exposed to everyday miracles performed by your half-celestial friends, and some of this magic rubbed off.

Requirements: Tianjing

Benefits: At will, you can touch a dying creature to stabilize it as a standard action.

walls of half-truths (samsaran).name: Walls of Half-Truths (Samsaran)
walls of half-truths (samsaran).description:
As soon as you made contact with other samsarans, they encouraged your talent for magic that befuddles foes.

Requirements: Samsaran

Benefits: Treat your caster level as 1 higher for the purpose of determining duration of your figment and glamer spells.

contextNotes.text: Treat your caster level as 1 higher for the purpose of determining duration of your figment and glamer spells.

mistrusted (half-orc).name: Mistrusted (Half-Orc)
mistrusted (half-orc).description:
Requirements: Half-Orc

Benefits: Choose humans or orcs. You learned during your earliest interactions with that race that some automatically consider you a threat because of your hybrid nature, and they can’t be easily placated. Against foes of the chosen race, you gain a +1 trait bonus on attacks of opportunity and a +1 trait bonus to your CMD.

Usage: Select humans or orcs when you take this trait.

Update the changes on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus  on attacks of opportunity against foes of the chosen race <>.
+[[1]] Trait bonus  to your CMD against foes of the chosen race <>.

ancestor's blade (shizuru).name: Ancestor's Blade (Shizuru)
ancestor's blade (shizuru).description:
You were trained by your family with a weapon passed down from a venerable ancestor.

Requirements: Shizuru

Benefits: You begin play with an heirloom masterwork weapon worth up to 400 gp.

You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls with weapons of this type (not just your initial heirloom weapon) when you @UUID[Compendium.pf1.class-abilities.Item.2TsDfK0SKC8IGVx4]{smite evil}.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attack rolls with weapons that share the same type as the heirloom weapon you began with when you smite evil.

hurricane savvy (the shackles).name: Hurricane Savvy (The Shackles)
hurricane savvy (the shackles).description:
You are used to sailing in the terrible winds that roll out from the Eye of Abendego, and can apply the skills you’ve learned to any storm.

Requirements: The Shackles

Benefits: You treat wind effects as one wind force category less than they actually are, both for any personal checks you must make and for any you make to captain or steer a ship.

contextNotes.text: You treat wind effects as one wind force category less than they actually are.
You treat wind effects as one wind force category less than they actually are.

storm hunter (hongal).name: Storm Hunter (Hongal)
storm hunter (hongal).description:
You learned to hunt in the relentless winds of Hongal, and still make your arrows fly true.

Requirements: Hongal

Benefits: When fighting in windy weather, you treat the wind category as one level lower for the purposes of determining penalties on ranged attacks made with normal weapons.

contextNotes.text: You treat the wind category as one level lower for the purposes of determining penalties on ranged attacks made with normal weapons.

ex-iomedaean (hell's vengeance).name: Ex-Iomedaean (Hell's Vengeance)
ex-iomedaean (hell's vengeance).description:
You, or perhaps your family, were once worshipers of Iomedae, but something happened that made you lose your faith. Perhaps your family was accused of being dishonorable or cowardly, or maybe one of your family members was the victim of church “justice.” Or perhaps you simply met someone who opened your eyes and showed you the truth—that the Inheritor’s so-called honor, valor, and justice are just tin plating on a strict dogma that seeks to control people by restricting their free will. In any event, you left the church, and as a result, your family was subjected to scorn and holier-than-thou disapproval. Ever since, you’ve vowed to some day get revenge against the church of Iomedae—and with the current situation brewing in Cheliax, you might finally have the opportunity.

Benefits: Choose one:

Against followers of Iomedae, including many (but not all) archons and angels, you gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls and weapon damage rolls, or you gain a +1 trait bonus on the save DCs of your spells.

Usage: Select either (attack and weapon damage rolls) or spell save DCs when you take this trait.

Update the target of the note on the changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice (Attack Rolls or Spell Effect) and delete the change relating to the other choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attack rolls and weapon damage rolls against followers of Iomedae.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to the save DCs of your spells against followers of Iomedae.

arms master (tiefling; faultspawn).name: Arms Master (Tiefling; Faultspawn)
arms master (tiefling; faultspawn).description:
The legendary battle prowess of your ancestors ensures that you have a certain amount of innate martial skill.

Requirements: Faultspawn Tiefling Heritage

Benefits: You take a -2 penalty on attack rolls made with weapons with which you are not proficient instead of the normal -4.

contextNotes.text: -[[2]] non-proficiency penalty   instead of the normal -[[4]].

freedom fighter (andoran).name: Freedom Fighter (Andoran)
freedom fighter (andoran).description:
You’ve developed a reputation as a privateer who fights for freedom.

Requirements: Andoran

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks. This bonus becomes a –2 penalty when dealing with slavers or those who support the slave trade. Diplomacy becomes a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: When dealing with slavers,  or those that support the slave trade,  your normal +[[2]] bonus becomes a -[[2]] penalty (-4 total from the norm).

expert duelist (sovereign court).name: Expert Duelist (Sovereign Court)
expert duelist (sovereign court).description:
In your youth, you spent countless hours perfecting the art of the duel, focusing your efforts on defeating a single foe.

Requirements: Sovereign Court

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus to your Armor Class so long as you are adjacent to a single foe. This trait bonus is not applied to your Armor Class for touch attacks or when you are denied your Dexterity bonus.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus  to your Armor Class so long as you are adjacent to a single foe. This trait bonus is not applied to your Armor Class for touch attacks or when you are denied your Dexterity bonus.

proper training (grand lodge).name: Proper Training (Grand Lodge)
proper training (grand lodge).description:
Your time at the Grand Lodge of Absalom has served you well.

Requirements: Grand Lodge

Benefits: Choose either Knowledge (geography) or Knowledge (history). You gain a +1 trait bonus on all checks for the chosen skill, and the chosen skill becomes a class skill for you.

Usage: Choose either Knowledge (geography) or Knowledge (history) under Details > Class Skills and Changes

empty heart, full heart (naderi).name: Empty Heart, Full Heart (Naderi)
empty heart, full heart (naderi).description:
Because of the strength of your forbidden love you are more resistant to charms.

Requirements: Naderi

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on all saves against charm effects, and any targets of your own charm spells suffer a —1 on their saves.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus  against charm effects
Any targets of your own charm spells suffer a -[[1]] on their saves.

celestial contact (aasimar).name: Celestial Contact (Aasimar)
celestial contact (aasimar).description:
Witnessing a celestial intervention by your ancestor or a similar being inspired you with the power of good.

Requirements: Aasimar

Benefits: Treat your caster level as 1 higher when you cast spells with the good descriptor.

contextNotes.text: Treat your caster level as 1 higher when you cast spells with the good descriptor.

hedge magician.name: Hedge Magician
hedge magician.description:
You apprenticed for a time to a craftsman who often built magic items, and he taught you many handy shortcuts and cost-saving techniques.

Benefits: Whenever you craft a magic item, you reduce the cost of gp required to make the item by 5%.

contextNotes.text: 5% cost reduction when crafting magic items.

enemy of the undead (geb).name: Enemy of the Undead (Geb)
enemy of the undead (geb).description:
Your disgust at the way the undead aristocracy of Geb treats the living has inspired hatred in you.

Requirements: Geb

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against any spells or spell-like abilities from an undead creature.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against any spells or spell-like abilities from an undead creature.

warrior poet (lands of the linnorm kings.name: Warrior Poet (Lands of the Linnorm Kings)
warrior poet (lands of the linnorm kings.description:
You were trained by the skalds, keepers of the oral tradition of Kalsgard.

Requirements: Lands of the Linnorm Kings

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge checks related to dwarves (dwarven history, dwarven construction, and so on) and a +1 trait bonus on Fortitude saves in any round in which you use a bardic performance ability.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus in any round in which you use a bardic performance ability.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks related to dwarves.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks related to dwarves.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks related to dwarves.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks related to dwarves.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks related to dwarves.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks related to dwarves.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks related to dwarves.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks related to dwarves.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks related to dwarves.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks related to dwarves.

pure legion recruit (rahadoum).name: Pure Legion Recruit (Rahadoum)
pure legion recruit (rahadoum).description:
You have spent time among the famed Pure Legion and understand some of their techniques for spotting signs of religion and the faithful.

Requirements: Rahadoum

Benefits
You gain a +2 trait bonus on Perception checks to see through disguises and mundane attempts to conceal religious iconography or dress. You also gain a +2 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks to see through lies about a target’s religious beliefs and to understand secret messages relating to the gods.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to see through disguises and mundane attempts to conceal religious iconography or dress.
+[[2]] Trait bonus  to see through lies about a target’s religious beliefs and to understand secret messages relating to the gods.

outcast's intuition.name: Outcast's Intuition
outcast's intuition.description:
You are able to sense the motives of others and use that sense to bolster your magic against dispelling.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks, and Sense Motive is always a class skill for you. In addition, your caster level is treated as 1 level higher for the purposes of any attempts to dispel your magic.

contextNotes.text: Your caster level is treated as 1 level higher on any attempts to dispel your magic.

student of giantkind (giantslayer).name: Student of Giantkind (Giantslayer)
student of giantkind (giantslayer).description:
You’ve always been fascinated by the giant races, and have devoted a considerable amount of time to studying their history and societies, gaining insight into the way they think and use their abilities.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks against creatures with the giant subtype and a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (local) checks regarding creatures with the giant subtype, and one of these skills (your choice) becomes a class skill for you. In addition, you know the Giant language (this does not count toward your number of languages). 

Usage

Select Diplomacy or Knowledge (local) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against creatures with the giant subtype.
+[[1]] Trait bonus regarding creatures with the giant subtype,

dowsing (suli).name: Dowsing (Suli)
dowsing (suli).description:
You are sensitive to the presence of all elements, allowing you to find water, landmarks, and evidence of prior camps or settlements.

Requirements: Suli

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Survival checks. Whenever you succeed at a Survival check to follow tracks in desert terrain, you can always tell how long ago the tracks were made (though supernatural abilities and spells still impede this ability as appropriate).

contextNotes.text: Whenever you succeed at a Survival check to follow tracks in desert terrain, you can always tell how long ago the tracks were made (though supernatural abilities and spells still impede this ability as appropriate).

criminal roots.name: Criminal Roots
criminal roots.description:
Your family has long operated outside the law.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks to influence criminals and on Intimidate checks to influence law-abiding citizens, but take a -2 penalty on Diplomacy checks to influence law-abiding citizens. Diplomacy or Intimidate (your choice) is a class skill for you.

Usage: Select Diplomacy or Intimidate when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to influence criminals, but take a -[[2]] penalty to influence law-abiding citizens.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to influence criminals.

iadaran illusionist (kyonin).name: Iadaran Illusionist (Kyonin)
iadaran illusionist (kyonin).description:
You have lived in Iadara for so long that you are very familiar with illusions.

Requirements: Kyonin

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on caster level checks with illusion spells and a +1 trait bonus on Will saves to disbelieve illusions.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to disbelieve illusions.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on caster level checks with illusion spells.

unbalanced insight.name: Unbalanced Insight
unbalanced insight.description: Benefits: You gain Aklo as a bonus language, and gain a +2 trait bonus on all saving throws to resist any effects or spells originating from aberrations that cause confusion or fear.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus vs confusion or fear effects originating  from aberrations.

enduring stoicism (strange aeons).name: Enduring Stoicism (Strange Aeons)
enduring stoicism (strange aeons).description:
Even though your pulse is pounding in your ears when you awake in the asylum, you feel strangely nonplussed. You can recognize that the situation you find yourself in is horrific, but you refuse to let that fear control you. It doesn’t feel like it’s worth worrying over too much. You know that when you face unspeakable forces, keeping your cool will keep you alive.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on all saving throws against spells or effects with the fear or emotion descriptors. In addition, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Intimidate checks due to your intimidating presence, as people don’t know what to expect from you.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against spells or effects with the fear or emotion descriptors.

impressive presence (sovereign court).name: Impressive Presence (Sovereign Court)
impressive presence (sovereign court).description:
Your grandiose posturing often makes it difficult for anyone to concentrate around you.

Requirements: Sovereign Court

Benefits: Once per day as a full-round action, you may attempt to distract adjacent foes with a lengthy display of your martial prowess. All adjacent foes must succeed at a Will save (DC 10 + 1/2 your level + your Charisma modifier) or gain the shaken condition. This condition persists for 1 round.

for the money.name: For the Money
for the money.description:
Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on skill checks to haggle prices or negotiate payment; this bonus increases to +4 if you are in a position of military or economic power over your target.

You also gain a +1 trait bonus on Appraise checks and Appraise is always a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to haggle prices or negotiate payment; this bonus increases to +[[4]] if you are in a position of military or economic power over your target.

strength of body (skinwalker; wereboar-k.name: Strength of Body (Skinwalker; Wereboar-Kin)
strength of body (skinwalker; wereboar-k.description:
You took longer than most to learn control over your transforming abilities, but in that time you learned a trick to make the most of your strength.

Requirements: Wereboar-Kin Skinwalker Heritage

Benefits: Once per day, you can use your Strength modifier in place of your Constitution modifier on a single Fortitude saving throw. You must announce that you are using this ability before you roll the saving throw.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you can use your Strength modifier in place of your Constitution modifier on a single Fortitude saving throw. You must announce that you are using this ability before you roll the saving throw.

ecumenical.name: Ecumenical
ecumenical.description:
Your childhood in Magnimar (or in a similar cosmopolitan city) taught you to recognize signs of worship of demigods such as empyreal lords.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Knowledge (religion) checks involving entities other than the most influential gods. (see page 43 of the Pathfinder RPG Core Rulebook)

You also gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks to influence worshipers of such entities.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus involving entities other than the most influential [core] gods.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to influence worshipers of entities other than the most influential [core] gods.

perfectionist's brew.name: Perfectionist's Brew
perfectionist's brew.description:
You know that potion recipes should be followed with exact precision.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on all Craft (alchemy) checks and on Spellcraft checks made to brew potions.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on checks made to brew potions.
+[[2]] Trait bonus on checks made to brew potions.

artifact hunter (giantslayer).name: Artifact Hunter (Giantslayer)
artifact hunter (giantslayer).description:
You’ve always had an interest in magic, but you’re fascinated with those legendary magic items known as artifacts. You’ve spent years researching these mythical relics and committed the stories and legends about them to memory, making you something of an expert on the subject.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Spellcraft checks to identify the properties of magic items and a +1 trait bonus on Use Magic Device checks, and one of these skills (your choice) becomes a class skill for you. In addition, whenever you first encounter an artifact, there is a 50% chance (+1% per level) that you recognize the artifact and know its name, origin, and something of that artifact’s powers, abilities, or dangers. Exactly how much knowledge you possess is left to the GM’s discretion.

Usage: Select Spellcraft or Use Magic Device when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to identify the properties of magic items. In addition whenever you first encounter an artifact, there is a 50% chance (+1% per level) that you recognize the artifact and know its name, origin, and something of that artifact’s powers, abilities, or dangers. Exactly how much knowledge you possess is left to the GM’s discretion.

blind zeal (vildeis).name: Blind Zeal (Vildeis)
blind zeal (vildeis).description:
You are blind but trained by followers of Vildeis to sense your surroundings by sound and touch.

Requirements: Vildeis

Benefits: You need not attempt a skill check to move at full speed. You take a –4 penalty from blindness on only Acrobatics, Ride, Sleight of Hand, and Stealth checks. You gain Blind-Fight as a bonus feat. You lose the benefits of this trait whenever you can see, but regain them if you once again become blind.

contextNotes.text: Ignore the -[[4]] penalty when blind.
Ignore the -[[4]] penalty when blind.
Ignore the -[[4]] penalty when blind.
Ignore the -[[4]] penalty when blind.
Ignore the -[[4]] penalty when blind.

desperate focus.name: Desperate Focus
desperate focus.description:
You’ve often found yourself in situations where a lack of focus can lead to worse than a lost spell.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on concentration checks.

elemental resilience (any elemental lord.name: Elemental Resilience (any elemental lord)
elemental resilience (any elemental lord.description:
Requirements: Any Elemental Lord

Benefits: Choose one of the following energy types: acid, cold, electricity, or fire. You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against spells with that descriptor. Once the energy type is chosen, it cannot be changed. 

Usage: Select one of permitted energy types: <acid, cold, electricity, or fire> when you take this trait. Update the target of the note on the changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against spells with your chosen <acid, cold, electricity, or fire> descriptor .

soul eater (tiefling; grimspawn).name: Soul Eater (Tiefling; Grimspawn)
soul eater (tiefling; grimspawn).description:
The act of murder allows you to draw vitality from fleeing souls.

Requirements: Grimspawn Tiefling Heritage

Benefits: Whenever you use a coup de grace action to kill a creature (either by damage or through a failed Fortitude save), you gain a number of temporary hit points equal to half your character level (minimum 1) for 1 minute. These temporary hit points do not stack with multiple coups de grace.

contextNotes.text: Whenever you use a coup de grace action to kill a creature, you gain  [[min(1, floor(@attributes.hd.total /2))]] temporary hit points for 1 minute. These temporary hit points do not stack with multiple coups de grace.

oregent desperation (andoran; oregent).name: Oregent Desperation (Andoran; Oregent)
oregent desperation (andoran; oregent).description:
Your instinct to survive easily overpowers your intellect, and you consider this a more than fair trade.

Requirements: Oregent in Andoran

Benefits: Once per day you can improve your condition from dying to disabled by taking 4 points of Intelligence damage.

veteran of battle (gorum).name: Veteran of Battle (Gorum)
veteran of battle (gorum).description:
You have fought in several battles, and each time felt the presence of Gorum guiding your sword-arm, making you ready to act at a moment's notice.

Requirements: Gorum

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Initiative checks and, if you are able to act during a surprise round, you may a draw a weapon (but not a potion or magic item) as a free action during that round.

contextNotes.text: If you are able to act during a surprise round, you may a draw a weapon (but not a potion or magic item) as a free action during that round.

dirty trickster.name: Dirty Trickster
dirty trickster.description:
You learned early that there’s no point in fighting fair when you’re fighting for your life. Others may look down their noses at your tactics, but you know you wouldn’t have survived this long without breaking a few conventions of fair play.

Benefits: You can take the @UUID[Compendium.pf1.feats.Item.LUOETW7Fm0oPKKWj]{Improved Dirty Trick}} feat without meeting its prerequisites, and you gain a +1 trait bonus on combat maneuver checks to attempt a dirty trick.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to attempt a dirty trick

aballonian resilience (aballon).name: Aballonian Resilience (Aballon)
aballonian resilience (aballon).description:
You spent at least part of your youth in Horsethroat and are acclimated to the frigid cold of Aballon’s Ice Wells.

Requirements: Aballon

Benefits: You gain a +4 trait bonus on saves to avoid nonlethal damage from cold environments. In addition, you begin play with a @UUID[Compendium.pf1.items.Item.RfQvOISVKf3WU8Nl]{ Cold-Weather Outfit} at no cost.

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Trait bonus to avoid nonlethal damage from cold environments.

elemental accident.name: Elemental Accident
elemental accident.description:
A traumatic experience in your childhood, such as surviving a violent earthquake, fleeing a shipwreck, escaping a devastating fire, or getting struck by lightning, caused you to develop a fear of the corresponding element. You can instinctively shield yourself against elemental dangers of that type.

Benefits: Select acid, cold, electricity, or fire at character creation (once chosen, this damage type can’t be changed). Once per day when you are subjected to an attack or effect that deals energy damage of that type, you can gain resistance 10 against that damage type for 1 round as an immediate action. This resistance does not stack with any existing resistance you might have against that energy type.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, +[[10]] resistance against the chosen energy type.

iron mind (hellknight).name: Iron Mind (Hellknight)
iron mind (hellknight).description:
Requirements: Hellknight

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against spells and effects that would reduce your Intelligence score or cause you to forget something.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against spells and effects that would reduce your Intelligence score or cause you to forget something.

rage of storms (half-orc, orc).name: Rage of Storms (Half-Orc, Orc)
rage of storms (half-orc, orc).description:
Summoned creatures can inherit your ferocity.

Requirements: Half-Orc, Orc

Benefits: Once per day, a creature you summon can have the @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-universal-monster-rules.Item.itiLDlB5BHlUB8os]{ferocity} ability.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, a creature you summon can have the @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-universal-monster-rules.Item.itiLDlB5BHlUB8os]{ferocity} ability.

varisian tattoo (human; varisian).name: Varisian Tattoo (Human; Varisian)
varisian tattoo (human; varisian).description:
You bear the elaborate tattoos of your people, marking you as a free son or daughter of the road.

Requirements: Varisian Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against charm and compulsion effects. Additionally, you are proficient with bladed scarves and starknives.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against charm and compulsion effects.

courageous.name: Courageous
courageous.description:
Your childhood was brutal, yet you persevered primarily through force of will and the hope that no matter how hard things might get, as long as you kept a level head you'd make it through.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Saving Throws against fear effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait vs fear effects

rich parents.name: Rich Parents
rich parents.description:
You were born into a rich family, perhaps even the nobility, and even though you turned to a life of adventure anyway, you enjoyed a one-time benefit to your initial finances.

Benefits: Your starting wealth increases to 900 gp.

scion of the shory (mwangi expanse).name: Scion of the Shory (Mwangi Expanse)
scion of the shory (mwangi expanse).description:
The blood of a Shory aeromancer flows through your veins.

Requirements: Mwangi Expanse

Benefits: You gain a +2 bonus on concentration checks when casting flight spells such as @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.plou8h168bfn5hq6]{levitate}, @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.7d6sv5ecvi7kho3m]{fly}, or @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.wqvy12w1xgk6l9b0]{overland flight}. Once per day, you can use @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.msaqovkgfxi6q8s8]{message} as a spell-like ability.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] bonus on concentration checks when casting flight spells.

broken mind (groetus).name: Broken Mind (Groetus)
broken mind (groetus).description:
You are used to living with your own madness,

Requirements: Groetus

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saves against madness and confusion effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against madness and confusion effects.

inspired.name: Inspired
inspired.description:
A positive force, philosophy, or divine presence fills you with hope, and is a guiding force of inspiration.

Benefits: Once per day as a free action, roll twice and take the better result on a skill check or ability check.

contextNotes.text: Once per day as a free action, roll twice and take the better result.
Once per day as a free action, roll twice and take the better result

regional recluse - ilsurian (varisia).name: Regional Recluse - Ilsurian (Varisia)
regional recluse - ilsurian (varisia).description:
Your home community prides itself on remaining independent from Varisia’s city-states.

Requirements

Varisia

Benefits

Choose one of Varisia’s unaffiliated settlements from this list below. You gain a +1 trait bonus on Survival checks and one of the following benefits.

Ilsurian

Gain proficiency with light shields.

Special Note

This is one of five versions of this trait. Other versions can be found here:

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.QezjzIjvbfIxTLPD]{Regional Recluse - Arsmeril or Crying Leaf (Varisia)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.qDxSXompaz69Ceqj]{Regional Recluse - Sanos Forest or Whistledown (Varisia)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.EtUNYMr9ykH2XhZA]{Regional Recluse - Turtleback Ferry (Varisia)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.BDByEeeSK08bn8gp]{Regional Recluse - Urglin (Varisia)}

voice of velvet (human; vudrani).name: Voice of Velvet (Human; Vudrani)
voice of velvet (human; vudrani).description:
You have the ability to sing a song and spin a story that captures the hearts of those around you.

Requirements: Vudrani Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy and Perform (oratory) checks.

Usage

Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the Perform (oratory) skill.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect Perform (oratory).

steady strength (trudd).name: Steady Strength (Trudd)
steady strength (trudd).description:
Your strength training conditioned you to wearing heavy armor.

Requirements: Trudd

Benefits: When you wear medium or heavy armor, your armor check penalty on Strength-based skills is reduced by 2.

contextNotes.text: When you wear medium or heavy armor, your armor check penalty on Strength-based skills is reduced by 2.

sacred orienteer (elion).name: Sacred Orienteer (Elion)
sacred orienteer (elion).description:
You have traveled far with only your compass and faith as your guides.

Requirements: Elion

Benefits: You begin play with a @UUID[Compendium.pf1.items.Item.hhprblxrbmqvoine]{compass}, and any compass or wayfinder in your possession also functions as a holy symbol of Elion.

Either Knowledge (geography) or Survival becomes a class skill for you.

Once per day you can consult your compass or wayfinder as a full round action to gain a +2 trait bonus on one Knowledge (geography) or Survival check.

Usage: Select Knowledge (geography) or Survival when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus,  gained no more than once per day, when you  consult your compass or wayfinder as a full round action.
+[[2]] Trait bonus,  gained no more than once per day, when you  consult your compass or wayfinder as a full round action.

skeptic.name: Skeptic
skeptic.description:
Growing up, you were always around magical effects to the extent that you realized that much of it was smoke and mirrors.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on all saving throws against illusions.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against illusions.

flame of the dawnflower (sarenrae).name: Flame of the Dawnflower (Sarenrae)
flame of the dawnflower (sarenrae).description:
You have been raised to view yourself as a blade in Sarenrae's service, or you have taken that duty on for yourself.

Requirements: Sarenrae

Benefits: Whenever you score a critical hit with a scimitar, you deal an additional 2 points of fire damage to your target.

contextNotes.text: Whenever you score a critical hit with a scimitar, you deal an additional [[2]] points of fire damage to your target.

house of green mothers pupil.name: House of Green Mothers Pupil
house of green mothers pupil.description:
You studied at Anuli’s center for druidic magic long enough to begin bonding with a familiar.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Handle Animal checks. You may substitute this trait for Iron Will as the prerequisite for the Familiar Bond feat.

Usage: If you choose to substitute the trait bonus which is added by default with this trait, remove the trait bonus from the changes tab in this trait.

alchemical intuition.name: Alchemical Intuition
alchemical intuition.description:
You are often struck with epiphanies about alchemical processes and substances.

Benefits: Once per day as a free action, you may gain a trait bonus equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum 0) on a Craft (alchemy) check you make. You can apply this bonus after you roll the check, possibly turning a failure into a success.

contextNotes.text: +[[max(@abilities.cha.mod, 0)]] trait bonus on one craft (alchemy) check you make per day.

bred for war (human; shoanti).name: Bred for War (Human; Shoanti)
bred for war (human; shoanti).description:
You tower above most other humans and possess a physique of hard, corded muscle.

Requirements: Shoanti Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Intimidate checks and a +1 trait bonus on your CMB because of your great size. You must be at least 6 feet tall.

reviving rest (skinwalker; werebear-kin).name: Reviving Rest (Skinwalker; Werebear-Kin)
reviving rest (skinwalker; werebear-kin).description:
When you first demonstrated your werebear ancestry, you slept for a week and awoke feeling more refreshed than ever before.

Requirements: Werebear-Kin Skinwalker Heritage

Benefits: Whenever you undergo complete bed rest for 24 hours, you recover an amount of hit points equal to three times your character level.

illuminator (sarenrae).name: Illuminator (Sarenrae)
illuminator (sarenrae).description:
You speak unusually well when you are filled with the light of Sarenrae.

Requirements: Sarenrae

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks, and Diplomacy is always a class skill for you.

erutaki sky reader (crown of the world).name: Erutaki Sky Reader (Crown of the World)
erutaki sky reader (crown of the world).description:
Born in the Crown of the World, you learned to read the aurora and the night sky for portents.

Requirements: Crown of the World

Benefits: Once per day, you gain a +2 luck bonus on a single saving throw. You can apply this bonus after the saving throw is rolled but before the results are revealed.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you gain a +[[2]] Luck bonus on a single saving throw. You can apply this bonus after the saving throw is rolled but before the results are revealed.

honeyed tongue (amanandar).name: Honeyed Tongue (Amanandar)
honeyed tongue (amanandar).description:
Having matured in the melting pot of New Oppara, you know the customs of the Tian-Shus as well as those of the Taldans, and you utilize this knowledge to create peace between rival groups.

Requirements: Amanandar

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks, and Diplomacy becomes a class skill for you.

flounderer (zogmugot).name: Flounderer (Zogmugot)
flounderer (zogmugot).description:
source: Goblins of Golarion pg. 16
You are adept at swimming and holding your breath.

Requirements: Goblin; Zogmugot

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Swim checks and can hold your breath for a number of rounds equal to three times your Constitution score before you risk drowning.

contextNotes.text: You can hold your breath for a number of rounds equal to three times your Constitution score before you risk drowning.
You can hold your breath for a number of rounds equal to three times your Constitution score before you risk drowning.

well-informed (halfling).name: Well-Informed (Halfling)
well-informed (halfling).description:
You make it a point to know everyone and to be connected to everything around you. You frequent the best taverns, attend all of the right events, and graciously help anyone who needs it.

Requirements: Halfling

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks to gather information and Knowledge (local) checks. One of these skills (your choice) is always a class skill for you.

Usage

Select Diplomacy or Knowledge (local) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to gather information.

dump salvager (westcrown).name: Dump Salvager (Westcrown)
dump salvager (westcrown).description:
You grew up in or around your city's rubbish-strewn shores and spent your spare time digging through trash looking for things worth selling.

Requirements: Westcrown

Benefits: You gain a +3 trait bonus on Perception checks to search for concealed items.

contextNotes.text: +[[3]] Trait bonus  to search for concealed items.

avid reader.name: Avid Reader
avid reader.description:
As a youth, you voraciously consumed books and scrolls provided by a member of an adventurer’s guild or a learned organization like the Pathfinder Society, and you have internalized these stories of bold adventurers

Benefits: Choose one Knowledge skill. You can always choose to take 10 on checks with the chosen Knowledge skill, even when distracted or threatened.

Suggested Characters

Osirians, Pathfinders, Taldans

purity of faith (iomedae).name: Purity of Faith (Iomedae)
purity of faith (iomedae).description:
Your soul is free from impurity, and you are deeply committed to fulfilling your duties to the church.

Requirements: Iomedae

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Will saving throws and a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against spells and effects originating from an outsider with the evil subtype.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against spells and effects originating from an outsider with the evil subtype.

river navigator (river kingdoms).name: River Navigator (River Kingdoms)
river navigator (river kingdoms).description:
You grew up in the River Kingdoms and are familiar with the many winding streams that cross the land.

Requirements: River Kingdoms

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Survival checks or Profession (sailor) checks dealing with lakes, rivers, and their immediate surroundings, and one of these skills becomes a class skill for you. 

Usage: Select Survival or Profession (sailor) when you take this trait.

Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the Profession (sailor) skill.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

Update the target of the Context Note on the changes tab of this trait to Profession (sailor).

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on checks dealing with lakes, rivers, and their immediate surroundings
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks dealing with lakes, rivers, and their immediate surroundings

bekyar slave maestro (human; bekyar or s.name: Bekyar Slave Maestro (Human; Bekyar or Shoanti)
bekyar slave maestro (human; bekyar or s.description:
You spent years using your magic to bend the will of captured slaves to suit your whims.

Requirements: Bekyar Human Heritage or Shoanti Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy and Intimidate checks against anyone you have held captive for at least 24 hours.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on checks against anyone you have held captive for at least 24 hours.
+[[2]] Trait bonus on checks against anyone you have held captive for at least 24 hours.

weatherworn (vampire; moroi).name: Weatherworn (Vampire; Moroi)
weatherworn (vampire; moroi).description:
Lairing in desolate mountains or on coastal cliff tops has taught you to deal with the effects of strong winds.

Requirements: Moroi Vampire Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus against the effects of spells that create wind or move air, such as @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.cnuin981hdq7ryit]{gust of wind}. You treat natural weather conditions as one step weaker. These abilities also apply when you are in gaseous form.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against the effects of spells that create wind or move air.

indomitable faith.name: Indomitable Faith
indomitable faith.description:
You were born in a region where your faith was not popular, yet you never abandoned it. Your constant struggle to maintain your own faith has bolstered your drive.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Will saves.

snake handler (osirion; apep or wadjet).name: Snake Handler (Osirion; Apep or Wadjet)
snake handler (osirion; apep or wadjet).description:
You have spent time as a member of a cult of either Apep or Wadjet, the serpent gods of Osirion. Membership involved the handling of dangerous snakes during rituals, and you have learned how to keep yourself and others safe around them.

Requirements: Osirion native, worshiper of Apep or Wadjet

Benefits: You gain a +2 bonus on Fortitude saves against poison. Handle Animal is always a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against poison.

zealot (gnome).name: Zealot (Gnome)
zealot (gnome).description:
You aren’t just faithful to the worship of your patron deity—you are obsessively loyal to all of the faith’s tenets and guidelines. Your devotion is an anchor that keeps you from drifting away into the throes of the Bleaching, and you cling to it like the life raft it is.

Requirements: Gnome, alignment must match chosen deity exactly

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (religion) checks, and that skill is a class skill for you.

As long as you remain completely faithful to the tenets of your faith (as determined by the GM), you never suffer from the Bleaching.

adventurous imbiber (cayden cailean).name: Adventurous Imbiber (Cayden Cailean)
adventurous imbiber (cayden cailean).description:
You’ve never been afraid of good, strong drink, and that has prepared you for the perils of more dangerous substances.

Requirements: Cayden Cailean

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against poison.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on saving throws against poison.

unidentifiable appeal (dhampir; ajibacha.name: Unidentifiable Appeal (Dhampir; Ajibachana)
unidentifiable appeal (dhampir; ajibacha.description:
The unnatural symmetry of your face gives you an attractiveness that is at once captivating but hard to quantify.

Requirements: Ajibachana Dhampir Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Disguise checks and a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks made to influence those who would be attracted to you.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to influence those who would be attracted to you.

pillager.name: Pillager
pillager.description:
Growing up in a place where authorities, if there were any, rarely enforced property laws, you learned quickly that you have only what you can hold—but you gain whatever you can take.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on combat maneuver checks to perform disarm and steal combat maneuvers.

Suggested Characters

Halflings, River Kingdoms natives, Shackles natives

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to perform disarm and steal combat maneuvers

child of kintargo (hell's rebels).name: Child of Kintargo (Hell's Rebels)
child of kintargo (hell's rebels).description:
You had the fortune (or perhaps the misfortune, depending on your viewpoint) to be born into one of Kintargo’s noble families. Your experience growing up among the city’s well-to-do has given you an upper hand when it comes to knowledge of high society, and you start the game with a modest inheritance. With the new situation in Kintargo brewing, there is much concern about an eventual restructuring of the city’s nobility. Already, one noble estate has burned to the ground under what can best be described as suspicious circumstances, but whether the government or rebels were responsible depends on whom you ask.

Requirements: With this trait, the assumption is that you belong to a minor noble family (and can make up your family name). In this case, your family keeps a small manor in the Greens. If you want to be a member of one of Kintargo’s major noble families, you must take the Noble Scion feat at 1st level.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (nobility) checks, and Knowledge (nobility) is always a class skill for you. The Noble Scion feat (see the sidebar on page 11) does not have a Charisma prerequisite for you. In addition, you start play with a noble’s outfit, a signet ring, and a single additional nonmagical item worth no more than 200 gp. If you take the Noble Scion feat, your last name is probably Aulamaxa, Aulorian, Delronge, Jarvis, Jhaltero, Sarini, Tanessen, or Vashnarstill; if you’re not human, you were adopted into the family. If you don’t take this feat, you can make up your last name.

nightstalls escapee (katapesh; ifrit).name: Nightstalls Escapee (Katapesh; Ifrit)
nightstalls escapee (katapesh; ifrit).description:
The slavers of Katapesh were determined to sell you as living property, but you had other plans. You know not whether your slavers hunt you still, but you are careful about whom you trust and you always sleep with one eye open.

Requirements: Ifrit native to Katapesh

Benefits: You need to sleep only 4 hours to get a full night’s rest (restoring 1 hit point per character level). You do not receive extra benefits for sleeping additional hours, and you must still rest for 24 hours to receive the benefits of complete bed rest.

escaped captive.name: Escaped Captive
escaped captive.description:
Whether you were on your way to becoming a drow slave or slated for the ghoul chattel pits, you very narrowly avoided a life of miserable captivity.

Benefits: You make a habit of avoiding restraints or tight spaces, gaining a +1 trait bonus on Escape Artist checks and a +2 trait bonus to CMD to avoid being pinned.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to avoid being pinned.

faith unshakable and unassailable.name: Faith Unshakable and Unassailable
faith unshakable and unassailable.description:
Your dedication to your ideals is stronger than steel.

Exemplar Type: Faith Exemplar

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Will saves against charm, compulsion, and fear effects. For every two other faith traits you have, this bonus increases by 1.

Exemplar Traits: A character can have only one exemplar trait, and selecting an exemplar trait takes the place of two regular traits. Each exemplar trait is tied to a trait category, such as combat traits or regional traits; a character with an exemplar trait is no longer restricted to a single trait of that category and can select any number of such traits when gaining further traits. In addition, a character with an exemplar trait can select the Additional Traits feat any number of times and use the feat to gain an exemplar trait of a valid trait category for which they qualify.

Usage: Increase bonus in changes tab by one for every two faith traits you have.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus  against charm, compulsion, and fear effects. For every two other faith traits you have, this bonus increases by 1.

lovesick.name: Lovesick
lovesick.description:
Your love for another occupies your every thought, and you become despondent when you are away from that person.

Effects: A person is chosen by your GM (with consideration to your roll on the Romantic Relationships table below if you used the background generator). At the start of each day, if you are away from this person you take a –2 penalty on initiative checks and Perception checks. If you ever lose or fall out of love with this person, exchange this drawback for the Doubt drawback.

Romantic Relationships

d20

RESULT

1–2

One Significant Relationship: You had a true love once, but that time has passed.

3–6

A Few Significant Relationships: You’ve tried to make deep connections with individuals on several occasions, but it’s never worked out.

7–9

Several Significant Relationships: You’ve engaged in a number of partnerships, but for some reason or another your relationships always fail.

10–12

Current Lover: You are currently involved in a romantic relationship. You gain access to the True Love story feat.

13–16

Several Inconsequential Relationships: You have had many lovers but no long-lasting, meaningful relationships.

17–18

Experience But No Substantial Relationships: You’ve had a fling or two, but have so far shied away from any ties or commitments.

19–20

No Experience: You have never experienced any kind of romantic connection whatsoever.

contextNotes.text: -[[2] penalty if, at the start of each day, if you are away from your focus person. (GM choice)
-[[2] penalty if, at the start of each day, if you are away from your focus person. (GM choice)

call for help (chaldira zuzaristan).name: Call for Help (Chaldira Zuzaristan)
call for help (chaldira zuzaristan).description:
Offering your devotion to and modeling your life on Desna's impetuous companion means you frequently find yourself in a jam but also ensures that you can always rely upon your friends to bail you out.

Requirements: Chaldira Zuzaristan

Benefits: When you take the total defense action, allies gain a +1 trait bonus on rolls made against a creature threatening you.

contextNotes.text: When using the total defense action, allies gain a +[[1]] trait bonus on rolls made against a creature threatening you.

trickster (vanara).name: Trickster (Vanara)
trickster (vanara).description:
You learned early that in order to get what you want, sometimes you have to act secretly.

Requirements: Vanara

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Bluff and Stealth checks, and one of these skills (your choice) is always a class skill for you.

Usage

Select Bluff or Stealth when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

chivalrous (taldor).name: Chivalrous (Taldor)
chivalrous (taldor).description:
You were raised on tales of heroic knights and benevolent wizards from Taldor's Golden Age, and try to emulate their great deeds.

Requirements: Taldor

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy and Knowledge (history) checks.

jungle diplomat (valashmai jungle).name: Jungle Diplomat (Valashmai Jungle)
jungle diplomat (valashmai jungle).description:
You know the ways and mores of the tribes of the Valashmai Jungle, and how to handle delicate communications with different groups of people.

Requirements: Valashmai Jungle

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy and Sense Motive checks.

loyal bond (grundinnar).name: Loyal Bond (Grundinnar)
loyal bond (grundinnar).description:
You are at your best when you serve others.

Requirements: Grundinnar

Benefits: When you cast spells that offer protection to an ally at your expense (such as shield other) or spells whose duration is divided when shared among many (such as water breathing or any of the communal spells), you do so at +1 caster level.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] caster level on spells that protect an ally at your expense  or whose duration is divided when shared among many.

flames of hell (any archdevil).name: Flames of Hell (Any Archdevil)
flames of hell (any archdevil).description:
Your bond with the archdevil you worship strengthens your ability to channel powers of the divine.

Requirements: Any Archdevil

Benefits: Add 1 to the DC of saving throws made to resist the effects of your channel energy ability.

Usage: Update your actor's Channel Energy Feature.

On the details tab of that feature, edit the Use.

On the Action tab of the use, add 1 to the dc formula.

stargazer (lirgen).name: Stargazer (Lirgen)
stargazer (lirgen).description:
Requirements: Lirgen

Benefits: Having spent your youth staring at the night sky, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (arcana) and Knowledge (nature) checks. One of these skills becomes a class skill for you.

Usage: Select Knowledge (arcana) or Knowledge (nature) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

ordinary.name: Ordinary
ordinary.description:
The only thing extraordinary about your appearance is its ordinariness. You carry yourself in an understated way, and many people who see your face soon forget it.

Benefits: You gain a +4 trait bonus on Stealth checks whenever you attempt to hide in a crowd.

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Trait bonus to hide in a crowd.

ear for music (shelyn).name: Ear for Music (Shelyn)
ear for music (shelyn).description:
You spent countless hours of your youth in one of Shelyn's temples, listening to wonderful musicians and singers.

Requirements: Shelyn

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on one category of Perform checks and a +2 trait bonus on any Knowledge (local) checks that deal with the local art or music scene. 

Usage: Select one category of Performance when you take this trait. Update skills section of your actor to add the performance type chosen. Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to checks that deal with the local art or music scene.

gifted adept.name: Gifted Adept
gifted adept.description:
Your interest in magic was inspired by witnessing a spell being cast in a particularly dramatic method, perhaps even one that affected you physically or spiritually. This early exposure to magic has made it easier for you to work similar magic on your own.

Benefits: Pick one spell when you choose this trait. Whenever you cast that spell, its effects manifest at +1 caster level.

Usage: Choose one spell in your sheet and open its details page. Set "Caster Level Offset" to 1.

theoretical magician.name: Theoretical Magician
theoretical magician.description:
You’ve studied more magic than what you can actually perform.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Spellcraft checks, or a +3 bonus if you aren’t a spellcaster.

Usage: Increase the bonus in Changes tab to +3 if you aren't a spell caster

self-actualization.name: Self-Actualization
self-actualization.description:
Inspired by stories of mortals ascending to godhood, you’re certain that you can do the same, and do all you can to hone your talents and reach your utmost potential.

Benefits: Once per day, when you take 20 on a roll or check, you can complete the action in only five times the normal amount of time for that action, rather than 20 times the normal amount.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, when you take 20 on a roll or check, you can complete the action in only five times the normal amount of time for that action, rather than 20 times the normal amount.
Once per day, when you take 20 on a roll or check, you can complete the action in only five times the normal amount of time for that action, rather than 20 times the normal amount.

anuli engineer (anuli).name: Anuli Engineer (Anuli)
anuli engineer (anuli).description:
Your family helped to rebuild the ruined city of Anuli, and that tradition runs in your blood.

Requirements: Anuli

Benefits: You gain a +2 bonus on Perception checks to notice unusual stonework, traps, and hidden doors in worked stone and artificial constructions. Knowledge (engineering) is always a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to notice unusual stonework, traps, and hidden doors in worked stone and artificial constructions.

persecuted expatriate (tiefling).name: Persecuted Expatriate (Tiefling)
persecuted expatriate (tiefling).description:
You were born in a nation that persecuted you for your heritage. Although you were under terrible duress, you escaped either with the aid of family or by your own cunning.

Requirements: Tiefling

Benefits: You gain a +1 bonus on one skill depending on your fiendish heritage: asura (Knowledge [local]), daemon (Sleight of Hand), demodand (Intimidate), demon (Perception), devil (Sense Motive), div (Diplomacy), kyton (Escape Artist), oni (Disguise), qlippoth (Survival), or rakshasa (Sense Motive). That skill is always a class skill for you.

Usage: Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect the skill associated above with your Heritage.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect the skill associated above with your Heritage.

superior clutch (tiefling; hungerseed).name: Superior Clutch (Tiefling; Hungerseed)
superior clutch (tiefling; hungerseed).description:
Your hands not only are bigger than normal, but also have a strong grip useful for wielding large weapons.

Requirements: Hungerseed Tiefling Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on damage rolls when using weapons intended for creatures of a larger size.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on damage rolls when using weapons intended for creatures of a larger size.

expressive pantomime.name: Expressive Pantomime
expressive pantomime.description:
You have learned to express yourself without having to depend on words.

Benefits: When attempting a Bluff check to deliver a secret message, you can take a –5 penalty on the check to communicate a simple message even to creatures that do not share a language with you. You cannot communicate with mindless creatures.

contextNotes.text: When attempting  to deliver a secret message, you can take a -[[5]] penalty on the check to communicate a simple message even to creatures that do not share a language with you. You cannot communicate with mindless creatures.

mastery of form (skinwalker).name: Mastery of Form (Skinwalker)
mastery of form (skinwalker).description:
You’ve never had trouble changing shape between beast and humanoid, and your ability to control your form is unparalleled.

Requirements: Skinwalker

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against polymorph effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against polymorph effects.

exhibition fighter (lung wa).name: Exhibition Fighter (Lung Wa)
exhibition fighter (lung wa).description:
You fight at your best in martial arts tournaments, such as those once popular in Lung Wa and still common in Quain. While others may attempt to cheat or chafe against the constraints placed upon them, you thrive under such limitations.

Requirements: Lung Wa

Benefits: You gain a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls when fighting as part of a tournament or other structured setting with rules and restrictions, so long as you do not break these rules.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] bonus on attack and damage rolls when fighting as part of a tournament or other structured setting with rules and restrictions, so long as you do not break these rules.

enduring heritage (tiefling).name: Enduring Heritage (Tiefling)
enduring heritage (tiefling).description:
You can channel your evil ancestry to take advantage of any effects for which such an identity would matter (such as detect evil).

Requirements: Tiefling

Benefits: You detect as an evil outsider with the same number of HD as you possess. You can begin or end this effect as a swift action. You can maintain this effect for a number of rounds per day equal to your HD.

water brave (river kingdoms).name: Water Brave (River Kingdoms)
water brave (river kingdoms).description:
Travel through the dangerous waters of the River Kingdoms has given your tribe special skills in relation to them.

Requirements: River Kingdoms

Benefits: You receive a +1 trait bonus on Swim checks, and it becomes a class skill.

rising star (war for the crown).name: Rising Star (War for the Crown)
rising star (war for the crown).description:
All too often, great minds must suffer for their art, but you’re one of the lucky ones. Your skills attracted the attention of a noble patron who pays for your food, housing, and tools. As your patron introduces you to other cultural elites, you’re slowly learning to navigate the treacherous waters of high society, but you still remember the lean times when each meal was a hard-won treasure.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Sense Motive and Survival checks, as well as checks using a single Craft or Perform skill of your choice; one of these skills is always a class skill for you. Once each day, you can invoke your patron’s reputation or name one of your own prominent works to gain a +2 circumstance bonus on a single Bluff, Diplomacy, or Intimidate check. You begin the game with a masterwork musical instrument or tool appropriate to the Craft or Perform skill you selected, a gift from your patron.

Usage

  1. Select a single Craft or Perform skill when you take this trait.

  2. Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the skill chosen at step 1

  3. Update the target of the untargeted change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice at step 1.

  4. Select Sense Motive, Survival, or the skill chosen at step 1.

  5. Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect your choice at step 4.

contextNotes.text: Once each day, you can gain a +[[2]] Circumstance bonus on a single check. ([[@resources.risingStarWarForTheCrown.value]] remaining uses)
Once each day, you can gain a +[[2]] Circumstance bonus on a single check. ([[@resources.risingStarWarForTheCrown.value]] remaining uses)
Once each day, you can gain a +[[2]] Circumstance bonus on a single check. ([[@resources.risingStarWarForTheCrown.value]] remaining uses)

underling (tiefling).name: Underling (Tiefling)
underling (tiefling).description:
People tend to assume you either are, or are willing to become, a minor cog in some evil cabal or conspiracy.

Requirements: Tiefling

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Bluff and Diplomacy checks made to either join or imply that you already belong to an evil organization.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on checks made to either join, or imply that you already belong to, an evil organization.
+[[2]] Trait bonus on checks made to either join, or imply that you already belong to, an evil organization.

sovyrian intellectual (elf, 1st-level).name: Sovyrian Intellectual (Elf, 1st-level)
sovyrian intellectual (elf, 1st-level).description:
Having grown up in one of the intellectual centers of Castrovel, you speak precisely and persuasively, especially among elves.

Requirements: Elf, 1st-level only

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks. This bonus increases to +3 against creatures with the elf subtype.

contextNotes.text:
Increase to a total of +[[3]] Trait bonus 
 against creatures with the elf subtype.
(this trait always provides +[[1]] Trait bonus)

nervous.name: Nervous
nervous.description:
Living under constant threat of death or worse from your oppressive government, your nerves have frayed terribly.

Effects: Whenever you take 10 on an ability check or skill check that imparts any penalty for failure (aside from wasted time), you treat your check as if you had rolled an 8 instead of a 10, even if you are able to take your time.
