contextNotes.text: Whenever you take 10 on an ability check or skill check that imparts any penalty for failure (aside from wasted time), you treat your check as if you had rolled an 8 instead of a 10, even if you are able to take your time.

Whenever you take 10 on an ability check or skill check that imparts any penalty for failure (aside from wasted time), you treat your check as if you had rolled an 8 instead of a 10, even if you are able to take your time.

divine deceiver (razmiran).name: Divine Deceiver (Razmiran)
divine deceiver (razmiran).description:
You are trained in the methods of Razmir’s priesthood, and can use your arcana to fuel minor healing powers.

Requirements: Razmiran

Benefits: Once per day as a full-round action, you can sacrifice a prepared arcane spell or spell slot to manifest healing magic. This acts as @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.xoc3vhg3btmfjauq]{Cure Light Wounds}, except you heal 1d6 points of damage per level of the spell or spell slot sacrificed (maximum 5d6) and the hit points healed are temporary (lasting 1 hour).

unnatural presence (old cults).name: Unnatural Presence (Old Cults)
unnatural presence (old cults).description:
Your prolonged association with alien forces leaks from your pores.

Requirements: Old Cults

Benefits: You can use your Intimidate skill to demoralize animals and vermin; Intimidate is a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: You can  demoralize animals and vermin.

strict judgment (kols).name: Strict Judgment (Kols)
strict judgment (kols).description:
Your respect for the letter of the law bleeds into your spellcasting.

Requirements: Kols

Benefits: Any spells you cast that prescribe certain behaviors with a consequence for breaking these directives (such as @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.3jaumkutti1ytulq]{mark of justice} or @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.pfv5zv744x9dwg38]{geas}) have their save DC increased by 1.

contextNotes.text: Any spells you cast that prescribe certain behaviors with a consequence for breaking these directives (such as mark of justice or geas) have their save DC increased by 1.

wise teacher (erastil).name: Wise Teacher (Erastil)
wise teacher (erastil).description:
You are skilled in passing along knowledge of rural matters.

Requirements: Erastil

Benefits: If you have 4 or more ranks in Survival or Knowledge (Nature), when you aid another with these skills, you grant a +4 bonus on the check you are aiding instead of the normal +2.

contextNotes.text: If you have 4 or more ranks, when you aid another, you grant a +4 bonus on the check you are aiding instead of the normal +2.
If you have 4 or more ranks, when you aid another, you grant a +4 bonus on the check you are aiding instead of the normal +2.

calculated risk (vampire; jiang-shi).name: Calculated Risk (Vampire; Jiang-Shi)
calculated risk (vampire; jiang-shi).description:
Requirements: Jiang-Shi Vampire Heritage

Benefits: Once per day, you may voluntarily fail a saving throw against a harmful spell or effect. After using this ability, for the next minute you may add a +4 trait bonus on one saving throw, after which the bonus is expended. You may use this bonus on an additional saving throw against the spell or effect you voluntarily failed (such as a recurring poison save or a follow-up save against hold person). If you do not use this bonus within 1 minute, it is lost.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you may voluntarily fail a saving throw against a harmful spell or effect.  for the next minute you may add a +[[4]] Trait bonus on one saving throw, after which the bonus is expended. You may use this bonus on an additional saving throw against the spell or effect you voluntarily failed (such as a recurring poison save or a follow-up save against hold person). If you do not use this bonus within 1 minute, it is lost.

acolyte of razmir (razmiran).name: Acolyte of Razmir (Razmiran)
acolyte of razmir (razmiran).description:
You are an adherent of the Razmiri faith, or once were and have since renounced it.

Requirements: Razmiran

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (local) and Knowledge (religion) checks, and one of these skills is always a class skill for you. This bonus increases to +2 when dealing with specifics of the Razmiri faith.

Usage

Select Knowledge (local) or Knowledge (religion) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: Your Trait bonus increases to +[[2]] when dealing with specifics of the Razmiri faith.
Your Trait bonus increases to +[[2]] when dealing with specifics of the Razmiri faith.

near-death experience (belkzen).name: Near-Death Experience (Belkzen)
near-death experience (belkzen).description:
You had a close brush with death that you survived only thanks to one of the witch doctors of Belkzen, and your body has learned to cling to life in dire situations.

Requirements: Must hail from the Belkzen or have lived there for at least 1 year

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Constitution checks to stabilize, and you don’t die until your hit points drop to a negative amount equal to 2 + your Constitution score.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on checks to become stable when dying. In addition, you die only once your hit points equal or exceed a negative amount equal to your Constitution score + 2. (hp=[[@attributes.hp.value]] , con+2=[[@abilities.con.total +2]])

outcast (half-orc).name: Outcast (Half-Orc)
outcast (half-orc).description:
Driven from town after town because of your heritage, you have become adept at living apart from others.This content is from material published by Paizo Publishing, LLC, but is not part of the Pathfinder Core Rules.

Requirements: Half-Orc

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Survival skill checks, and Survival is always a class skill for you.

wild wanderer (mwangi expanse).name: Wild Wanderer (Mwangi Expanse)
wild wanderer (mwangi expanse).description:
You’re a child of the wild, living on your own without need of others.

Requirements: Mwangi Expanse

Benefits: Each time you attempt a Survival check to get along in the wild or resist severe weather, you can choose to gain a +4 trait bonus on that check. If you do so, you can’t use that check to provide assistance to others, even if your result exceeds the DC.

contextNotes.text: Each time you attempt a Survival check to get along in the wild or resist severe weather, you can choose to gain a +4 trait bonus on that check. If you do so, you can’t use that check to provide assistance to others, even if your result exceeds the DC.

regional influence - korvosa (varisia).name: Regional Influence - Korvosa (Varisia)
regional influence - korvosa (varisia).description:
Although you live some distance from the city-state your community owes fealty to, you’ve been influenced by its sophisticated urban ways.

Requirements: Varisia

Benefits: Determine the city-state of which your home is a holding. You gain a +1 trait bonus on checks with one Profession skill and one of the following benefits.

Korvosa

Gain Infernal as a bonus language. If you already have this language, gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks.

Usage

  1. Select one Profession skill when you take this trait.

  2. Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the chosen skill.

  3. Update the target of the first untargeted change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

  4. If you already have the Infernal language, Update the target of the second untargeted change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect Diplomacy.

  5. if this trait is your only source of the Infernal language, delete the second untargeted Change.

Special Note

This is one of three versions of this trait. Other versions can be found here:

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.PEF5ztrAhR9wRIOa]{Regional Influence - Magnimar (Varisia)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.im0JBzMqeObourHw]{Regional Influence - Riddleport (Varisia)}

water-trained (lastwall jasper).name: Water-Trained (Lastwall Jasper)
water-trained (lastwall jasper).description:
Requirements: Horse (Lastwall Jasper)

Benefits: Lastwall jaspers gain a +2 trait bonus on Swim checks, and Swim is a class skill for them.

balancer's banishing.name: Balancer's Banishing
balancer's banishing.description:
The interference of the Outer Planes in mortal affairs angers you to no end and invigorates the potency of your magic.

Benefits: As long as you remain neutral, whenever you cast a spell that would send an outsider with the chaotic, evil, good, or lawful subtype back to its home plane, the spell’s saving throw DC increases by 1.

contextNotes.text: Spells that would send an outsider with the chaotic, evil, good, or lawful subtype have their DC increased by [[1]].

agent of the sultana (plane of earth).name: Agent of the Sultana (Plane of Earth)
agent of the sultana (plane of earth).description:
You have learned many adventuring skills—as well as hatred for efreet—during your years of military service to Sultana Ashadieeyah bint Khalid, ruler of the Opaline Vault on the Plane of Earth.

Requirements: Plane of Earth

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Disable Device checks to disarm any traps that you know deal fire damage, and you gain a +2 trait bonus on attack rolls to confirm critical hits against creatures with the fire subtype.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to disarm any traps that you know deal fire damage
+[[2]] Trait bonus on attack rolls to confirm critical hits against creatures with the fire subtype

scamper (ratfolk).name: Scamper (Ratfolk)
scamper (ratfolk).description:
In desperate times, your adrenaline kicks in to help you avoid danger.

Requirements: Ratfolk

Benefits: During the first round of combat in which you can act, you ignore difficult terrain and gain an additional 5 feet of movement. This benefit does not apply when you are carrying a medium or heavier load.

noble-in-exile (gralton).name: Noble-in-Exile (Gralton)
noble-in-exile (gralton).description:
Though forced to live in the River Kingdoms, you never forget you’re a Galtan noble.

Requirements: Gralton

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks while interacting with aristocrats and nobles, and a +1 trait bonus on Intimidate checks while interacting with commoners.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus while interacting with aristocrats and nobles.
+[[1]] Trait bonus while interacting with commoners.

best friend (jade regent).name: Best Friend (Jade Regent)
best friend (jade regent).description:
You are best friends with one of the NPCs; you've grown up together, and while your friend has had a much more exciting life than you so far, you hope one day to follow in your friend's footsteps and see the world - or perhaps even become an adventurer yourself. You've picked up a lot from your friend, but particularly his or her skill in interacting with others.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on all attack rolls against foes that threaten your friend as well as one of the following:

If Ameiko is your best friend, you gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks.

If Sandru is your best friend, you gain a +2 trait bonus on Bluff checks.

Whichever skill you gain a trait bonus on, that skill is always considered a class skill for you.

NPC Choices: Ameiko, Sandru

Usage

  1. Select Ameiko (Diplomacy) or Sandru (Bluff) when you take this trait.

  2. Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect your choice. (Diplomacy or Bluff)

  3. Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice. (Diplomacy or Bluff)

  4. Update the text of the context note on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice. (Ameiko or Sandru)

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on all attack rolls against foes that threaten <Chosen companion>

rat squeeze (skinwalker; wererat-kin).name: Rat Squeeze (Skinwalker; Wererat-Kin)
rat squeeze (skinwalker; wererat-kin).description:
You first discovered your wererat-related abilities while escaping someone, whether the authorities or a rival, and ever since you have honed your ability to squeeze out of tight situations.

Requirements: Wererat-Kin Skinwalker Heritage

Benefits: Whenever you use your skinwalker ability to change shape into your bestial form, you can squeeze through narrow spaces at least half as wide as your normal space without slowing your movement; each move into or through a narrow space counts as 1 square, though you still take all the normal penalties associated with squeezing.

wanderer's shroud.name: Wanderer's Shroud
wanderer's shroud.description:
Your wanderings often go unnoticed.

Benefits: Efforts to use the Diplomacy skill to gather information about you take a –1 penalty, and you gain a +1 trait bonus on all saving throws against scrying and mind-reading effects that allow saving throws.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against scrying and mind-reading effects that allow saving throws.

self-doubting.name: Self-Doubting
self-doubting.description:
Your ever-present fear of failure causes a downward spiral.

Effects: The first time each day that you fail a Will saving throw or skill check, you take a –2 penalty on the next Will saving throw or skill check of that kind.

contextNotes.text: The first time each day that you fail a Will saving throw or skill check, you take a -[[2]] penalty on the next Will saving throw or skill check of that kind.
The first time each day that you fail a Will saving throw or skill check, you take a -[[2]] penalty on the next Will saving throw or skill check of that kind.

smuggler (the exchange).name: Smuggler (The Exchange)
smuggler (the exchange).description:
Whether you’re carrying illegal contraband or simply regulated products, you have a knack for sneaking goods past watchful eyes.

Requirements: The Exchange

Benefits: You gain a +3 bonus on Sleight of Hand checks made to hide an object, and Sleight of Hand is a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[3]] bonus  to hide objects

dog-sniff-hate (goblin).name: Dog-Sniff-Hate (Goblin)
dog-sniff-hate (goblin).description:
Requirements: Goblin

Benefits: You gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-universal-monster-rules.Item.j0idsqgoSZLLNDH2]{Scent} special quality but only against canine creatures, like dogs or wolves, including monstrous hounds such as hell hounds, yeth hounds, and even werewolves. You gain a +2 trait bonus on attack rolls against dogs you can smell.

contextNotes.text: You gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-universal-monster-rules.Item.j0idsqgoSZLLNDH2]{Scent} special quality but only against canine creatures, like dogs or wolves, including monstrous hounds such as hell hounds, yeth hounds, and even werewolves.

You gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-universal-monster-rules.Item.j0idsqgoSZLLNDH2]{Scent} special quality but only against canine creatures, like dogs or wolves, including monstrous hounds such as hell hounds, yeth hounds, and even werewolves.
+[[2]] Trait bonus on attack rolls against dogs you can smell.

isger fixer (halfling; isger).name: Isger Fixer (Halfling; Isger)
isger fixer (halfling; isger).description:
Everyone in Isger knows how to make do with substandard equipment, and you can improvise tools and materials in order to keep a damaged object working for just a little bit longer.

Requirements: Halfling; Isger

Benefits: Once per day, you can tinker with a broken object to allow anyone who uses it to ignore the penalties for its broken condition for 24 hours (this does not affect the object's hit points). At the end of this time, it regains the broken condition (but you can use this trait on it over and over again).

secret-keeper (svirfneblin).name: Secret-Keeper (Svirfneblin)
secret-keeper (svirfneblin).description:
You trained from birth to never reveal secrets and never betray anyone’s trust.

Requirements: Svirfneblin

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against divinations or enchantments that would compel you to reveal a secret or allow anyone to read your mind.

contextNotes.text:
+[[2]] Trait bonus 
 against divinations or enchantments that would compel you to reveal a secret or allow anyone to read your mind.

earth-touched.name: Earth-Touched
earth-touched.description:
You share an affinity with elemental earth.

Benefits: You gain DR 1/— against creatures and attacks with the earth type.

contextNotes.text: DR 1/— against creatures and attacks with the earth type.

pugnacious (strange aeons).name: Pugnacious (Strange Aeons)
pugnacious (strange aeons).description:
You awake in the asylum with sore muscles and bloody knuckles, as if you were recently in a fight. You even have a vague feeling that you won. You can’t explain it, but you know that you’ve always been easy to set off, and your first instinct when pressed into a corner is to lash out. Stuck in the asylum sets you on edge, and you not only want to figure out what happened to you, you want to free yourself and find whoever did this to you—and make them pay.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls when threatened by two or more enemies. In addition, once per day you can gain one of the following effects as an immediate action: You can increase the reach of your melee attacks by 5 feet for 1 round, or you can treat your weapon as one size category larger than it actually is for purposes of determining damage for 1 round.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attack rolls when threatened by two or more enemies.

Once per day, as an immediate action, you can chose either: A) To increase the reach of your melee attacks by 5 feet for 1 round; or  B)To treat your weapon as one size category larger for purposes of determining damage for 1 round. ([[@resources.pugnaciousStrangeAeons.value]] remaining uses)

strong swimmer (gozreh).name: Strong Swimmer (Gozreh)
strong swimmer (gozreh).description:
Your faith in Gozreh's grace gives you the confidence to swim without fear.

Requirements: Gozreh

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on all Swim checks and can hold your breath for an extra 2 rounds when underwater.

contextNotes.text: You can hold your breath for an extra 2 rounds when underwater.
You can hold your breath for an extra 2 rounds when underwater.

liquid healing (rahadoum).name: Liquid Healing (Rahadoum)
liquid healing (rahadoum).description:
Growing up in Rahadoum, you did not have access to clerics and other divine magic for healing, so you relied heavily on potions to cure your wounds. As a result, potions heal you more effectively. 

Requirements: Rahadoum

Benefits: Whenever you consume a potion that heals any number of hit points, you heal 2 additional hit points.

repulsive (tiefling; foulspawn).name: Repulsive (Tiefling; Foulspawn)
repulsive (tiefling; foulspawn).description:
Your repulsiveness causes people to almost instinctively shy away from making physical contact with you.

Requirements: Foulspawn Tiefling Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on combat maneuver checks made to overrun and reposition opponents.

contextNotes.text:
+[[1]] Trait bonus on  checks
made to overrun and reposition opponents.

bloodthirsty.name: Bloodthirsty
bloodthirsty.description:
You have a vicious streak, and nothing satisfies you more than warm blood on your hands and blade.

Benefits: Whenever you make an attack that reduces a foe to 0 hit points or fewer or you confirm a critical hit, your attack deals 1 additional point of damage. The additional damage is a trait bonus, and is multiplied by your weapon’s critical hit multiplier.

the vessel between (daggermark).name: The Vessel Between (Daggermark)
the vessel between (daggermark).description:
The assassin ushers her victims from the kingdoms of the living to the kingdom of the dead.

Requirements: Daggermark

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls that would deal sneak attack damage on a hit.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attack rolls that would deal sneak attack damage on a hit.

sky spirit worshiper (shanguang).name: Sky Spirit Worshiper (Shanguang)
sky spirit worshiper (shanguang).description:
As the child of a Mutabi-qi shaman, you have seen the sky spirits that roam above and know that the world is rife with unexplainable and terrifying mysteries.

Requirements: Shanguang

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saves against fear effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against fear effects.

sky quester (dwarf).name: Sky Quester (Dwarf)
sky quester (dwarf).description:
You grew up hearing stories of the lost Sky Citadels, and have sworn to find and reclaim one.

Requirements: Dwarf

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (geography) and Knowledge (history) checks. One of these skills (your choice) is always a class skill for you.

Usage

Select Knowledge (geography) or Knowledge (history) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

eastern mysteries (qadira faction).name: Eastern Mysteries (Qadira Faction)
eastern mysteries (qadira faction).description:
Ever since you were a young child, you have studied the ancient arcane traditions of several Tian societies, and those studies have made your spellcasting ability difficult to resist.

Requirements: Qadira Faction

Benefits: Once per day, you may select one single spell that you are casting that allows for a saving throw. You may increase the DC of that spell by +2.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, for one single spell with a saving throw, you may increase the save DC by +2. ([[@resources.easternMysteriesQadiraFaction.value]] remaining uses)

awakened from stasis (apostae).name: Awakened from Stasis (Apostae)
awakened from stasis (apostae).description:
Although you were born and lived on Golarion, you recently awakened in a strange vault with no memory of how you got there, surrounded by sleeping creatures of every shape and size in smooth glass eggs, all watched over by uncommunicative crablike automatons.

Requirements: Apostae

Benefits: As a side effect of your stasis, you gain all the benefits of 8 hours of sleep in only 2 hours. This allows a spellcaster who must rest to prepare spells to do so after only 2 hours, but does not allow a spellcaster to prepare spells more than once per day.

folgrit's mercy (folgrit).name: Folgrit's Mercy (Folgrit)
folgrit's mercy (folgrit).description:
You were called upon to defend your home or family at a vulnerable time and led with a clarity of mind you did not know you had.

Requirements: Folgrit

Benefits: Once per day when you use @UUID[Compendium.pf1.class-abilities.Item.WhE4j0Jc82npdMCP]{lay on hands}, you also cure the target of 1d4 points of Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma damage. You choose which ability score gains the healing effect.

dockside avenger (curse of the crimson t.name: Dockside Avenger (Curse of the Crimson Throne)
dockside avenger (curse of the crimson t.description:
No crime is too despicable for Gaedren Lamm and his thugs—a gang of lowlife murderers, kidnappers, and drug dealers that operate in Korvosa’s Midland district. You’ve lost someone to Lamm and now you’re out for revenge.

Benefits: While Lamm remains alive and free, you are not slain until your hit point total reaches a negative number equal to your Constitution score + 3. Once Lamm is imprisoned or killed, you lose the former benefit but gain 3 permanent hit points.

contextNotes.text: While Lamm remains alive and free, you are not slain until your hit point total reaches a negative number equal to your Constitution score + 3 ([[@abilities.con.total +3]]). Once Lamm is imprisoned or killed, you lose the former benefit but gain 3 permanent hit points.

wasteland vargrant.name: Wasteland Vargrant
wasteland vargrant.description:
You have spent most of your life learning how to survive in wastelands, and a lack of normal survival resources apparent in the environment is not a problem for you.

Benefits: When you gain this trait, pick one type of wasteland from the following list: blighted, Abyssal, desert, or waterlogged. You gain a +2 trait bonus on Survival checks in that type of wasteland terrain. (The GM determines whether terrain falls into the chosen type of wasteland.) Once you choose a type of wasteland terrain to gain this benefit in, you cannot change it later.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus in chosen wasteland terrain type

desperate speed.name: Desperate Speed
desperate speed.description:
Your eidolon excels at a movement type you do not possess.

Benefits: Your eidolon receives a +5-foot enhancement bonus to one type of speed it has that you do not, such as a climb or swim speed. If your eidolon doesn’t already possess such a mode of movement, you may apply this trait bonus later if it gains an evolution that grants it such a mode. Once this bonus is applied to a mode of movement, it can’t be switched to another mode.

Usage: Increase one type of speed your eidolon has that you do not by 5 enhancement bonus.

hwan artist (hwanggot).name: Hwan Artist (Hwanggot)
hwan artist (hwanggot).description:
You grew up in Hwanggot, and your artistic talents were cherished and honed from an early age. You have been nurtured on the artistic spirit of Hwanggot.

Requirements: Hwanggot

Benefits: Choose one Perform skill. You gain a +1 trait bonus on checks with the chosen performance skill, and it becomes a class skill for you.

Usage: Select one Perform skill when you take this trait.

Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the chosen skill.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

nature’s mimic.name: Nature’s Mimic
nature’s mimic.description:
Your knowledge of nature informs your fighting style, and that style gives you insight into related aspects of the natural world.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on all Knowledge (nature) checks, and Knowledge (nature) is always a class skill for you. You can make Knowledge (nature) checks pertaining to animals that correspond to your style feats untrained.

contextNotes.text: You can make Knowledge (nature) checks pertaining to animals that correspond to your style feats untrained

resourceful scavenger (numeria).name: Resourceful Scavenger (Numeria)
resourceful scavenger (numeria).description:
Living in Numeria required you to become an expert scavenger, searching through ruins and crash sites for valuables and sustenance. Years spent in this pursuit have given you a keen eye for finding valuables and potentially useful resources in the unlikeliest places.

Requirements: Numeria native

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Perception checks when you search a location for valuables or resources. Appraise is always a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus when you search a location for valuables or resources

framed (curse of the crimson throne).name: Framed (Curse of the Crimson Throne)
framed (curse of the crimson throne).description:
Someone you know and love was accused of murder. A supposed eyewitness account from a local fisherman seemed to be enough to seal the case, but the accused had enough alibis that sentencing wasn't immediate. Someone confronted the fisherman and discovered he was intimidated into providing false witness and forced into planting the murder weapon by the actual murderer,a local crimelord named Gaedren Lamm. Lamm's thugs killed the fisherman before he could recant his testimony. Although this removed the key witness and resulted in the accused being set free, the stigma was enough to badly damage his reputation. If you can find Gaedren, you're sure you can find evidence that ties him to the murder and can clear the accused's name.

Benefits: Choose one of the following benefits:

Family Honor: The person framed was a family member, perhaps a father or brother. You managed to trick the fisherman into revealing the truth with your skilled tongue, and thus gain a +2 bonus on Bluff checks.

Dropout: You were the one accused. Although you were eventually freed when a friend confronted the fisherman and got the truth, the damage had been done. You were forced to leave your school (perhaps the Acadamae) or church. As a result, you were forced to self-train and promised yourself you would become better at your chosen profession despite the spurning of your peers. You gain a +2 bonus on Spellcraft checks.

Usage: Select Family Honor or Dropout when you take this trait.

  1. if you select Family Honor: Update the target of the untargeted change on the changes tab of this trait to reflect Specific Skills> Bluff

  2. if you select Dropout: Update the target of the untargeted change on the changes tab of this trait to reflect Specific Skills> Spellcraft

Special Note: This is the 3.5 Ruleset version of this trait. To view the updated version for the Pathfinder rules click the link below:

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.VUOfSY91afNk3jP3]{Framed (Curse of the Crimson Throne)}

canopy prowler (catfolk).name: Canopy Prowler (Catfolk)
canopy prowler (catfolk).description:
Growing up in the Mwangi Expanse, you learned to hunt from above, where your prey seldom searches for danger.

Requirements: Catfolk

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Climb checks, and Climb is always a class skill for you. If you possess the @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-racial-traits.Item.qBIFC8W3VkEuxpls]{climber} racial trait, you also gain a +1 trait bonus on Stealth checks while climbing.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus  while climbing if you possess the climber racial trait.

true name caller (the abyss).name: True Name Caller (The Abyss)
true name caller (the abyss).description:
You are skilled at discovering the true names of outsiders.

Requirements: The Abyss

Benefits: Choose a plane other than the Material Plane. When attempting to discover the true name of an outsider from the chosen plane, you halve the amount of research time required and gain a +4 trait bonus on the Knowledge (planes) check made to learn the outsider’s name. For more information on binding outsiders using their true names, see page 101 of Pathfinder RPG Ultimate Magic.

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Trait bonus check made to learn the name of an outsider from your chosen plane and you halve the amount of research time required to do so.

enduring spellcraft (human; azlanti).name: Enduring Spellcraft (Human; Azlanti)
enduring spellcraft (human; azlanti).description:
You have studied the enduring spells of ancient Azlant.

Requirements: Azlanti Human Heritage

Benefits: Treat your caster level as 1 higher for the purpose of determining duration only.

contextNotes.text: Treat your caster level as 1 higher for the purpose of determining duration only.

to the last.name: To The Last
to the last.description: Benefits: Choose one type of medium or heavy armor (such as chainmail or half-plate). If you have no allies within 60 feet when you are wearing that type of armor, you gain DR 1/—. This stacks with any one other source of damage resistance you have.

blessing of the feast (zura).name: Blessing of the Feast (Zura)
blessing of the feast (zura).description:
You come from a cannibal tribe, and find strength in the flesh of your foes.

Requirements: Zura

Benefits: Whenever you eat the flesh or drink the blood of another orc—a full-round action that provokes attacks of opportunity— you gain the favor of Zura and receive a +1 natural armor bonus for 1 minute/level. You can only receive this benefit once per day.

dark magic affinity (tiefling).name: Dark Magic Affinity (Tiefling)
dark magic affinity (tiefling).description:
You have an instinctive talent for unleashing the vilest spells, letting the taint in your blood empower your malicious magic.

Requirements: Tiefling

Benefits: Whenever you cast a spell with the [evil] descriptor, you act as if you were one level higher for the purpose of determining that spell's effects.

contextNotes.text: Whenever you cast a spell with the [evil] descriptor, you act as if you were one level higher for the purpose of determining that spell's effects.
Whenever you cast a spell with the [evil] descriptor, you act as if you were one level higher for the purpose of determining that spell's effects.

alchemical adept.name: Alchemical Adept
alchemical adept.description:
You are skilled in creating alchemical items.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on all Craft (alchemy) checks made to craft alchemical items. Furthermore, when you fail a Craft (alchemy) check by 5 or more but don’t roll a natural 1, you don’t ruin any raw materials or have to pay that cost again.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] trait bonus on all Craft (alchemy) checks made to craft alchemical items. Unless you roll a natural 1 you don’t ruin any raw materials or have to pay that cost again.

born under the dreamer.name: Born under the Dreamer
born under the dreamer.description:
Your birth sign aligned significantly with Liavara on the day you were born, and you are touched by the instinctual dreaming spirit of that planet, making you naturally poised and hard to surprise.

Benefits: Once per day as a free action, you can take 10 on your initiative check.

vain.name: Vain
vain.description:
You are sensitive about the way others perceive you.

Effects: Whenever you fail an opposed Charisma-based check, you take a –2 penalty on all Charisma-based checks for the next 24 hours.

contextNotes.text: Whenever you fail an opposed Charisma-based check, you take a -[[2]] penalty on all Charisma-based checks for the next 24 hours.
Whenever you fail an opposed Charisma-based check, you take a -[[2]] penalty on all Charisma-based checks for the next 24 hours.

bellflower contact (halfling).name: Bellflower Contact (Halfling)
bellflower contact (halfling).description:
Before you were old enough to be a Bellflower Network member, you kept an ear out for information useful to slave liberators.

Requirements: Halfling

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Perception checks, Sense Motive checks to understand secret messages, and Bluff checks to send secret messages.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus  to send secret messages.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to understand secret messages.

armor expert.name: Armor Expert
armor expert.description:
You have worn armor as long as you can remember, either as part of your training to become a knight’s squire or simply because you were seeking to emulate a hero. Your childhood armor wasn’t the real thing as far as protection, but it did encumber you as much as real armor would have, and you’ve grown used to moving in such suits with relative grace.

Benefits: When you wear armor of any sort, reduce that suit’s armor check penalty by 1, to a minimum check penalty of 0.

punish insurrection.name: Punish Insurrection
punish insurrection.description:
You relish reminding people who’s in charge.

Benefits: Against members of the same organization or hierarchy, who formally answer to you, you gain a +1 trait bonus on attack and damage rolls. You also gain this bonus against members who have openly defied the authority or rules of that body, provided the infraction is serious enough that their standing is now less than yours.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attack and damage rolls versus members of the same organization or hierarchy, who formally answer to you or who have openly defied the authority or rules of that body, provided the infraction is serious enough that their standing is now less than yours.

eye of arqueros (arqueros).name: Eye of Arqueros (Arqueros)
eye of arqueros (arqueros).description:
You once fell asleep during a task that required your attention. A vision of Arqueros awakened you just in time to avoid failing at your task.

Requirements: Arqueros

Benefits: You gain a +3 trait bonus on Perception checks attempted while you are alone on guard duty.

contextNotes.text: +[[3]] Trait bonus on checks attempted while you are alone on guard duty.

shared curse (skinwalker; werewolf-kin).name: Shared Curse (Skinwalker; Werewolf-Kin)
shared curse (skinwalker; werewolf-kin).description:
A portion of your ancestor’s fell curse still courses through your blood.

Requirements: Werewolf-Kin Skinwalker Heritage

Benefits: Once per day as a standard action, you can share a portion of your accursed heritage with a willing ally by dealing 1 point of damage to that ally with a natural weapon. The ally gains a natural bite attack, as if from your skinwalker ability, for 2 rounds.

planar negotiator (the abyss).name: Planar Negotiator (The Abyss)
planar negotiator (the abyss).description:
You are used to dealing with outsiders that would fluster a less experienced negotiator.

Requirements: The Abyss

Benefits: When dealing with an outsider within one step of your alignment, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy and Sense Motive checks. Increase these bonuses to +2 if you and the outsider worship the same deity.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus when dealing with an outsider within one step of your alignment. Increase to +[[2]] Trait bonus if you and the outsider worship the same deity.
+[[1]] Trait bonus when dealing with an outsider within one step of your alignment. Increase to +[[2]] Trait bonus if you and the outsider worship the same deity.

sword scion (kingmaker).name: Sword Scion (Kingmaker)
sword scion (kingmaker).description:
You have lived all your life in and around the city of Restov, growing up on tales of Baron Sirian Aldori and the exploits of your home city's heroic and legendary swordlords. Perhaps one of your family members was an Aldori swordlord, you have a contact among their members, or you have dreamed since childhood of joining. Regardless, you idolize the heroes, styles, and philosophies of the Aldori and have sought to mimic their vaunted art. Before you can petition to join their ranks, however, you feel that you must test your mettle. Joining an expedition into the Stolen Lands seems like a perfect way to improve your skills and begin a legend comparable to that of Baron Aldori.

Benefits: You begin play with a @UUID[Compendium.pf1.weapons-and-ammo.Item.zWRlna42PMJVX6un]{longsword} or @UUID[Compendium.pf1.weapons-and-ammo.Item.ioPvNyyv6L4hv0aW]{Aldori dueling sword} and gain a +1 trait bonus on all attacks and combat maneuvers made with such weapons.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on all attacks and combat maneuvers made with longswords or Aldori dueling swords

unhappy childhood (curse of the crimson.name: Unhappy Childhood (Curse of the Crimson Throne)
unhappy childhood (curse of the crimson.description:
You spent a period of time as one of Gaedren Lamm’s enslaved orphans, doing all manner of dirty work for him. Maybe you were abducted from your parent’s home or during a trip to the market. Perhaps the irresponsible matron who ruled your orphanage traded you to him in return for a desperately needed financial loan. Or perhaps you, like most of Gaedren’s slaves, were merely a child of the street who succumbed to his promise of regular meals and a roof in return for what he said would be “a little light work.” Whatever the case, you spent several years of your life as one of “Lamm’s Lambs” before escaping. You’ve nursed a grudge against the old man ever since.

Benefits

Choose one of the following benefits:

Religious

Today, while on a job for Gaedren, you found a holy symbol of the god you worship, intrigued by it, you snuck off to attend services. When Gaedren found out, he beat you to within an inch of your life and broke your holy symbol. Your faith let you block out the pain, and you escaped his control and took shelter in the church, where you spent the rest of your youth. You gain a +2 bonus on Concentration checks and Constitution checks to stabilize at negative hit points.

Tortured

After you made one too many errors, Gaedren tortured you and left you for dead on a garbage heap. Your scars and memories have honed your reaction speed and make you rather jumpy. You gain a +1 bonus on Reflex saves.

Usage

  1. Select Tortured, or Religious when you take this trait.

  2. If you select Religious, Update the target of the untargeted change on the changes tab of this trait to reflect a value of 2 and a target of Spells> Concentration then Update the UnTargeted Context Note to Ability Checks> Constitution

  3. If you select Tortured, Update the target of the untargeted Change on the changes tab of this trait to reflect Saving Throws>Reflex, Then Delete the untargeted Context Note

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to stabilize at negative hit points.

sun-blasted (any desert).name: Sun-Blasted (Any Desert)
sun-blasted (any desert).description:
Desert orcs have learnt to survive with practically nothing in the desert.

Requirements: Any Desert

Benefits: You receive a +2 trait bonus on Survival checks made in a desert, and resolve all checks at becoming lost in deserts as though you had proper navigational tools (a +4 bonus). The presence of such tools does not increase your ability, however.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus in a desert.
Resolve all checks at becoming lost in deserts as though you had proper navigational tools (a +4 bonus). The presence of such tools does not increase your ability, however.

touched by the sea (skull and shackles).name: Touched by the Sea (Skull and Shackles)
touched by the sea (skull and shackles).description:
You've always felt the call of the sea and your blood surges with the ebb and flow of the tides. Perhaps one of your parents was a sailor or pirate, or maybe one of your ancestors had a bit of aquatic elf or undine blood in them. Whatever the reason, you're as comfortable in the water as you are on land. You came to Port Peril in search of your destiny, and after a few drinks at a tavern called the Formidably Maid, you went down to the docks to take in a view of the sea. The last thing you remember is a blow to the back of your head and the waves rushing up to meet you.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Swim checks and Swim is a class skill for you. In addition, penalties on attack rolls made underwater are lessened by 1.

contextNotes.text: Penalties on attack rolls made underwater are lessened by 1.

mummy-touched (osirion faction).name: Mummy-Touched (Osirion Faction)
mummy-touched (osirion faction).description:
As a small child, you survived the damning curse of a mummy.

Requirements: Osirion Faction

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws versus diseases and curses.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus versus diseases and curses.

wasteland hunter (human; kellid).name: Wasteland Hunter (Human; Kellid)
wasteland hunter (human; kellid).description:
You are an expert hunter among your tribe.

Requirements: Kellid Human Heritage

Benefits: Whenever you throw a @UUID[Compendium.pf1.weapons-and-ammo.Item.vu3N4zCRDTUJ5FW4]{club}, @UUID[Compendium.pf1.weapons-and-ammo.Item.N37FFAdWjVV6EolR]{shortspear}, @UUID[Compendium.pf1.weapons-and-ammo.Item.s0LFk8Jk5CsgOj7V]{spear}, or @UUID[Compendium.pf1.weapons-and-ammo.Item.KmbhZ4Z674Yk9yIh]{javelin}, reduce the penalty for throwing the weapon beyond its initial range increment by 2 (to a minimum penalty of 0). For example, a club (with a range of 10 feet) thrown at a target 25 feet away would incur only a –2 penalty with this trait.

Usage

For each club, shortspear, spear, or javelinyour actor wields, modify as follows:

  • Edit the weapon.
  • On the weapon's details tab, edit the attack.
  • On the attack's Conditional Modifiers tab, and click the "+ Add Conditional" button.
  • Give this any name you like such as "range penalty reduction".
  • Click the "+ Add Effect" button.
  • add an untyped +2 bonus to attack rolls.

 Conditional modifiers can be dragged to other weapons so that all these steps do not have to be repeated.

Apply the modify on attacks where it is appropriate (at distances in range increments 2 through 5).

marid's fury (undine).name: Marid's Fury (Undine)
marid's fury (undine).description:
Your marid ancestry is particularly strong, and magic runs thick through your veins.

Requirements: Undine

Benefits: When you use your @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.ohy0ty2dawfaaqwd]{hydraulic push} spell-like ability, it can extinguish magical fires as well as nonmagical fires as long as you succeed at an opposed caster level check (the DC is equal to 11 + the magical fire’s caster level).

Usage: Add the benefit text " it can extinguish magical fires as well as nonmagical fires as long as you succeed at an opposed caster level check (the DC is equal to 11 + the magical fire’s caster level)." to the description of the Spell-like ability on your actor.

the lawbringer (tyrant's grasp).name: The Lawbringer (Tyrant's Grasp)
the lawbringer (tyrant's grasp).description:
The frontier is a land of risk and disorder, and while people are welcome to brave those elements themselves, they all too often bring pain down upon folk who never wanted such a risk. You might serve on the town guard or as part of a detachment of soldiers supported by Vigil, or you could be simply a concerned farmer who endures when others cannot, but your purpose is to stand as a bulwark against the cruel whims of fate for anyone who needs it.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against fear effects. If you have the aura of courage class feature, the bonus provided by your aura increases by 1. Your courage is inspiring; the first time each day you are knocked unconscious or killed, all allies within 30 feet of you immediately gain a number of temporary hit points equal to your level plus your Charisma modifier, as they rally to your defense. These temporary hit points last for 1 minute.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against fear effects.

zealous (drawback).name: Zealous (Drawback)
zealous (drawback).description:
You are fanatical in your beliefs, ruled by emotion over reason.

Effects: When you attack a creature that you know worships a different religion than you do, you take a –5 penalty on the attack roll and a +2 trait bonus on the damage roll with your first attack.

contextNotes.text: -[[5]] penalty to attack roll and +[[2]] Trait bonus to damage on first attack vs creatures who you know worship a different religion than you

first memories (gnome).name: First Memories (Gnome)
first memories (gnome).description:
For your whole life, you've felt there was a world just beyond the fabric of the material objects around you, and sometimes you can hear the north wind calling you or smell the dread taint of polluted water before you taste it.

Requirements: Gnome

Benefits: You do not gain the normal list of spell-like abilities for the gnome magic racial trait presented in the Pathfinder RPG Core Rulebook. Instead, if you have a Wisdom of 11 of higher, you gain the following spell-like abilities: 1/day—detect poison, know direction, stabilize, and speak with animals. The caster level for these effects is equal to your character level. The DC for these spells is equal to 10 + the spell’s level + your Wisdom modifier.

Usage: When you add the spells to the spellbook, you can adjust the "0/0" display to be "1/1", change the "Casts Per Day Used" formula to 1, then change the spellbook's spell slots to match.

totemist.name: Totemist
totemist.description:
You grew up in a culture where the touch of totem spirits was felt in everyday life, their subtle urgings constantly guiding you and keeping you safe. As a result, you learned quickly to ask for their help when trouble arises.

Benefits: Once per day, you may call upon your totem’s wisdom as a standard action. You gain the benefits of @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.o8ds5jtvf0hf92dw]{guidance}, except that the granted competence bonus is equal to half your character level (minimum +1). This is a supernatural ability.

obari veteran.name: Obari Veteran
obari veteran.description:
You’ve spent years aboard ships traveling the lucrative Obari Crossing, transporting Garundi and Vudrani goods.

Benefits: You gain +1 bonus on Profession (sailor) checks, and Profession (sailor) is a class skill for you. You also gain a +1 trait bonus on weapon damage rolls against creatures with the aquatic subtype.

Usage: Add Profession (Sailor) as a Skill in Skills tab of your Actor Sheet. Then, check Profession (Sailor) as a class skill in Details tab, and set the target of bonus to Profession (Sailor) in Changes tab.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on damage rolls against creatures with the aquatic subtype
+[[1]] Trait bonus on damage rolls against creatures with the aquatic subtype

fangwood diplomat (nirmathas).name: Fangwood Diplomat (Nirmathas)
fangwood diplomat (nirmathas).description:
You've lived and worked among the disparate guerrilla groups of the Fangwood.

Requirements: Nirmathas

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks, and Diplomacy is always a class skill for you.

meditative rest.name: Meditative Rest
meditative rest.description: Benefits: When you rest, you ignore the first time you are interrupted during that rest for the purposes of determining how long you need to rest in full to regain the capacity to regain spells, provided the interruption lasts no more than 15 minutes. If you cast spells during this interruption, you don’t count these against your daily limit of spells when you wake and prepare spells.

contextNotes.text: If you cast spells during the first time you are interrupted during your rest, you don’t count these against your daily limit of spells when you wake and prepare spells provided the interruption lasts no more than 15 minutes.

iron grip (gorum).name: Iron Grip (Gorum)
iron grip (gorum).description:
You and your weapon are practically one.

Requirements: Gorrum

Benefits: You and your weapon are practically one. You receive a +2 trait bonus to your CMD against disarm attempts.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus vs. being disarmed

chelish noble (hell's vengeance).name: Chelish Noble (Hell's Vengeance)
chelish noble (hell's vengeance).description:
You had the fortune to be born into one of Cheliax’s noble families, and even more importantly, your family is one of those loyal to House Thrune. Perhaps your family has been allied with Thrune since the Chelish Civil War, or maybe Thrune granted your family its title in exchange for its support during that conflict. In any case, your experience growing up among the nation’s well-to-do has given you an upper hand when it comes to knowledge of high society, and you start the game with a modest inheritance. With the nascent uprising of the Glorious Reclamation in Cheliax, there is much concern among the aristocracy, and House Thrune needs the support of loyal nobles now more then ever.

Requirements: This trait assumes you belong to a minor noble human family with a small manor or estate in some backwater of the empire. If you’re not human, either you were adopted into a noble human family, or your family was recently granted a nonhereditary noble title

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (nobility) checks, and Knowledge (nobility) is a class skill for you. In addition, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Charisma-based checks against other members of the Chelish aristocracy. The Noble Scion feat does not have a Charisma prerequisite for you. Lastly, you start play with a noble’s outfit, a signet ring, and a single additional nonmagical item worth no more than 200 gp.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against other members of the Chelish aristocracy.
+[[1]] Trait bonus against other members of the Chelish aristocracy.

accident resistant (zyphus).name: Accident Resistant (Zyphus)
accident resistant (zyphus).description:
Your understanding of pointless and unexpected death grants you a special awareness.

Requirements: Zyphus

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Reflex saves when denied your Dexterity bonus or during a surprise round.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus when denied your Dexterity bonus or during a surprise round.

young reformer (war for the crown).name: Young Reformer (War for the Crown)
young reformer (war for the crown).description:
Perhaps you were born at the bottom of Taldan society and you’re tired of seeing your friends and family toil endlessly with no hope of a better life, or maybe your privileged outlook was shattered by empathy or tragedy. Either way, you know the system is broken, and you’ve dedicated your life to fixing it. But tradition rules Taldor as much as any Grand Prince, and change requires more influence than you have… so far. You’ve got a few friends and allies, and plenty of determination, but while you work toward the power you need to change the nation, you’ll have to resort to more discrete ways of righting wrongs and curbing the abuses of the upper class.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Disable Device and Knowledge (local) checks, and one of these skills is always a class skill for you. Once per day, you can call upon your coconspirators to have “made arrangements” on your behalf, allowing you to attempt a Knowledge (local) check in place of a single Bluff, Disable Device, Intimidate, or Sleight of Hand check. The DC of this Knowledge (local) check is equal to the DC of the skill check it replaces. Whatever arrangements you make—for doors left unlocked, guards strong-armed out of your way, stolen keys left for you to find—must be reasonable to have anticipated and achieved beforehand; you could arrange to have a noble convinced you’re a very important diplomat traveling in disguise, for example, but if a fight breaks out you could not rely on your network of contacts to feint for you in combat. You don’t have to use this ability in advance; you can announce its use as you encounter a challenge, implying you foresaw this challenge and made arrangements prior to your arrival. 

Usage: Select Disable Device or Knowledge (local) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

clever wordplay.name: Clever Wordplay
clever wordplay.description:
Your cunning and logic are more than a match for another’s confidence and poise.

Benefits: Choose one Charisma-based skill. You attempt checks with that skill using your Intelligence modifier instead of your Charisma modifier.

Usage: Select one Charisma-based skill when you take this trait.

Update the chosen skill on the skills tab of the actor to reflect the actor's Intelligence-mod instead of the default Charisma-mod.

collector (gnome).name: Collector (Gnome)
collector (gnome).description:
You have a small, bizarre collection of apparently worthless items, such as rusty nails, animal droppings, clockwork cogs, or bent spoons. You find this strange menagerie useful in ways no non-gnome could understand. Seeking new items to add to this collection is endlessly fascinating to you, and while no one else may understand why a Chelish horsenail is worthy to add to your collection when a dwarven wheelbarrow nail is not, it all makes sense to you.

Requirements: Gnome

Benefits: Select one of the following skills: Craft (any one), Disable Device, Escape Artist, Perform (any one), or Spellcraft. Whenever you have your collection within 5 feet of you, you gain a +2 trait bonus on all checks made with the selected skill. Once this skill has been selected, it cannot be changed, nor can you gain it for any other collection you begin amassing.

Usage: Select one of skills permitted [Craft (any one), Disable Device, Escape Artist, Perform (any one), or Spellcraft] when you take this trait.

Update the target of the note on the changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on all checks whenever you have your collection within 5 feet of you.

slave taker (okeno).name: Slave Taker (Okeno)
slave taker (okeno).description:
You specialize in disabling foes without wounding them, preserving their value as slaves.

Requirements: Okeno

Benefits: Whenever you deal nonlethal damage in combat, that damage increases by +1.

ethical leader (aasimar).name: Ethical Leader (Aasimar)
ethical leader (aasimar).description:
You attract followers who agree with your philosophical outlook on life. Your followers and cohorts are more willing to serve you because of their confidence in your strong moral position.

Requirements: Aasimar

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus to your Leadership score if all your cohorts and followers have an alignment within one step of your alignment.

the thrush.name: The Thrush
the thrush.description:
Like the curious songbirds that welcome weary travelers, your voice rings with sweet clarity.

Requirements: 18 Kuthona - 20 Abadius

Benefits: You gain a +1 bonus on Perform (sing) checks, and Perform (sing) becomes a class skill for you.

Usage Add Sing as a subskill of Perform in the character sheet's skills tab. Set Target of bonus to Perform (Sing) in Changes tab. Despite what the sheet tells you, only Perform (Sing) is a class skill (unless your classes give you Perform).

domineering.name: Domineering
domineering.description:
When you were young, you attracted a group of devoted companions thanks to your impressive talent, and the experience inspired in you a powerful selfassurance and air of superiority. Your confidence is so strong that others feel naturally compelled to follow your commands.

Benefits: Choose one 1st-level enchantment spell you are capable of casting. The DC of that spell increases by 1.

Suggested Characters

Chelaxians, Irriseni, Mwangi.

Usage: Increase one enchantment spell's DC by 1.

monastic.name: Monastic
monastic.description:
Either your parents sent you to be raised in a monastery, or you traveled there yourself later in life in order to escape the conf lict and distractions of the greater world. There you learned to eschew the material concerns that plague so many and cloud their minds, instead embracing an ascetic lifestyle. You readily let go and step away from trouble, free from any tie or baggage that might slow your path to enlightenment—figuratively or literally.

Benefits: Once per day as a swift action, you gain a +1 trait bonus to Armor Class as long as you are wearing no armor, not using a shield, carrying a light load, and have nothing in your hands. This bonus lasts for 1 minute.

contextNotes.text: Once per day as a swift action, you gain a +[[1]] trait bonus to Armor Class as long as you are wearing no armor, not using a shield, carrying a light load, and have nothing in your hands. This bonus lasts for 1 minute.

paraheen weaponsmith (qadira).name: Paraheen Weaponsmith (Qadira)
paraheen weaponsmith (qadira).description:
You are a descendant of a long line of skilled dwarven weapon makers or have apprenticed under such artisans. Either way, you learned their long-held secrets relating to the production of magical arms and armor.

Requirements: Qadira

Benefits: You treat your caster level as 1 higher for purposes of creating magical weapons, and gain a +1 trait bonus on Craft checks to create magical armor, shields, or weapons.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to create magical armor, shields, or weapons and you treat your caster level as 1 higher for purposes of creating magical weapon.
You treat your caster level as 1 higher for purposes of creating magical weapon.

larger than life.name: Larger Than Life
larger than life.description:
You wield firearms with truly terrifying menace.

Benefits: When you use the Intimidate skill on a creature while wielding a firearm, you are considered one size category larger than your actual size.

contextNotes.text: You are considered one size category larger than your actual size while you are using a firearm.

rahadoumi disbeliever (rahadoum).name: Rahadoumi Disbeliever (Rahadoum)
rahadoumi disbeliever (rahadoum).description:
As a Rahadoumi who rejects covenants with gods, your belief is strong enough to repel divine spells.

Requirements: Rahadoum

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against divine spells, but you must make a saving throw even when that magic is beneficial to you.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against divine spells, but you must make a saving throw even when that magic is beneficial to you.

born damned (tiefling).name: Born Damned (Tiefling)
born damned (tiefling).description:
The inherent sacrilege that taints your soul sometimes crowds out lesser banes.

Requirements: Tiefling

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against curses and magical effects that produce curses.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against curses and magical effects that produce curses.

deadly rush (tiefling; pitborn).name: Deadly Rush (Tiefling; Pitborn)
deadly rush (tiefling; pitborn).description:
When you hurl yourself against a foe with reckless abandon, you often land particularly telling blows.

Requirements: Pitborn Tiefling Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on critical hit confirmation rolls made as part of a charge.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to critical hit confirmation rolls made as part of a charge.

honored fist of the society (monk, pathf.name: Honored Fist of the Society (Monk, Pathfinder Society)
honored fist of the society (monk, pathf.description:
You have studied dozens of ancient texts on martial arts that only the Society possesses, and are more learned in these arts than most.

Requirements: Monk, Pathfinder Society

Benefits: You increase your ki pool by 1 point.

Usage: On the Features tab of your Actor, edit the "Ki Pool" class feature. On the Details tab of "Ki Pool" class feature, update the "Limited Uses" formula.

scourge of the darkness.name: Scourge of the Darkness
scourge of the darkness.description:
Stories of the depredations present in the Darklands drive you to explore its shadowy depths and purge the corruption from within.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against fear while in the Darklands and a +1 trait bonus on Intimidate checks against creatures native to the Darklands.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against fear while in the Darklands.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks against creatures native to the Darklands.

half-forgotten secrets (dhampir; ajibach.name: Half-Forgotten Secrets (Dhampir; Ajibachana)
half-forgotten secrets (dhampir; ajibach.description:
A whisper of knowledge was transferred to you at the time of your creation.

Requirements: Ajibachana Dhampir Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on checks with two Knowledge skills of your choice, and one of these skills becomes a class skill. 

Usage: Select two Knowledge skills when you take this trait.

Update the target of the two changes on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choices.

Also select one of the two previously selected Knowledge skills and update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect this choice.

kami respect (kitsune).name: Kami Respect (Kitsune)
kami respect (kitsune).description:
Close contact with kami has given you an intuition for how to stay on their good side.

Requirements: Kitsune

Benefits: Kami automatically have a starting attitude one step friendlier toward you, to a maximum of Friendly.

contextNotes.text: Kami automatically have a starting attitude one step friendlier toward you, to a maximum of Friendly.

fruit merchant (jungle).name: Fruit Merchant (Jungle)
fruit merchant (jungle).description:
Requirements: Jungle

Benefits: When bartering for mundane items like food, water, and clothing, you can purchase supplies for 10% less and sell them for 10% more.

creative manipulator (changeling).name: Creative Manipulator (Changeling)
creative manipulator (changeling).description:
Requirements: Changeling

Benefits: Once per day, you may reroll a Charisma check you just made. You must take the result of the second roll, even if it is worse.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you may reroll a Charisma check you just made. You must take the result of the second roll, even if it is worse.([[@resources.creativeManipulatorChangeling.value]] remaining uses)

swamp rebel (wanshou).name: Swamp Rebel (Wanshou)
swamp rebel (wanshou).description:
You grew up hiding in the swamps from the monstrous overlords who enslaved you and your people.

Requirements: Wanshou

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Stealth checks in swampy terrain, and Stealth becomes a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus in swampy terrain.

perseverance (five kings mountains).name: Perseverance (Five Kings Mountains)
perseverance (five kings mountains).description:
Your time in the Five Kings Mountains has taught you that any obstacle can be endured and overcome.

Requirements: Five Kings Mountains

Benefits: When you have more than one Will saving throw to overcome an effect (such as from greater command or hold person, or by using the good fortune ability of the Luck domain), you receive a +3 trait bonus on the extra Will saves against that effect.

contextNotes.text: When you have more than one Will saving throw to overcome an effect you receive a +[[3]] Trait bonus on the extra Will saves against that effect.

azlanti inheritor (human; azlanti).name: Azlanti Inheritor (Human; Azlanti)
azlanti inheritor (human; azlanti).description:
The blood of the ancient, magical empire of Azlant flows in your veins, even if your family members have no knowledge of their mythic pedigree. All your life you've had dreams that seem more like memories of a forgotten age.

Requirements

Human; Azlanti*

Benefits

You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (history) checks and a +2 trait bonus on Appraise checks related to objects of Azlanti origin or made to identify ioun stones.

(*At the GM's discretion, ethnicities other than Azlanti might take this trait, marking a character as a throwback to these legendary people.)

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on checks related to objects of Azlanti origin or made to identify ioun stones.

river sniper (river kingdoms).name: River Sniper (River Kingdoms)
river sniper (river kingdoms).description:
Unlike seagoing pirates, you’re used to fighting most of your ship-to-ship battles at close range, and understand the value of a well-placed arrow.

Requirements: River Kingdoms

Benefits: When on the deck of a waterborne vessel, you gain a +1 trait bonus on attacks with ranged weapons.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attacks with ranged weapons when on the deck of a waterborne vessel.

mathematical prodigy.name: Mathematical Prodigy
mathematical prodigy.description:
Mathematics has always come easily for you, and you have always been able to “see the math” in the physical and magical world.

Benefits: You gain a +1 bonus on Knowledge (arcana) and Knowledge (engineering) checks, and one of these skills (your choice) is always a class skill for you.

Usage: Go to the Details tab on this trait and check either Knowledge (arcana) or Knowledge (engineering).

scorned by magic.name: Scorned by Magic
scorned by magic.description:
As a youth, you assisted a wise woman or witch doctor who removed magical maladies from the needy and was present for the breaking of many spells—or perhaps you passed through a dead magic zone. Whatever the cause, magic seems a little more reluctant to affect you than others.

Benefits: Reduce the caster level of spells and spell-like abilities cast upon you by 1 for the purpose of attempting dispel checks and overcoming your spell resistance (if any).

Suggested Characters

Kellids, Mana Wastes natives, Shoanti.

contextNotes.text: Reduce the caster level of spells and spell-like abilities cast upon you by 1 against spells that attempt to overcome your spell resistance.
Reduce the caster level of spells and spell-like abilities cast upon you by 1 when dispel checks are attempted.

world-weary (ironfang invasion).name: World-Weary (Ironfang Invasion)
world-weary (ironfang invasion).description:
You’ve seen the horrors of war, and had hoped you’d seen the end of it. You’ve retreated from the fighting—likely from the front line with Molthune, but maybe from the crusade of Lastwall or the political infighting of Ustalav—and now just want to protect and provide for those you care about.

Benefits: You gain one of the following as a permanent class skill: Appraise, Heal, Knowledge (history), Sense Motive, or Survival. When you perform an aid another action to improve a creature’s Armor Class, you increase its AC by +4 rather than +2.

Usage: Select Appraise, Heal, Knowledge (history), Sense Motive, or Survival when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: When you perform an aid another action to improve a creature’s Armor Class, you increase its AC by +[[4]] rather than +2.

fight with the flock (skinwalker; wereba.name: Fight with the Flock (Skinwalker; Werebat-Kin)
fight with the flock (skinwalker; wereba.description:
You have always been most comfortable in a group. Since awakening to your werebat powers, this feeling has only grown.

Requirements: Werebat-Kin Skinwalker Heritage

Benefits: As long as you are within 10 feet of at least two allies, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Intimidate checks and attack rolls to make attack of opportunity.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus as long as you are within 10 feet of at least two allies.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on attack rolls to make attacks of opportunity  as long as you are within 10 feet of at least two allies.

ghost survivor (ustalav).name: Ghost Survivor (Ustalav)
ghost survivor (ustalav).description:
You spent a single night in Harrowstone and managed to survive the terrifying ordeal, though you bore witness to ghosts and numerous other horrors during your short stay.

Requirements: Ustalav

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Knowledge (religion) checks to identify incorporeal undead and a +2 trait bonus on Fortitude saving throws against effects that inflict negative levels.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus  to identify incorporeal undead
+[[2]] Trait bonus against effects that inflict negative levels.

greater link.name: Greater Link
greater link.description:
The link between you and your eidolon is stronger than most.

Benefits: Whenever you succeed at a Knowledge (planes) check to identify a creature and its special powers or vulnerabilities, you gain one additional piece of useful information (as if your skill check result had been 5 higher).

contextNotes.text: Gain one additional piece of useful information whenever you succeed on the check.

fierce flanker (wyvaran).name: Fierce Flanker (Wyvaran)
fierce flanker (wyvaran).description:
You were bullied in your youth but supported by one or more good friends, impressing upon you the importance of teamwork.

Requirements: Wyvaran

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on damage rolls against foes you flank.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on damage rolls against foes you flank.

irreverent.name: Irreverent
irreverent.description:
You are suspicious of those who lead spiritual lives and are quick to seek out the smallest hypocrisy in even the most pious individuals. Of course, you are also keenly aware of the power the faithful can draw from their worship, and you are stubbornly resistant to it.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against divine spells that target only you.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against divine spells that target only you.

subject of study (carrion crown).name: Subject of Study (Carrion Crown)
subject of study (carrion crown).description:
Professor Lorrimor approached you as part of his studies, as he had heard that you had survived a recent encounter with a strange monster or had another fateful encounter. Interested in the conditions of the run-in and the means by which you avoided death or injury, he met with you and maintained frequent correspondence until several months ago. The scars of your experience and his continual reminders of the encounter prompted you to hone your skills lest you someday face the same type of creature again. The professor assisted you in this endeavor, providing you with insight into the anatomy and defenses of the creature that attacked you. Years of study have improved your combat effectiveness against your chosen foe.

Benefits: Choose a non-humanoid creature type (and subtype if outsider). You gain a +1 bonus on damage rolls against creatures of this type.

Usage: Select one non-humanoid creature type (and subtype if outsider) when you take this trait.

Update the text of the note on the changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] bonus on damage rolls against creatures of the <chosen> type.

battlefield caster (angradd, gorum, tora.name: Battlefield Caster (Angradd, Gorum, Torag, Trudd)
battlefield caster (angradd, gorum, tora.description:
Your faith protects you even as you unleash spells in the thick of battle.

Requirements: Angradd, Gorum, Torag, or Trudd

Benefits: You receive a +1 trait bonus on concentration checks to cast defensively and a +1 trait bonus to AC against attacks provoked by or readied against spellcasting.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to cast defensively.
+[[1]] Trait bonus against attacks provoked by or readied against spellcasting.

spirit of the law.name: Spirit of the Law
spirit of the law.description:
You have seen the rules of society endlessly twisted in their implementation, and as such, you are especially wary of those who exploit the law to impose their own will.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attacks against lawful-aligned humanoids and a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against effects with the lawful descriptor.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against lawful-aligned humanoids.
+[[2]] Trait bonus against effects with the lawful descriptor.

wayang spellhunter (minata).name: Wayang Spellhunter (Minata)
wayang spellhunter (minata).description:
You grew up on one of the wayang-populated islands of Minata, and your use of magic while hunting has been a boon to you.

Requirements: Minata

Benefits: Select a spell of 3rd level or below. When you use the chosen spell with a metamagic feat, it uses up a spell slot one level lower than it normally would.

the follower.name: The Follower
the follower.description:
You are born under the sign of the dark figure that trails the Caravan—the specter of death itself.

Requirements: 30 Neth - 17 Kuthona

Benefits: You receive a +2 bonus on saving throws against death effects. All allies within 10 feet (3 meters) of you take a –1 penalty on saving throws against death effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] bonus against death effects; allies within 10 feet (3 meters) take a -[[1]] Penalty against death effects.

rider of paresh (qadira).name: Rider of Paresh (Qadira)
rider of paresh (qadira).description:
You call the Plains of Paresh home, whether you were born among the plains' tribes or in the glittering towers of Katheer. The horses are your kin.

Requirements: Qadira

You must have the @UUID[Compendium.pf1.feats.Item.0spsP9ylE3goD3jc]{Mounted Combat} feat to take this trait.

Benefits: When mounted and making a charge, your mount's speed is increased by 10 feet. You must have the Mounted Combat feat to take this trait.

eyes of the wild (green faith).name: Eyes of the Wild (Green Faith)
eyes of the wild (green faith).description:
Having spent much time immersed in the wilderness, you’re now attuned to its ways.

Requirements: Green Faith

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Perception checks in natural settings.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus in natural settings.

disillusioned.name: Disillusioned
disillusioned.description:
Your childhood was ended by a great disaster or atrocity that you witnessed. To cope with the horror, you have learned to reflexively suppress all emotion.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Will saving throws against emotion and fear effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against emotion and fear effects

aspect of the quah.name: Aspect of the Quah
aspect of the quah.description:
You call upon your quah’s totem to grant you power.

Benefits: You gain a unique totem aspect that replaces one of the animal aspects granted to you by the animal focus feature. You must be a hunter or have access to the animal aspect class feature to select this trait, and you must choose the Shoanti quah to which you belong. You gain access to the totem aspect listed for that clan, and you lose access to the animal aspect listed. Once made, this choice cannot be changed.

Cliff (Shadde-Quah, Frog)

The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Acrobatics checks to avoid falling and on Constitution checks to avoid drowning. This bonus increases to +4 at 8th level and +6 at 15th level.

Emberstorm (Sklar-Quah, Bear)

The creature gains fire resistance 5. This increases to fire resistance 10 at 8th level and fire resistance 15 at 15th level.

Horse (Shriikirri-Quah, Owl)

The creature gains a +4 competence bonus on Ride checks. This bonus increases to +6 at 8th level and +8 at 15th level.

Star (Lyrune-Quah, Falcon)

The creature gains low-light vision and a +2 competence bonus on Perception checks. This bonus increases to +4 at 8th level and +6 at 15th level.

Storm (Tamiir-Quah, Snake)

The creature gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against cold dangers, disease, heat dangers, starvation, and thirst. This bonus increases to +6 at 8th level and +8 at 15th level.

Storm Roc (Shundar-Quah, Mouse)

The creature gains a +2 enhancement bonus to Charisma. This bonus increases to +4 at 8th level and +6 at 15th level.

Will-o’-Wisp (Skoan-Quah, Wolf )

The creature gains +2 bonus on saving throws against fear effects and mindaffecting effects. This bonus increases to +4 at 8th level and +6 at 15th level.

shadow whispers (norgorber).name: Shadow Whispers (Norgorber)
shadow whispers (norgorber).description:
You have a penchant for just knowing others’ secrets.

Requirements: Norgorber

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (local) checks and a +2 trait bonus on Intimidate checks made to demoralize opponents.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to demoralize opponents.

skilled telepath (lashunta).name: Skilled Telepath (Lashunta)
skilled telepath (lashunta).description:
Requirements: Lashunta

Benefits: The range of your limited telepathy racial ability increases by 5 feet.

viking blood (lands of the linnorm kings.name: Viking Blood (Lands of the Linnorm Kings)
viking blood (lands of the linnorm kings.description:
You have the imposing build of a Viking, and people of the south fear your unpredictable nature.

Requirements: Lands of the Linnorm Kings

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Intimidate checks, and Intimidate is always a class skill for you.

foul brand (hand).name: Foul Brand (Hand)
foul brand (hand).description: Effects: You have the symbol of an evil deity burned into your flesh. If the symbol is on your hand, you take a –1 penalty on Disable Device, Disguise, and Sleight of Hand checks. If the symbol is on your face, you take a –2 penalty on Bluff, Diplomacy, and Disguise checks. This does not count as a holy symbol for the purposes of a divine focus for spellcasting.

whole-hearted (averaka).name: Whole-Hearted (Averaka)
whole-hearted (averaka).description:
Though most outsiders expect your mind to be conflicted, full of self-doubt, and mired in resentment and suspicion, you’re truly comfortable in your skin.

Requirements: Averaka

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against charms and compulsions.
