contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against charms and compulsions.

deadeye (half-orc, orc).name: Deadeye (Half-Orc, Orc)
deadeye (half-orc, orc).description:
You have only one working eye, but that eye is especially keen.

Requirements: Half-Orc, Orc

Benefits: You receive a +2 trait bonus on all Perception checks involving sight.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on all checks involving sight.

knowing the enemy.name: Knowing the Enemy
knowing the enemy.description:
You know a good deal about your most dire enemies.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on checks with the Knowledge skill corresponding to your first favored enemy choice, and that Knowledge skill is always a class skill for you.

Usage: To benefit from this trait you must have a @UUID[Compendium.pf1.class-abilities.Item.XmALhWqXdN9yOmtd]{Favored enemy}, like the Ranger class feature, from some source.

On the Changes tab, update the Target of the change to the Knowledge skill that corresponds to your (first) Favored enemy choice's Creature type

Favored Enemy Creature type

Knowledge Skill type

Aberrations

Dungeoneering

Animals

Nature

Constructs

Arcana

Dragons

Arcana

Fey

Nature

Humanoids

Local

Magical Beasts

Arcana

Monstrous Humanoids

Nature

Oozes

Dungeoneering

Outsiders

Planes

Plants

Nature

Undead

Religion

Vermin

Nature

amoral mercenary (hell's vengeance).name: Amoral Mercenary (Hell's Vengeance)
amoral mercenary (hell's vengeance).description:
You have always looked after yourself first, and done whatever you need to do to survive. You’ve killed before, and you’ll kill again, but you don’t lose sleep over it. If it’s you or them, you invariably choose yourself. You follow no code, unless it’s doing whatever brings you to the top. Of course, sometimes you have to work with others, and that’s fine too, as long as the pay is right—whatever it takes to get the job done. With the current unrest in Cheliax, it’s starting to look like a good deal to work with House Thrune. You know Thrune rewards those who serve it well, and there’s no one else in Cheliax who can offer the power, prestige, and money that Thrune can.

Requirements: Neutral evil

Benefits: You’ve got a keen eye for what makes a good arrangement and when to walk away from a bad deal. You gain a +1 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks, and Sense Motive is a class skill for you. In addition, as an immediate action once per day, you can shift your effective alignment so that you are considered neutral instead of evil for the purpose of good effects that target evil creatures (such as holy smite or a paladin’s smite evil ability). Your alignment does not actually change when you use this ability.

holdout.name: Holdout
holdout.description:
You were robbed at a young age by bullies, an experience that impressed upon you the importance of being able to hold on to whatever you cared to keep.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus to CMD against disarm and steal combat maneuvers.

Suggested Characters

Half-orcs, River Kingdoms natives, Varisians

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against disarm and steal combat maneuvers.

strength of submission.name: Strength of Submission
strength of submission.description:
Your faith in the church of Razmir and willingness to surrender yourself to him lend you strength and clarity.

Benefits: Whenever you are under the effects of a compulsion effect, you gain a +1 trait bonus on attack and damage rolls.

contextNotes.text: When under the effects of a compulsion effect, you gain a +[[1]] trait bonus on attack and damage rolls.

zealous (human; garundi).name: Zealous (Human; Garundi)
zealous (human; garundi).description:
You felt lost and aimless in your youth until a patron’s emotional intervention showed you the power of throwing yourself completely into what you do.

Requirements: Garundi Human Heritage

Benefits: Once per day, you can add a +2 trait bonus on any attack roll, saving throw, or skill check. You must choose to apply this bonus before rolling the die. When you use this ability, you take a –2 penalty to your AC and on saving throws until the end of your next turn.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you can add a +[[2]] Trait bonus on any attack roll, saving throw, or skill check. You must choose to apply this bonus before rolling the die. When you use this ability, you take a -[[2]] penalty to your AC and on saving throws until the end of your next turn.

Once per day, you can add a +[[2]] Trait bonus on any attack roll, saving throw, or skill check. You must choose to apply this bonus before rolling the die. When you use this ability, you take a -[[2]] penalty to your AC and on saving throws until the end of your next turn.

Once per day, you can add a +[[2]] Trait bonus on any attack roll, saving throw, or skill check. You must choose to apply this bonus before rolling the die. When you use this ability, you take a -[[2]] penalty to your AC and on saving throws until the end of your next turn.

Once per day, you can add a +[[2]] Trait bonus on any attack roll, saving throw, or skill check. You must choose to apply this bonus before rolling the die. When you use this ability, you take a -[[2]] penalty to your AC and on saving throws until the end of your next turn.

spell intuition.name: Spell Intuition
spell intuition.description:
Blessed by a priest of a deity of magic at birth, you demonstrated a remarkable knack for guessing the nature of spells without the schooling of your instructors.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Spellcraft checks, and Spellcraft becomes a class skill for you.

Suggested Characters

Gebbites, Nethys worshipers, Nexians.

tattooed focus (human; varisian).name: Tattooed Focus (Human; Varisian)
tattooed focus (human; varisian).description:
You possess an intricate tattoo that proclaims your skill at your chosen vocation.

Requirements: Varisian Human Heritage

Benefits: Choose a Craft, Perform, or Profession skill. You gain a +2 trait bonus on checks with that skill, and it is always a class skill for you.

Usage

Select one of skills permitted [Craft (any one), Perform (any one), or Profession (any one)] when you take this trait.

Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the chosen skill.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

nontraditional native (shattered star).name: Nontraditional Native (Shattered Star)
nontraditional native (shattered star).description:
You are a member of the Shoanti or Varisian ethnicity who left your people to learn more about the mysteries of your strange homeland. Your travels have recently brought you to the Pathfinder lodge in Magnimar. Although none of your family members are present, you carry them and your family traditions with you.

Benefits: If you are a Varisian, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (geography) checks made relating to Varisia and gain a +1 trait bonus on damage rolls made during the surprise round when you're wielding a bladed scarf or starknife.

If you are a Shoanti, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Survival checks made while in Varisia and gain a +1 trait bonus on rolls to confirm critical hits made using an earth breaker or a klar.

Usage: If you are a Varisian,

  1. Update the target of the Context note on the changes tab of this trait marked as Varisian 1 to Knowledge (geography)

  2. Update the target of the Context note on the changes tab of this trait marked as Varisian 2 to Attack Rolls

  3. Delete both context notes relating to Shoanti options

If instead you are a Shoanti,

  1. Update the target of the Context note on the changes tab of this trait marked as Shoanti 1 to Survival

  2. Update the target of the Context note on the changes tab of this trait marked as Shoanti 2 to Attack Rolls

  3. Delete both context notes relating to Varisian options

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on checks made relating to Varisia. (Varisian 1)
+[[1]] Trait bonus on damage rolls made during the surprise round when you're wielding a bladed scarf or starknife.   (Varisian 2)
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks made while in Varisia.  (Shoanti 1)
+[[1]] Trait bonus on rolls to confirm critical hits made using an earth breaker or a klar.   (Shoanti 2)

poverty-stricken.name: Poverty-Stricken
poverty-stricken.description:
Your childhood was tough, and your parents had to make every copper piece count. Hunger was your constant companion, and you often had to live off the land or sleep in the wild.

Benefits: You gain a +1 bonus on Survival checks, and Survival is always a class skill for you.

business venturer (ratfolk).name: Business Venturer (Ratfolk)
business venturer (ratfolk).description:
Your mercantile pursuits have sent you across Golarion, making you better traveled than most ratfolk.

Requirements: Ratfolk

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Appraise, Knowledge (local), and Profession (merchant) checks, and one of these skills (your choice) is a class skill for you.

Usage: Select one (1) of Appraise, Knowledge (local), and Profession (merchant) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the Profession (merchant) skill.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

monument scholar (shattered star).name: Monument Scholar (Shattered Star)
monument scholar (shattered star).description:
As a child in Magnimar, the city's ancient monuments inspired you with wonder. Your fascination with these relics has only grown in the years since, encouraging you to join the ranks of the Pathfinder Society so you can further quench your thirst for knowledge. Though you know little about the actual lore of the monuments, you've studied these colossal works extensively, developing an in-depth knowledge of some of the city's most famous landmarks.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (history) checks made in Varisia due to your familiarity with its historical remnants. This bonus increases to +2 if the check relates to the city of Magnimar. Additionally, you gain a +2 trait bonus on any skill check made to receive a boon from one of Magnimar's magically imbued monuments.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus when in Varisia. This bonus increases to +[[2]] if the check relates to the city of Magnimar.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to receive a boon from Magnimar's monuments

beachcomber (coastal or river).name: Beachcomber (Coastal or River)
beachcomber (coastal or river).description:
You have a knack for finding what you need as it washes ashore.

Requirements: Coastal or River

Benefits: Once per week, if you spend 8 hours in a single day searching along a shoreline, you can attempt a Survival check. You find any combination of mundane objects whose value in gold pieces is less than or equal to the result of the check, in a usable (if somewhat waterlogged) condition.

contextNotes.text: Once per week, if you spend 8 hours in a single day searching along a shoreline, you can attempt a Survival check. You find any combination of mundane objects whose value in gold pieces is less than or equal to the result of the check, in a usable (if somewhat waterlogged) condition.

selective health (aasimar).name: Selective Health (Aasimar)
selective health (aasimar).description:
You are resistant to most illnesses that strike those around you, but vulnerable to the contagions of evil.

Requirements: Aasimar

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Fortitude saves against diseases. However, whenever you must make a Fortitude saving throw against a disease inflicted by an undead creature or evil outsider (such as mummy rot or a vrock's spores), you do not gain this bonus, and instead take a -2 penalty on your Fortitude save.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against diseases.
-[[2]] penalty  against a disease inflicted by an undead creature or evil outsider (such as mummy rot or a vrock's spores).

shaper of reality (any eldest).name: Shaper of Reality (any Eldest)
shaper of reality (any eldest).description:
You can mimic the Eldest’s ability to reshape the world.

Requirements: Any of the Eldest

Benefits: Once per day, you can cast either a conjuration spell or a transmutation spell at +1 caster level.

guerrilla tactics.name: Guerrilla Tactics
guerrilla tactics.description:
Benefits

You gain a +2 trait bonus on Stealth checks and Initiative checks in forests.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus in forests.
+[[2]] Trait bonus in forests.

tough skin (tiefling; beastbrood).name: Tough Skin (Tiefling; Beastbrood)
tough skin (tiefling; beastbrood).description:
You retain a small amount of your ancestors' unyielding skin.

Requirements: Beastblood Tiefling Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus to AC when opponents attempt to confirm critical hits with either bludgeoning or slashing weapons.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus  to AC when opponents attempt to confirm critical hits with either bludgeoning or slashing weapons.

animal rustler (orc).name: Animal Rustler (Orc)
animal rustler (orc).description:
You frequently helped capture and wrangle dangerous animals and megafauna for your tribe and you had to control them well enough to avoid being killed.

Requirements: Orc

Benefits: Once per day, you can add your Strength modifier in addition to your Charisma modifier when attempting a Handle Animal check.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you can add your Strength modifier [[@abilities.str.mod]], in addition to your Charisma modifier, when attempting a Handle Animal check.

magical flair.name: Magical Flair
magical flair.description:
At a young age, you saw a witch burned by fearful peasants, druids falsely punished for demon worship, or another sort of spellcaster persecuted for merely practicing her dubious craft. Intent on not suffering the same fate, you approach spellcasting with a powerful desire to control how your magic is perceived by others. You can cause spells you cast to manifest with strange visual, auditory, or olfactory features that make them hard to identify.

Benefits: Choose a school of magic. The Spellcraft DC to identify spells you cast from that school of magic increases by 2. Onlookers who fail a Spellcraft check to identify your spell by 2 or less incorrectly identify the spell as another randomly selected spell of the same school and level.

Suggested Characters

Gnomes, Razmir worshipers, witches.

Usage: add choosen school in context note of Changes tab.

contextNotes.text: Spellcraft DC made to identify spells from the school you've chosen increases by 2. Onlookers who fail a Spellcraft check to identify your spell by 2 or less incorrectly identify the spell as another randomly selected spell of the same school and level.

teacher's pet (carrion crown).name: Teacher's Pet (Carrion Crown)
teacher's pet (carrion crown).description:
Professor Lorrimor traveled the Inner Sea region lecturing and teaching at universities in locations as far-flung as Manaket and Magnimar, and as different in scope as the bardic colleges of Taldor and the battle colleges of the River Kingdoms. Speaking before standing-room-only lecture halls on topics as widely ranging as military strategy, planar anomalies, theology, and agriculture, Lorrimor's time was highly valued among those ambitious to benefit from his expertise. Despite this, he still managed to provide one-on-one assistance to the most promising of his acolytes. When he saw the potential for greatness in one of his charges, he took it upon himself to nurture their spark. You were such a student. Over the course of several months, you and the late professor spent hours debating the finer points of your topic of interest, and the intellectual doors he opened for you continue to flavor your outlook on the world.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus to one Knowledge skill of your choosing and consider it a class skill.

Usage: Select Knowledge (any 1) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

expert swimmer (river kingdoms).name: Expert Swimmer (River Kingdoms)
expert swimmer (river kingdoms).description:
Most pirates on the ocean view falling overboard as a death sentence, but in the River Kingdoms, it’s often a key part of the strategy.

Requirements: River Kingdoms

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Swim checks, and it becomes a class skill for you.

self-sacrifice (shelyn; halfling).name: Self-Sacrifice (Shelyn; Halfling)
self-sacrifice (shelyn; halfling).description:
You honor your goddess through your willingness to take risks for those you care about.

Requirements: Shelyn; Halfling

Benefits: As a move action, you can use your own body to grant soft cover to an adjacent ally regardless of the difference in your respective sizes.

drake anatomist.name: Drake Anatomist
drake anatomist.description: Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on damage rolls against creatures with the dragon creature type, and a +2 trait bonus on Knowledge (arcana) checks to identify dragons and their special powers and vulnerabilities.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on damage rolls against creatures with the dragon creature type.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to identify dragons and their special powers and vulnerabilities.

child of nature (gozreh).name: Child of Nature (Gozreh)
child of nature (gozreh).description:
You have been blessed by Gozreh to be as comfortable in the wilderness as you are at home.

Requirements: Gozreh

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Survival checks to find food and water, and a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (nature) checks. One of these skills (your choice) is always a class skill for you.

Usage: Select either Survival or Knowledge (nature), when selecting this trait. Go to the Details tab on this trait, and check the selected choice under Class Skills.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to find food and water.

oppressive expectations.name: Oppressive Expectations
oppressive expectations.description:
You were raised with the belief that your family was special, and that those of your status always succeed at whatever they do. Due to your concern about upholding the family name and your memories of the consequences for disappointing them (such as violence or unsettling disapproval), you are easily frustrated.

Effects: When you fail at a skill check, you take a –2 penalty on checks with that skill until you succeed or until you fail at a different skill check, whichever comes first.

contextNotes.text: When you fail at a skill check, you take a -[[2]] penalty on checks with that skill until you succeed or until you fail at a different skill check, whichever comes first.

vigilant spycatcher.name: Vigilant Spycatcher
vigilant spycatcher.description:
Paranoid parents or a military trainer taught you to keep vigilant for spies.

Benefits: If you roll less than 10 on Perception checks to see through disguises, determine your result as if you had rolled 10.

contextNotes.text: If you roll less than 10 on Perception checks to see through disguises, determine your result as if you had rolled 10.

love lost (curse of the crimson throne).name: Love Lost (Curse of the Crimson Throne)
love lost (curse of the crimson throne).description:
Someone you loved was knifed to death in a dark alley one night. You were called to the scene by the Korvosan Guard to identify the body, and as rough as that was for you, you also noticed a ring was missing from the body. Whoever murdered your loved one stole that ring,you're convinced of it. You've done some investigation on your own and recently found the ring for sale at a local merchant. Although, to your great frustration, you can't afford yet to buy it back, the merchant did tell you from whom he purchased the ring: a man named Gaedren Lamm. It seems likely this criminal is the one who killed your loved one, or at the very least knows who did. The only problem is finding him.

Benefits

Choose one of the following benefits:

All Alone

The murder victim was a lover. With your lover's death, a part of you died as well, leaving you haunted, grim, and prone to dark musings. You gain a +1 bonus on Intimidate checks, and Intimidate is a class skill for you.

Orphaned

The murder victim was your only surviving parent.You had to work hard to make ends meet for yourself and any siblings, and often had to scavenge for food. You gain a +1 trait bonus on Survival checks, and Survival is a class skill for you.

Usage

Select All Alone or Orphaned when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect the Skill associated with your choice.

Update the target of the Change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect the Skill associated with your choice.

nexian corpse hunter (nex).name: Nexian Corpse Hunter (Nex)
nexian corpse hunter (nex).description:
After years spent fighting the undead legions of Geb, you have developed a knack for killing undead creatures with magic.

Requirements: Nex

Benefits: You gain a +1 bonus on damage rolls from your spells against undead.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] bonus on damage rolls against undead.

restless wayfarer (reign of winter).name: Restless Wayfarer (Reign of Winter)
restless wayfarer (reign of winter).description:
You have long led a nomadic life—perhaps because your parents were travelers (whether roaming Varisian caravaneers or traveling merchants who traded far and wide), you belonged to a nomadic tribe, or you ran away from home to discover the world at a young age. Some call it wanderlust, but to you the thought of new places and experiences is truly what makes life worth living, and no region catches your imagination like the windswept wilderness of the North. You are used to getting along in unfamiliar lands and interacting with interesting new people.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (geography) and Knowledge (local) checks, and one of these skills (your choice) is a class skill for you. You can also speak one additional language (this does not count toward your number of languages).

Usage

  1. Select Knowledge (geography) or Knowledge (local) when you take this trait.

  2. Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

  3. Select one additional language when you take this trait.

  4. Update the Granted Proficiencies>Languages section of the details tab of this trait to reflect this choice.

arcane prodigy (drow).name: Arcane Prodigy (Drow)
arcane prodigy (drow).description:
Requirements: Drow;

Benefits: Once per day, as an immediate action, you can double the duration of one of your racial spell-like abilities, as if using the @UUID[Compendium.pf1.feats.Item.btXWioB3SKJv8YEG]{Extend Spell} metamagic feat.

secret keeper.name: Secret Keeper
secret keeper.description:
Avoiding inquisitors and agents of your enemies has required you to become a practiced liar.

Benefits: You gain a +3 trait bonus on Bluff checks when they are opposed by another’s Sense Motive check.

contextNotes.text: +[[3]] trait when opposed by Sense Motive

spiritual attachment.name: Spiritual Attachment
spiritual attachment.description:
The union between spirits and spiritualists is one of the strongest (and strangest) bonds shared between the mortal realm and the spirit world. Spiritualists can use the knowledge of their linked phantoms to better negotiate with other spirits. Whether you’re a spiritualist or not, you’ve always had a similar knack when it comes to interactions with the spirit world.

Benefits: You gain a +4 trait bonus on checks to notice haunts, and when interacting with someone who is possessed by an incorporeal undead creature, you gain a +2 trait bonus on Bluff, Diplomacy, Intimidate, and Sense Motive checks against that target.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus when interacting with someone who is possessed by an incorporeal undead creature.
+[[2]] Trait bonus when interacting with someone who is possessed by an incorporeal undead creature.
+[[2]] Trait bonus when interacting with someone who is possessed by an incorporeal undead creature.
+[[2]] Trait bonus when interacting with someone who is possessed by an incorporeal undead creature.
+[[4]] Trait bonus to notice haunts.

infernal influence (human).name: Infernal Influence (Human)
infernal influence (human).description:
Your family boasts secret diabolical ties that may go back many generations.

Requirements: Human

Benefits: You gain fire resistance 1 and a +1 trait bonus on Fortitude saves against poison.

Usage: Update your Actor, the Attributes tab>energy resistances, to add fire resistance 1.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against poison.

balloon headed (goblin).name: Balloon Headed (Goblin)
balloon headed (goblin).description:
Your head is particularly wide and large, even for a goblin.

Requirements: Goblin

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Perception checks, and Perception is always a class skill for you. Any Escape Artist checks that requires you to squeeze your head through a tight space take a –8 penalty.

contextNotes.text: –[[8]] Penalty to squeeze your head through a tight space.

memorable.name: Memorable
memorable.description:
Growing up amid a large number of children, you learned quickly how to get others’ attention and keep it.

Benefits: When you modify a character’s attitude with the Diplomacy or Intimidate skill, the attitude change lasts 1-1/2 times longer than it otherwise would.

Whenever you create a fear or mind-affecting effect that imposes a penalty or bonus with a duration of at least 2 rounds, it lasts 1 additional round after it would normally end.

For example, if you demoralize a foe with an Intimidate check and beat the DC by 5, the victim is shaken for 3 rounds instead of the usual 2 rounds.

Suggested Characters

Free Captains, gnomes, Keleshites.

contextNotes.text: When you modify a character’s attitude, the attitude change lasts 1-1/2 times longer than it otherwise would.

When you modify a character’s attitude, the attitude change lasts 1-1/2 times longer than it otherwise would. 

Whenever you create a fear or mind-affecting effect that imposes a penalty or bonus with a duration of at least 2 rounds, it lasts 1 additional round after it would normally end.

brevoy bandit (mivon).name: Brevoy Bandit (Mivon)
brevoy bandit (mivon).description:
You fled the persecution of Brevoy authorities and wound up in Mivon. Whether or not you are guilty of your alleged crimes, you seek to build a new life amid new neighbors.

Requirements: Mivon

Benefits: Choose one Profession skill and one ability score other than Wisdom. You add that ability score’s modifier in addition to your Wisdom modifier on checks with the chosen Profession skill.

Usage

Select one Profession skill and one ability score other than Wisdom when you take this trait.

Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the chosen skill.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

Update the formula of the change to one of the 5 options below reflecting the selected ability score (other than Wisdom)

@abilities.str.mod

@abilities.dex.mod

@abilities.con.mod

@abilities.int.mod

@abilities.cha.mod

jinx eater (tengu; shackles).name: Jinx Eater (Tengu; Shackles)
jinx eater (tengu; shackles).description:
Your presence dampens curses and other ill effects.

Requirements: Tengu native to the Shackles

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saves against curses, including the hex class ability of shamans and witches. Once per day, you can grant this bonus to all allies within 30 feet for 1 round as an immediate action.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on saves against curses, including the hex class ability of shamans and witches.

brewmaster (dwarf).name: Brewmaster (Dwarf)
brewmaster (dwarf).description:
Your family brought the secrets of dwarven brewing to non-dwarven lands. Though this has given you skill in the brewer’s craft, it’s also earned you distrust among dwarven purists.

Requirements: Dwarf

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Profession (brewer) and Craft (alchemy) checks, but you take a –1 penalty on Diplomacy checks made to change the attitude of dwarves who know that your family has shared brewing secrets with non-dwarves.

Usage: Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the Profession (brewer) and Craft (alchemy) skills.

Update the target of the changes on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect Profession (brewer) and Craft (alchemy).

contextNotes.text: -[[1]] penalty  to change the attitude of dwarves who know that your family has shared brewing secrets with non-dwarves.

vandal.name: Vandal
vandal.description: Benefits: You gain a +2 bonus on Strength checks to break objects, and when damaging an object with a weapon, natural weapon, or unarmed attack, you ignore 2 points of its hardness.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Bonus to break objects.

brigand (kingmaker).name: Brigand (Kingmaker)
brigand (kingmaker).description:
You hail from the River Kingdoms or the more lawless reaches of Brevoy. Life has been hard for you. Perhaps your parents and siblings were crooks and con artists, or maybe your rough, lonely life lead you to fall in with thieves and worse. You know how to ambush travelers, bully traders, avoid the law, and camp where no one might find you. Recently, you've run into some trouble, either with the law or with other bandits, and you're looking to get away to somewhere no one would ever think to look for you. An expedition into the rugged wilderness seems like a perfect way to lie low until the trouble blows over.

Benefits: You begin the campaign with an extra 100 gp in ill-gotten gains. You also gain a +1 trait bonus on Bluff, Diplomacy, Intimidate, and Sense Motive checks when dealing with brigands, thieves, bandits, and their ilk.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus when dealing with brigands, thieves, bandits, and their ilk.
+[[1]] Trait bonus when dealing with brigands, thieves, bandits, and their ilk.
+[[1]] Trait bonus when dealing with brigands, thieves, bandits, and their ilk.
+[[1]] Trait bonus when dealing with brigands, thieves, bandits, and their ilk.

shoanti tribesman (varisia).name: Shoanti Tribesman (Varisia)
shoanti tribesman (varisia).description:
You were born or adopted into a Shoanti tribe.

Requirements: Varisia

Benefits: Choose one of the following skills: Climb, Survival, or Swim.You gain a +1 trait bonus on this skill, and it is always a class skill for you.

Usage: Select Climb, Survival, or Swim when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

kin guardian.name: Kin Guardian
kin guardian.description:
You are dedicated to defending members of your family.

Benefits: When you use the aid another action to give a member of your family a bonus to AC, increase the bonus by 2. This increase is a trait bonus (and therefore doesn’t stack with increases granted by other family members using this trait). This trait has no effect when using the aid another action to increase a family member’s next attack roll.

contextNotes.text: When you use the aid another action to give a member of your family a bonus to AC, increase the bonus by 2. This increase is a trait bonus.

fey protection.name: Fey Protection
fey protection.description:
Your birth brought an unexpected guest to your parents’ home: a helpful fey who prophesied about your future and gave you a magical gift in exchange for your parents’ hospitality, a cup of milk, and a drop of your blood.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus to AC against attacks of opportunities made by fey and a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against the effects of fey creatures.

Suggested Characters

Drumans, Eldest worshipers, Ulfen.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against attacks of opportunities made by fey creatures.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on saving throws against the effects of fey creatures.

skilled surgeon (qadira, ustalav; alchem.name: Skilled Surgeon (Qadira, Ustalav; Alchemist)
skilled surgeon (qadira, ustalav; alchem.description:
Requirements: Alchemist healers who hail from Qadira or Ustalav, or who have lived there for at least 1 year

Benefits: Whenever you perform surgery or otherwise must deal damage to remove a condition or affliction, you roll the damage but use the minimum result unless you rolled the maximum result. The amount of hit points or points of ability damage restored each day when you provide long-term care is increased by 1.

contextNotes.text: Whenever you perform surgery or otherwise must deal damage to remove a condition or affliction, you roll the damage but use the minimum result unless you rolled the maximum result. The amount of hit points or points of ability damage restored each day when you provide long-term care is increased by 1.

hard to kill.name: Hard to Kill
hard to kill.description:
Your strong will to live and spread good combined with your pure physicality makes you a tough opponent to take down. You may have discovered this as a child after a tragic accident or during the course of your first battle.

Benefits: When you are attempting a Constitution check to stabilize when dying, the penalty on the check is only half your negative hit point total instead of your full negative hit point total.

contextNotes.text: Stabilize DC Stabilize DC [[max(10,10-ceil(@attributes.hp.value/2))]] due to @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.ZIVapxN3deRaIc7F]{Hard to Kill}.

caravan drover (half-orc).name: Caravan Drover (Half-Orc)
caravan drover (half-orc).description:
With your hand on the lash, your caravans always arrive on time.

Requirements: Half-Orc

Benefits: You treat whips as martial weapons, and up to 10 animals and humanoids traveling with you can hustle or make a forced march for 1 hour longer than normal before taking nonlethal damage and becoming fatigued.

Usage: if your actor has Martial Weapon proficiency, in the granted proficiencies section of the details tab of this trait, edit the weapon proficiencies and add Whip.

beneficent touch (silver crusade).name: Beneficent Touch (Silver Crusade)
beneficent touch (silver crusade).description:
Requirements: Silver Crusade

Benefits: Once per day, when you cast a spell or use a class ability that heals hit point damage, reroll any 1s that appear on the dice and take the new roll (even if it is another 1).

obsessed with success (human; chelaxian).name: Obsessed with Success (Human; Chelaxian)
obsessed with success (human; chelaxian).description:
A Chelaxian without a visible degree of success is marked as pathetic, and so you grew up careful not to let failure stick.

Requirements: Chelaxian Human Heritage

Benefits: Once per day, when you re-attempt a skill check you have previously failed within the last minute, you can add a +4 trait bonus to that check.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, when you re-attempt a skill check you have previously failed within the last minute, you can add a +[[4]] Trait bonus to that check.

tempestuous combatant (human; keleshite).name: Tempestuous Combatant (Human; Keleshite)
tempestuous combatant (human; keleshite).description:
You move confidently in the face of danger.

Requirements: Keleshite Human Heritage

Benefits: Once per day as a swift action, you can choose one opponent. You and your mount (if any) do not provoke attacks of opportunity from that opponent for moving within or exiting his threatened area.

contextNotes.text: Once per day as a swift action, you can choose one opponent. You and your mount (if any) do not provoke attacks of opportunity from that opponent for moving within or exiting his threatened area.

cunning breed (taldor jennet).name: Cunning Breed (Taldor Jennet)
cunning breed (taldor jennet).description:
Requirements: Horse (Taldor Jennet)

Benefits: A Taldor jennet can learn a total of eight tricks when trained using the Handle Animal skill (as opposed to the standard six for having an Intelligence score of 2).

oathkeeper's vow (kols).name: Oathkeeper's Vow (Kols)
oathkeeper's vow (kols).description:
The clergy of Kols trained you to make unbreakable vows.

Requirements: Kols

Benefits: You add the following spells to the paladin spell list at the indicated levels: command (1st), forbid action (1st), suggestion (to fulfill a prior promise only; 2nd), lesser geas (3rd), geas/quest (4th).

methodical mind (strange aeons).name: Methodical Mind (Strange Aeons)
methodical mind (strange aeons).description:
When you awake with nothing but hazy memories, you begin carefully cataloging your thoughts to determine the reasons for your condition and where you might be. This comforts you, and you know deep down that you have always relied on your ordered mind and pragmatic approach to face challenges. You use this focus and sensibility to your advantage as you investigate your current dire situation.

Benefits: Choose three Knowledge skills. You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge checks of these kinds and on concentration checks. In addition, choose one Knowledge skill. When researching, you are considered to have Skill Focus for that Knowledge skill when determining what die you roll to reduce a library’s knowledge points (kp).

Usage:

  1. Select three Knowledge skills when you take this trait.

  2. Update the targets of the 3 untargeted changes on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choices.

  3. Select one Knowledge skill, it need not be one of the 3 previously selected.

  4. Update the target of the Context note on the changes tab of this trait to reflect this choice.

contextNotes.text: When researching, you are considered to have Skill Focus when determining what die you roll to reduce a library’s knowledge points (kp). (1d12,  see ultimate intrigue page 148)

the flexing arm (kurgess).name: The Flexing Arm (Kurgess)
the flexing arm (kurgess).description:
You are practiced at using physical might to escape your bonds.

Requirements: Kurgess

Benefits: You can use Strength instead of Dexterity as your base ability for Escape Artist skill checks to free yourself from bondage. Additionally, you gain a +1 trait bonus on such Escape Artist checks.

Usage: Update the Skills tab of the actor to reflect Strength rather than Dexterity for the Escape Artist skill.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to free yourself from bondage.

elven reflexes (half-elf).name: Elven Reflexes (Half-Elf)
elven reflexes (half-elf).description:
One of your parents was a member of a wild elven tribe, and you've inherited a portion of your elven parent's quick reflexes.

Requirements: Half-Elf

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Initiative checks.

whitethrone gourmand (irrisen).name: Whitethrone Gourmand (Irrisen)
whitethrone gourmand (irrisen).description:
You grew up privileged in the city of Whitethrone, and indulged in many exotic and unnerving pleasures.

Requirements: Irrisen

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saves against any effect causing the nauseated or sickened condition and against all ingested poisons.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against any effect causing the nauseated or sickened condition and against all ingested poisons.

unshackled.name: Unshackled
unshackled.description:
You have escaped slavery or other imprisonment and have vowed never to be shackled again.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Escape Artist checks, and Escape Artist is a class skill for you.

condescending.name: Condescending
condescending.description:
Raised with the assurance that only those like you are truly worthy of respect, you have an offputting way of demonstrating that you look down on those not of your race and ethnicity or nationality.

Effects: You take a –5 penalty on Diplomacy and Intimidate checks to improve other creatures’ attitudes toward you.

contextNotes.text: –[[5]] Penalty checks to improve other creatures’ attitudes toward you.
–[[5]] Penalty checks to improve other creatures’ attitudes toward you.

sun-blessed.name: Sun-Blessed
sun-blessed.description:
You have shown a significant talent for the magical arts, gaining the attention of the famed Acadamae of Korvosa.

Benefits: Whenever you’re affected by a healing effect that would heal more hit points than your maximum hit point total, you gain the excess healing as temporary hit points. You can gain up to a number of temporary hit points per day equal to your character level in this way. These temporary hit points last for 1 minute.

contextNotes.text: Any healing that exceeds your maximum hit points is gained as temporary hit points up to your character level per day.

scourge of the seas (the shackles).name: Scourge of the Seas (The Shackles)
scourge of the seas (the shackles).description:
You have long appreciated the flamboyance and versatility of the whip, both as a weapon and a tool for enforcing discipline.

Requirements: The Shackles

Benefits: When you wield a whip or scourge, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Intimidate checks. Additionally, once per day when you use a whip or scourge to perform a successful disarm or trip combat maneuver against an opponent, you regain 1 point of grit or panache.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus when you wield a whip or scourge.
Once per day, when you use a whip or scourge to perform a successful disarm or trip combat maneuver against an opponent, you regain 1 point of grit or panache.

folgrit's bounty (folgrit).name: Folgrit's Bounty (Folgrit)
folgrit's bounty (folgrit).description:
You were taught to tend the sacred hearth-fire and cook nourishing meals as a child.

Requirements: Folgrit

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Profession (cook) checks and can prepare a nourishing meal of up to eight portions with 1 hour of effort at a cost of 20 gp.

Anyone who eats a portion within 1 day (which takes 10 minutes) gains a +1 morale bonus that can be applied to any one skill check or attack roll in the next 8 hours.

The bonus must be applied before the roll is made and cannot be renewed by another meal for 1 day. 

Usage: Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the Profession (cook) skill.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect this skill.

keeper of the veil (qadira).name: Keeper of the Veil (Qadira)
keeper of the veil (qadira).description:
Even when not cloaked in magic, your training has taught you how to conceal your true nature.

Requirements: Qadira

Benefits: You gain a +1 competence bonus on Bluff and Disguise checks, and one of these skills (your choice) is always a class skill for you.

Usage: Select Bluff or Disguise when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

deep marker (dwarf).name: Deep Marker (Dwarf)
deep marker (dwarf).description:
You've dared uncharted tunnels in pursuit of the Gladdringgar (the chiseling of name-runes in subterranean depths).

Requirements: Dwarf

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Survival checks, and a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against fear effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against fear effects.

eye for the wondrous (katapesh).name: Eye for the Wondrous (Katapesh)
eye for the wondrous (katapesh).description:
Your experience with magic items clues you in to their properties more quickly than others.

Requirements: Katapesh

Benefits: You can identify the properties of a magic item using @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.uqh87jz757r2cb7r]{detect magic} in 1 round.

contextNotes.text: You can identify magic items using Detect Magic in only 1 round.

charming.name: Charming
charming.description:
Blessed with good looks, you've come to depend on the fact that others find you attractive.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus when you use Bluff or Diplomacy on a character that is (or could be) sexually attracted to you, and a +1 trait bonus to the save DC of any language-dependent spell you cast on such characters or creatures.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against a character that is (or could be) sexually attracted to you.
+[[1]] Trait bonus against a character that is (or could be) sexually attracted to you.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to the save DC of any language-dependent spell you cast on a character that is (or could be) sexually attracted to you.

pyromancer (aasimar; emberkin).name: Pyromancer (Aasimar; Emberkin)
pyromancer (aasimar; emberkin).description:
You're especially adept at casting flame-based spells.

Requirements: Emberkin Aasimar Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on damage rolls for any spell you cast with the fire descriptor. Spells that do not deal damage do not benefit from this trait.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on damage rolls for any spell you cast with the fire descriptor. Spells must  deal damage to benefit from this trait.

sensitive mind (strange aeons).name: Sensitive Mind (Strange Aeons)
sensitive mind (strange aeons).description:
You awake in the asylum horribly aware of dangers lurking nearby. While your memories are hazy and indistinct, everything around you looks vibrant and clear. You have an inkling that you have always been perceptive and can tell a lot about a person even in a quick meeting. This sensitivity can overwhelm you at times, especially in your current situation.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Appraise, Perception, and Sense Motive checks, and one of these (your choice) is a class skill for you. In addition, once per week you can use the psychometry occult skill unlock even if you are not capable of casting psychic spells or have the Psychic Sensitivity feat. 

Usage: Select Appraise, Perception, or Sense Motive when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: Once per week you can use the psychometry occult skill unlock even if you are not capable of casting psychic spells or have the Psychic Sensitivity feat.

swamp survivor (ustalav).name: Swamp Survivor (Ustalav)
swamp survivor (ustalav).description:
Leaving Graidmere Swamp is forbidden, and to flee the mire is to court Seryzilian’s wrath. However, you are one of the few to successfully make it out of the swamp alive. Now, you’re resolved to liberate your kinfolk.

Requirements: Ustalav

Benefits: You gain a +2 bonus on Survival checks to find fresh water, and magical liquids in your possession gain a +2 bonus on Will saves to avoid being corrupted or otherwise spoiled.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to find fresh water.

heirloom weapon.name: Heirloom Weapon
heirloom weapon.description:
You carry a non-masterwork simple or martial weapon that has been passed down from generation to generation in your family (pay the standard gp cost for the weapon).

Benefits: When you select this trait, choose one of the following benefits: proficiency with that specific weapon, a +1 trait bonus on attacks of opportunity with that specific weapon, or a +2 trait bonus on one kind of combat maneuver when using that specific weapon.

canon of coin.name: Canon of Coin
canon of coin.description:
You find the pursuit of earning money to be a religious experience.

Benefits: Appraise is a class skill for you. So long as you are carrying coins worth an amount equal to at least 100 gp per character level you have, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Will saves. This bonus increases to +2 against effects that would force you to discard, drop, or give away coins, gems, or trade goods in your possession.

contextNotes.text: If you have at least [[100 * @attributes.hd.total]] gp, you gain a +[[1]] trait bonus. This bonus increases to +[[2]] against effects that would force you to discard, drop, or give away coins, gems, or trade goods in your possession.

chaldira's luck (chaldira zuzaristan).name: Chaldira's Luck (Chaldira Zuzaristan)
chaldira's luck (chaldira zuzaristan).description:
You found yourself in deep trouble in your youth but stumbled upon an unlikely way out after praying to Chaldira Zuzaristan for luck.

Requirements: Chaldira Zuzaristan

Benefits: Once per day, you can roll twice and take the better result on a skill check.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you may reroll twice and take the better result.

seer of reality (irori).name: Seer of Reality (Irori)
seer of reality (irori).description:
You have a deep understanding of the world around you, and thus you are more perceptive about what belongs and what does not.

Requirements: Irori

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on any saves involving illusion magic.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against illusion magic.

issian noble (brevoy).name: Issian Noble (Brevoy)
issian noble (brevoy).description:
You are a scion of one of the warring noble houses of Brevoy, and are well versed in maneuvering though the cutthroat world of Brevic politics.

Requirements: Brevoy

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (nobility) and Sense Motive checks.

disdainful defender.name: Disdainful Defender
disdainful defender.description:
You are resistant to the magic of other faiths.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on all Will saves you attempt against divine spells. This bonus does not apply against divine spells cast by a caster who worships the same deity you do.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against divine spells. Does not apply against casters who worship the same deity you do.

dispelled battler.name: Dispelled Battler
dispelled battler.description:
When deprived of magic, you fight harder.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on weapon attack and damage rolls while within an area of antimagic such as that created by an @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.niqxt53lhxrjfaty]{antimagic field}.

You also gain this bonus for 1 round after you are subject to an area or targeted @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.gmgwyjfpeuuc4t4o]{dispel magic} spell, regardless of the effect’s success or failure in dispelling your spells and magical effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to weapon attack and damage rolls while within an area of antimagic. You also gain this bonus for 1 round after you are subject to an area or targeted dispel magic spell, regardless of the effect’s success or failure in dispelling your spells and magical effects.

performer’s surprise.name: Performer’s Surprise
performer’s surprise.description:
Varisian wanderers take pride in out-thinking and surprising any foes they encounter, and often specialize in easily concealed weapons like daggers or unconventional and symbolic weapons such as the @UUID[Compendium.pf1.weapons-and-ammo.Item.vkUakr0V4RE9CAyk]{bladed scarf}

You are practiced at surprising combat tricks.

Benefits: Against foes denied their Dexterity bonus to AC, you gain a +1 trait bonus on damage rolls with improvised weapons, exotic weapons, and thrown weapons.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on damage rolls with improvised, exotic, and thrown weapons against foes denied their Dexterity bonus to AC.

slayer of the deep (tian xia darklands).name: Slayer of the Deep (Tian Xia Darklands)
slayer of the deep (tian xia darklands).description:
You have hunted monsters throughout the Darklands and know how to take advantage of their weaknesses.

Requirements: Tian Xia Darklands

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on damage rolls made against creatures of the aberration type.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on damage rolls made against aberrations.

carpenden roof runner (andoran; carpende.name: Carpenden Roof Runner (Andoran; Carpenden)
carpenden roof runner (andoran; carpende.description:
Your childhood playing atop Carpenden’s buildings has given you a remarkable head for heights.

Requirements: Carpenden [in Andoran]

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Acrobatics checks made when you are at least 20 feet above the ground.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus when you are at least 20 feet above the ground.

devil's mark (dark archive).name: Devil's Mark (Dark Archive)
devil's mark (dark archive).description:
You bear the stain of a higher fiend upon you, and any evil creature who sees it may think twice before crossing you.

Requirements: Dark Archive

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on all Bluff, Diplomacy, Intimidate, and Sense Motive checks when dealing with outsiders of the evil subtype.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus vs evil outsiders.
+[[2]] Trait bonus vs evil outsiders.
+[[2]] Trait bonus vs evil outsiders.
+[[2]] Trait bonus vs evil outsiders.

whiteout (undine).name: Whiteout (Undine)
whiteout (undine).description:
When the air is full of water, your elemental blood allows you to blend with your surroundings and become one with the precipitation.

Requirements: Undine

Benefits: In areas of nonmagical fog, rain, snow, or similar conditions (such as the spray of a waterfall), you gain the effects of concealment, but with a miss chance of 10%. If the precipitation would already grant you concealment, the miss chances stack.

contextNotes.text: In areas of nonmagical fog, rain, snow, or similar conditions (such as the spray of a waterfall), you gain the effects of concealment, but with a miss chance of 10%. If the precipitation would already grant you concealment, the miss chances stack.

low blow.name: Low Blow
low blow.description: Benefits: When targeting a good-aligned creature with a spell or spell-like ability with the pain descriptor or that imposes a morale penalty, increase its effective caster level by 1. If you are of an evil alignment, increase the save DC of the effect by 1 as well.

contextNotes.text: When casting spells and spell-like abilities with the pain descriptor or morale penalties against good-aligned creatures, treat your caster level as 1 level higher; for evil-aligned casters, the save DC is also increased by 1.

centered (irori).name: Centered (Irori)
centered (irori).description:
Having anchored yourself in your faith and your knowledge of yourself, you make it difficult for others to dominate you.

Requirements: Irori

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saves against charms and compulsions, and the DC of any attempts to use the Intimidate skill on you increases by +1.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against charms and compulsions, and the DC of any attempts to use the Intimidate skill on you increases by +[[1]].

self-taught scholar.name: Self-Taught Scholar
self-taught scholar.description:
Being self-taught has made it necessary for you to scour all documentation you can get your hands on.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Linguistics checks to decipher unfamiliar languages, and Linguistics is always a class skill for you. In addition, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Spellcraft checks made to decipher the writing on a scroll.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to decipher unfamiliar languages.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to decipher the writing on a scroll.

competitive.name: Competitive
competitive.description:
You grew up and were raised around fabulous sporting events, and your youth was consumed with a driving need to be better than everyone else. Any hint of a contest spurs you to your greatest heights of potential.

Benefits: Whenever another creature within 30 feet attempts a skill check and you attempt a check with the same skill before the start of that creature’s next turn, you gain a +1 trait bonus on your check (this includes attempting an opposed skill check against the creature).

Suggested Characters

Brevans, Kurgess worshipers, Sargavans.

contextNotes.text: Whenever another creature within 30 feet attempts a skill check and you attempt a check with the same skill before the start of that creature’s next turn, you gain a +[[1[[ Trait bonus on your check (this includes attempting an opposed skill check against the creature).

akitonian ferocity (akiton).name: Akitonian Ferocity (Akiton)
akitonian ferocity (akiton).description:
You were raised on Akiton, where you were hardened against the ravages of battle.

Requirements: Akiton

Benefits: Once per day, the first time you are brought below 0 hit points but not killed, you can fight on for 1 more round as if disabled. At the end of your next turn, unless brought to above 0 hit points, you immediately fall unconscious and begin dying.

mwangi scholar (serpent's skull).name: Mwangi Scholar (Serpent's Skull)
mwangi scholar (serpent's skull).description:
The Mwangi Expanse is one of the richest regions in all of Golarion, and also one of the most mysterious. Despite its history of countless years and its fostering of nameless empires, very little is known of the Mwangi Expanse by outsiders or even natives of the primeval, jungle-covered land. You have long studied the region and its lore, and count yourself among a small handful of genuine experts throughout the Inner Sea. The Jenivere promises to take you to the very subject of your fascination, and you eagerly await firsthand investigation of the deep jungles and cyclopean ruins of Garund’s interior.

Benefits: You gain Polyglot as a bonus language and receive a +1 trait bonus on all Knowledge (history) checks regarding the Mwangi Expanse.

Characters with this trait likely boarded the Jenivere in Ilizmagorti or one of the cities of the Shackles or the Mwangi Expanse.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on checks regarding the Mwangi Expanse.

zealous striker.name: Zealous Striker
zealous striker.description:
Your zeal is a danger to those of “lesser” faiths.

Benefits: While you have the destruction judgment active, you gain a +1 trait bonus on damage rolls when attacking a foe who can cast divine spells and worships a different deity than you.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus  on damage rolls while you have the destruction judgment active and when attacking a foe who can cast divine spells and worships a different deity than you.

arcane researcher.name: Arcane Researcher
arcane researcher.description:
Your curiosity and ingenuity have driven you to experiment and push new boundaries, and you have a knack for tampering with magic.

Benefits: You gain a +4 trait bonus on Knowledge (arcana) and Spellcraft checks to research, modify, and create new spells.

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] trait bonus on Knowledge (arcana) and Spellcraft checks to research, modify, and create new spells.
+[[4]] trait bonus on Knowledge (arcana) and Spellcraft checks to research, modify, and create new spells.

snap ignition (ifrit).name: Snap Ignition (Ifrit)
snap ignition (ifrit).description:
You are a pyromaniac by nature and love nothing more than setting fires.

Requirements: Ifrit

Benefits: As a full-round action, you can produce a small flame at the end of your finger that ignites combustible materials as a @UUID[Compendium.pf1.items.Item.tdocjgyuereurcno]{tindertwig}. Alternatively, you can maintain a flame equivalent to a candle by spending a standard action each round to concentrate on it.

founders' heritage.name: Founders' Heritage
founders' heritage.description:
You can trace your family line back to one of the founding families of Absalom that was called by Aroden to aid him in establishing the city because of their vision and potential.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Charisma-based skill checks to influence citizens of Absalom.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to influence citizens of Absalom.

the lantern bearer.name: The Lantern Bearer
the lantern bearer.description:
You are bathed in the light of the Lantern Bearer, who guides the Caravan through the darkest nights.

Requirements: 21 Abadius - 16 Calistril

Benefits: You can cast @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.ip9onjfp6nf1vlv9]{light} three times per day as a spell-like ability, treating your character level as your caster level.

giant dodger (desert).name: Giant Dodger (Desert)
giant dodger (desert).description:
In your youth, you were chased by giants for trespassing—or for sport—and you have learned to evade the reach of your opponents.

Requirements: Desert

Benefits: You gain a +3 bonus on Acrobatics checks to avoid provoking an attack of opportunity when moving out of a threatened space.

Likely Regions

Katapesh, Osirion, Qadira, Rahadoum, Thuvia

contextNotes.text: +[[3]] Trait bonus to avoid provoking an attack of opportunity when moving out of a threatened space.

adroit (kasatha).name: Adroit (Kasatha)
adroit (kasatha).description:
Requirements: Kasatha

Benefits: If one of your hands is free and your base attack bonus is +1 or higher, you can draw a weapon as a free action combined with any move action (including move actions to direct spells, manipulate items, stand up, and so on).

aspiring hellknight (cheliax).name: Aspiring Hellknight (Cheliax)
aspiring hellknight (cheliax).description:
Your family has a long tradition of service in the Hellknights, and your strict upbringing and training have given you a forceful aura of command.

Requirements: Cheliax

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Intimidate checks, and Intimidate is always a class skill for you.

master of peaks (wall of heaven).name: Master of Peaks (Wall of Heaven)
master of peaks (wall of heaven).description:
You have made it to the summits of a number of the Wall of Heaven's peaks, and your body is used to the effects of both extreme cold and high altitude.

Requirements: Wall of Heaven

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Fortitude saving throws made against the effects of cold dangers and altitude sickness.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against the effects of cold dangers and altitude sickness.

terrifying lunge (skinwalker; werecrocod.name: Terrifying Lunge (Skinwalker; Werecrocodile-Kin)
terrifying lunge (skinwalker; werecrocod.description:
Once badly bitten by a stronger werecrocodile-kin, you learned the importance of showing your strength.

Requirements: Werecrocodile-Kin Skinwalker Heritage

Benefits: Once per day, when you hit a flat-footed foe with a melee attack, you can make an Intimidate check to demoralize that foe as a swift action. Intimidate becomes a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, when you hit a flat-footed foe with a melee attack, you can make an Intimidate check to demoralize that foe as a swift action

prismati player (andoran).name: Prismati Player (Andoran)
prismati player (andoran).description:
You’ve grown up playing the confusing game of prismati and throw stones with accuracy and ease.

Requirements: Andoran

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls with fistsized rocks or similarly shaped objects, and a +1 trait bonus on concentration checks.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attack rolls with fistsized rocks or similarly shaped objects

favored prey (ketephys).name: Favored Prey (Ketephys)
favored prey (ketephys).description:
Those you track feel the sting of your arrows.

Requirements: Ketephys

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on damage rolls when making bow attacks against a creature whose tracks you have successfully followed in the last hour.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on damage rolls when making bow attacks against a creature whose tracks you have successfully followed in the last hour.

abendego spellpiercer (the sodden lands).name: Abendego Spellpiercer (the Sodden Lands)
abendego spellpiercer (the sodden lands).description:
You have spent time in the gales of the perpetual hurricane, and can cast spells even when battered by the elements.

Requirements: The Sodden Lands

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on concentration checks when spellcasting.

emissary (varisia; kaer maga).name: Emissary (Varisia; Kaer Maga)
emissary (varisia; kaer maga).description:
Accustomed to the strange residents of Kaer Maga, you have few prejudices.

Requirements: Kaer Maga in Varisia

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks when dealing with aberrations, monstrous humanoids, undead, and creatures with the giant subtype.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus when dealing with aberrations, monstrous humanoids, undead, and creatures with the giant subtype.

internal compass.name: Internal Compass
internal compass.description:
You maintain a sense of direction, even on planes where direction is relative.

Benefits: If you are on a plane that has no analogue to north, you automatically know the direction toward the plane’s major dominating feature (for instance, Pharasma’s Spire in the Boneyard, or the sun in Nirvana). If there is no such dominating feature, you instead know the direction of the closest feature significant to one of the plane’s divinities or demigods.

principled.name: Principled
principled.description:
You hold yourself to a strict code of behavior that guides all of your decisions and actions.

Benefits: You take a –2 penalty on Bluff checks and gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against charm, compulsion, and emotion effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against charm, compulsion, and emotion effects.

chelish sympathizer.name: Chelish Sympathizer
chelish sympathizer.description:
You feed intelligence to the Thrice-Damned House of Thrune for your own gain or ideological reasons and are given valuable clues gathered by other Chelish spies in exchange.

Benefits: Once per month while in an Inner Sea region settlement of at least 2,000 people, you can call upon these connections to gain an edge in a verbal duel (Pathfinder RPG Ultimate Intrigue 176) or gain a +2 bonus on one Bluff, Diplomacy, or Intimidate check.

axe to grind.name: Axe to Grind
axe to grind.description:
There is a fire in your heart that can’t be quenched.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on damage against foes who are threatened by only you.

contextNotes.text: You gain a [[+1]] trait bonus on damage against foes who are threatened by only you
You gain a [[+1]] trait bonus on damage against foes who are threatened by only you

insistent benefactor.name: Insistent Benefactor
insistent benefactor.description:
Pathfinders of different backgrounds don’t always realize what’s best for them, and you have perfected the art of forcing your magical boons on your allies.

Benefits: Whenever you cast a harmless spell, you gain a +2 trait bonus on caster level checks to overcome spell resistance, and the saving throw DC of the spell (if any) is increased by 2.

contextNotes.text: DC of harmless spells (if any) are increased by [[2]].
+[[2]] trait bonus on caster level checks to overcome spell resistance when casting harmless spells .

wati native (mummy's mask).name: Wati Native (Mummy's Mask)
wati native (mummy's mask).description:
You were born and raised in the city of Wati, and you know its streets and secrets well. Although it’s frowned upon by the city’s authorities, you have sneaked into the necropolis on multiple occasions to wander its dusty, abandoned streets. Out of respect for the deceased, you’ve never actually entered one of the necropolis’s silent tombs, but you have no fear of what might lie inside.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saves against fear effects. In addition, your knowledge of the city grants you a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (local) checks, and that skill is always a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus vs fear effects.

mother's teeth (lamashtu; goblin).name: Mother's Teeth (Lamashtu; Goblin)
mother's teeth (lamashtu; goblin).description:
Your teeth are more jagged and pointed than normal.

Requirements: Lamashtu, Goblin

Benefits: You can make a bite attack for 1d2 points of damage as a secondary attack.

Usage: Add a Bite attack from the Monster Abilities Compendium to your Actor

Modify the bite attack damage formula from sizeRoll(1, 6, @size) to sizeRoll(1, 2, @size, 3)

minor wishcraft (geniekin).name: Minor Wishcraft (Geniekin)
minor wishcraft (geniekin).description:
You gained some power of wishcraft from a genie ancestor.

Requirements: Geniekin

Benefits: You can spend a use of a racial spell-like ability or your elemental assault racial trait for the day to instead use any 0-level spell as a spelllike ability. The spell must be used to produce an effect requested aloud by a humanoid within 30 feet since the end of your last turn.

calistrian courtesan (calistria).name: Calistrian Courtesan (Calistria)
calistrian courtesan (calistria).description:
You worked in one of Calistria's temples as a sacred prostitute, and you know how to flatter, please, and (most of all) listen. 

Requirements: Calistria

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks and Diplomacy checks to gather information, and one of these skills (your choice) is always a class skill for you.

Usage: Select either Diplomacy or Sense Motive, when selecting this trait. Go to the Details tab on this trait, and check the selected choice under Class Skills.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to gather information.

planar negotiator (aasimar).name: Planar Negotiator (Aasimar)
planar negotiator (aasimar).description:
You are skilled at convincing outsiders that you require their aid.

Requirements: Aasimar

Benefits: Whenever you cast @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.c3vws58yy0pk47gs]{Planar Ally, Lesser}, @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.gfvnfa5k1tnpvg8v]{Planar Ally}, or @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.0wamdkl9gp19l55w]{Planar Ally, Greater}, you receive a 10% discount on the monetary cost required by the summoned outsiders. This bonus has no effect on services the outsiders may demand in order to strike a bargain.

pretend pirate (andoran).name: Pretend Pirate (Andoran)
pretend pirate (andoran).description:
You enjoy luring slave ships to you. Sometimes you run a pirate flag to lull criminals into a false sense of security; other times you disguise yourself as a merchant ship.

Requirements: Andoran

Benefits: You gain a +2 bonus on Disguise checks made to disguise yourself as a pirate or merchant sailor. In addition, you can take 5 minutes to alter your ship’s appearance to add +2 to the DC of any check made to identify your ship’s allegiance or purpose.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to disguise yourself as a pirate or merchant sailor.

traumatic shift (skinwalker).name: Traumatic Shift (Skinwalker)
traumatic shift (skinwalker).description:
Your first transformation was in a public place, observed by loved ones who later shunned you or townsfolk who chased you away. You have become good at hiding your nature.

Requirements: Skinwalker

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Bluff checks, and Bluff becomes a class skill for you.

genie heir (geniekin).name: Genie Heir (Geniekin)
genie heir (geniekin).description:
You once met one of your genie ancestors, who taught you the ways of her race and their allies.

Requirements: Geniekin

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Charisma checks and Charisma-based skill checks to influence outsiders from the home plane of your genie ancestor.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to influence outsiders from the home plane of your genie ancestor.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to influence outsiders from the home plane of your genie ancestor.

lost origins (any city).name: Lost Origins (Any City)
lost origins (any city).description:
You are disconnected from your heritage and do what you can to learn more about where your family is or where your people came from.

Requirements: Any City

Benefits: Knowledge (local) is always a class skill for you, and you can always take 10 on Knowledge (local) checks, even when stress or distractions would normally prevent you from doing so.

contextNotes.text: You can always take 10.

grief-filled.name: Grief-Filled
grief-filled.description:
You are no stranger to loss and intense emotions.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on all saving throws against emotion spells and effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus vs. emotion spells and effects.

mediator.name: Mediator
mediator.description:
You have a way with calming tempers, using cool logic to sooth heated disagreements, and you were always the one to settle arguments among your friends, family, and community.

Benefits: You receive a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks.

In addition, you receive a +1 trait bonus to the DC of any charm or compulsion effect that does not provide ongoing control and results in peaceful acts, such as @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.6orhthk4g27rncuu]{calm emotions}, @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.3plb67e51webutdz]{sleep}, or a @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.zqj5qzyl46af27v0]{suggestion} to lay down arms.

envy.name: Envy
envy.description:
You grew up in or near an opulent, decadent culture that valued nothing more than showing up the material wealth or accomplishments of others, causing the seed of envy to be planted in your heart.

Effects: For each day you spend without stealing at least 10 gp worth of valuables or other wealth, attempt a DC 20 Will save. If you fail, you take a cumulative –1 penalty on Will and Reflex saving throws and concentration checks (up to a –5 penalty). If you succeed, the penalty is reset to 0.

contextNotes.text: For each day you spend without stealing at least 10 gp worth of valuables or other wealth, attempt a DC 20 Will save. If you fail, you take a cumulative -[[1]] penalty on Will and Reflex saving throws and concentration checks (up to a -[[5]] penalty). If you succeed, the penalty is reset to 0.

For each day you spend without stealing at least 10 gp worth of valuables or other wealth, attempt a DC 20 Will save. If you fail, you take a cumulative -[[1]] penalty on Will and Reflex saving throws and concentration checks (up to a -[[5]] penalty). If you succeed, the penalty is reset to 0.

extremely fashionable.name: Extremely Fashionable
extremely fashionable.description:
You really know how to make a good impression when you’re dressed well.

Benefits: Whenever you are wearing clothing and/or jewelry worth at least 150 gp (and not otherwise covered in gore, sewage, or other things that mar your overall look), you gain a +1 trait bonus on Bluff, Diplomacy, and Intimidate checks. One of these skills (your choice) is a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] trait bonus whenever you are wearing clothing and/or jewelry worth at least 150 gp (and not otherwise covered in gore, sewage, or other things that mar your overall look)
+[[1]] trait bonus whenever you are wearing clothing and/or jewelry worth at least 150 gp (and not otherwise covered in gore, sewage, or other things that mar your overall look)
+[[1]] trait bonus whenever you are wearing clothing and/or jewelry worth at least 150 gp (and not otherwise covered in gore, sewage, or other things that mar your overall look)

daring doomsayer (second darkness).name: Daring Doomsayer (Second Darkness)
daring doomsayer (second darkness).description:
The end times are coming, and the shadow in the sky over Riddleport is just the first piece of proof. Convinced that the end of the world is nigh, you’ve decided to take part in the Golden Goblin's gambling tournament—after all, what do you have to lose?

Benefits

Whenever you roll a “1” on an attack roll, you gain a +1 trait bonus on your next attack roll so long as it is made within 1 minute. You also begin play with a placard bearing an apocalyptic slogan of your choice—this wooden sign can be used as a @UUID[Compendium.pf1.weapons-and-ammo.Item.vu3N4zCRDTUJ5FW4]{club}.

contextNotes.text: Whenever you roll a “1” on an attack roll, you gain a +[[1]] Trait bonus on your next attack roll so long as it is made within 1 minute.

planar wayfarer (onos).name: Planar Wayfarer (Onos)
planar wayfarer (onos).description:
The countless natural hazards of the planes cannot deter you from witnessing their grandeur.

Requirements: Onos

Benefits: Once per day you can meditate on the nature of the planes for 1 hour to gain energy resistance 2 against acid, cold, electricity, fire, or sonic damage. This resistance lasts 24 hours or until you perform this meditation again and choose a new resistance.

bellflower contact (delete - duplicate).name: Bellflower Contact (delete - duplicate)
bellflower contact (delete - duplicate).description:
Before you were old enough to be a Bellflower Network member, you kept an ear out for information useful to slave liberators.

Requirements: Halfling

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Perception checks, Sense Motive checks to understand secret messages, and Bluff checks to send secret messages.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to send secret messages.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to understand secret messages.

clergy member (aasimar).name: Clergy Member (Aasimar)
clergy member (aasimar).description:
You belong to a particular faith, and your religious leaders see you as a mortal aspect of their deity. You are treated with respect and graciousness whenever you visit a temple of your faith.

Requirements: Aasimar

Benefits: You receive a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks when interacting with ordained members of your religion, and once per week you can request aid from a temple of your faith. The GM has final say in what sort of aid you can reasonably request, but in general you may ask for the casting of a 1st-level spell or for a non-magical item worth 50 gp or less.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus when interacting with ordained members of your religion.

inoculated (urgathoa).name: Inoculated (Urgathoa)
inoculated (urgathoa).description:
Requirements: Urgathoa

Benefits: Your regular exposure to plagues grants you a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against disease effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against disease effects.

bitter heart (changeling).name: Bitter Heart (Changeling)
bitter heart (changeling).description:
Requirements: Changeling

Benefits: You share a sliver of your mother’s supernatural hatred, gaining a +2 trait bonus to confirm critical hits.

ancestral grounds (dwarf).name: Ancestral Grounds (Dwarf)
ancestral grounds (dwarf).description:
You recall the dangers the dwarves faced during the Quest for Sky.

Requirements: Dwarf

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (dungeoneering) checks and a +2 trait bonus on Knowledge (history) checks pertaining to the Quest for Sky.

Select one of these skills to be a class skill for you. 

Usage: Select Knowledge (dungeoneering) checks or Knowledge (history) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on checks pertaining to the Quest for Sky.

the pessimist (tyrant's grasp).name: The Pessimist (Tyrant's Grasp)
the pessimist (tyrant's grasp).description:
You expect the worst and have rarely been disappointed. Your dour outlook may hail from the original destruction of Roslar’s Coffer at the hands of the Twisted Nail tribe, the exile of the faithful from the nearby Sarenite temple, or some more personal misfortune, but it has set you apart among the largely forward-thinking population of Lastwall.

Benefits: Your faith that the worst possible outcome will occur grants you a +1 trait bonus on Will saving throws.

Your frustrating pessimism can spur friends to succeed just to spite you; once per day as a free action, you can force an ally within 30 feet to reroll a saving throw she just failed, using the better of the two results.

harvester.name: Harvester
harvester.description:
You were trained to harvest all parts of an animal with care and precision.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Profession (tanner) or Profession (trapper) checks, and you may make these checks as if you were trained in the skill even if you have no ranks.
