Additionally, you do not risk poisoning yourself whenever you handle or apply poison taken from a venomous creature.

Usage: Add Profession (tanner) and Profession (trapper) to your Actor on the Actor:Skills tab

Change the Target of the Changes on the changes tab of this trait to the stated Profession skills once you have added those skills to your actor.

contextNotes.text: You may make Profession (tanner) or Profession (trapper) checks as if you were trained in the skill even if you have no ranks.

dervish (qadira faction).name: Dervish (Qadira Faction)
dervish (qadira faction).description:
You trained at a secret fighting school in Katheer, where you learned the art of rapid movement in combat.

Requirements: Qadira Faction

Benefits: You gain a +1 dodge bonus to Armor Class against attacks of opportunity caused when you move out of or within a threatened area.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Dodge bonus against attacks of opportunity caused when you move out of or within a threatened area.

darkblight survivor (nirmathas).name: Darkblight Survivor (Nirmathas)
darkblight survivor (nirmathas).description:
Your woodland home in Nirmathas struggles against the tenacious Darkblight, forcing you to steel yourself against its poisonous influence.

Requirements: Nirmathas

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Fortitude saving throws against diseases and poisons inflicted by fey, plants, and forest-related hazards.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus  against diseases and poisons inflicted by fey, plants, and forest-related hazards.

infernal bastard (council of thieves).name: Infernal Bastard (Council of Thieves)
infernal bastard (council of thieves).description:
You are a tiefling. You might be an escaped slave, a hidden shame, or a homeless vagabond, but whatever your upbringing, life has been particularly hard on you. You have suffered greatly, nearly starving to death one winter, nearly being beaten to death by racist sailors one summer, and so on. Whether or not these experiences have made you a bitter and cynical scoundrel or a pious and hopeful optimist is up to you, but one thing is certain—you are something less than even your tiefling kin.

Benefits: Perhaps it is a result of your hard life, or perhaps it is due to some fault in your fiendish heritage, but you lack a tiefling’s standard resistances to cold, electricity, and fire—instead, you merely have a +2 bonus on all saving throws made against these effects. Likewise, you do not have the ability to use darkness as a spell-like ability once per day—instead, you may choose any one 0-level spell that you can instead use at will as a spell-like ability.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] bonus on all saving throws made against (cold, electricity, and fire) effects, but you lose standard racial resistances to those 3 effect types.

provincial.name: Provincial
provincial.description:
You have only one way of looking at things: the right way.

Effects: You take a –2 penalty on Diplomacy checks and Sense Motive checks made against all creatures whose religion or alignment differs from your own.

contextNotes.text: -[[2]] penalty vs creatures of different religion or alignment.
-[[2]] penalty vs creatures of different religion or alignment.

mindspin mountain goat (ram).name: Mindspin Mountain Goat (Ram)
mindspin mountain goat (ram).description:
Requirements: Ram

Benefits: Mindspin mountain goats gain a +2 trait bonus on Climb checks, and Climb is a class skill for them.

numerian archaeologist (iron gods).name: Numerian Archaeologist (Iron Gods)
numerian archaeologist (iron gods).description:
Numeria is a land ripe for archaeological exploration, since so many of the strange technological dungeons have been either avoided by the superstitious barbarian tribes or have been locked down by the Technic League, leaving many of them untouched and ripe for exploration. You’ve studied the strange language associated with these eerie technological ruins, and are eager to start exploring them—you suspect that Torch’s namesake is in fact part of a larger buried ruin, and you hope to enter these ruins and learn their original purpose by exploring the caves below town.

Benefits: You gain Androffan as an additional language. In addition, you possess a knack for technological items; when you use a timeworn technological item, roll twice when determining any glitches the item might cause and choose which result to use as your actual result. Talk to your GM for more information about resolving glitches.

scrounger (halfling).name: Scrounger (Halfling)
scrounger (halfling).description:
Others may call it junk, but you call it a bargain.

Requirements: Halfling

Benefits: You can buy objects with the broken condition at half price (instead of the normal three-quarters price). The value of the unbroken version of these objects cannot exceed four times your character level. The price you pay for this object can never exceed more than twice your character level in gp (for example, at 5th level you can spend up to 10 gp on a broken object, meaning you can buy any object that, if not broken, has a value of 20 gp or less).

solitary.name: Solitary
solitary.description:
You are skilled at slipping away from crowds.

Benefits: Once per day, you can attempt a Bluff check to create a diversion to hide with a +2 trait bonus on the check, and if successful, you take only a –5 penalty on your Stealth check to get to an unobserved place while observers are distracted (instead of the normal –10 penalty).

meticulous.name: Meticulous
meticulous.description:
Whether due to the influence of malign magic, disease, or the scorn of the gods, you were born with a troublesome deformity that interferes with your movement.

Effects: You take a –2 penalty on all Dexterity-based skill checks.

scrambling servant (warm mountains).name: Scrambling Servant (Warm Mountains)
scrambling servant (warm mountains).description:
You were hired or captured by giants and found yourself frequently scrambling up and down giant-sized furniture or slave pit walls. As a result, you are an agile climber.

Requirements: Warm Mountains

Benefits: You retain your Dexterity bonus to Armor Class while climbing and gain a +1 trait bonus on Climb checks.

Likely Regions

Osirion, Rahadoum, the Shackles, Thuvia, Varisia.

contextNotes.text: You retain your Dexterity bonus to Armor Class while climbing.

on the payroll (carrion crown).name: On the Payroll (Carrion Crown)
on the payroll (carrion crown).description:
Whether he needed a bodyguard in a rough neighborhood, a guide to an isolated archeological dig, or information on a specialized topic, Professor Lorrimor was never shy about hiring professionals to help him attain his goals. Over the course of his long career, thousands of people throughout the world served his needs and benefited from his generous wages (usually covered by his academic benefactor at the time). He had contacts in most areas of expertise in every corner of the known world, a knack for recognizing talent, and a desire to be surrounded by the best and brightest at all times. Whatever job the professor originally hired you for, your performance captured his attention, and he hired you many times throughout your career, sometimes even for jobs away from your home, always paying your expenses and compensating you well for your time. In your area of expertise, you are among the best.

Benefits: Your years of hard work have paid off, granting you an additional 150 gp in starting wealth.

varisian immunity (curse of the crimson.name: Varisian Immunity (Curse of the Crimson Throne)
varisian immunity (curse of the crimson.description:
Your family is particularly hearty, and has even passed down tales of a Varisian ancestor who sought to cure (or, some loose-lipped relatives say, create) diseases.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws made to resist diseases. Additionally, you are immune to the diseases Vorel’s phage and blood veil. You do not need to be of the Varisian ethnicity to take this campaign trait, but if you’re not, your background should explain how you have a Varisian ancestor.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to resist diseases. Additionally, you are immune to the diseases Vorel’s phage and blood veil

blade bravado (human; taldan).name: Blade Bravado (Human; Taldan)
blade bravado (human; taldan).description:
You grew up fighting in one of the many Taldan dueling academies and have honed your skills with one-handed weapons.

Requirements: Taldan Human Heritage

Benefits: When wielding a one-handed weapon, and nothing in your off hand, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Acrobatics and Bluff checks.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus when wielding a one-handed weapon and nothing in your off hand.
+[[1]] Trait bonus when wielding a one-handed weapon and nothing in your off hand.

kraggodan castaway (ironfang invasion).name: Kraggodan Castaway (Ironfang Invasion)
kraggodan castaway (ironfang invasion).description:
You hail from the dwarven Sky Citadel of Kraggodan in southern Nirmathas and have spent the past several years among the surface people serving as a mercenary in the war, trading with Nirmathi towns, or simply seeing the surface world. Molthune’s recent siege of Kraggodan has squelched any hopes you had of returning home, and now you struggle to find a home on the surface.

Requirements: Dwarf

Benefits: Thanks to your travels, you gain one of the following as a bonus language: Common, Hallit, Varisian, or Undercommon. In addition, your homesickness means time spent underground revitalizes your spirits; whenever you are underground—either in natural caverns or an artificial complex—you automatically stabilize if brought below 0 hit points, and if reduced to 0 hit points (or you are stable and conscious when below 0 hit points) you do not take the usual 1 point of damage disabled characters take from performing a standard action. This revitalizing effect fades after 4 consecutive days spent underground, but returns after you spend more than a week above ground again.

Usage: Select one (1) of Common, Hallit, Varisian, or Undercommon when you take this trait.

Update the Granted Proficiencies>Languages section of the details tab of this trait to reflect this choice.

contextNotes.text: Whenever you are underground you automatically stabilize if brought below 0 hit points, and if reduced to 0 hit points (or you are stable and conscious when below 0 hit points) you do not take the usual 1 point of damage disabled characters take from performing a standard action. This revitalizing effect fades after 4 consecutive days spent underground, but returns after you spend more than a week above ground again.

light sleeper (hanspur).name: Light Sleeper (Hanspur)
light sleeper (hanspur).description:
Because of a blessing from your god, or worries inspired by the way he died as a mortal, you are a light sleeper.

Requirements: Hanspur

Benefits: Your modifier to Perception DCs when sleeping is +5 instead of +10.

contextNotes.text: When sleeping, your DCs  are +5 instead of +10.

rousing oratory (liberty's edge).name: Rousing Oratory (Liberty's Edge)
rousing oratory (liberty's edge).description:
You are particularly adept at expressing your beliefs in a way that resonates with others.

Requirements: Liberty's Edge

Benefits: Perform (act, comedy, oratory, or sing) becomes a class skill for you. Once per day you can spend 1 minute addressing and inspiring your allies within 60 feet, after which you can attempt a DC 15 Perform (act, comedy, oratory, or sing) check. If you succeed, your allies gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against fear effects for 5 minutes. If you exceed the check DC by 10 or more, increase the bonus by 1.

Usage

Select one of Perform (act, comedy, oratory, or sing) when you take this trait.

Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the chosen skill.

Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

scholar of the great beyond.name: Scholar of the Great Beyond
scholar of the great beyond.description:
Your great interests as a child did not lie with current events or the mundane, you have always felt out of place, as if you were born in the wrong era. You take to philosophical discussions of the Great Beyond and of historical events with ease.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (history) and Knowledge (planes) checks, and one of these skills (your choice) is always a class skill for you.

Usage: Choose which skill is a class skill and mark it in the Details tab. Knowledge (history) is chosen by default.

godless resolve (bachuan, rahadoum, or t.name: Godless Resolve (Bachuan, Rahadoum, or Touvette; Bard)
godless resolve (bachuan, rahadoum, or t.description:
Some brilliant and earnest speech or other performance inspired you to take charge of your own health rather than begging indifferent or outlawed deities for deliverance.

Requirements: Bard who hails from Bachuan, Rahadoum, or Touvette, or who has lived there for at least 1 year

Benefits: Heal is a class skill for you, and you gain a +2 trait bonus on Heal checks to stabilize dying creatures and stop bleeding.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to stabilize dying creatures and stop bleeding.

elemental caller (suli).name: Elemental Caller (Suli)
elemental caller (suli).description:
You learned conjuration magic in close contact with friendly elemental beings.

Requirements: Suli

Benefits: Treat your caster level as 1 higher to determine the duration of spells you cast that call or summon genies or outsiders with the elemental subtype.

contextNotes.text: Treat your caster level as 1 higher to determine the duration of spells you cast that call or summon genies or outsiders with the elemental subtype.

offspring of the ascension (osirion).name: Offspring of the Ascension (Osirion)
offspring of the ascension (osirion).description:
You are descended from one of Ancient Osirion’s pharaohs, and a sliver of her otherworldly power has been passed onto you.

Requirements: Osirion

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Will saves against fear effects. Any time you become confused, reduce the duration of the effect by a number of rounds equal to your Wisdom modifier (to a minimum of 1 round of confusion).

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against fear effects

shadow shaman (nidal).name: Shadow Shaman (Nidal)
shadow shaman (nidal).description:
Shamans in the shadowy kingdom of Nidal learn to speak with the spirits that live in shadow

Requirements: Nidal

Benefits: Add @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.kq916lznxo0ig68v]{Silent Image} and @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.18kryvj2dfuymk9d]{Blur} to your shaman spell list as 1st- and 2nd-level spells, respectively.

motherless (tiefling).name: Motherless (Tiefling)
motherless (tiefling).description:
Your birth killed your mother, and you learned, even before words, how to manipulate others into looking after you.

Requirements: Tiefling

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Bluff and Disguise checks made for the purpose of making you seem injured, sickly, or weak in some other way.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus  on checks made for the purpose of making you seem injured, sickly, or weak in some other way.
+[[2]] Trait bonus  on checks made for the purpose of making you seem injured, sickly, or weak in some other way.

natural flyer.name: Natural Flyer
natural flyer.description:
All your life, the sky has beckoned to you, and you have dreamed of reveling in the freedom and bliss of flight.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Fly checks, as well as Acrobatics checks attempted while flying. Fly is always a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to checks attempted while flying.

acknowledged scion (dhampir).name: Acknowledged Scion (Dhampir)
acknowledged scion (dhampir).description:
You grew up in Nidal, Geb, or another place where your undead lineage was considered a mark of power.

Requirements: Dhampir

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (nobility) and Knowledge (religion) checks. One of these skills (your choice) is a class skill for you.

Usage: Add class skill flag for Knowledge (nobility) or Knowledge (religion) in Details tab.

startling report.name: Startling Report
startling report.description:
Through either perfect timing or the determination of your action, the sound of your firearm going off sets your enemies on edge.

Benefits: When you shoot a firearm, you gain a +2 trait bonus on Intimidate checks for 1 round against those who heard the shot.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus for 1 round against those who heard your firearm the shot.

enlightened warrior (aasimar; idyllkin).name: Enlightened Warrior (Aasimar; Idyllkin)
enlightened warrior (aasimar; idyllkin).description:
You have always found it easy to maintain inner peace and enlightenment that translate well to the battlefield.

Requirements: Idyllkin Aasimar Heritage

Benefits: You may take levels in monk even while maintaining a neutral or neutral good alignment.

stabbing spells.name: Stabbing Spells
stabbing spells.description:
Aroden wrote much about ways to use weapons to enhance magic’s effectiveness, and you have taken to heart the insights gleaned from his words.

Benefits: Whenever you strike a foe with a weapon, you gain a +2 trait bonus on caster level checks to overcome that foe’s spell resistance until the end of your next turn.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on caster level checks to overcome the spell resistance of a creature you've hit until the end of your next turn.
+[[2]] Trait bonus on caster level checks to overcome the spell resistance of a creature you've hit until the end of your next turn.

swordlord's page (brevoy).name: Swordlord's Page (Brevoy)
swordlord's page (brevoy).description:
You have trained under the Aldori swordlords, and though not yet a swordlord yourself, you have learned how to strike hard with your weapon.

Requirements: Brevoy

Benefits: You gain a +1 attack bonus to confirm critical hits when using an edged weapon you are proficient with.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Attack bonus to confirm critical hits when using an edged weapon you are proficient with.

sargavan guard (sargava).name: Sargavan Guard (Sargava)
sargavan guard (sargava).description:
You served in the Sargavan Guard, either as a colonial sub-praetor or as a native Mwangi regular, and have grown accustomed to marching in hot temperatures while wearing armor.

Requirements: Sargava

Benefits: When you wear armor of any sort, reduce the armor check penalty by 1, to a minimum penalty of 0.

noble born - medvyed (kingmaker).name: Noble Born - Medvyed (Kingmaker)
noble born - medvyed (kingmaker).description:
You claim a tangential but legitimate connection to one of Brevoy’s noble families. If you aren’t human, you were likely adopted by one of Brevoy’s nobles or were instead a favored servant or even a childhood friend of a noble scion. Whatever the cause, you’ve had a comfortable life, but one far from the dignity and decadence your distant cousins know. Although you are associated with an esteemed name, your immediate family is hardly well to do, and you’ve found your name to be more of a burden to you than a boon in many social situations. You’ve recently decided to test yourself, to see if you can face the world without the aegis of a name you have little real claim or care for. An expedition into the storied Stolen Lands seems like just the test to see if you really are worth the title “noble.”

Benefits: Choose one of the following noble families and associated benefits:

Medvyed

Your family has long a deep respect for the wilderness and is superstitious about the creatures that dwell therein. You gain a +2 trait bonus on all Diplomacy checks made to deal with fey creatures and a +1 trait bonus on Will saves made against their spells and supernatural abilities. Your family motto is “Endurance Overcomes All.”

Special Note

This is one of six versions of this trait. Other versions can be found here:

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.79pUkpcI6MYSTShf]{Noble Born - Garress (Kingmaker)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.nWlZ25PwQDz2zhoH]{Noble Born - Lebeda (Kingmaker)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.stEGVAsBCoaY5Ifk]{Noble Born - Lodovka (Kingmaker)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.BMzDJlUF3aWiDEJF]{Noble Born - Orlovsky (Kingmaker)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.9RUX6RB5UQYnNIxR]{Noble Born - Surtova (Kingmaker)}

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to deal with fey creatures.
+[[1]] Trait bonus against the spells and supernatural abilities of fey creatures.

child of the crusades (mendev).name: Child of the Crusades (Mendev)
child of the crusades (mendev).description:
Your parents stood strong against the demons of the Worldwound, facing down the worst of this world and the Great Beyond.

Requirements: Mendev

Benefits: You have inherited their indomitable spirit, and you gain a +2 trait bonus on all saves against fear.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus  against fear.

holy schemer (calistria).name: Holy Schemer (Calistria)
holy schemer (calistria).description:
You are well versed in the arts of misdirection, plotting, and intrigue, and you know how to recognize them when used against you.

Requirements: Calistria

Benefits: You earn a +1 trait bonus on Sense Motive, and it becomes a class skill for you.

ease of faith.name: Ease of Faith
ease of faith.description:
Your mentor, the person who invested your faith in you from an early age, took steps to ensure that you understood that what powers your divine magic is no different than that which powers the magic of other religions. This philosophy makes it easier for you to interact with others who may not share your views.

Benefits: You gain a +1 bonus on Diplomacy checks, and Diplomacy is always a class skill for you.

mindlessly cruel (half-orc, orc).name: Mindlessly Cruel (Half-Orc, Orc)
mindlessly cruel (half-orc, orc).description:
You take delight in delivering vicious blows with cruel weapons.

Requirements: Half-Orc, Orc

Benefits: Whenever you have a morale bonus on weapon attack rolls, you also receive a +1 trait bonus on weapon damage rolls.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus   on weapon damage rolls whenever you have a morale bonus on weapon attack rolls.

outsea native (river kingdoms; undine).name: Outsea Native (River Kingdoms; Undine)
outsea native (river kingdoms; undine).description:
Raised amid or near the transitive aquatic kingdom known as Outsea, your predilection for watery environs has been strong since birth and you know the types of creatures who call the ocean home.

Requirements: Undine native to the River Kingdoms

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Knowledge checks to identify aquatic monsters. Anytime you are looking through water and would take a penalty on vision-based Perception checks due to murkiness or other natural circumstances, reduce the penalty by 2.

contextNotes.text: Anytime you are looking through water and would take a penalty on vision-based checks due to murkiness or other natural circumstances, reduce the penalty by 2.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to identify aquatic monsters.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to identify aquatic monsters.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to identify aquatic monsters.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to identify aquatic monsters.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to identify aquatic monsters.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to identify aquatic monsters.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to identify aquatic monsters.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to identify aquatic monsters.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to identify aquatic monsters.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to identify aquatic monsters.

discerning beliefs (zi ha).name: Discerning Beliefs (Zi Ha)
discerning beliefs (zi ha).description:
You helped the samsarans maintain the magical boundaries around valleys in the Enlightened Peaks, and learned the secrets of avoiding such illusions yourself.

Requirements: Zi Ha

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws to disbelieve illusions.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to disbelieve illusions.

thuvian merchant (thuvia).name: Thuvian Merchant (Thuvia)
thuvian merchant (thuvia).description:
You have traveled the great circular trade route around Thuvia, following the sale of the sun orchid elixir from city to city, and are used to haggling with foreigners.

Requirements: Thuvia

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Appraise, Bluff, and Sense Motive checks made for business deals and transactions.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on checks made for business deals and transactions.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks made for business deals and transactions.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks made for business deals and transactions.

cultist lineage (mediogalti island).name: Cultist Lineage (Mediogalti Island)
cultist lineage (mediogalti island).description:
Your parents were both Red Mantis assassins and your family has a long tradition of service in the Red Mantis cult. Your strict, fanatical upbringing and familiarity with murder have bestowed upon you an air of confidence and menace.

Requirements: Mediogalti Island

Benefits: This grants you a +1 trait bonus on Intimidate checks. If you act during the surprise round, as a free action during that round you can draw a light weapon (or two if you have the Two-Weapon Fighting feat .

contextNotes.text: If you act during the surprise round, as a free action during that round, you can draw a light weapon (or two if you have the Two-Weapon Fighting feat .

water shaping (undine).name: Water Shaping (Undine)
water shaping (undine).description:
Your tight-knit undine community helped you practice your water magic safely.

Requirements: Undine

Benefits: Whenever you cast a spell or use a spell-like ability that has the cold or water descriptor and affects an area, you can choose one square within the area. The spell or spell-like ability does not affect that square or creatures in it.

contextNotes.text: You can choose one square within the area of a spell or  spell-like ability that has the cold or water descriptor and affects an area. The spell or spell-like ability does not affect that square or creatures in it.

practiced gambler (human; varisian).name: Practiced Gambler (Human; Varisian)
practiced gambler (human; varisian).description:
Your upbringing around the Sczarni has exposed you to games with cards and dice.

Requirements: Varisian Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Bluff and Sleight of Hand checks; for the purpose of gambling, these bonuses increase by 1.

contextNotes.text: For the purpose of gambling,  increase the Trait bonus granted by this trait from 1 to 2.
For the purpose of gambling,  increase the Trait bonus granted by this trait from 1 to 2.

wind-carried voices (sylph).name: Wind-Carried Voices (Sylph)
wind-carried voices (sylph).description:
You are a master of seeing through falsehoods and sharing secrets—the wind itself seems to carry your voice to those you trust.

Requirements: Sylph

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks to tell when someone is lying to you. Additionally, at will, as long as you are outdoors or in an area where there is noticeable air movement, you can whisper to any creature from up to 30 feet away. You can only designate one person in sight to hear your voice, and nearby creatures can hear these messages with a successful DC 20 Perception check. This is an extraordinary ability that otherwise functions as @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.msaqovkgfxi6q8s8]{message}.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to tell when someone is lying to you.

promising.name: Promising
promising.description:
Your superiors have identified you as an Eagle Knight candidate, and their praise encourages you to strive to meet their expectations.

Benefits: Each day after getting a full night’s sleep, you gain a +1 trait bonus to your AC when wearing armor or carrying a shield. This bonus lasts until you are first hit by an attack within the next 24 hours.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to your AC when wearing armor or carrying a shield, each day after getting a full night’s sleep. This bonus lasts until you are first hit by an attack within the next 24 hours.

unafraid (ustalav).name: Unafraid (Ustalav)
unafraid (ustalav).description:
The horrors of the fog-shrouded land of terror have hardened you.

Requirements: Ustalav

Benefits: You receive a +2 bonus on all saving throws made against fear affects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against fear affects.

adaptable flatterer (fetchling).name: Adaptable Flatterer (Fetchling)
adaptable flatterer (fetchling).description:
A life spent avoiding racial mistrust has taught you to humor others effectively when it is useful to do so.

Requirements: Fetchling

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Bluff checks and on attack rolls against opponents who are denied their Dexterity bonus to AC against you.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against opponents who are denied their Dexterity bonus to AC against you

inspiring speaker (galt).name: Inspiring Speaker (Galt)
inspiring speaker (galt).description:
You have learned the arts of oratory, and know how to speak to crowds to rile them up or to calm them down.

Requirements: Galt

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Bluff and Diplomacy checks when addressing crowds of 10 people or more.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus when addressing crowds of 10 people or more.
+[[1]] Trait bonus when addressing crowds of 10 people or more.

child of the temple.name: Child of the Temple
child of the temple.description:
You have long served at a temple in a city, and not only did you pick up on many of the nobility's customs, you spent much time in the temple libraries studying your faith.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (nobility) and Knowledge (religion) checks, and one of these skills (your choice) is always a class skill for you.

Usage: Choose which skill is a class skill and mark it in the Details tab.

nimble fingers, keen mind (brigh).name: Nimble Fingers, Keen Mind (Brigh)
nimble fingers, keen mind (brigh).description:
You have an in depth understanding of clockwork mechanisms and other mechanical wonders.

Requirements: Brigh

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Disable Device checks, and Disable Device becomes a class skill for you.

diligence (any city).name: Diligence (Any City)
diligence (any city).description:
You grew up helping with a family business or were mentored by an expert in some craft or profession, who taught you diligence.

Requirements: Any City

Benefits: Select one specific Craft or Profession skill. You can take 20 with that skill in half the amount of time normally required to take 20.

Usage

Select one Craft or Profession skill when you take this trait.

Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the chosen skill.

Update the target of the context note on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: You can take 20 in half the amount of time normally required to take 20.

exposed to awfulness (wrath of the right.name: Exposed to Awfulness (Wrath of the Righteous)
exposed to awfulness (wrath of the right.description:
When you were a child, you were nearly slain by a demon that managed to make its way through the wardstones into the lands beyond. The demon was slain before it could kill you, but you lingered at death’s door in a coma for weeks before waking. Ever since then, you’ve been unusually hale and hearty, as if your body had endured its brush with awfulness by becoming supernaturally fit. But still, the scars (whether physical or purely mental) of your brush with death remain, and nightmares of what could have happened often plague your sleep. Something, be it your own personal force of will, some strange “infection” from the assault, or perhaps a combination of both, has made you stronger than before. You’re not sure what to make of the theories that you survived this exposure to awfulness because you yourself have some trace of demonic heritage that helped give you the advantage you needed to survive— but whatever it was, you’re glad for it! Your strange resistance to demonic attacks persists to this day.

Benefits: Once per day when you fail a saving throw against an effect created by a demon that would kill or physically incapacitate you, you can immediately reroll that saving throw as a free action. You must take the second result, even if it is worse.

Associated Mythic Path

Guardian

Multiple Characters

You and any others with this trait are related, if only distantly. You could be siblings or cousins—a condition that perhaps lends some credence to the theory that all of you share more than just a common bloodline.

contextNotes.text: Once per day when you fail a saving throw against an effect created by a demon that would kill or physically incapacitate you, you can immediately reroll that saving throw as a free action. You must take the second result, even if it is worse.

insular (dwarf).name: Insular (Dwarf)
insular (dwarf).description:
You hail from one of the remote dwarven holds of the North, and have learned that the people of these harsh lands are as fickle as the weather. Experience has taught you that outsiders are not to be trusted.

Requirements: Dwarf

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks, and Sense Motive is always a class skill for you, but you take a –1 penalty on Diplomacy checks with non-dwarves.

contextNotes.text: -[[1]] penalty on checks with non-dwarves.

draconic infusion.name: Draconic Infusion
draconic infusion.description:
You were exposed to an artifact or other powerful magic that infused you with the power of dragons.

Benefits: Choose the acid, cold, electricity, or fire spell descriptor when you take this trait. Once per day for every 2 caster levels you have (minimum once per day), when you cast a spell that has your chosen elemental descriptor, you can deal an additional 1d4 points of damage of that energy type to one target of that spell.

Usage

Choose the acid, cold, electricity, or fire spell descriptor when you take this trait.

Update the text of the note on the changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

On the details tab of this trait, edit the action to include the appropriate damage type.

contextNotes.text: [[max(1, floor(@spells.primary.cl.total /2))]] times per day,  when you cast a spell with the <chosen element> descriptor you can add 1d4  damage of same type to one target of  the spell.  ([[@resources.draconicInfusion.value]] remaining uses)

lion's audacity (taldor).name: Lion's Audacity (Taldor)
lion's audacity (taldor).description:
You attack life with the ferocity of a charging lion and inspire others with your audacious deeds.

Requirements: Taldor

Benefits: Once per day when you make a charge attack, you can inspire allies within 30 feet. They gain an additional +2 trait bonus on attack and damage rolls when they make a charge attack before the beginning of your next turn.

unlearned.name: Unlearned
unlearned.description:
You have always had difficulty learning and retaining information, and know very little about the world beyond the obvious and what pertains to your profession.

Effects: Choose one Knowledge skill. You cannot attempt untrained Knowledge checks with any other Knowledge skills, even if the check’s DC is 10 or lower. If you have access to a library that covers a specific Knowledge skill, you may attempt an untrained Knowledge check with a –2 penalty.

Usage

Select one Knowledge skill when you take this drawback.

Delete the one Note on the changes tab related to this skill only.

contextNotes.text: You cannot attempt untrained checks  even if the check’s DC is 10 or lower. If you have access to a library that covers a specific Knowledge skill, you may attempt an untrained check in that skill with a -[[2]] penalty.

You cannot attempt untrained checks  even if the check’s DC is 10 or lower. If you have access to a library that covers a specific Knowledge skill, you may attempt an untrained check in that skill with a -[[2]] penalty.

You cannot attempt untrained checks  even if the check’s DC is 10 or lower. If you have access to a library that covers a specific Knowledge skill, you may attempt an untrained check in that skill with a -[[2]] penalty.

You cannot attempt untrained checks  even if the check’s DC is 10 or lower. If you have access to a library that covers a specific Knowledge skill, you may attempt an untrained check in that skill with a -[[2]] penalty.

You cannot attempt untrained checks  even if the check’s DC is 10 or lower. If you have access to a library that covers a specific Knowledge skill, you may attempt an untrained check in that skill with a -[[2]] penalty.

You cannot attempt untrained checks  even if the check’s DC is 10 or lower. If you have access to a library that covers a specific Knowledge skill, you may attempt an untrained check in that skill with a -[[2]] penalty.

You cannot attempt untrained checks  even if the check’s DC is 10 or lower. If you have access to a library that covers a specific Knowledge skill, you may attempt an untrained check in that skill with a -[[2]] penalty.

You cannot attempt untrained checks  even if the check’s DC is 10 or lower. If you have access to a library that covers a specific Knowledge skill, you may attempt an untrained check in that skill with a -[[2]] penalty.

strength of the barghest (hadregash).name: Strength of the Barghest (Hadregash)
strength of the barghest (hadregash).description:
The blood of the barghests is strong in you.

Requirements: Goblin, Hadregash

Benefits: Once per day as a free action, you can gain a +2 trait bonus on any Strength check or Strength-based skill check.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you can gain a +[[2]] Trait bonus on any check.
Once per day, you can gain a +[[2]] Trait bonus on any check.

greasy palm (the exchange).name: Greasy Palm (The Exchange)
greasy palm (the exchange).description:
You know how to get people to do what you want with little effort.

Requirements: The Exchange

Benefits: When bribing an NPC, you pay 10% less than a character without this trait would, garnering the same results.

dragon tracker (dahak).name: Dragon Tracker (Dahak)
dragon tracker (dahak).description:
You have been taught to identify the subtle and not-so-subtle signs of a dragon’s passage.

Requirements: Dahak

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Survival checks to track dragons, and you can track a dragon with Survival untrained if the check’s DC is 15 or lower.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to track dragons, and you can track a dragon untrained if the check’s DC is 15 or lower.

zealot's bearing.name: Zealot's Bearing
zealot's bearing.description:
Your fiery passion for your faith or philosophy is obvious to everyone around you. Those who share your convictions automatically find themselves wanting to defer to your zealous authority, making you a natural leader of your faith.

Unfortunately, your scorn for those who haven’t yet seen the wisdom of converting is equally obvious, and makes you a poor ambassador or missionary to the “ignorant masses” (as you not-so-secretly think of them).

Benefits: You gain a +2 bonus on Diplomacy and Intimidate checks when dealing with adherents of your faith or philosophy, but a –1 penalty on Diplomacy and Intimidate checks when dealing with anyone else.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] bonus with adherents of your faith or philosophy, but a -[[1]] penalty  with anyone else.
+[[2]] bonus with adherents of your faith or philosophy, but a -[[1]] penalty  with anyone else.

prophesied.name: Prophesied
prophesied.description:
Your coming was foretold in prophecy, and people familiar with your legend regard you with awe and fear.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks and Intimidate checks when interacting with anyone familiar with you or your reputation.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus when interacting with anyone familiar with you or your reputation.
+[[1]] Trait bonus when interacting with anyone familiar with you or your reputation.

guardian smite (trudd).name: Guardian Smite (Trudd)
guardian smite (trudd).description:
Paladins of Trudd taught you to be a blessed, living barrier against enemies who threaten those you hold dear.

Requirements: Trudd

Benefits: Once per day, when you strike a foe with @UUID[Compendium.pf1.class-abilities.Item.2TsDfK0SKC8IGVx4]{smite evil}, you can halve the total damage dealt to the foe to reduce that foe’s speed to 5 feet for 1 round.

prideful temper (tiefling).name: Prideful Temper (Tiefling)
prideful temper (tiefling).description:
Memories of the cruel abuse and taunts you suffered as a child cause you to strike back with great fury at anyone who slanders you.

Requirements: Tiefling

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls made against creatures that have insulted you until you actually succeed in dealing damage.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attack rolls made against creatures that have insulted you until you actually succeed in dealing damage.

charlatan (gnome).name: Charlatan (Gnome)
charlatan (gnome).description:
Your talent for illusion goes beyond a mere knack for figments and phantasms. You can actually channel spell energy into simple illusions that augment your attempts at deception.

Requirements: Gnome, nonlawful alignment

Benefits: As a swift action, you may expend one prepared spell or spell slot to gain a bonus on the next Bluff check you make, provided the check takes place before the beginning of your next turn. The bonus on this check is equal to the level of the spell consumed. You may use this ability 3 times per day.

contextNotes.text: Before the beginning of your next turn, you may expend one prepared spell or spell slot to gain a bonus on the next Bluff check you make.

the pack.name: The Pack
the pack.description:
You are blessed by the beasts that trail the caravan, which represent protection in numbers.

Requirements: 11 Arodus - 16 Rova

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Handle Animal checks to teach animals tricks, and Handle Animal becomes a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to teach animals tricks

master messenger.name: Master Messenger
master messenger.description:
Your secret messages are rarely misunderstood.

Requirement(s) Riddleport

Benefits: If you fail your Bluff check to pass a simple message by 5 or more, you deliver no message instead of delivering the wrong message.

contextNotes.text: If you fail your check to pass a simple message by 5 or more, you deliver no message instead of delivering the wrong message.

martyr's blood (aasimar).name: Martyr's Blood (Aasimar)
martyr's blood (aasimar).description:
You carry the blood of a self-sacrificing celestial, and strive to live up to your potential for heroism.

Requirements: Aasimar

Benefits: As long as your current hit point total is less than half of your maximum hit points possible, you gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls against evil foes.

contextNotes.text: +[[(ifelse(lt(@attributes.hp.value, (@attributes.hp.max / 2)), 1, 0))]] Trait bonus on attack rolls against evil foes as long as your current hit point total [[@attributes.hp.value]] is less than half of your maximum hit points [[@attributes.hp.max / 2]] possible.

animal-spirit vitality (kyonin).name: Animal-Spirit Vitality (Kyonin)
animal-spirit vitality (kyonin).description:
The elven druids of Kyonin taught you to commune with the natural healing powers of animals.

Requirements: Must hail from Kyonin or have lived there for at least 1 year

Benefits: Once per day when you cast a spell that restores hit points, you restore 1 additional hit point for every die rolled as long as a creature of the animal type is within 50 feet. You must decide to use this ability before rolling to determine the number of hit points that your spell restores.

contextNotes.text: Once per day when you cast a spell that restores hit points, add +1 hit point per  die rolled, if an animal is within 50 feet. You must decide to use this ability before rolling.([[@resources.animalspiritVitalityKyonin.value]] remaining uses)

rapscallion (gnome).name: Rapscallion (Gnome)
rapscallion (gnome).description:
You've spent your entire life thumbing your nose at the establishment, and you take pride in your run-ins with the law. Somehow, despite all the mischievous behavior in your life, you've never been caught.

Requirements: Gnome

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Escape Artist checks and a +1 trait bonus on Initiative checks.

erratic malefactor (hell's vengeance).name: Erratic Malefactor (Hell's Vengeance)
erratic malefactor (hell's vengeance).description:
Some people follow codes and traditions; others look out for only themselves. You don’t care—you do what you want, when you want, sometimes for a good reason, and sometimes for no reason at all. You revel in your status as an outlier, and take pride in the fact that you follow no one else’s orders—at least most of the time. In a lawful land like Cheliax, one has to be more careful and at least pay lip service to the rules and restrictions of society. As a result, you try to manage the chaos that rages within your soul, but you’re always on the lookout for the opportunity to truly unleash destruction. These days, there’s plenty of work for someone who will do what needs to be done, no questions asked, and the rewards from House Thrune for those who work with it are a strong incentive as well. If working with others gives you the freedom to carry out some of your darkest instincts without fear of repercussion, then why not—at least for now. You can worry about the future when it comes.

Requirements: Chaotic evil

Benefits: Your unpredictability and volatile temperament gives you an advantage over your foes. You gain a +2 trait bonus on initiative checks. In addition, you are willing to work with others, and have learned to use your allies’ strengths to reinforce your own skills and abilities. Once per day, as long as an ally is within 10 feet of you, you can reroll a single attack roll or skill check before success or failure is known. You must take the results of the second roll, even if it is worse.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, as long as an ally is within 10 feet of you, you can reroll a single attack roll or skill check before success or failure is known. You must take the results of the second roll, even if it is worse.

Once per day, as long as an ally is within 10 feet of you, you can reroll a single attack roll or skill check before success or failure is known. You must take the results of the second roll, even if it is worse.

veils upon veils (sivanah).name: Veils upon Veils (Sivanah)
veils upon veils (sivanah).description:
You have learned a holy secret that makes your illusions more powerful.

Requirements: Sivanah

Benefits: Once per day when casting an illusion spell, you can do so at +1 caster level.

contextNotes.text: Once per day when casting an illusion spell, you can do so at +1 caster level.

bound by honor.name: Bound by Honor
bound by honor.description:
Your code of honor, obligations, and taboos stem from the shame of a misspent youth. Now, a personal code reminds you how to live, and you have an impressive ability to resist temptation.

Benefits: Once per day, if a mindaffecting effect would compel you to take an action that would cause you to lose Honor Points, you may make a new saving throw against the original DC to end the effect.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, if a mindaffecting effect would compel you to take an action that would cause you to lose Honor Points, you may make a new saving throw against the original DC to end the effect.

cheap to feed (halfling; osirion, varisi.name: Cheap to Feed (Halfling; Osirion, Varisia)
cheap to feed (halfling; osirion, varisi.description:
Life in a big city taught the consequences of pride. Now you have no qualms about pointing out how little of a burden your upkeep would prove to potential employers, captors, innkeepers, or anyone else able to provide you with the necessities of life.

Requirements: Halfling; Osirion, Varisian

Benefits: You gain a +3 trait bonus on Bluff checks made in order to gain food, water, or shelter.

contextNotes.text: +[[3]] Trait bonus on checks made in order to gain food, water, or shelter.

self-aware (vampire; vetala).name: Self-Aware (Vampire; Vetala)
self-aware (vampire; vetala).description:
Wisdom stolen from an enlightened mortal has opened your own mind. Knowing yourself helps you to understand others.

Requirements: Vetala Vampire Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on all Sense Motive checks, and Sense Motive becomes a class skill for you.

greater adept of the society (wizard, pa.name: Greater Adept of the Society (Wizard, Pathfinder Society)
greater adept of the society (wizard, pa.description:
Months of meditation and practice with the greatest wizards of the Society have given you an increased capacity for arcane knowledge.

Requirements: Wizard, Pathfinder Society

Benefits: You gain one additional 0-level spell slot.

Usage: In your character sheet, go to the spells tab and add one 0-level spell slot in the spells tab's configuration.

the stranger.name: The Stranger
the stranger.description:
Like the lone traveler that walks alongside the caravan for but a brief while, you have a way with strangers.

Requirements: 21 Neth - 29 Neth

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy and Knowledge (local) checks to gather information. Additionally, either Diplomacy or Knowledge (local) becomes a class skill for you.

Usage: Set either Diplomacy or Knowledge (local) as a class skill in the Details tab.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to gather information
+[[1]] Trait bonus to gather information

steel skin.name: Steel Skin
steel skin.description: Benefits: You gain the ability to don or remove heavy armor in half the normal time. Additionally, you begin play with a great helm bearing the iconography of your family (this helm grants no special defensive benefits). While wearing this helm with a suit of heavy armor, you gain a +2 trait bonus on Intimidate checks.

contextNotes.text: +[[ifelse(eq(@armor.type, 3), 2, 0)]] Trait bonus while wearing the helm granted from @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.dbNVSPSh7FyAXkzY]{Steel Skin} trait.

wedded bliss (bolka).name: Wedded Bliss (Bolka)
wedded bliss (bolka).description:
Requirements: Bolka

Benefits: Your commitment to your own marriage or your burning desire to be wed grants you a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against charm and compulsion effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against charm and compulsion effects.

enchanted by giants (forests).name: Enchanted by Giants (Forests)
enchanted by giants (forests).description:
You are familiar with the primeval magic of forest-dwelling giants and have witnessed their close attention to the natural world around them, whether because your tribe engaged in trade with those giants or because you stumbled into their territory. The experience opened your eyes to the workings of nature and magic.

Requirements: Forests

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Survival checks and Spellcraft checks. You can always take 10 on Spellcraft checks to identify spell-like abilities.

Likely Regions

Kyonin, Lands of the Linnorm Kings, Mwangi Expanse, Nirmathas, River Kingdoms, Taldor.

contextNotes.text: You can always take 10  to identify spell-like abilities.

less effort.name: Less Effort
less effort.description:
Benefits: Select one spell belonging to the abjuration or transmutation school on your spell list. When you cast that spell or it is cast on you, treat the caster level as 2 higher for the purpose of determining the duration.

Usage: Adjust duration of selected spell

close allies (return of the runelords).name: Close Allies (Return of the Runelords)
close allies (return of the runelords).description:
You and another PC are close allies—friends, lovers, siblings, or even twins. Work with the other player to build a shared history—both you and the other player must choose Close Allies as a campaign trait, and both must agree to the nature of the relationship between the two characters. (At the GM’s option, more than two PCs can be linked by this trait.) You and your close ally have always supported each other. You might have the tendency to complete each other’s sentences, dress similarly, share the same religion or faith, or have endured a dangerous event together in the past. If you and the other player agree (and have chosen either the siblings or twins option below), you might not even particularly like your close “ally,” yet you still respect them and can utilize this trait’s benefits all the same.

Benefits: If your relationship with your close ally ends (as a result of death, a parting of ways, or other catastrophe), you immediately lose this campaign trait and replace it with @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.5d6YUNTd47cocJLF]{Spirit Touched} as a result of your emotional distress.

When you and the other PC agree to take this shared trait, you must also agree on the nature of your characters’ bond by choosing one of the following: friends, lovers, siblings, or twins. If you choose twins, both PCs must be of the same race. If you choose siblings, one or both of you are adopted if you aren’t of the same race.

Whenever you are adjacent to your close ally, you gain a +1 trait bonus on all saving throws.

Once per adventure as an immediate action, you may grant your close ally a bonus equal to the result of 1d6 to a single d20 roll. You must have line of sight to your ally to use this ability. You may choose to grant this bonus after your ally has rolled, but must do so before the result of the roll is known.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus whenever you are adjacent to your close ally.

fey-wise (sevenarches).name: Fey-Wise (Sevenarches)
fey-wise (sevenarches).description:
You grew up around fey and are accustomed to their tricks.

Requirements: Sevenarches

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against the spell-like and supernatural abilities of fey and a +1 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks against fey.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against the spell-like and supernatural abilities of fey.
+[[1]] Trait bonus against fey.

employee (ruins of azlant).name: Employee (Ruins of Azlant)
employee (ruins of azlant).description:
You have worked for the Bountiful Venture Company or you are the child of one of its current employees. This connection helped you get a leg up on the competition to get involved in the establishment of Talmandor’s Bounty. The company didn’t want nepotism to show through too strongly, so you weren’t included in the first wave of colonists and instead have been selected to accompany the second wave of colonists to the island. You don’t want people to know that you’re part of the company, but your contacts in the Bountiful Venture Company didn’t necessarily tell you that you needed to keep it a secret.

Benefits: You begin play with an additional 150 gp of equipment and know (at least in some capacity) one of the other company employees: Lyra Heatherly, Perrell Beys, or Ramona Avandth. Due to your association with the Bountiful Venture Company, your words and ideas may carry additional weight with the other employees of the company. (Your GM can grant a +1 circumstance bonus on Diplomacy checks in regards to these people in certain situations.) In addition, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Bluff checks, and Bluff is always a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: Due to your association with the Bountiful Venture Company, your words and ideas may carry additional weight with the other employees of the company such as Lyra Heatherly, Perrell Beys, or Ramona Avandth. (Your GM can grant a +1 circumstance bonus on Diplomacy checks in regards to these people in certain situations.) I

spirit guide (pharasma).name: Spirit Guide (Pharasma)
spirit guide (pharasma).description:
As someone who has performed or observed funeral rites for a wide variety of people, you have a basic understanding of many different religions.

Requirements: Pharasma

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Knowledge (religion) checks, and Knowledge (religion) becomes class skill for you.

black powder interjection.name: Black Powder Interjection
black powder interjection.description:
Your confidence rises when you foil spellcasting.

Benefits: Once per day, when you interrupt the casting of a spell with a firearm attack, you may regain 1 grit point.

aeromantic affinity.name: Aeromantic Affinity
aeromantic affinity.description:
One of your distant ancestors was either a powerful aeromancer or a creature of elemental air, granting you greater skill with air magic.

Benefits: You treat your caster level as 1 higher when casting spells with the air or electricity descriptor.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] CL when casting spells with the air or electricity descriptor.

enemy of slavers.name: Enemy of Slavers
enemy of slavers.description:
If you follow this code, you are known to be single-minded with regard to slavery, and this fervent belief is easily perceived by others. Those who deal in flesh have a greater desire to appease you before matters escalate to confrontation, knowing that your wrath will be unwavering and swift.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy and Intimidate checks made to sway the minds of slavers and anyone who otherwise keeps innocent people against their will.

Usage: to take this trait you must follow the Freedom Knightly code of conduct.

If a knight violates their code of conduct, they are disgraced and must perform penance to atone for their transgressions. until the penance is completed, disgraced knights no longer receive benefits from their knightly traits.

Freedom: Knights who follow the code of freedom believe that no man, woman, or child should be owned by another individual. They believe that all of the humanoid races, even those of monstrous descent, deserve to be given a chance to make their own decisions in life.

Their vows are as follows.

  • I will not tolerate slavery in any form. If I witness one individual being sold to another, I will intervene with words or actions as necessary.

  • If I encounter any escaped slaves, I will make it my duty to take them under my care until they find a safe home.

  • I shall not willingly deal with any who support or benefit from slavery.

  • I keep no slaves. Those who wait upon me do so of their own volition or because they are in my employ and are treated with the utmost respect.

  • When visiting others who keep servants, I will treat them honorably. I shall inquire as to their happiness and treatment. Those who are treated as no more than slaves will gain my

    strength to support them.

  • Shackles and bonds are forbidden unless used on an individual convicted of a crime. Placing another person in chains is akin to being a slaver.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to sway the minds of slavers and anyone who otherwise keeps innocent people against their will.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to sway the minds of slavers and anyone who otherwise keeps innocent people against their will.

shield bearer (human; ulfen).name: Shield Bearer (Human; Ulfen)
shield bearer (human; ulfen).description:
You have survived many battles thanks to your skill with your shield.

Requirements: Ulfen Human Heritage

Benefits: When performing a shield bash, you deal 1 additional point of damage. Also, once per day on your turn as a free action, you may provide one adjacent ally a +2 trait bonus to his Armor Class. This bonus lasts for 1 round, so long as you and the target remain adjacent to one another. You can only use this ability if you are using a shield. You retain your shield bonus to your armor class when using this ability.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]]  damage when performing a shield bash.

Once per day on your turn as a free action, you may provide one adjacent ally a +[[2]] Trait bonus to his AC. This bonus lasts for 1 round, so long as you and the target remain adjacent to one another. You can only use this ability if you are using a shield. You retain your shield bonus to your armor class when using this ability.

traditionalist (faiths and philosophies).name: Traditionalist (Faiths and Philosophies)
traditionalist (faiths and philosophies).description:
You spent a part of your youth far away from your homeland and your people. The experience of being a stranger in a strange land taught you to appreciate the support and understanding of the other members of your communal belief, and to never take it for granted. Though you still respect other cultures, you have come to see the way in which your particular culture keeps the knowledge of your people and their history alive, and feel that you have a responsibility to guard their traditions even when you are far from home.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (history) and Knowledge (local) checks, and one of those skills (your choice) becomes a class skill for you.

Usage

Select Knowledge (history) or Knowledge (local) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

divine the mystery (human; kellid).name: Divine the Mystery (Human; Kellid)
divine the mystery (human; kellid).description:
Your time spent exploring the ruins of Silver Mount has taught you much about the strange ways of the metal creatures from the stars.

Requirements: Kellid Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Knowledge (engineering) checks to identify creatures with the robot subtype and relics of Numerian technology. When using Bluff to feint against an intelligent construct, you do not take a penalty for feinting against a non-humanoid.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to  checks to identify creatures with the robot subtype and relics of Numerian technology.
When using Bluff to feint against an intelligent construct, you do not take a penalty for feinting against a non-humanoid.

smoke resistant (kobold).name: Smoke Resistant (Kobold)
smoke resistant (kobold).description:
You’ve spent so much of your life around fires that smoke no longer affects you the way it does other creatures.

Requirements: Kobold

Benefits: Your vision isn’t impaired by nonmagical smoke, and you gain a +5 trait bonus on Fortitude saves to avoid coughing and choking because of smoke inhalation (Pathfinder RPG Core Rulebook 426). This trait confers no benefits against magically created smoke, such as that produced by pyrotechnics.

contextNotes.text: +[[5]] Trait bonus to avoid coughing and choking because of smoke inhalation.
Your vision isn’t impaired by nonmagical smoke.

inured to death.name: Inured to Death
inured to death.description:
You have been around death or the undead all of your life; you have become accustomed to its shadowy presence, and are no longer overwhelmed by it.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against death effects.

Suggested Characters

Gebbites, Urgathoans, Ustalavs.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on saving throws against death effects.

jungle resilience (nagajor).name: Jungle Resilience (Nagajor)
jungle resilience (nagajor).description:
Having grown up in Nagajor, you are used to the myriad poisons of its people, and have developed a strong immune system.

Requirements: Nagajor

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Fortitude saves against poison effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against poison effects.

goblin bravery (goblin).name: Goblin Bravery (Goblin)
goblin bravery (goblin).description:
You have a worrying propensity for overconfidence in combat.

Requirements: Goblin

Benefits: When facing an enemy that's larger than you, if you have no allies in any adjacent squares, your posturing, bravado, and cussing grant a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls with non-reach melee weapons.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus with non-reach melee weapons vs enemies larger than you, if no allies in adjacent squares.

nagaji nationalist (nagaji).name: Nagaji Nationalist (Nagaji)
nagaji nationalist (nagaji).description:
Your loyalty to the nagaji nation of Nagajor is powerful.

Requirements: Nagaji

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls and saving throws against foes that directly threaten the safety of Nagajor.

contextNotes.text:
+[[1]] Trait bonus 
on attack rolls  against foes that directly threaten the safety of Nagajor.

+[[1]] Trait bonus 
 against foes that directly threaten the safety of Nagajor.

faithful arodenite (aroden).name: Faithful Arodenite (Aroden)
faithful arodenite (aroden).description:
You worship a dead god, and are skilled at making that sound reasonable.

Requirements: Aroden

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks; this bonus increases to +3 if you are dealing with a follower of Aroden or Iomedae.

contextNotes.text: +[[3]] Trait bonus if you are dealing with a follower of Aroden or Iomedae.

frightening speed (achaekek).name: Frightening Speed (Achaekek)
frightening speed (achaekek).description:
Your careful study of Achaekek’s kills has unlocked a divine speed in you.

Requirements: Achaekek

Benefits: Once per day during a combat encounter, you can move an additional 5 feet as a part of a move action without penalty.

contextNotes.text: Once per day during a combat encounter, you can move an additional 5 feet as a part of a move action without penalty.

devotee of the old gods (mummy's mask).name: Devotee of the Old Gods (Mummy's Mask)
devotee of the old gods (mummy's mask).description:
Osirion has a history stretching back over 8,000 years, and the deities worshiped today in modern Osirion are not the same ones revered in Ancient Osirion’s heyday—deities with names like Anubis, Osiris, Ra, and Set, among others. Your family never lost the faith of your ancestors, however, and your devotion to one of the deities of Ancient Osirion has helped keep the memory of Osirion’s past alive—a past that still lingers on in the untouched necropolis of Wati.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (history) and Knowledge (religion) checks, and one of those skills is always a class skill for you. In addition, your faith in the old gods of Osirion grants you a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against divine magic.

Usage

Select Knowledge (history) or Knowledge (religion) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus vs divine magic.

chillblight emissary (irrisen).name: Chillblight Emissary (Irrisen)
chillblight emissary (irrisen).description:
You have spent considerable time at Chillblight , but have escaped this unearthly fey outpost.

Requirements: Irrisen

Benefits: You gain DR 1/cold iron but become @UUID[Compendium.pf1.pf1e-rules.JournalEntry.NSqfXaj4MevUR2uJ.JournalEntryPage.7LwCMwrX3tchvrjW]{Sickened} for 1 round if damaged by a cold iron weapon.

contextNotes.text: DR 1/cold iron but become sickened for 1 round if damaged by a cold iron weapon.

harrow born (human; varisian).name: Harrow Born (Human; Varisian)
harrow born (human; varisian).description:
You grew up around the mysterious fortune-tellers known throughout Ustalav and Varisia.

Requirements: Varisian Human Heritage

Benefits: You start play with a harrow deck passed down from a relative. Because of your skill with fortune-telling, you gain a +1 trait bonus on initiative checks.

two-world magic.name: Two-World Magic
two-world magic.description:
You have bridged the gap between the natural magic of the Mwangi peoples and the refined magic of the Chelish colonists.

Benefits: Select one 0-level spell from a class spell list other than your own. This spell is a 0-level spell on your class spell list (or a 1st-level spell if your class doesn't have 0-level spells). For example, if you are a druid, you could select mage hand and thereafter prepare it as a 0-level druid spell; if you are a sorcerer, you could select know direction as a 0-level sorcerer spell known.

the wagon.name: The Wagon
the wagon.description:
Like the vehicle that conveys travelers across the heavens, you are blessed with extraordinary movement capabilities.

Requirements: 21 Erastus - 10 Arodus

Benefits: Once per day as a swift action, you can increase you move speed by 10 feet for 1 round.

goblin foolhardiness (goblin).name: Goblin Foolhardiness (Goblin)
goblin foolhardiness (goblin).description:
You have a tendency toward gross overconfidence in combat.

Requirements: Goblin

Benefits: When facing an enemy that's larger than you are, if you have no allies in any adjacent squares, your posturing, bravado, and cussing grant you a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls with non-reach melee weapons.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attack rolls with non-reach melee weapons, when facing a larger enemy, if you have no allies in any adjacent squares.

survivalist (human).name: Survivalist (Human)
survivalist (human).description:
Requirements: Human

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against natural environmental hazards.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against natural environmental hazards.

stoic optimism (desna).name: Stoic Optimism (Desna)
stoic optimism (desna).description:
You realize that accepting the pain in life accentuates its joys by contrast, and so your hope sustains you where others despair.

Requirements: Desna

Benefits: You receive a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against fear effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against fear effects.

shadow dealer (ustalav).name: Shadow Dealer (Ustalav)
shadow dealer (ustalav).description:
You have survived and thrived in the dark forests of Ustalav by knowing how to make deals with terrible things.

Requirements: Ustalav

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against fear effects and a +1 trait bonus on Bluff and Diplomacy skills checks against evil fey, monstrous humanoids, and undead.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against fear effects.
+[[1]] Trait bonus against evil fey, monstrous humanoids, and undead.
+[[1]] Trait bonus against evil fey, monstrous humanoids, and undead.

foe of the strange (strange aeons).name: Foe of the Strange (Strange Aeons)
foe of the strange (strange aeons).description:
You awake feeling emboldened after a terrifying nightmare. Instead of being frightened by the bizarre creatures from your nightmares, these experiences have steeled you against these startling alien beings. You know that you fell victim to strange circumstances and loathsome forces, but you refuse to give in to a creeping sense of helplessness. Instead, you vow to fight back against whoever or whatever is responsible for your current situation. The creatures of your nightmares won’t hold fear over your head. You’ll take the fight to them.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against extraordinary, spell-like, and supernatural abilities of aberrations (for example, a gibbering mouther’s gibbering or an intellect devourer’s confusion). In addition, whenever you’re confused and roll to determine behavior in a given round, subtract 10 from the result.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against aberrations. In addition, whenever you’re confused and roll to determine behavior, subtract 10 from the result.

history of heresy.name: History of Heresy
history of heresy.description:
You were raised with heretical views that have made it difficult for you to accept most religious beliefs and often caused you or those you love to be treated as pariahs. As a result, you have turned your back on religious teachings.

Benefits: As long as you do not possess any levels in a class that grants divine spellcasting power, you gain a +1 trait bonus on all saving throws made against divine spells.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus vs divine spells as long as you do not possess any levels in a class that grants divine spellcasting power.

crocodile swim (skinwalker; werecrocodil.name: Crocodile Swim (Skinwalker; Werecrocodile-Kin)
crocodile swim (skinwalker; werecrocodil.description:
You grew up near crocodiles, learning to move as they do.

Requirements: Werecrocodile-Kin Skinwalker Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Swim and Stealth checks while moving at half speed or less and mostly or completely underwater. One of these skills (your choice) becomes a class skill for you. 

Usage: Select Swim or Stealth when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus  while moving at half speed or less and mostly or completely underwater.
+[[2]] Trait bonus  while moving at half speed or less and mostly or completely underwater.

cross-disciplined.name: Cross-Disciplined
cross-disciplined.description:
Your dabbling has granted you greater insight into the proper casting of certain spells.

Benefits: Once per day, you may cast a spell you have prepared that appears on both the magus and wizard spell lists as if your caster level were 1 level higher.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you may cast a spell from both the wizard and magus spell lists with a +[[1]] bonus to your CL.

narrow escape (the shackles).name: Narrow Escape (The Shackles)
narrow escape (the shackles).description:
You were captured by kuru cannibals and nearly devoured in a sacrificial ritual. At the last second you wriggled free from your bonds and escaped into the jungle. Your narrow escape has left you with a paranoid fear of capture. You secretly conduct regular sessions in which you slip out of different types of knots.

Requirements: The Shackles

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Escape Artist checks, and Escape Artist becomes a class skill for you.

thoughtful wish-maker (plane of fire).name: Thoughtful Wish-Maker (Plane of Fire)
thoughtful wish-maker (plane of fire).description:
You are well acquainted with the many ways words can be twisted.

Requirements: Plane of Fire

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks. Furthermore, if you succeed at a DC 25 Sense Motive check prior to making any wish granted by an outsider, you become aware of your wish’s potential pitfalls. If you succeed at this check by 5 or more, you figure out how to word your wish in such a way that your words are not twisted.

contextNotes.text: If you succeed at a DC 25 Sense Motive check prior to making any wish granted by an outsider, you become aware of your wish’s potential pitfalls. If you succeed at this check by 5 or more, you figure out how to word your wish in such a way that your words are not twisted.

szaezan crags miner (bachuan).name: Szaezan Crags Miner (Bachuan)
szaezan crags miner (bachuan).description:
You were a miner of the now scarce ores of the Szaezan Crags, and have become adept at identifying minerals and gems.

Requirements: Bachuan

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Appraise checks, and Appraise becomes a class skill for you.

nature worshiper.name: Nature Worshiper
nature worshiper.description:
When you were young, you saw a dead creature’s body fertilize a field. Witnessing the connection between life and death moved you to recognize your own connection with the world, and ever since you have revered nature and feared death less.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against death effects and effects that use negative energy, such as energy drain and inflict spells.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against death effects and effects that use negative energy, such as energy drain and inflict spells.

surface stranger (underground).name: Surface Stranger (Underground)
surface stranger (underground).description:
You were born and raised in a subterranean home with little or no bright light.

Requirements: Underground

Benefits: Your miss chance for concealment or total concealment due to either mundane or magical darkness decreases by 10%. You are dazzled for 1 round when you are suddenly exposed to bright light.

contextNotes.text: Your miss chance for concealment or total concealment due to either mundane or magical darkness decreases by 10%. You are dazzled for 1 round when you are suddenly exposed to bright light.

guiding spirit.name: Guiding Spirit
guiding spirit.description:
Few understand the medium’s willingness to harbor powerful spirits, but even if you aren’t one of these talented few, you’ve always identified with their abilities. You’ve always felt that you were not alone in this world and that a mysterious guiding spirit watches over you with singular focus. You may have briefly glimpsed this strange spirit—or maybe you simply feel its presence in times of need.

Benefits: Once per day as a swift action, you can look to your guiding spirit for advice. The next time you roll a d20 in that same round, roll twice and pick the better result. If one of these two rolls is a natural 20, you can use this ability again that same day.

contextNotes.text: Once per day as a swift action, the next time you roll a d20 in that same round, roll twice and pick the better result. If one of these two rolls is a natural 20, you can use this ability again that same day.

Once per day as a swift action, the next time you roll a d20 in that same round, roll twice and pick the better result. If one of these two rolls is a natural 20, you can use this ability again that same day.

Once per day as a swift action, the next time you roll a d20 in that same round, roll twice and pick the better result. If one of these two rolls is a natural 20, you can use this ability again that same day.

Once per day as a swift action, the next time you roll a d20 in that same round, roll twice and pick the better result. If one of these two rolls is a natural 20, you can use this ability again that same day.

Once per day as a swift action, the next time you roll a d20 in that same round, roll twice and pick the better result. If one of these two rolls is a natural 20, you can use this ability again that same day.

Once per day as a swift action, the next time you roll a d20 in that same round, roll twice and pick the better result. If one of these two rolls is a natural 20, you can use this ability again that same day.

Once per day as a swift action, the next time you roll a d20 in that same round, roll twice and pick the better result. If one of these two rolls is a natural 20, you can use this ability again that same day.

oblivious.name: Oblivious
oblivious.description:
Whether because you are an avid daydreamer or you are accustomed to everything being handed to you and spelled out for your benefit, you pay little attention to your surroundings and other people.

Effects: You take a –2 penalty on Sense Motive and sight-based Perception checks.

contextNotes.text: -[[2]] penalty to sight-based checks.

calculated bribe.name: Calculated Bribe
calculated bribe.description:
If you follow this code, you are keenly aware of the value of actions as well as goods.

Requirements: Code of Perseverance

Benefits: Once per day while haggling with a merchant or striking a bargain with another intelligent creature, you can spend a number of gold pieces equal to your level times; 10 in order to reroll a Diplomacy check you just made, before the results are revealed. You must take the result of the reroll, even if it's worse than the original roll. In addition, you begin play with a set of extravagant noble's clothes worth 100 gp.
