Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on weapon damage rolls against Large or larger creatures of the animal type.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on weapon damage rolls against Large or larger creatures of the animal type.

eagle knight recruit (ruins of azlant).name: Eagle Knight Recruit (Ruins of Azlant)
eagle knight recruit (ruins of azlant).description:
Since Talmandor's Bounty is an Andoren colony and is unlikely to see the threat of slavery, the presence of the Eagle Knights isn’t strongly needed, and the Bountiful Venture Company would prefer to not have that organization immediately associated with the colony (despite the colony’s name having been derived from the Eagle Knights’ patron, Talmandor). However, the need for safety can’t be overlooked. You were a soldier in the Andoren army or you were a civilian who showed great promise in military matters. The Eagle Knights recruited you and offered unofficial Eagle Knight status if you managed to make it into the ranks of the colonists. There, you would see to the safety of the colony and send back reports with each supply ship regarding the general state of the colony and any threats you notice. The Bountiful Venture Company admired your military prowess and selected you for the second wave of colonists to arrive at Talmandor’s Bounty.

Benefits: You begin play with a breastplate, a longsword (or some other martial or simple weapon), and an additional 100 gp of gear. In addition, you gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against mind-affecting effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against mind-affecting effects.

cold and calculating.name: Cold and Calculating
cold and calculating.description:
You are adept at tricking favored enemies of your race or those related to your race.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on all Bluff checks against favored enemies that share one of your subtypes. You also gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls made against such favored enemies during the surprise round.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against favored enemies that share one of your subtypes.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on attack rolls against favored enemies that share one of your subtypes during suprise round.

fugitive.name: Fugitive
fugitive.description:
You crossed the law (or those in charge thought you did), and you are now a wanted criminal. As such, you have grown used to looking over your shoulder wherever you go.

Benefits: While in settlements, you gain a +3 trait bonus on Perception checks to determine awareness for the purpose of a surprise round.

contextNotes.text: +[[3]] Trait bonus to determine awareness for the purpose of a surprise round.

betrayed (sh).name: Betrayed (SH)
betrayed (sh).description:
You were reported to a dangerous authority such as the Gray Gardeners of Galt, the high inquisitors of Cheliax, or the Council of Mwanyisa of Mzali, and narrowly escaped death. You second-guess your instincts constantly, leaving you never sure whether or not to trust someone and endangered if you encounter that group’s agents again.

Effects: You can roll twice and take the lower result on Sense Motive checks to get hunches. You cannot reroll this result, even if you have another ability that would normally allow you to do so.

contextNotes.text: You roll twice and take the lower result on Sense Motive checks to get hunches. You cannot reroll this result, even if you have another ability that would normally allow you to do so.

style sage.name: Style Sage
style sage.description:
You have a passion for history and news concerning monastic disciplines.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on checks with your choice of either Knowledge (local) or Knowledge (history), and the one you choose is always a class skill for you. In addition, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks made to gather information about any person with levels in monk.

Usage: Choose either Knowledge (local) or Knowledge (history) when selecting this trait. Then add the chosen Skill as a class skill on the details tab and add that skill to the undefined change on the changes tab.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to gather information about any person with levels in monk.

diseased heart (ghlaunder).name: Diseased Heart (Ghlaunder)
diseased heart (ghlaunder).description:
Your work with infections has infused your spirit with disease.

Requirements: Ghlaunder

Benefits: Any creature that performs a blood drain or swallow whole attack on you must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw (DC = 10 + your character level + your Constitution modifier) or become infected with filth fever. This is a poison effect.

contextNotes.text: Any creature that performs a blood drain or swallow whole attack on you must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw (DC = [[10 + @attributes.hd.level + @abilities.con.mod]]) or become infected with filth fever. This is a poison effect.

regional influence - riddleport (varisia.name: Regional Influence - Riddleport (Varisia)
regional influence - riddleport (varisia.description:
Although you live some distance from the city-state your community owes fealty to, you’ve been influenced by its sophisticated urban ways.

>Requirements

Varisia

Benefits

Determine the city-state of which your home is a holding. You gain a +1 trait bonus on checks with one Profession skill and one of the following benefits.

Riddleport

Gain a +1 trait bonus on Swim checks.

Usage: Select one Profession skill when you take this trait.

Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the chosen skill.

Update the target of the untargeted change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

Special Note

This is one of three versions of this trait. Other versions can be found here:

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.PEF5ztrAhR9wRIOa]{Regional Influence - Magnimar (Varisia)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.UNEA8OGKTfw7UdSb]{Regional Influence - Korvosa (Varisia)}

cloistered upbringing (any city).name: Cloistered Upbringing (Any City)
cloistered upbringing (any city).description:
Growing up among acolytes or monks, you were exposed to their supernatural power.

Requirements: Any City

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against the supernatural effects of channeled energy or abilities powered by ki, and regain 1 additional hit point when healed by supernatural effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus  against the supernatural effects of channeled energy or abilities powered by ki.

burned.name: Burned
burned.description:
You were badly burned once by volcanic ash, torch-wielding mobs, or some fiery accident, and the scars pain you terribly you whenever you are too near to fire.

Effects: You take a –1 penalty on saving throws against fire effects. In addition, whenever you are adjacent to open flames or are on fire, you take a –1 penalty on all attack rolls, saving throws, and skill checks until you spend an entire round away from fire. These penalties are not cumulative. (An instantaneous fire effect adjacent to you or affecting you causes this penalty to apply until 1 round after it is gone.)

contextNotes.text:
–[[1]] Penalty on saving throws against fire effects.
–[[1]] Penalty on all saving throws whenever you are adjacent to open flames or are on fire until you spend an entire round away from fire
These penalties are not cumulative
–[[1]] Penalty on all attack rolls whenever you are adjacent to open flames or are on fire until you spend an entire round away from fire
–[[1]] Penalty on all skill checks whenever you are adjacent to open flames or are on fire until you spend an entire round away from fire

genie-caller (qadira).name: Genie-Caller (Qadira)
genie-caller (qadira).description:
Your exploration of the magic for calling genies has increased your overall aptitude for summoning.

Requirements: Qadira

Benefits: Once per day, you may cast one conjuration (summoning) spell as if your caster level were 2 higher than normal.

trap finder (mummy's mask).name: Trap Finder (Mummy's Mask)
trap finder (mummy's mask).description:
Forgotten dungeons and ancient tombs have always held an appeal for you, and you’ve never been able to resist the urge to delve into these lost sites in search of knowledge, treasure, or both. You may not have received any formal training in the roguish arts, but you’ve nonetheless become skilled at spotting and disabling hidden traps. The tombs of Wati’s necropolis, just opened for exploration, seem like the perfect place to put your skills to the test.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Disable Device checks, and that skill is always a class skill for you. In addition, you can use Disable Device to disarm magic traps, like a rogue.

contextNotes.text: You can disarm magic traps.

shoanti spirit-singer (human; bekyar or.name: Shoanti Spirit-singer (Human; Bekyar or Shoanti)
shoanti spirit-singer (human; bekyar or.description:
The spirit voices of your ancestors sing to you of the world around you.

Requirements: Bekyar Human Heritage or Shoanti Human Heritage

Benefits: Once per day, you can act in the surprise round even if you aren’t aware of your attackers; you can use this action only to start a bardic song or raging song, and you’re still considered flat-footed until you act in the first normal round of combat.

loyalty (grand lodge).name: Loyalty (Grand Lodge)
loyalty (grand lodge).description:
You resist attempts to dissuade you from obeying the Decemvirate’s will.

Requirements: Grand Lodge

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against enchantment spells and spelllike abilities.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus vs enchantment spells and spell-like abilities.

colonial (serpent's skull).name: Colonial (Serpent's Skull)
colonial (serpent's skull).description:
You come from a long line of Sargavan colonists, stretching back to Cheliax's original expansion into the land during the Everwar. While your people's control of Sargava wanes every day, and Eleder has functioned independently from Cheliax since House Thrune brought diabolism to the political forefront, you nevertheless maintain a hardy Chelish constitution and natural sense of survival. What took you away from home is up to you, but you return to the land of your birth on the Jenivere in the hope of putting Sargava back on the map.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (local) checks relating to Sargavan settlements and politics and a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against disease. Characters with this trait are Sargavan natives and might be returning via any city, but especially from the Chelish ports of Kintargo, Pezzack, or Corentyn.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on checks relating to Sargavan settlements and politics.
+[[1]] Trait bonus against disease.

traveler of a hundred lands.name: Traveler of a Hundred Lands
traveler of a hundred lands.description:
You have learned the ways of many lands.

Exemplar Type: Regional Exemplar

Benefits: Choose any two skills; each of those skills becomes a class skill for you. For every other regional trait you have, select an additional skill to treat as a class skill.

Exemplar Traits: A character can have only one exemplar trait, and selecting an exemplar trait takes the place of two regular traits. Each exemplar trait is tied to a trait category, such as combat traits or regional traits; a character with an exemplar trait is no longer restricted to a single trait of that category and can select any number of such traits when gaining further traits. In addition, a character with an exemplar trait can select the Additional Traits feat any number of times and use the feat to gain an exemplar trait of a valid trait category for which they qualify. 

Usage

  1. Select any two skills when you take this trait.

  2. If any of your selected skills are craft, perform, or profession skills, first update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the chosen skill(s).

  3. Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

  4. if you gain additional Regional traits, you add additional class skills by repeating steps 2-3 above for each selected class skill.

too many secrets.name: Too Many Secrets
too many secrets.description:
You’ve told too many lies, and made up too many cover stories, and now even you can’t keep it all straight. The lies have become reality to you, which makes it difficult to think of a new convincing lie. You hesitate at crucial moments when trying to fool those around you, and likely shouldn’t be in the field at all anymore.

Effects: You take a -2 penalty on Bluff checks and saving throws against illusions.

contextNotes.text: -[[2]] penalty vs illusions.

supportive.name: Supportive
supportive.description:
You were forced to get by in an isolated enclave, whether surrounded by a community that feared and suspected you or in a remote locale far from other people, and you became practiced at assisting your friends.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on skill checks to use the aid another action.

Suggested Characters

Halflings, Mwangi, Tians

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to use the aid another action.

roof racer (varisia; korvosa).name: Roof Racer (Varisia; Korvosa)
roof racer (varisia; korvosa).description:
You’ve spent more than your fair share of time among the strange sloping slums that cover the rooftops of much of Old Korvosa.

Requirements: Korvosa in Varisia

Benefits: When using the Acrobatics skill to make a long jump, you treat the DC of the skill check as if the distance being jumped were 5 feet shorter than it actually is.

contextNotes.text: When making a long jump, you treat the DC of the check as if the distance being jumped were 5 feet shorter than it actually is.

family ties.name: Family Ties
family ties.description:
Your family is extremely important to you, and you feel disheartened when you can't do what they ask.

Effects: When a family member makes a request of you, you must fulfill that request or take a –2 penalty on all Wisdom- and Charisma-based ability checks and skill checks until you either do what was requested or succeed at a DC 20 Will saving throw, which you can attempt once per day at the start of each day. You can't take this drawback if you have no family. If you ever lose your family or lose contact with your family, exchange this drawback for the Doubt drawback.

contextNotes.text: When a family member makes a request of you, you must fulfill that request or take a -[[2]] penalty on all Wisdom and Charisma based ability checks and skill checks until you either do what was requested or succeed at a DC 20 Will saving throw, which you can attempt once per day at the start of each day.

When a family member makes a request of you, you must fulfill that request or take a -[[2]] penalty on all Wisdom and Charisma based ability checks and skill checks until you either do what was requested or succeed at a DC 20 Will saving throw, which you can attempt once per day at the start of each day.

When a family member makes a request of you, you must fulfill that request or take a -[[2]] penalty on all Wisdom and Charisma based ability checks and skill checks until you either do what was requested or succeed at a DC 20 Will saving throw, which you can attempt once per day at the start of each day.

When a family member makes a request of you, you must fulfill that request or take a -[[2]] penalty on all Wisdom and Charisma based ability checks and skill checks until you either do what was requested or succeed at a DC 20 Will saving throw, which you can attempt once per day at the start of each day.

orphaned by giants (giantslayer).name: Orphaned by Giants (Giantslayer)
orphaned by giants (giantslayer).description:
You grew up in a tranquil, happy home near the mountains, but that peace was shattered when giants came down from the mountains to raid your settlement. The giants killed your parents and left you a young orphan, and since that day, you’ve sworn to avenge the deaths of your kin.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls against creatures with the giant subtype, and a +2 trait bonus on rolls to confirm critical hits against creatures with the giant subtype.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attack rolls, and a +[[2]] Trait bonus to confirm critical hits, against creatures with the giant subtype.

unabashed gall.name: Unabashed Gall
unabashed gall.description:
Your total indifference to the law even as you’re breaking it is such that those watching you are stunned.

Benefits: Whenever you commit an unexpected and outrageously unlawful act (such as knowingly destroying or stealing something) that would elicit a hostile response from another creature, you may attempt a Bluff check opposed by the opponent’s Sense Motive check. If you’re successful, you and any allies who were willfully expecting your unlawful deed may act in a surprise round.

For each ally willfully expecting your unlawful deed, you take a cumulative –2 penalty on your Bluff check. If your Bluff check fails, there is no surprise round. Regardless of whether your Bluff check succeeds, you and any allies willfully taking advantage of your unlawful deed treat your initiative checks for the duration of combat as though you had each rolled a 1.

family connections (tiefling).name: Family Connections (Tiefling)
family connections (tiefling).description:
Your dark ancestry gives you a special insight into how to motivate or placate fiends and their kin.

Requirements: Tiefling

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Bluff and Diplomacy checks made against outsiders belonging to the evil subtype.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against outsiders belonging to the evil subtype.
+[[2]] Trait bonus against outsiders belonging to the evil subtype.

surprise weapon.name: Surprise Weapon
surprise weapon.description:
You are skilled at fighting with objects not traditionally considered weapons.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on attack rolls with improvised weapons.

Usage: In Combat tab, add `+ 2[Trait Bonus - Suprise Weapon]` to Attack Roll Bonus on all improved weapons.

lunar birth.name: Lunar Birth
lunar birth.description:
Born during a lunar eclipse, you were forever marked by the moon’s mysterious and ancient magic.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against the spells and spell-like abilities cast by humans.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against spells and spell-like abilities cast by humans.

aquatic survivalist (plane of water).name: Aquatic Survivalist (Plane of Water)
aquatic survivalist (plane of water).description:
For whatever reason, you spent much of your formative years fighting to keep yourself alive amid the fearsome creatures that inhabit the infinite oceans of the Plane of Water, and you learned how to stay hidden even against predators with unusual senses.

Requirements: Plane of Water

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Stealth checks while underwater. Once per day, you can attempt a Stealth check to @UUID[Compendium.pf1.class-abilities.Item.ytotO22rp5ji6rT1]{hide in plain sight} (as the ranger class feature of the same name) while underwater. This is only effective against creatures that rely on @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-universal-monster-rules.Item.VkXlIb0zYsH4IDxz]{blindsense}, @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-universal-monster-rules.Item.6M7h341H35f5t83M]{blindsight}, or @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-universal-monster-rules.Item.rzESliiwisWAdLTr]{tremorsense}. Creatures with other senses perceive you normally.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus while underwater.

Once per day, you can attempt  to @UUID[Compendium.pf1.class-abilities.Item.ytotO22rp5ji6rT1]{hide in plain sight} while underwater. This is only effective against creatures that rely on blindsense, blindsight, or tremorsense.

reassuring advice.name: Reassuring Advice
reassuring advice.description:
You’ve studied the speeches and writings of a leader or thinker who served as a reassuring voice in her community in the turbulent times after Aroden died, painfully learning which portions of Aroden’s advice no longer applied and what of his writings could still be usefully applied.

Benefits: Once per day, you can speak Aroden’s words of encouragement to grant any human a +1 insight bonus on any attack roll, saving throw, or skill check of his choice within the following hour. The choice to use the insight bonus must be made before the related roll is made.

savant (kitharodian academy).name: Savant (Kitharodian Academy)
savant (kitharodian academy).description:
From a very young age, the ability to master performance types has come quite naturally to you.

Requirements: Kitharodian Academy

Benefits: Chose a performance type. You gain a +2 trait bonus on all Perform checks made using that performance type. Perform is always a class skill for you.

Usage: Add your chosen performance type to the target of the change on the Changes tab

frustrating (gathlain).name: Frustrating (Gathlain)
frustrating (gathlain).description:
You can get anyone’s attention, even when they are focused elsewhere.

Requirements: Gathlain

Benefits: You can make Bluff checks to create a diversion for allies within 30 feet to hide (provided they readied an action to hide), and you can make Bluff checks to feint for one adjacent ally of your choice. Either use of this ability is a standard action.

contextNotes.text: You can make Bluff checks to create a diversion for allies within 30 feet to hide (provided they readied an action to hide), and you can make Bluff checks to feint for one adjacent ally of your choice. Either use of this ability is a standard action.

gold finger (the exchange).name: Gold Finger (The Exchange)
gold finger (the exchange).description:
Your family comes from a long, proud tradition of housebreaking and thievery. You are a strong part of that tradition.

Requirements: The Exchange

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Disable Device and Sleight of Hand checks, and one of these skills (your choice) becomes a class skill for you.

Usage: Choose either Disable Device or Sleight of Hand when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

chain master (tiefling; shackleborn).name: Chain Master (Tiefling; Shackleborn)
chain master (tiefling; shackleborn).description:
Your fiendish ancestry has granted you unnatural skill with the chain, and your enemies know to stay away when you wield it as a weapon.

Requirements: Shackleborn Tiefling Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on combat maneuver checks made to trip opponents with a spiked chain or whip.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to trip opponents with a spiked chain or whip.

born under the stranger.name: Born under the Stranger
born under the stranger.description:
Your birth was marked by the alignment of Aucturn with your birth sign and by strange happenings, giving you a link to alien beings.

Benefits: When feinting against aberrations, you do not take a –4 penalty for feinting against a non-humanoid creature, and you take only a –4 penalty if you feint against an aberration of animal Intelligence (instead of –8). You still cannot feint against mindless creatures.

contextNotes.text: Against aberrations, you ignore the -[[4]] Penalty for feinting vs non-humanoids. If they have animal intelligence, you take only a -[[4]] Penalty instead of -[[8]] to feint.

storyteller (lantern lodge).name: Storyteller (Lantern Lodge)
storyteller (lantern lodge).description:
Your long journeys between the Inner Sea and Tian Xia have exposed you to a wide variety of campfire tales.

Requirements: Lantern Lodge

Benefits: Once per scenario, you can make a Knowledge check with a trait bonus equal to your Intelligence modifier plus 3 (minimum +1). You can make this check even if you do not have ranks in the required Knowledge skill.

rahadoumi cultist (rahadoum).name: Rahadoumi Cultist (Rahadoum)
rahadoumi cultist (rahadoum).description:
You're a secret member of a forbidden faith in Rahadoum. You have learned to hide your beliefs and how to identify others of your faith.

Requirements: Rahadoum

Benefits: You gain a +5 trait bonus on Bluff checks to receive secret messages, and a +5 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks to intercept secret messages from others of your faith.

contextNotes.text: +[[5]] Trait bonus on Bluff checks to receive secret messages.
+[[5]] Trait bonus to intercept secret messages from others of your faith.

dragon-hunted.name: Dragon-Hunted
dragon-hunted.description:
You’ve survived being hunted by the draconic nightmares on the Shackles islands of Dahak’s Fang, Dahak’s Horn, or Dahak’s Tooth.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Will saves against fear effects, and against any saving throw from a spell or effect created by a dragon.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against fear effects
+[[1]] Trait bonus against any spell or effect created by a dragon.

schooled inquisitor.name: Schooled Inquisitor
schooled inquisitor.description:
Your additional training aids in identifying the wiles of your faith’s enemies.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on all Knowledge (planes) and Knowledge (religion) checks made to identify the abilities and weaknesses of creatures.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on checks made to identify the abilities and weaknesses of creatures.
+[[2]] Trait bonus on checks made to identify the abilities and weaknesses of creatures.

tiger brigadier (bachuan).name: Tiger Brigadier (Bachuan)
tiger brigadier (bachuan).description:
As a member of the Tiger Brigade, you are ready to guide the people of Bachuan and other nations.

Requirements: Bachuan

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Intimidate checks made against anyone whose age is one or more aging steps above yours, and Intimidate becomes a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against anyone whose age is one or more aging steps above yours

battlefield disciple.name: Battlefield Disciple
battlefield disciple.description:
You are skilled at supporting your allies in the heat of battle.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Heal checks made to stabilize a dying creature. In addition, when you successfully use the aid another action in combat to grant an ally a bonus on her next attack roll, the bonus you grant increases by 1.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus made to stabilize a dying creature
If you successfully use the Aid Another action in combat to grant an ally a bonus on their next attack roll, increase the bonus by +[[1]]

veiled disciple.name: Veiled Disciple
veiled disciple.description:
Trained in espionage, you’re skilled at passing messages and staying on task.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Bluff checks made to pass secret messages and a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against charm and compulsion effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to pass secret messages.
+[[1]] Trait bonus against charm and compulsion effects.

soul-searcher's strength (rivethun).name: Soul-Searcher's Strength (Rivethun)
soul-searcher's strength (rivethun).description:
Your introspections have taught you to understand your own flaws and proclivities and how to work with them.

Requirements: Must follow the traditions of the Rivethun

Benefits: Choose one saving throw. You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws of the chosen kind.

Usage: By default the trait gives the trait bonus to Fortitude. Change it in the changes tab to your chosen saving throw.

black eagle recruit (lambreth).name: Black Eagle Recruit (Lambreth)
black eagle recruit (lambreth).description:
You were recruited to serve as one of Lord Arnefax’s feared Black Eagles for a time, but you have since left that life.

Requirements: Lambreth

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saves against fear effects; this bonus increases to +3 if an evil outsider caused the effect.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus  against fear effects; this increases to +3 if an evil outsider caused the effect.

deft dodger.name: Deft Dodger
deft dodger.description:
Growing up in a rough neighborhood or a dangerous environment has honed your senses.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Reflex saves.

magical lineage.name: Magical Lineage
magical lineage.description:
One of your parents was a gifted spellcaster who not only used metamagic often, but developed many magical items and perhaps even a new spell or two,and you have inherited a fragment of this greatness.

Benefits: Pick one spell when you choose this trait. When you apply metamagic feats to this spell, treat its actual level as 1 lower for determining the spell's final adjusted level.

valknar alumnus (tymon).name: Valknar Alumnus (Tymon)
valknar alumnus (tymon).description:
You trained at the exclusive Valknar Gladiatorial College.

Requirements: Tymon

Benefits: Select one combat maneuver when you take this trait. You gain a +1 trait bonus on performance combat checks and a +1 trait bonus on combat maneuver checks to perform the selected maneuver.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on combat maneuver checks to perform the selected maneuver.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on performance combat checks to perform the combat maneuver selected.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on performance combat checks to perform the combat maneuver selected.

glint-tongued (human; ulfen).name: Glint-Tongued (Human; Ulfen)
glint-tongued (human; ulfen).description:
You have a gift for poetic turns of phrase and vicious streams of insults.

Requirements: Ulfen Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Charisma-based skill checks made to impress or persuade other Ulfen (or, at the GM’s option, other poetically-minded people), and a +2 trait bonus on Intimidate checks.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on  checks made to impress or persuade other Ulfen (or, at the GM’s option, other poetically-minded people).

trustworthy.name: Trustworthy
trustworthy.description:
People find it easy to put their faith in you.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Bluff checks made to fool someone. You also gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks, and Diplomacy is always a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to fool someone.

forgetful.name: Forgetful
forgetful.description:
You are easily distracted and prone to forgetting important things.

Effects: Whenever you leave a location you have been at for longer than 1 hour, you must attempt a DC 10 Wisdom check. If you fail, you leave behind one random mundane item (a purse of money counts as an item).

suspicious.name: Suspicious
suspicious.description:
You discovered at an early age that someone you trusted, perhaps an older sibling or a parent, had lied to you, and lied often, about something you had taken for granted, leaving you quick to question the claims of others.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks, and Sense Motive is always a class skill for you.

hellknight initiate.name: Hellknight Initiate
hellknight initiate.description:
You have laboriously studied the training rites of various Hellknight orders with the intent of joining one yourself.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on damage rolls against outsiders summoned through conjuration (calling) and conjuration (summoning) spells and effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on damage rolls against outsiders summoned through conjuration (calling) and conjuration (summoning) spells and effects.

scholar of ruins (human).name: Scholar of Ruins (Human)
scholar of ruins (human).description:
From the moment you could walk and talk, the ruins of ancient civilizations have fascinated you. Because of this, you have special insight into geography as well as expertise in exploring lost places.

Requirements: Human

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (geography) and Knowledge (dungeoneering) checks. One of these skills (your choice) is always a class skill for you.

Usage: Select Knowledge (geography) or Knowledge (dungeoneering) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

gunworker (dwarf).name: Gunworker (Dwarf)
gunworker (dwarf).description:
You lived among the dwarven alchemists who work with firearm technology in Dongun Hold.

Requirements: Dwarf

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Craft checks involving firearms, explosives, and alchemical weapons that deal fire damage.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus checks involving firearms, explosives, and alchemical weapons that deal fire damage.

eoxian experiment (eox).name: Eoxian Experiment (Eox)
eoxian experiment (eox).description:
You were raised on Eox, where you were overseen by alien forces and subjected to horrifying experimentation.

Requirements: Eox

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against pain effects (Pathfinder RPG Ultimate Magic 138) and the spell-like abilities of undead.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against the spell-like abilities of undead.
+[[1]] Trait bonus against pain effects.

simple disciple.name: Simple Disciple
simple disciple.description:
You picked up skill in a craft or a profession during your time at a monastery.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on checks with a Profession or Craft skill of your choice.

Usage: Add a single profession or craft skill to your actor. Add the chosen trait as the target on the Changes tab of this trait.

adherent of ancient osirion (osirion; ca.name: Adherent of Ancient Osirion (Osirion; Catfolk)
adherent of ancient osirion (osirion; ca.description:
Although Bastet has been all but forgotten by the native Osirians, you either sojourned with or were raised in a cult dedicated to the cat-headed goddess of Ancient Osirion.

Requirements: Catfolk native to Osirion; Bastet

Benefits: You treat the @UUID[Compendium.pf1.weapons-and-ammo.Item.kRFQLnjgl2cTlTrc]{tekko-kagi} as a martial weapon instead of an exotic weapon.

You also treat the tekko-kagi as if it were a set of @UUID[Compendium.pf1.weapons-and-ammo.Item.1TiILWBLdzYpnPky]{claw blades} when determining how it interacts with other effects.

Usage: Modify the weapon on the inventory of the actor to reflect the above noted changes

ironfang survivor (ironfang invasion).name: Ironfang Survivor (Ironfang Invasion)
ironfang survivor (ironfang invasion).description:
Whether you were serving in the military or simply beset by a surprise attack, you barely survived an encounter with the Ironfang Legion, one of Molthune’s infamous monster regiments. Maybe you even survived the horrors of the Ramgate Massacre. You can’t scrub the memories of their brutality from your waking or sleeping mind, and you keenly recall their distinctive fighting styles.

Benefits: You gain a +2 dodge bonus to AC against goblinoids, and once per day when you attempt an Intimidate, Sense Motive, or Stealth check against a goblinoid, you can roll two dice and use the better result.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Dodge bonus to AC against goblinoids.
Once per day, on a check against a goblinoid, you can roll two dice and use the better result. ([[@resources.ironfangSurvivorIronfangInvasion.value]] remaining uses)
Once per day, on a check against a goblinoid, you can roll two dice and use the better result. ([[@resources.ironfangSurvivorIronfangInvasion.value]] remaining uses)
Once per day, on a check against a goblinoid, you can roll two dice and use the better result. ([[@resources.ironfangSurvivorIronfangInvasion.value]] remaining uses)

flame-touched.name: Flame-Touched
flame-touched.description:
You share an affinity with elemental fire.

Benefits: You gain DR 1/— against creatures and attacks with the fire type.

contextNotes.text: DR 1/— against creatures and attacks with the fire type.

demented inventor (half-orc, orc).name: Demented Inventor (Half-Orc, Orc)
demented inventor (half-orc, orc).description:
Requirements: Half-Orc, Orc

Benefits: You adore crafting new and ever more creative weapons, and some of them even work. You gain a +2 trait bonus on Craft (weapons) checks, and it becomes a class skill. 

Usage: Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the Craft (weapons) skill.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to Craft (weapons).

elven pantheist (any elven deity).name: Elven Pantheist (any elven deity)
elven pantheist (any elven deity).description:
Your knowledge of the elven gods has given you a broad perspective.

Requirements: Any elven deity

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on all Knowledge (religion) skill checks, and Knowledge (religion) is always a class skill for you.

vile domain.name: Vile Domain
vile domain.description:
Your connection with a malignant force has granted you great power. 

Benefits: Choose a domain granted by an evil aligned deity, such as an archdevil, demon lord, or Great Old One. You gain a +1 trait bonus to your caster level when casting spells that are granted by that domain.

This doesn’t grant you the ability to cast such spells—you must still have access to the spells through some other means.

Usage: Select both an evil aligned deity and a domain granted by the chosen deity when you take this trait.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus when casting spells that are normally granted by the chosen domain.

vindictive (taldor faction).name: Vindictive (Taldor Faction)
vindictive (taldor faction).description:
Your family is prone to endless feuds, and you were raised by people who simply do not forgive a slight easily.

Requirements: Taldor Faction

Benefits: Once per day, you can declare a creature that has dealt you hit point damage to be your vindictive target as an immediate action when the damage is dealt. You receive a +1 trait bonus on damage rolls against this target for 1 minute.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you can declare a creature that has dealt you hit point damage to be your vindictive target as an immediate action when the damage is dealt. You receive a +[[1]] Trait bonus on damage rolls against this target for 1 minute. ([[@resources.vindictiveTaldorFaction.value]] remaining uses)

golden scales (kobold).name: Golden Scales (Kobold)
golden scales (kobold).description:
Your distinctive golden scales strongly reflect the light, making other kobolds mistrust you, but aboveground races favor you.

Requirements: Kobold

Benefits: You take a –1 penalty on Bluff checks and Diplomacy checks when dealing with other kobolds, but a +2 bonus on Bluff checks and Diplomacy checks when dealing with non-reptilian humanoids.

contextNotes.text: -[[1]] penalty  when dealing with other kobolds; +[[2]] bonus when dealing with non-reptilian humanoids.
-[[1]] penalty  when dealing with other kobolds; +[[2]] bonus when dealing with non-reptilian humanoids.

precision hearing (skinwalker; werebat-k.name: Precision Hearing (Skinwalker; Werebat-Kin)
precision hearing (skinwalker; werebat-k.description:
You have long relied on your enhanced hearing to warn you of those who would persecute you for your nature.

Requirements: Werebat-Kin Skinwalker Heritage

Benefits: Whenever you succeed at a Perception check to hear a creature you can’t see and your check exceeds the DC by 10 or more, you can pinpoint the exact square where the sound originated.

contextNotes.text: Whenever you succeed at a Perception check to hear a creature you can’t see and your check exceeds the DC by 10 or more, you can pinpoint the exact square where the sound originated.

intrepid delver (jinin).name: Intrepid Delver (Jinin)
intrepid delver (jinin).description:
One of the elder elves in Ayajinbo told you many stories of the Darklands beneath the city, and your nerves have hardened to horrors others can hardly fathom.

Requirements: Jinin

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against fear effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against fear effects.

andoletta's consolation (andoletta).name: Andoletta's Consolation (Andoletta)
andoletta's consolation (andoletta).description:
You had a close encounter with a servant or worshiper of Andoletta who provided you with a memorable, if unconventional, consolation after a loss.

Requirements: Andoletta

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against emotion and fear effects. If you are immune to fear, you instead gain a +4 trait bonus on saves against emotion effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against emotion and fear effects or a +[[4]] trait bonus against emotion effects if you are immune to fear effects.

pesh addict (katapesh).name: Pesh Addict (Katapesh)
pesh addict (katapesh).description:
You spent several years addicted to pesh, a habit that you may or may not have kicked.

Requirements: Katapesh

Benefits: You start with only half of the normal starting cash, but your knowledge of the pesh lifestyle grants you a +1 trait bonus on Bluff, Knowledge (local), and Sense Motive checks. One of these skills (your choice) is always a class skill for you.

Usage: Select Bluff, Knowledge (local), or Sense Motive when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

adaptive magic (reign of winter).name: Adaptive Magic (Reign of Winter)
adaptive magic (reign of winter).description:
The wonders of magic have always fascinated you, and you find the urge to tinker and experiment with magic almost irresistible. You could be the child of an alchemist, wizard, or witch; a member of the Pathfinder Society; or maybe someone with a touch of fey or dragon blood. You may not be trained in magic, and you’ve had your share of accidental mishaps, but you possess a natural knack for activating magic items. You’ve always been intrigued by the cold magic of the winter witches and ice mages of the North, and would love to get your hands on some of their magic items.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (arcana) and Use Magic Device checks, and Use Magic Device is a class skill for you.

deep guardian (dwarf).name: Deep Guardian (Dwarf)
deep guardian (dwarf).description:
The subtle power of the deep places of the world flows within you, and grants you mastery of summoning beings of earth and stone.

Requirements: Dwarf

Benefits: Any creature you conjure with a summon spell that has the earth subtype or a burrow speed gains a +1 morale bonus on attack rolls and a +1 enhancement bonus to Armor Class for the duration of the spell that summoned it.

Usage: Add context note to corresponding summon spell.

frontier-forged (any frontier).name: Frontier-Forged (Any Frontier)
frontier-forged (any frontier).description:
A hard life on the edge of civilization has made you resourceful but has also given you a streak of self-preservation bordering on paranoia.

Requirements: Any Frontier Area

Benefits: You receive a +1 trait bonus on Perception checks and a +1 bonus on Survival checks made to get along in the wild.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to get along in the wild.

arodenite sword training.name: Arodenite Sword Training
arodenite sword training.description:
Your family has passed down training in Aroden’s favored weapon, even though the god no longer answers their prayers.

Benefits: You are proficient with the longsword. If your class grants longsword proficiency as well, you gain a +1 trait bonus on attacks of opportunity with longswords.

contextNotes.text: If your class grants longsword proficiency, you gain +[[1]] Trait bonus on attacks of opportunity with longswords.

bully burner (goblin).name: Bully Burner (Goblin)
bully burner (goblin).description:
You were forced to serve cruel hobgoblins at some point, much to your abject humiliation and frustration, and memories of this inspire a rage that lends you strength.

Requirements: Goblin

Benefits: Against creatures with the goblinoid subtype, you gain a +1 trait bonus on combat maneuver checks and a +1 trait bonus to CMD.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against creatures with the goblinoid subtype.
+[[1]] Trait bonus against creatures with the goblinoid subtype.

horse lord (qadira faction).name: Horse Lord (Qadira Faction)
horse lord (qadira faction).description:
Your people are masters of battling on horseback, and you have mastered their rare skill.

Requirements: Qadira Faction

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Ride checks, and the Ride skill becomes a class skill for you.

taldan patriot (war for the crown).name: Taldan Patriot (War for the Crown)
taldan patriot (war for the crown).description:
You love your country, its history, and its people... even though they may not love you back. Whether you’re a bureaucrat, a minor noble, or a soldier, you want your community to be the best it can be and you channel that love into a position in service to the people of Taldor.

Benefits: You gain a +1 bonus on all Sense Motive and Knowledge (history) checks, and these skills are always class skills for you. In addition, once per day you can recall a specific fact about a Taldan noble’s personality—quirks such as a hobby or pet peeve— including information you would normally learn as a discovery check in social combat.

animal whisperer (ironfang invasion).name: Animal Whisperer (Ironfang Invasion)
animal whisperer (ironfang invasion).description:
Ironfang Invasion Player's Guide pg. 7

You’ve spent more of your life around animals than people, and find them easier to understand.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Handle Animal checks, and Handle Animal is always a class skill for you. Your trait bonus increases to +5 whenever you attempt to “push” a wild animal to perform a trick for you, so long as its attitude is indifferent or friendlier. You may target creatures of the animal type with charm or compulsion spells as if they were humanoids, but unless you have another ability to make yourself understood to animals, you must still succeed at a Handle Animal check to “push” your target in order to communicate specific requests.

contextNotes.text: +[[5]] Trait bonus whenever you attempt to “push” a wild animal to perform a trick for you, so long as its attitude is indifferent or friendlier.

faithful feedback (any religion).name: Faithful Feedback (any religion)
faithful feedback (any religion).description:
A shared religious fervor makes your healing magic more effective.

Requirements: Any religion

Benefits: Whenever you cast a spell belonging to the healing subschool on someone of your religion, increase the hit points healed by +1.

contextNotes.text: Whenever you cast a spell belonging to the healing subschool on someone of your religion, increase the hit points healed by +[[1]].

fashionable (sovereign court).name: Fashionable (Sovereign Court)
fashionable (sovereign court).description:
You spent your formative years as a young blade in Oppara and learned the ins and outs of using fashion to improve your relations with others.

Requirements: Sovereign Court

Benefits: So long as you are wearing clothing and jewelry worth more than 80 gp, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Bluff, Diplomacy, and Sense Motive checks. One of these skills (your choice) becomes a class skill for you. 

Usage

Select Bluff or Diplomacy or Sense Motive when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus while  you are wearing clothing and jewelry worth more than 80 gp.
+[[1]] Trait bonus while  you are wearing clothing and jewelry worth more than 80 gp.
+[[1]] Trait bonus while  you are wearing clothing and jewelry worth more than 80 gp.

winged aloofness (syrinx).name: Winged Aloofness (Syrinx)
winged aloofness (syrinx).description:
You learned from your syrinx elders that your race is better than non-winged races and you take care to keep above them physically as well.

Requirements: Syrinx

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Fly checks. This bonus increases to +4 on checks to avoid losing altitude when damaged.

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Trait bonus  to avoid losing altitude when damaged. (This trait always provides a +[[2]] Trait bonus).

inheritor's immunity (iomedae).name: Inheritor's Immunity (Iomedae)
inheritor's immunity (iomedae).description:
Iomedae’s valor fills you with divine energy, allowing you to stave off even the worst plague.

Requirements: Iomedae

Benefits
Once per day, you can reroll a saving throw against a disease or poison. You must take the second result, even if it is worse.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you can reroll a saving throw against a disease or poison. You must take the second result, even if it is worse. ([[@resources.inheritorsImmunity.value]] remaining uses)

hidden faith.name: Hidden Faith
hidden faith.description:
You were raised in a religion forbidden by your government, and draw strength from your hidden faith.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against the divine spells of creatures that worship a god other than your own.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against the divine spells of creatures that worship a god other than your own.

unpredictable.name: Unpredictable
unpredictable.description:
Your actions often seem random and chaotic to others, but there is a method to your madness.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Bluff checks, and Bluff is always a class skill for you.

jenivere crew (serpent's skull).name: Jenivere Crew (Serpent's Skull)
jenivere crew (serpent's skull).description:
This trip to Eleder is just one of many in a long career as a sailor aboard the Jenivere and other cargo vessels. Whether you joined the crew with the hope of one day captaining your own ship, as the means of paying your way to a foreign port with sweat and blood, or as a slave or indentured servant, you now call the ship home.

Benefits: Pick one of the following skills: Acrobatics, Climb, Knowledge (nature), Knowledge (geography), Swim, or Survival. You gain a +1 trait bonus on that skill, and that skill is always treated as a class skill for you.

Characters with this trait have likely been on the Jenivere since it departed Magnimar.

Usage: Select Acrobatics, Climb, Knowledge (nature), Knowledge (geography), Swim, or Survival when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

pustular (goblin).name: Pustular (Goblin)
pustular (goblin).description:
Your face is covered with unpleasant pimples and outright boils that have a tendency to pop at inopportune moments. Although this makes you particularly ugly, you're also used to discomfort.

Requirements: Goblin

Benefits: Whenever you're subjected to an effect that causes the sickened condition, you may make two saving throws to avoid the effect (if a saving throw is allowed), taking the better of the two results as your actual saving throw.

contextNotes.text: Whenever you're subjected to an effect that causes the sickened condition, you may make two saving throws to avoid the effect (if a saving throw is allowed), taking the better of the two results as your actual saving throw.

thirst for knowledge (nethys).name: Thirst for Knowledge (Nethys)
thirst for knowledge (nethys).description:
You have a need to bring old knowledge into the light, no matter what language it’s written in.

Requirements: Nethys

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Linguistics checks, and Linguistics is a class skill for you.

providential passenger (halfling).name: Providential Passenger (Halfling)
providential passenger (halfling).description:
Those who travel for a living understand that your mere presence could make the difference between success and failure once a journey begins, and this gives you an edge when negotiating your fare or other privileges.

Requirements: Halfling

Benefits: Improve the starting attitude that ship captains, caravan leaders, and those with similar sorts of responsibilities have toward you by one step.

contextNotes.text: Improve the starting attitude that ship captains, caravan leaders, and those with similar sorts of responsibilities have toward you by one step.

pathfinder recruit (ruins of azlant).name: Pathfinder Recruit (Ruins of Azlant)
pathfinder recruit (ruins of azlant).description:
Explore, report, and cooperate. This is the credo you’ve lived by since you’ve joined the Pathfinder Society, and after hearing about the expedition to found Talmandor's Bounty you applied, eager to see the remains of Azlant. However, you quickly learned that the Bountiful Venture Company wasn’t interested in involving the Pathfinder Society in the formation of the colony. This struck you as strange, because who else has as keen an understanding of some of the elements of ancient Azlant as the Pathfinder Society? Regardless, you went through the application process, making sure to highlight all of your applicable skills and experience while keeping your involvement in the Society to yourself. You were elated to receive notice that you were selected for the second wave of colonists bound for the broken continent of Azlant.

Benefits: You begin play with a standard @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-wondrous.Item.Ff39cyNs7PYK0TXF]{wayfinder} and you gain a +1 trait bonus on initiative checks.

resourceful (ruins of azlant).name: Resourceful (Ruins of Azlant)
resourceful (ruins of azlant).description:
You’ve always had a knack for building and repairing things. As a kid, you always got into your parents’ tools and tinkered with things around the house. As you got older, you started making things not only to entertain yourself but also to take in a little extra money. People have noticed your skill and have frequently come to you to make something for them or to repair a pesky device or item. Every time you’ve amazed them at how quickly you do your work and how few resources you squander in the process. Likewise, the Bountiful Venture Company noticed your skill at not only creating wonderful wares, but also how effortlessly and efficiently you work in your trade. You always seem to make do with less, often in unexpected ways.

Benefits: Choose one Craft or Profession skill when you gain this trait. You gain a +1 trait bonus on checks with that skill. In addition, the time required for you to create a magic item is decreased. You require only 8 hours for every 1,500 gp in the item’s base price (instead of the normal 8 hours per 1,000 gp). You can create potions and scrolls whose base price is 375 gp in just 2 hours; potions and scrolls with a base price more than 375 gp but less than 1,500 gp take 8 hours to create, just like any other magic item.

Usage: Select one Craft or Profession skill when you take this trait.

Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the chosen skill.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

pain is pleasure (zon-kuthon).name: Pain Is Pleasure (Zon-Kuthon)
pain is pleasure (zon-kuthon).description:
You have discovered a hint of the dark truths that lay hidden within blood and flesh.

Requirements: Zon-Kuthon

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Fortitude and Will saves whenever you have fewer than half your maximum number of hit points.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus whenever you have fewer than half your maximum number of hit points.
+[[1]] Trait bonus whenever you have fewer than half your maximum number of hit points.

child of the streets.name: Child of the Streets
child of the streets.description:
You grew up on the streets of a large city, and as a result you have developed a knack for picking pockets and hiding small objects on your person.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Sleight of Hand checks, and Sleight of Hand is always a class skill for you.

potent concoctions (ghlaunder).name: Potent Concoctions (Ghlaunder)
potent concoctions (ghlaunder).description:
You have dedicated your life to honoring famine and disease.

Requirements: Ghlaunder

Benefits: Choose any two of the following poisons; when you attempt to inflict either of these poisons on an enemy, the DC to resist it increases by 1.

Poisons

Arsenic, Belladonna, Black adder venom, Black lotus extract, Bloodroot, Blue whinnis, Burnt othur fumes, Dark reaver powder, Deathblade, Dragon bile, Drow poison, Giant wasp poison, Greenblood oil, Green prismatic poison, Hemlock, Id moss, Insanity mist, King's sleep, Large scorpion venom, Lich dust, Malyass root paste, Medium spider venom, Nightmare vapor, Nitharit, Oil of taggit, Purple worm poison, Sassone leaf residue, Shadow essence, Small centipede poison, Striped toadstool, Tears of death, Terinav root, Ungol dust, Wolfsbane, Wyvern poison.

coin hoarder (mindspin mountains).name: Coin Hoarder (Mindspin Mountains)
coin hoarder (mindspin mountains).description:
Everything has a price - and it's your job to know it.

Requirements: Mindspin Mountains

Benefits: Your starting cash increases to 500 gp

animal friend (gnome).name: Animal Friend (Gnome)
animal friend (gnome).description:
You've long been a friend to many among Katapesh's animal life, and feel safer when there are animals nearby.

Requirements: Gnome

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Will saving throws as long as an animal (Tiny or larger, must be at least indifferent toward you) is within 30 feet, and Handle Animal is always a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus as long as an animal (Tiny or larger, must be at least indifferent toward you) is within 30 feet.

switched at birth (changeling).name: Switched at Birth (Changeling)
switched at birth (changeling).description:
You were traded for another child in infancy and remained unaware of your origin as a changeling until some recent tragedy revealed the truth. Now the guilt of knowing you live because another child met a horrible fate haunts you and drives you to push forward in that child’s name.

Requirements: Changeling

Benefits: Once per day, you can attempt a Will saving throw in place of a Fortitude saving throw against a spell, effect, or condition.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you can attempt a Will saving throw in place of a Fortitude saving throw against a spell, effect, or condition.

centaur vengeance (iobaria).name: Centaur Vengeance (Iobaria)
centaur vengeance (iobaria).description:
Your nomadic tribe wanders the Ice Steppes of Iobaria. They live in constant fear of the great white wyrm Sjohvornor and feel helpless to stop the dragon’s aggression. Your tribe is filled with wise sages and great warriors, and they resent being cowed by any foe. You vowed that one day you would not have to be afraid of Sjohvornor or any other wyrm.

Requirements: Iobaria

Benefits: You gain a +2 bonus on saving throws against any dragon’s frightful presence.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] bonus  against any dragon’s frightful presence.

warded against nature.name: Warded Against Nature
warded against nature.description: Effects: Animals do not willingly approach within 30 feet of you, unless you or the animal’s master succeeds at a DC 20 Handle Animal, Ride, or wild empathy check. Animal companions, familiars, and mounts granted by your class abilities are immune to this effect.

soaring sprinter.name: Soaring Sprinter
soaring sprinter.description:
As a youth, you often leaped and ran at treacherous heights, either because it was the only place with enough freedom for you to play or because it was not a good idea to play any lower.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Acrobatics checks to keep your balance or jump, and Acrobatics becomes a class skill for you.

Suggested Characters

Chelaxians, gnomes, Korvosans

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to keep your balance or jump.

bullied.name: Bullied
bullied.description:
You were bullied often as a child, and you are now constantly ready to defend yourself with your fists when an enemy comes near.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attacks of opportunity attack rolls made with unarmed strikes.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attacks of opportunity rolls made with unarmed strikes

mummy-cursed (mummy's mask).name: Mummy-Cursed (Mummy's Mask)
mummy-cursed (mummy's mask).description:
One of your ancestors ran afoul of a mummy’s curse while exploring an ancient tomb. This curse was passed down to later generations of your family, but over time, your line has become more resistant to curses. You’ve come to Wati to explore its untouched necropolis, and while you hope you won’t have to face a real undead mummy, at least you have some defense if you do.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against curses and curse effects (including mummy rot and spells with the curse descriptor) and a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against a mummy’s aura of despair.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] trait bonus vs curses and curse effects (including mummy rot and spells with the curse descriptor).
+[[2]] trait bonus vs a mummy’s aura of despair.

student of history.name: Student of History
student of history.description:
One can move toward enlightenment by understanding the mistakes of past generations.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (history) checks, and Knowledge (history) is a class skill for you.

proud progenitor (vampire; moroi).name: Proud Progenitor (Vampire; Moroi)
proud progenitor (vampire; moroi).description:
Your loneliness drove you to build your coven, and you zealously attack any who would harm it.

Requirements: Moroi Vampire Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on damage rolls against creatures you have seen attack or harm any of your vampire spawn.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus  on damage rolls against creatures you have seen attack or harm any of your vampire spawn.

mercenary.name: Mercenary
mercenary.description:
For everything there is a price, and you are a negotiator at heart.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy, Intimidate, and Sense Motive checks made while negotiating payment for a quest or a service that you provide.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to checks made while negotiating payment for any services that you provide.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to checks made while negotiating payment for any services that you provide.+[[2]] Trait bonus  to check made negotiating payment for any services that you provide.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to checks made while negotiating payment for any services that you provide.+[[2]] Trait bonus  to check made negotiating payment for any services that you provide.

bellflower tiller (bellflower network, c.name: Bellflower Tiller (Bellflower Network, Cheliax)
bellflower tiller (bellflower network, c.description:
You are a tiller in the secretive Bellflower Network in Cheliax and carefully move slaves from one hideout to the next.

Requirements: Bellflower Network , Cheliax

Benefits: You gain a +5 trait bonus on all Survival skill checks made to get along in the wild, travel in severe weather, keep from getting lost, or predict the weather.

contextNotes.text:
+[[5]] Trait bonus 
on all  checks made to: get along in the wild; travel in severe weather; keep from getting lost; or predict the weather.

river fighter (sargavan).name: River Fighter (Sargavan)
river fighter (sargavan).description:
You are used to fighting while swimming.

Requirements: Sargavan

Benefits: You may swim downstream or cross-current as a charge if you make your Swim DC by 5 or more.

magic in the blood (changeling).name: Magic in the Blood (Changeling)
magic in the blood (changeling).description:
Requirements: Changeling

Benefits: You possess natural magical ability. Select one of the following spells: @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.n390v9tbwxya1tz4]{bleed}, @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.snj78r5rw96btfjn]{create water}, @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.zymaptg3vmnvfvxl]{dancing lights}, @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.mkgysr5o92kxxn59]{detect good}, @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.mtxqp85izkb20djq]{ghost sound}, @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.5px118dfc2ww7lyz]{putrefy food and drink}. You can cast this spell three times per day as a spell-like ability.

reincarnated.name: Reincarnated
reincarnated.description:
You lived a previous life as someone—or something— else. For you, life and death are a cycle, and you have no fear of death.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against fear and death effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against fear and death effects.

shoanti tattoo (human; shoanti).name: Shoanti Tattoo (Human; Shoanti)
shoanti tattoo (human; shoanti).description:
Upon reaching adulthood, you received a tribal tattoo, marking you with the symbols of your quah and totem spirits (see the inside back cover for suggestions and designs for Shoanti tattoos).

Requirements: Shoanti Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against fear effects. Additionally, you are proficient with @UUID[Compendium.pf1.weapons-and-ammo.Item.b0hY6M0sYwvjnMZH]{Earth Breakers}, @UUID[Compendium.pf1.weapons-and-ammo.Item.mKHMd3cbVPWTwX17]{Klars}, and @UUID[Compendium.pf1.weapons-and-ammo.Item.4Ljn6CWNcYi1OebL]{Shoanti Bolas}.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against fear effects.

tanglebriar guerrilla (kyonin).name: Tanglebriar Guerrilla (Kyonin)
tanglebriar guerrilla (kyonin).description:
Your campaign against Treerazer has endeared you to Kyonin’s rangers, who have taught you their ways.

Requirements: Kyonin

Benefits: When you gain this trait, select one ranger trap that doesn’t deal ability or hit point damage. You can use this trap once per day within a forest. The DC for your trap is equal to 10 + half your character level + your Wisdom modifier (minimum +0), and it lasts for 1 day. If you are not a ranger, you can set only extraordinary traps with this feat; like all extraordinary ranger traps, this decreases the trap DC by 2.

horse lord (human; keleshite).name: Horse Lord (Human; Keleshite)
horse lord (human; keleshite).description:
You grew up racing along the plains of Paresh, hoping to one day compete in the Histaqen.

Requirements: Kelshite Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Handle Animal checks, and a +1 trait bonus on Ride checks. This trait applies only to horses.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus but it applies only to horses.
+[[1]] Trait bonus  but it applies only to horses.

life of toil.name: Life of Toil
life of toil.description:
You have lived a physically taxing life, working long hours for a master or to support a trade. Hard physical labor has toughened your body and mind.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Fortitude saves.

family resemblance.name: Family Resemblance
family resemblance.description:
You have some of the common, recognizable features of your family.

Benefits: You gain a +4 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks made to improve the attitude of a creature that has a friendly or helpful attitude toward another member of your family, and a +2 bonus on Disguise checks to look like a specific member of your family. You also take a –2 penalty on Disguise checks to look like anyone who is not a member of your family.

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Trait bonus to improve the attitude of a creature that has a friendly or helpful attitude toward another member of your family
+[[2]] bonus to look like a specific member of your family
–[[2]] Penalty to look like anyone who is not a member of your family.

inspiring.name: Inspiring
inspiring.description:
As a child, you learned the power of a simple expression of faith in another when you gave a hero the inspiration to fight on in the face of terrible odds and save you from danger.

Benefits: As a standard action, you can speak words of encouragement to grant a +1 morale bonus to an ally within 30 feet who can see and hear you.

The ally can use this bonus on any d20 roll of her choice before the start of your next turn.

You cannot use this trait to grant a bonus to yourself or to an ally who has already benefited from this trait within the last 24 hours.

Suggested Characters

Eagle Knights, Iomedaeans, Mendevian crusaders.

sacred smasher (rovagug).name: Sacred Smasher (Rovagug)
sacred smasher (rovagug).description:
Your love of carnage extends even to the defenseless, inanimate objects around you.

Requirements: Rovagug

Benefits: Whenever you make an attempt to break an object, you receive a +2 trait bonus on your Strength check.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to break an object.

slippery step (undine).name: Slippery Step (Undine)
slippery step (undine).description:
You are used to walking on wet and slippery surfaces.

Requirements: Undine

Benefits: You are always considered to have firm footing when fighting underwater, and you gain a +2 trait bonus on Acrobatics checks and Climb checks on wet surfaces or in wet terrain, including bogs, standing or running water, or a storm-tossed ship.

contextNotes.text: You are always considered to have firm footing when fighting underwater.
+[[2]] Trait bonus on wet surfaces or in wet terrain, including bogs, standing or running water, or a storm-tossed ship.
+[[2]] Trait bonus on wet surfaces or in wet terrain, including bogs, standing or running water, or a storm-tossed ship.

trickster (gnome).name: Trickster (Gnome)
trickster (gnome).description:
You are particularly adept with your racial spell-like abilities, and as child you quickly learned how dancing lights and ghost sound could be used to amuse your friends and fool your elders. This natural talent for illusion continued as you grew older, and before long you were being offered training in more advanced figments and glamers.

Requirements: Gnome

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus to your caster level when determining the duration, range, and area of all spells from the illusion school.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to your caster level when determining the duration, range, and area of all spells from the illusion school.

medic (shadow lodge).name: Medic (Shadow Lodge)
medic (shadow lodge).description:
Requirements: Shadow Lodge

Benefits: Your experience caring for wounded Pathfinder agents has granted you a +2 trait bonus on Heal checks made to treat diseases and poisons. Additionally, your caster level is considered one higher when casting spells of the healing subschool with remove in the spell’s name.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus  to treat diseases and poisons.
+[[1]] to  caster level  when casting spells of the healing subschool with remove in the spell’s name.

observant (grand lodge).name: Observant (Grand Lodge)
observant (grand lodge).description:
Sometimes it helps to pay attention to your surroundings and the people you meet.

Requirements: Grand Lodge

Benefits: Choose either Perception or Sense Motive. You gain a +1 trait bonus on all checks for the chosen skill, and the chosen skill becomes a class skill for you.

Usage: Select Perception or Sense Motive when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

demon-proof mind (sarkoris, worldwound).name: Demon-Proof Mind (Sarkoris, Worldwound)
demon-proof mind (sarkoris, worldwound).description:
You are used to the maddening energy fluctuations emanating from the Worldwound, and have steeled your mind against their influence.

Requirements: Sarkoris or the Worldwound

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against the mind-affecting effects of evil outsiders.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against the mind-affecting effects of evil outsiders.

blessed orphan (folgrit).name: Blessed Orphan (Folgrit)
blessed orphan (folgrit).description:
As an orphan, you have always looked to Folgrit for protection.

Requirements: Folgrit

Benefits: Once per day, you gain a +1 trait bonus on any saving throw. You must use this ability after the roll is made but before the result is revealed.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, +[[1]] Trait bonus. You must use this ability after the roll is made but before the result is revealed.

volatile conduit.name: Volatile Conduit
volatile conduit.description:
You discovered a secret that enhances the energy of some spells.

Benefits: Once per day as a free action, when you cast a spell that deals acid, cold, electricity, or fire damage, you can enhance that spell with volatile energy. When you do, it deals 1d4 points of extra damage of the same energy type.

emberkin imposter (qadira).name: Emberkin Imposter (Qadira)
emberkin imposter (qadira).description:
You have found it is easier to be mistaken for someone with celestial blood than to reveal your true nature.

Requirements: Qadira

Benefits: You gain a +5 trait bonus on Disguise checks when pretending to be an aasimar, and a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks against those you have convinced of your supposed celestial nature.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against those you have convinced of your supposed celestial nature.
+[[5]] Trait bonus when pretending to be an aasimar.

westcrown firebrand (council of thieves).name: Westcrown Firebrand (Council of Thieves)
westcrown firebrand (council of thieves).description:
There's something very wrong with the world. Spouses were not meant to huddle at their windows hoping and fearing day after day that their loved ones returned from work safely. Parents were not meant to hush their children when questioned about what happened to their neighbors. Citizens were not meant to avert their eyes and hurry by as guardsmen beat old friends in the street. The people of Westcrown have suffered long enough! It's time for a change. But how? You've heard rumors of bands of free-thinking individuals meeting after the citywide curfew. Perhaps they might share your ideals? You are quick to react to opportunity, both physically and mentally, and know that it is with sudden and swift action that many conflicts are best resolved.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Initiative checks, and if you act in a surprise round, you gain a +1 trait bonus on all attack rolls.

contextNotes.text: If you act in a surprise round, you gain a +[[1]] Trait bonus on all attack rolls.

the optimist (tyrant's grasp).name: The Optimist (Tyrant's Grasp)
the optimist (tyrant's grasp).description:
People don’t need to take your word that things are getting better; they only need to look around and see the proof before their eyes. The town’s recovery from the orc occupation more than a decade ago is all but complete, and the monster in the Sarenite temple outside of town has finally been bested. Your irrepressible sense of optimism seems to fly in the face of the hard life that most residents of Roslar’s Coffer face, but you’re convinced that your positive attitude lifts up your community.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against mind-affecting effects. Further, your optimism can be infectious. As a swift action a number of times per day equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1), you can grant this bonus to an ally within 10 feet. This bonus lasts for 1 minute.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against mind-affecting effects.  As a swift action, [[max(1, @abilities.cha.mod)]] times per day, you can grant this bonus to an ally within 10 feet. This bonus lasts for 1 minute. ([[@resources.theOptimistTyrantsGrasp.value]] remaining uses)

river trader (human; mwangi).name: River Trader (Human; Mwangi)
river trader (human; mwangi).description:
You have learned to ply the rivers and the markets with equal ease.

Requirements: Mwangi Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 bonus on Appraise and Swim checks, and one of these skills (your choice) is always a class skill for you.

Usage: Select Appraise or Swim when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

prayer breaker (tiefling; faultspawn).name: Prayer Breaker (Tiefling; Faultspawn)
prayer breaker (tiefling; faultspawn).description:
You have an instinctive flair for causing such pain to the faithful that they temporarily lose their connection with their god.

Requirements: Faultspawn Tiefling Heritage

Benefits: If a divine spellcaster makes a concentration check to avoid losing a spell because of an injury you caused, increase the DC of that check by +2.

contextNotes.text: If a divine spellcaster makes a concentration check to avoid losing a spell because of an injury you caused, increase the DC of that check by +2.

demonic persuasion (demon lords).name: Demonic Persuasion (Demon Lords)
demonic persuasion (demon lords).description:
Your lord grants you favor when interacting with demonic creatures.

Requirements: Any Demon Lord

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy and Intimidate checks when dealing with creatures of the demon subtype.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus when dealing with creatures of the demon subtype.
+[[2]] Trait bonus when dealing with creatures of the demon subtype.

beacon of faith.name: Beacon of Faith
beacon of faith.description:
You wield the might of your faith with power and clarity.

Benefits: Once per day as a free action, you may treat your caster level as if it were 2 levels higher when using one of the granted powers of your domain or inquisition, or when casting one of your domain spells.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you may treat your caster level as if it were 2 levels higher when using one of the granted powers of your domain or inquisition, or when casting one of your domain spells.

armored rider.name: Armored Rider
armored rider.description:
You are accustomed to riding while wearing armor, and your armor doesn’t hinder your riding skills.

Prerequisite(s): Mounted Combat, Ride 3 ranks

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Ride checks, and Ride is a class skill for you. You don’t take an armor check penalty on Ride checks while wearing light or medium armor.

contextNotes.text: Don’t take this ACP [[@attributes.acp.gear]] while wearing light or medium armor

trap savvy (darklands).name: Trap Savvy (Darklands)
trap savvy (darklands).description:
Growing up in a dangerous area, you were constantly surrounded by traps that had been set up to defend your lair from intruders. Sometimes these dangers were so densely packed that you couldn’t go more than a couple feet down a tunnel without encountering a new way to die.

Requirements: Darklands

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Craft (traps) checks, and gain a +1 bonus to AC against attacks by traps or on saving throws against effects created by traps.

Usage: Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the Craft (traps) skill.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect Craft (traps).

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] bonus to AC against attacks by traps
+[[1]] bonus against effects created by traps.

toilcrafter (droskar).name: Toilcrafter (Droskar)
toilcrafter (droskar).description:
You can craft passable if inferior magical weapons, armors, and shields through diligence and toil.

Requirements: Droskar

Benefits: Choose one Craft or Profession skill in which you have at least 1 rank. Ranks in your chosen skill count as your caster level for the purpose of qualifying for the Craft Magic Arms and Armor feat. You may craft +1 armor, weapons, or shields (with no special qualities), substituting your rank in the chosen skill for your caster level. You must use the chosen skill for the check to create the item. Crafting in this fashion takes twice as long as normal. These items cannot be upgraded with new abilities.

Usage: Select one Craft or Profession skill in which you have at least 1 rank when you take this trait.
