intrigued by thassilon (return of the ru.name: Intrigued by Thassilon (Return of the Runelords)
intrigued by thassilon (return of the ru.description:
The rediscovery of Thassilon’s legacy several years ago was an eye-opener for many, including you. After living so long in the shadow of these enormous ruins, you were intrigued to find out that they were much older than anyone could have guessed and that the runelords who built them may still exist. You’ve made a promise to yourself to uncover all that you can about Thassilon, its runelords, and the mysterious history of that ancient civilization.

Benefits: You gain Thassilonian as a bonus language, and at 1st level and every 2 levels thereafter, you gain a bonus skill rank that can only be applied to Knowledge (arcana), Knowledge (history), Linguistics, or Use Magic Device.

Once per adventure, you may either take 20 on a Use Magic Device check or activate a charged magic item without expending any charges.

eye of the father (torag).name: Eye of the Father (Torag)
eye of the father (torag).description:
You have an innate sense of what is right and wrong in the works you craft, based on your years studying with the church fathers at their forges.

Requirements: Torag

Benefits: Choose one Craft skill; you gain a +1 trait bonus on Craft checks of the selected type.

Usage: Select one Craft skill when you take this trait.

Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the chosen skill.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

malevolent temptations.name: Malevolent Temptations
malevolent temptations.description:
You have resisted or escaped the temptations of a disguised hag, and you learned much from your encounter.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks and a +2 trait bonus on saving throws to disbelieve illusions.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to disbelieve illusions.

time lost (return of the runelords).name: Time Lost (Return of the Runelords)
time lost (return of the runelords).description:
You’ve always felt separated from the era into which you were born. Perhaps you feel that you were born too late, and that you and your interests are better suited to a previous age. Maybe you have the conviction you were born too soon, and that your true destiny lies in some distant future. You might be suffering from missing time—whether a few hours, a few weeks, or even several months—for which you have no memories. You might instead receive brief visions of friends or enemies much older or younger than their actual age. You’ve long since grown used to this strange condition, but have not yet determined why you suffer from it. Your GM will be provided with additional information about this trait’s strange nature in the third adventure of Return of the Runelords.

Benefits: You have a knack for knowing when to act a few instants in advance and gain a +2 trait bonus on Initiative checks.

Once per adventure as an immediate action, you may use your strange link to the flow of time to force the GM to reroll a single d20 roll. You must declare the use of this ability immediately after the GM rolls the d20, but you can choose to do so after learning the results of the roll.

briar bandit (forest).name: Briar Bandit (Forest)
briar bandit (forest).description:
The time you’ve spent crawling through thorny brambles and other thick underbrush during raids and ambushes taught you how to move without disturbing the dense flora around you.

Requirements: Forest

Benefits: When in overgrown areas, you gain a +2 trait bonus on Stealth checks and a +1 trait bonus on damage rolls with sneak attacks.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on damage rolls with sneak attacks when in overgrown areas.
+[[2]] Trait bonus when in overgrown areas.

chance savior (carrion crown).name: Chance Savior (Carrion Crown)
chance savior (carrion crown).description:
Fate smiled on you and Professor Lorrimor one day in the not so distant past. Through a matter of pure chance, you were in a position to save the late scholar's life and did so. His gratitude was effusive, and he promised that he would never forget you. You are unsure of the nature of the summons in his will, but believe he may have listed you as a possible heir in thanks for saving him from an untimely demise. Your ability to think quickly on your feet has stayed with you, and you quite often feel that you're in the right place at the right time.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Initiative checks.

reckless.name: Reckless
reckless.description:
You have a tendency for rash behavior, often disregarding your own safety as you move across the battlefield.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Acrobatics checks, and Acrobatics is always a class skill for you.

besmara's blessing (skull and shackles).name: Besmara's Blessing (Skull and Shackles)
besmara's blessing (skull and shackles).description:
You were born aboard a ship at sea or down by the docks in a port city on an auspicious day. Old salts and sea dogs nod knowingly and say that Besmara the Pirate Queen, goddess of piracy, sea monsters, and strife, has marked you for a greater destiny. You don't know anything about that, but you've always felt more at home on the sea than on land, and your keen eyes can easily pick out a sail on the distant horizon. You came to a tavern in Port Peril called the Formidably Maid in search of your fate. You're not sure how many drinks you had, but they quickly went to your head and you passed out on the table before you found your destiny - unless it lay in the bottom of a bottle. Then again, maybe you found your destiny after all...

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Perception and Profession (sailor) checks. In addition, once per week you can reroll a Profession (sailor) check and take the higher result (you must announce that you are using this ability before the results of the check are known).

Usage: Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the Profession (sailor) skill.

Update the target of the untargeted change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect Profession (sailor).

intense artist (shelyn).name: Intense Artist (Shelyn)
intense artist (shelyn).description:
Your devotion to Shelyn has caused you to delve more deeply into your art.

Requirements: Shelyn

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on two Perform skills of your choice and Perform is always a class skill for you.

Usage: Select any two Perform skills when you take this trait.

Update the Skills tab of the actor to add the two chosen performance types.

Update the target of the two change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect each of your two your choices.

draconic vigil (lastwall).name: Draconic Vigil (Lastwall)
draconic vigil (lastwall).description:
As a defender of Lastwall, you have seen the damage the dragon Zedoran has wrought upon the land.

Requirements: Lastwall

Benefits: You gain a +1 bonus on initiative rolls when entering combat against creatures of the dragon type or humanoids of the orc subtype.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] bonus on initiative rolls when entering combat against creatures of the dragon type or humanoids of the orc subtype.

dependent.name: Dependent
dependent.description:
You are dependent upon the acceptance of others.

Effects: Whenever you fail a Diplomacy check, you become shaken for 1 hour.

contextNotes.text: Whenever you fail a check, you become shaken for 1 hour.

elemental ties (osirion).name: Elemental Ties (Osirion)
elemental ties (osirion).description:
Interactions with elementals are commonplace for you.

Requirements: Osirion

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks when dealing with creatures of the elemental type, and you begin play knowing one of the following languages as an additional bonus language: Aquan, Auran, Ignan, or Terran.

Usage

Select one (1) of Aquan, Auran, Ignan, or Terran when you take this trait.

Update the Granted Proficiencies>Languages section of the details tab of this trait to reflect this choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus  when dealing with creatures of the elemental type

bellis log roller (andoran; bellis).name: Bellis Log Roller (Andoran; Bellis)
bellis log roller (andoran; bellis).description:
The time you spent leaping between slippery logs as they whirled down the Sellen River taught you how to keep your footing.

Requirements: Bellis [in Andoran]

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Acrobatics checks and a +1 trait bonus to your CMB when attempting to resist trip attacks.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to your CMB when attempting to resist trip attacks.

worldly.name: Worldly
worldly.description:
You have acquired an unusual breadth of life experience—more than others of your age, race, or culture.

Benefits: Once per day when attempting a skill check for a skill you’re untrained in, you can roll twice and take the better result.

contextNotes.text: Once per day when making a check untrained, you can roll twice and take the better result.

tireless (the exchange).name: Tireless (The Exchange)
tireless (the exchange).description:
You are accustomed to working long hours and weathering difficult conditions just to get the job done.

Requirements: The Exchange

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Constitution checks made to resist nonlethal damage from swimming, forced marches, starvation, thirst, and hot and cold environments. In addition, you gain 1 hit point.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to resist nonlethal damage from swimming, forced marches, starvation, thirst, and hot and cold environments.

beast rider (half-orc, orc).name: Beast Rider (Half-Orc, Orc)
beast rider (half-orc, orc).description:
You have a knack for breaking animals to your will, though you know little of civilized training methods.

Requirements: Half-Orc, Orc

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Ride checks but, should you ever be forcibly dismounted in combat, your mount attacks you to the best of its ability.

contextNotes.text: If you are ever forcibly dismounted in combat, your mount attacks you to the best of its ability.

witness to nature's cruelty (human; kell.name: Witness to Nature's Cruelty (Human; Kellid)
witness to nature's cruelty (human; kell.description:
You saw firsthand the terrible toll of starvation and the elements, and refuse to allow even your worst enemy to suffer that way.

Requirements: Kellid Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Heal and Survival checks made on behalf of others. One of these skills (your choice) is always a class skill for you.

Usage

Select Heal or Survival when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on checks made on behalf of others.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on checks made on behalf of others.

resurrected (mummy's mask).name: Resurrected (Mummy's Mask)
resurrected (mummy's mask).description:
At some time in the recent past you died, but you were brought back to life—whether because of magic, a blessing of the gods, a destiny you have to fulf ill, or perhaps it just wasn’t your time to die yet. Whatever the nature of your resurrection, your experience gave you a fascination with death, and you hope to find some insight into the nature of mortality by exploring the tombs of Wati’s famous necropolis.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against death effects. In addition, you do not die until your hit points drop to a negative amount equal to or lower than your Constitution score + 4.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus vs death effects.
You do not die until your hit points drop to a negative amount equal to or lower than your Constitution score + 4. (hp=[[@attributes.hp.value]] , con+4=[[@abilities.con.total +4]])

instigator of rebellion (minkai).name: Instigator of Rebellion (Minkai)
instigator of rebellion (minkai).description:
Your rage over the political repositioning within Minkai has led you to incite rage within your compatriots. You have become adept at organizing rallies in opposition to the government.

Requirements: Minkai

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy and Intimidate checks.

cynical ear.name: Cynical Ear
cynical ear.description:
Neither honeyed tongues or righteous wrath move you much.

Benefits: Add 2 when determining your Hit Dice for Intimidate checks, and increase the DC for all Diplomacy checks against you by 2.

muscle of the society (pathfinder societ.name: Muscle of the Society (Pathfinder Society)
muscle of the society (pathfinder societ.description:
Your intense training has taught you how to infiltrate otherwise inaccessible ruins and extract their treasures without too much exertion.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Strength checks made to break doors and lift portcullises, and you treat your Strength score as 2 higher for the purpose of determining your carrying capacity.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to break doors and lift portcullises.

pain artist (tiefling; shackleborn).name: Pain Artist (Tiefling; Shackleborn)
pain artist (tiefling; shackleborn).description:
Some trace of your kyton heritage makes people realize you are capable of anything, especially when they are bound and at your mercy.

Requirements: Shackleborn Tiefling Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Intimidate checks made against helpless creatures.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against helpless creatures.

blood stalker (tiefling; hellspawn).name: Blood Stalker (Tiefling; Hellspawn)
blood stalker (tiefling; hellspawn).description:
Some infernal instinct makes it much easier for you to locate creatures whose blood you have drawn.

Requirements: Hellspawn Tielfling Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +4 trait bonus on Survival checks made to track or follow a trail so long as you have dealt damage to the creature you are hunting within the last week.

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Trait bonus to track, so long as you have dealt damage to the creature you are tracking within the last week.

trouper (sczarni faction).name: Trouper (Sczarni Faction)
trouper (sczarni faction).description:
You cling to the Varisian traveling performer traditions and can recognize the magic hidden in song and dance.

Requirements: Sczarni Faction

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on checks using a Perform skill of your choice and a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against abilities that rely upon the Perform skill to function, such as the bardic performance class ability.

Usage

Select one Perform skill when you take this trait.

Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the chosen skill.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against abilities that rely upon the Perform skill to function, such as the bardic performance class ability.

cosmopolitan (absalom).name: Cosmopolitan (Absalom)
cosmopolitan (absalom).description:
Your exposure to the huge variety of cultures across the Inner Sea has given you an ear for languages.

Requirements: Absalom

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Linguistics checks, and Linguistics is always a class skill for you.

charmed innocent (any city).name: Charmed Innocent (Any City)
charmed innocent (any city).description:
Your apparent good nature or playful charm makes nonevil creatures want to like you.

Requirements: Any City

Benefits: Once per day, before you roll any Charisma check or Charisma-based skill check against a nonevil creature, you can roll twice and take the better result.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, before you roll a check against a nonevil creature, you can roll twice and take the better result. ([[@resources.charmedInnocentAnyCity.value]] remaining uses)
Once per day, before you roll a check against a nonevil creature, you can roll twice and take the better result. ([[@resources.charmedInnocentAnyCity.value]] remaining uses)

subjective equilibrium.name: Subjective Equilibrium
subjective equilibrium.description:
You understand that gravity is far from constant when traversing the Great Beyond.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Wisdom checks to move on a plane with subjective directional gravity, and you gain a +10-foot enhancement bonus to your speed when “falling” on such a plane.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to move on a plane with subjective directional gravity.

noble born - lebeda (kingmaker).name: Noble Born - Lebeda (Kingmaker)
noble born - lebeda (kingmaker).description:
You claim a tangential but legitimate connection to one of Brevoy’s noble families. If you aren’t human, you were likely adopted by one of Brevoy’s nobles or were instead a favored servant or even a childhood friend of a noble scion. Whatever the cause, you’ve had a comfortable life, but one far from the dignity and decadence your distant cousins know. Although you are associated with an esteemed name, your immediate family is hardly well to do, and you’ve found your name to be more of a burden to you than a boon in many social situations. You’ve recently decided to test yourself, to see if you can face the world without the aegis of a name you have little real claim or care for. An expedition into the storied Stolen Lands seems like just the test to see if you really are worth the title “noble.”

Benefits: Choose one of the following noble families and associated benefits:

Lebeda

Your family’s history of trading along the shores of Lake Reykal pervades your blood. As a deft merchant of the region, you gain a bonus language: Dwarven, Elven, Hallit, Gnome, Giant, Halfling, Skald, or Sylvan. Your family motto is “Success through Grace.”

Usage: Select one (1) of Dwarven, Elven, Hallit, Gnome, Giant, Halfling, Skald, or Sylvan when you take this trait.

Update the Granted Proficiencies>Languages section of the details tab of this trait to reflect this choice.

Special Note

This is one of six versions of this trait. Other versions can be found here:

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.79pUkpcI6MYSTShf]{Noble Born - Garress (Kingmaker)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.stEGVAsBCoaY5Ifk]{Noble Born - Lodovka (Kingmaker)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.c8eiTJJmkH6EyxnY]{Noble Born - Medvyed (Kingmaker)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.BMzDJlUF3aWiDEJF]{Noble Born - Orlovsky (Kingmaker)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.9RUX6RB5UQYnNIxR]{Noble Born - Surtova (Kingmaker)}

child of mumia (osirion).name: Child of Mumia (Osirion)
child of mumia (osirion).description:
During your childhood, you knew someone who was addicted to @UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-items.Item.moOwdDOVvdf5XkE5]{mumia}, a drug made from the flesh of the dead, whether that was a drug-addled parent, an unsavory employer, or a libertine childhood friend.

Requirements: Osirion

Benefits: Although you did not indulge in the ghoulish narcotic yourself, your exposure to it grants you a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against curses and diseases, including magical diseases. (For more information on mumia, see page 21 of Pathfinder Campaign Setting: Lost Kingdoms.)

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against curses and diseases, including magical diseases.

walking ward (qadira).name: Walking Ward (Qadira)
walking ward (qadira).description:
Your training included constant exposure to elemental forces.

Requirements: Qadira

Benefits: Choose one of the following energy types: acid, cold, electricity, or fire; you gain a +1 bonus on saving throws against spells that deal damage of this type or have this subtype.

Usage: Select one of the following energy types: acid, cold, electricity, or fire when you take this trait.

Update the text of the context note on the changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] bonus against spells that deal <chosen energy type> damage or have this subtype.

overwhelming beauty.name: Overwhelming Beauty
overwhelming beauty.description:
Your wiles are particularly potent against people who share a race with you, as your beauty is often held up as a symbol of perfection.

Benefits: The DC of your mind-affecting effects increases by 1 against creatures that have at least one of the same subtypes as you.

contextNotes.text: The DC of your mind-affecting effects increases by [[1]] against creatures who share at least one subtype as you.

elven serenity (half-elf, elf-raised).name: Elven Serenity (Half-Elf, Elf-Raised)
elven serenity (half-elf, elf-raised).description:
You embody the peace of the elven way of life, and apply this philosophy in your interactions with others.

Requirements: Half-Elf, Elf-Raised

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy and Handle Animal checks to calm other creatures. You need not speak the same language to attempt a Diplomacy check in this manner.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to calm other creatures. You need not speak the same language to attempt a Diplomacy check in this manner.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to calm other creatures.

official ties.name: Official Ties
official ties.description:
Benefits: You maintain a friendship with one or more figures of authority. You have minor sway with one of these figures if your game uses individual influence (Ultimate Intrigue 102).

You gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks to influence people in positions of authority aware of your contact and who have an attitude of no worse than indifferent toward the government the contact represents.

You also take a -2 penalty on Diplomacy checks to influence criminals and agents of opposing governments, if those characters are aware of your association with authorities.

If you lose your contact, you lose the benefits of this trait for 1 month, after which you can make a new contact.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] trait bonus to influence people in positions of authority aware of your contact and who have an attitude of no worse than indifferent toward the government the contact represents.
-[[2]] Penalty to influence criminals and agents of opposing governments, if those characters are aware of your association with authorities.

tribal (half-orc, orc).name: Tribal (Half-Orc, Orc)
tribal (half-orc, orc).description:
Requirements: Half-Orc, Orc

Benefits: You are especially devoted to your tribe, and within 60 feet of a tribal standard, you gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls and Will saves against fear effects, as if you were the recipient of a bless spell.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attack rolls within 60 feet of a tribal standard.
+[[1]] Trait bonus against fear effects within 60 feet of a tribal standard.

triaxian dragonslayer (triaxian).name: Triaxian Dragonslayer (Triaxian)
triaxian dragonslayer (triaxian).description:
Requirements: Triaxian

Benefits: You receive a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against the spells and spell-like abilities of dragons and a +1 trait bonus on caster level checks to overcome dragons’ spell resistance.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against the spells and spell-like abilities of dragons
+[[1]] Trait bonus on caster level checks to overcome dragons’ spell resistance.

natural magic (yuelral).name: Natural Magic (Yuelral)
natural magic (yuelral).description:
Yuelral’s teachings revealed to you that all magic can ultimately be traced back to nature.

Requirements: Yuelral

Benefits: Choose two 0-level druid spells.

You treat each spell as being on your class spell list and as a spell known for all spellcasting classes you have.

hidden hand.name: Hidden Hand
hidden hand.description:
Your concealed weapons strike fast and true.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Sleight of Hand checks made to conceal light weapons and a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls when making an attack with a light weapon during a surprise round.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus made to conceal light weapons.
+[[1]] Trait bonus on attack rolls with a light weapon during a surprise round.

occult bargain.name: Occult Bargain
occult bargain.description:
You draw magical power from a source, such as a mysterious eidolon, shame-filled phantom, or First World patron, who insists that its identity remains secret.

Effects: You take a -1 penalty on concentration checks, and you must invoke the entity’s name by word or text each day or be unable to regain spell slots that day. You must be able to cast 0-level and 1st-level spells as a class feature to select this drawback.

guerilla (dtang ma).name: Guerilla (Dtang Ma)
guerilla (dtang ma).description:
You have spent much time in the rainforests of Dtang Ma, and know its secrets.

Requirements: Dtang Ma

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (nature) checks as well as Survival checks made in the rainforest. One of these skills becomes a class skill for you.

Usage

Select Knowledge (nature) or Survival when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus in the rainforest.

secret revolutionary (cheliax).name: Secret Revolutionary (Cheliax)
secret revolutionary (cheliax).description:
You seek to return Cheliax to its heyday before the rise of the House of the Thrune. You have trained yourself to resist any questioning or torture should you ever be caught.

Requirements: Cheliax

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Will saves against mind-affecting effects, and on Fortitude saves against drugs or poisons.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against mind-affecting effects.
+[[1]] Trait bonus against drugs or poisons.

naga emissary (jalmeray; naga).name: Naga Emissary (Jalmeray; Naga)
naga emissary (jalmeray; naga).description:
You were groomed from childhood to act as an emissary to Nagajor’s allies in Jalmeray, and you understand much about the Jalmeri people as a result.

Requirements: Naga native to Jalmeray

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks, and Diplomacy is a class skill for you. In addition, you gain Vudrani as a bonus language.

water lurker (wayang).name: Water Lurker (Wayang)
water lurker (wayang).description:
You grew up in a secretive community by the water, perhaps among the rivers of Shenmen or the islands of Minata.

Requirements: Wayang

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Swim checks and a +2 bonus on Stealth checks on watercraft and when at least half-submerged in water.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] bonus while on watercraft or when at least half-submerged in water.

open-minded explorer (averaka).name: Open-Minded Explorer (Averaka)
open-minded explorer (averaka).description:
You grew up constantly interacting with people who speak unfamiliar languages and have diverse customs; your manners may not be polished, but your acceptance of others is genuine.

Requirements: Averaka

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks. This bonus increases to +2 when you’re speaking the racial or ethnic language of the target creature.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus when you’re speaking the racial or ethnic language of the target creature. (this trait always provides a +1 trait bonus)

weathered (fetchling).name: Weathered (Fetchling)
weathered (fetchling).description:
Requirements: Fetchling

Benefits: Because of your natural resistances, you gain a +2 trait bonus on saves to resist cold or electricity effects and hazards.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to resist cold or electricity effects and hazards.

guardian of the forge (torag).name: Guardian of the Forge (Torag)
guardian of the forge (torag).description:
Torag's sacred duties are to protect the faithful, take lessons from the great craftsmen and strategists of the past, and prepare against dark times.

Requirements: Torag

Benefits: You receive a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (engineering) and Knowledge (history) checks. One of these skills (your choice) is a class skill for you.

Usage: Select Knowledge (engineering) or Knowledge (history) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

sentimental.name: Sentimental
sentimental.description:
You are sentimental, and your thoughts often stray to the past at inappropriate times.

Effects: You take a –2 penalty on Perception checks to avoid being surprised and on Reflex saving throws to avoid traps or hazards.

contextNotes.text: -[[2]] penalty to avoid being surprised.
-[[2]] penalty to avoid traps and hazards.

deep wounding (achaekek).name: Deep Wounding (Achaekek)
deep wounding (achaekek).description:
You have learned the bloody secret of striking true and deep.

Requirements: Achaekek

Benefits: When you deal bleed damage with a melee weapon, a successful DC 20 Heal check is required to stop the bleed damage. Magical healing stops the damage as normal.

contextNotes.text: When you deal bleed damage with a melee weapon, a successful DC 20 Heal check is required to stop the bleed damage. Magical healing stops the damage as normal.

possessed.name: Possessed
possessed.description:
You were, or are, possessed in some way by another entity that grants you occasional access to its knowledge.

Benefits: Once per day, you can attempt a Knowledge check of your choice even if you are not trained in that skill and it is not usually possible to use that skill untrained. If you can normally use that skill untrained, you gain a +2 trait bonus on the check.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you may roll this check even if you are not trained. You gain a +[[2]] trait bonus if you are trained.
Once per day, you may roll this check even if you are not trained. You gain a +[[2]] trait bonus if you are trained.
Once per day, you may roll this check even if you are not trained. You gain a +[[2]] trait bonus if you are trained.
Once per day, you may roll this check even if you are not trained. You gain a +[[2]] trait bonus if you are trained.
Once per day, you may roll this check even if you are not trained. You gain a +[[2]] trait bonus if you are trained.
Once per day, you may roll this check even if you are not trained. You gain a +[[2]] trait bonus if you are trained.
Once per day, you may roll this check even if you are not trained. You gain a +[[2]] trait bonus if you are trained.
Once per day, you may roll this check even if you are not trained. You gain a +[[2]] trait bonus if you are trained.
Once per day, you may roll this check even if you are not trained. You gain a +[[2]] trait bonus if you are trained.
Once per day, you may roll this check even if you are not trained. You gain a +[[2]] trait bonus if you are trained.

tireless avenger.name: Tireless Avenger
tireless avenger.description:
You restlessly pursue your enemies.

Benefits: When you’re in pursuit of one of your favored enemies, the DC for the Constitution check to avoid taking lethal damage during a forced march is 10 + 1 per extra hour, instead of 10 + 2 per extra hour.

contextNotes.text: When you’re in pursuit of one of your favored enemies, the DC for the Constitution check to avoid taking lethal damage during a forced march is 10 + 1 per extra hour, instead of 10 + 2 per extra hour.

monster scholar.name: Monster Scholar
monster scholar.description:
Your study of monsters keeps you well informed.

Benefits: Choose either Knowledge (arcana), Knowledge (dungeoneering), or Knowledge (nature). Whenever you succeed at a Knowledge check to identify a creature and its special powers—or vulnerabilities—with the chosen skill, you gain one additional piece of useful information (as if your skill check result had been 5 higher).

Usage: Choose one of the three permitted Knowledge skills when taking this trait. Update the Target of the Context Note on the Changes tab to the Specific skill you chose.

contextNotes.text: Whenever you succeed at a check to identify a creature and its special powers, or vulnerabilities, you gain one additional piece of useful information (as if your skill check result had been 5 higher).

thunderborn (sylph).name: Thunderborn (Sylph)
thunderborn (sylph).description:
The rumble in your voice and the spark in your eyes hint at a lineage of lightning and thunder, possibly tracing back to lightning elementals from the Plane of Air, or else to a magic-touched birth during a powerful thunderstorm.

Requirements: Sylph

Benefits: Once per day when you cast a spell that deals electricity damage, you can choose to make half of the damage electricity damage and the other half sonic damage.

contextNotes.text: Once per day when you cast a spell that deals electricity damage, you can choose to make half of the damage electricity damage and the other half sonic damage.

draconic destiny.name: Draconic Destiny
draconic destiny.description:
source: Legacy of Dragons pg. 3
Whether you are aware of it or not, the moment of your birth was but one part of a far-reaching machination in a dragon's grand design, and the influence of that mysterious plan makes you a little harder to kill.

Benefits: When dying, you gain a +2 trait bonus on Constitution checks to become stable. In addition, you die from hit point loss only once your hit points equal or exceed a negative amount equal to your Constitution score + 2.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on checks to become stable when dying. In addition, you die only once your hit points equal or exceed a negative amount equal to your Constitution score + 2. (hp=[[@attributes.hp.value]] , con+2=[[@abilities.con.total +2]])

runaway slave (osirion, thuvia, darkland.name: Runaway Slave (Osirion, Thuvia, Darklands)
runaway slave (osirion, thuvia, darkland.description:
Though the ghouls of Nemret Noktoria and the drow of Zirnakaynin seldom darken the surface of Golarion, both have pacts with influential slavers and warlords who periodically provide the demon worshipers with kidnapped slaves. You were once one of these unfortunate victims and were destined to become a sacrifice to a dreadful demon lord, but you managed to slip your bonds and escape back to the surface. The horrors you witnessed were beyond fathoming, and though visions of imprisoned loved ones and friends plague your dreams, you are stronger for the experience.

Requirements: Osirion, Thuvia, or any region with ties to the Darklands

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saves against fear effects and gain low-light vision 10 feet. If you already have low-light vision, its range extends by 10 feet.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against fear effects.

wild domesticator (half-elf).name: Wild Domesticator (Half-Elf)
wild domesticator (half-elf).description:
Requirements: Half-Elf

Benefits: Handle Animal is always a class skill for you. You gain a +2 trait bonus on Handle Animal checks to train an animal, and can teach a trained animal one additional trick beyond its normal maximum.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to train an animal. You can teach a trained animal one additional trick beyond its normal maximum.

fiend blood (any).name: Fiend Blood (Any)
fiend blood (any).description:
The blood of fiends taints your line, manifesting physically, though it may be barely noticeable.

Requirements: Any race

Benefits: Choose one of the following skills: Bluff, Intimidate, or Knowledge (planes). You gain a +1 trait bonus on checks with that skill, and it is always a class skill for you. 

Usage: Select Bluff, Intimidate, or Knowledge (planes) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

alchemical fence (katapesh).name: Alchemical Fence (Katapesh)
alchemical fence (katapesh).description:
Growing up in the vast markets of Katapesh, you developed connections to a great many alchemists and poisoners all over the world. You know many of the right people in any given locale as well as how to get in touch with useful contacts.

Requirements: Katapesh

Benefits: When determining whether a given potion is available in a settlement, there is a 90% chance that it is available if it is within the settlement’s base value for available magic items rather than a 75% chance. Additionally, when you use Diplomacy to gather information about a topic related to alchemy or poisons (including making poisons), you gain a +3 trait bonus on the check.

contextNotes.text: +[[3]] Trait bonus  to gather information about a topic related to alchemy or poisons.

untrained scholar.name: Untrained Scholar
untrained scholar.description:
While scholarship is not your primary focus, you have spent enough time in classrooms and libraries to learn a few things.

Benefits: Once per day, you can attempt a Knowledge (geography), Knowledge (history) or Knowledge (local) check untrained with a +2 trait bonus.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you can attempt a  check untrained with a +[[2]] Trait bonus.
Once per day, you can attempt a  check untrained with a +[[2]] Trait bonus.
Once per day, you can attempt a  check untrained with a +[[2]] Trait bonus.

tribal guide (kobold).name: Tribal Guide (Kobold)
tribal guide (kobold).description:
You were trained to guide the movements of larger creatures, perhaps travelers from Ilizmagorti or transports under Absalom.

Requirements: Kobold

Benefits: You provide food and water or a bonus against severe weather for two additional creatures whenever you succeed at the appropriate Survival check, and Survival is a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: You provide food and water or a bonus against severe weather for two additional creatures whenever you succeed at the appropriate check

stargazer (human; varisian).name: Stargazer (Human; Varisian)
stargazer (human; varisian).description:
You spent many years wandering the roads of Varisia and beyond, and during clear nights, you took to tracking the celestial bodies that adorn Golarion's skies.

Requirements: Varisian Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (geography) and Survival checks.

destined for greatness (absalom).name: Destined for Greatness (Absalom)
destined for greatness (absalom).description:
Ever since you were a child growing up in one of Absalom’s vast city quarters, you’ve been obsessed with the Starstone Cathedral and the prospect of acquiring true divinity by passing the Test of the Starstone. Though it remains to be seen whether you will ever achieve your long-standing dream, your fascination with the would-be deities who enter the Starstone Cathedral and never come back has granted you no small measure of savvy when it comes to preparing to explore dungeons.

Requirements: Absalom

Benefits: You start with a kit (such as those found on page 7 or in Pathfinder RPG Ultimate Equipment) worth no more than 300 gp, and the expendable contents of the kit are automatically restored to their original capacity at no cost to you whenever you enter a settlement with a population of at least 2,500.

Suggestions

Consider playing a character who worships, reveres, or perhaps even envies one of the Ascended— Cayden Cailean, Iomedae, or Norgorber.

shackles seafarer (the shackles).name: Shackles Seafarer (The Shackles)
shackles seafarer (the shackles).description:
You're familiar with the treacherous coastlines, currents, and tides of the Shackles, and can use that knowledge anywhere.

Requirements: The Shackles

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (nature) and Knowledge (geography) checks while on the ocean. You also gain a +1 trait bonus on Swim checks, and Swim is always a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus while on the ocean.
+[[1]] Trait bonus while on the ocean.

darklands delver (tian xia darklands).name: Darklands Delver (Tian Xia Darklands)
darklands delver (tian xia darklands).description:
You are familiar with the byways and passages of Tian Xia's underworld.

Requirements: Tian Xia Darklands

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (dungeoneering) checks, and a +1 trait bonus on Survival checks made in the Darklands. One of these skills becomes class skill for you.

Usage

Select Survival or Knowledge (dungeoneering) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on checks made in the Darklands.

canopy shooter (grippli).name: Canopy Shooter (Grippli)
canopy shooter (grippli).description:
Growing up in a dangerous jungle, you learned to take advantage of your mobility relative to larger predators and elusive prey.

Requirements: Grippli

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on ranged weapon damage rolls when you are on higher ground than your target and are within 30 feet.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on damage rolls when you are on higher ground than your target and are within 30 feet.

cypher resistance (riddleport).name: Cypher Resistance (Riddleport)
cypher resistance (riddleport).description:
You have grown up near the Cyphergate, passing under its arch countless times in your lifetime. You can recount the runes from memory, and repeated exposure has imbued you with a faint resistance to runic magic.

Requirements: Riddleport

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against spells with the word “glyph” or “symbol” in their name.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus  against spells with the word “glyph” or “symbol” in their name.

gifted medium (magrim).name: Gifted Medium (Magrim)
gifted medium (magrim).description:
Your understanding of Magrim’s teachings makes you a perfect conduit for mystical forces.

Requirements: Magrim

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on your caster level when using divinations to reach out to other entities, such as commune or speak with dead.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus when using divinations to reach out to other entities, such as with commune or speak with dead.

fortunate.name: Fortunate
fortunate.description:
You are blessed by fate.

Benefits: Once per day when you use a spell or magic item with a randomized effect (such as @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.n0bsyxchnigkkuqo]{confusion}, @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.97mcivnu2owb8119]{mirror image}, @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.mb819hvwpk0zmw53]{prismatic spray}, or a bag of tricks), before you roll to determine the result, you may choose to roll twice and pick either result.

Suggested Characters

Calistrians, harrow deck readers, Vudrani.

tongue of many towns (human; garundi).name: Tongue of Many Towns (Human; Garundi)
tongue of many towns (human; garundi).description:
You have spent many years traveling across Garund, and have met many different people on your journeys.

Requirements: Garundi Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on two of the following skills: Diplomacy, Knowledge (local), and Linguistics.

Usage:

Select two (2) of the following skills: Diplomacy, Knowledge (local), and Linguistics, when you take this trait.

Update the target of the changes on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choices.

shaer (qadira, native kelish speaker).name: Shaer (Qadira, native Kelish speaker)
shaer (qadira, native kelish speaker).description:
Qadira is a land of poetry, and you have grown up immersed in the words and language of that culture. Your facility with the language is evident to all when you speak.

Requirements: Qadira native, native Kelish speaker

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Bluff and Diplomacy checks when speaking with anyone fluent in Kelish.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus when speaking with anyone fluent in Kelish.
+[[2]] Trait bonus when speaking with anyone fluent in Kelish.

inciter (tiefling).name: Inciter (Tiefling)
inciter (tiefling).description:
You always seem to know the perfect words to sow discord.

Requirements: Tiefling

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Bluff checks made to convince creatures to attack each other.

contextNotes.text:
+[[2]] Trait bonus 
to convince creatures to attack each other.

favored son/daughter (any campaign).name: Favored Son/Daughter (Any Campaign)
favored son/daughter (any campaign).description:
You were born and raised in the town of Sandpoint or its surrounding farms. You know many of the region’s secrets, and the locals already know who you are. You are well liked in town, and you’ll have lots of friends in the region, but the town’s tougher side sees you as a snitch or a pansy.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (local) checks, and Knowledge (local) is always a class skill for you. In addition, you can choose any one law-abiding citizen of Sandpoint from below as an ally; depending on the person you pick, you’ll get different benefits.

Tavern Owner

One of the town’s most influential and beloved nobles. Her inn/tavern is the most popular in town, and as one of her friends, you’re guaranteed a place to eat and sleep for free. She’s got lots of great contacts with merchants as well, and she’ll sell any of your loot for you—as a result, you gain an additional 10% over the amount of gp you normally would get from selling off treasure.

Sheriff

The no nonsense arm of the law, is like the uncle you never had. The benefits of being close friends with the town sheriff are extensive, and you can call in favors from him once per game session. A favor can either get you out of a legal jam, hook you up with a town guard for help, or give you a one-time +10 bonus on a Bluff, Diplomacy, or Intimidate check made against any local person.

Usage: Select Tavern Owner or Sheriff when you take this trait.

rebuke the curse.name: Rebuke the Curse
rebuke the curse.description: Benefits: Once per day, you can attempt to suppress the effects of a curse on a single creature with a touch. This functions as a remove curse spell, treating your character level as your caster level. If you succeed at your caster level check against the curse, the curse’s effects are negated for 1 minute. This trait does not allow a person burdened with a cursed item to remove or discard it. Use of this trait is a supernatural ability.

hellknight devotion.name: Hellknight Devotion
hellknight devotion.description:
Your family has strong ties to a Hellknight order, and you harness this belief as a weapon.

Benefits: Once per day as a swift action, you can empower the next single melee attack you make to be treated as lawful-aligned for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction.

drug addict (curse of the crimson throne.name: Drug Addict (Curse of the Crimson Throne)
drug addict (curse of the crimson throne.description:
Someone you know has become addicted to shiver, a drug distilled from the venom of dream spiders. The drug induces sleep filled with vivid dreams, during which the user's body often shakes and shivers, giving the substance its street name. Shiver is particularly dangerous for the desperate, for its promises of dreams and oblivion are often viewed as the only alternative apart from suicide for escaping a dreary life. You've always thought of shiver as a problem of the lower class, but then someone you know overdosed on the stuff. You've done a bit of investigating and have learned that the one who got your friend addicted in the first place was a crimelord named Gaedren. Unfortunately, the guards seem to be more focused on the bigger dealers. They don't have time to devote many resources to what they've called, "a bit player in a beggar's problem." It would seem that if his operation is to be stopped, it falls to you.

Benefits: Choose one of the following benefits:

Addicted Friend: The addict is a friend or lover who might or might not have survived the overdose. Your research into the drug scene and local politics has given you a respectable education in street knowledge. You gain a +2 bonus on Knowledge (local) checks or Diplomacy checks to Gather Information (choose one when selecting this trait).

Personal Addiction: You were the addict. You blame Gaedren for your brush with death and hate how his drugs are causing similar problems among other youths. Fortunately, your body recovers quickly from toxins, and you have a +1 bonus on Fortitude saving throws.

Usage:

  1. Select Addicted Friend, or Personal Addiction when you take this trait.

  2. if you select Personal Addiction: Update the target of the untargeted change on the changes tab of this trait to reflect Saving Throws>Fortitude

  3. if you select Addicted Friend: Select Knowledge (local) or Diplomacy.

    a) if you select Diplomacy, Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect Diplomacy.

    b)if you select Knowledge (local), Update the target of the untargeted change on the changes tab of this trait to reflect a value of 2 and a target of Specific Skills> Knowledge (local)

  4. Delete any unused change or note

Special Note: This is the 3.5 Ruleset version of this trait. To view the updated version for the Pathfinder rules click the link below:

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.ox1UTgNrZAGHFm6C]{Drug Addict (Curse of the Crimson Throne)}

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus checks to Gather Information.

monk weapon skill.name: Monk Weapon Skill
monk weapon skill.description:
Your exposure to teaching from the Houses of Perfection makes your attacks with monk weapons even more deadly.

Benefits: Select one monk weapon with which you are proficient (unarmed attacks do not qualify for this purpose). You gain a +1 trait bonus on damage rolls with this weapon.

Usage: Select one permitted monk weapon when you take this trait. Each time you add a weapon of the chosen type to your actor, take the following steps:

  1. Edit the weapon, on the details tab of the weapon, edit the attack.
  2. On the action tab of the attack scroll down to the damage formula section then click the "add damage formula" button.
  3. Put a 1 in the formula area and add a custom damage type of "trait".

iobarian survivor (iobaria).name: Iobarian Survivor (Iobaria)
iobarian survivor (iobaria).description:
Requirements: Iobaria

Benefits: Whenever you are exposed to a disease while you are trying to treat an afflicted creature, you gain a +4 trait bonus on your Fortitude saving throw to resist contracting the disease yourself.

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Trait bonus whenever you are exposed to a disease while you are trying to treat an afflicted creature to resist contracting the disease yourself.

cold heart (hellknight).name: Cold Heart (Hellknight)
cold heart (hellknight).description:
Requirements: Hellknight

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against spells and effects with the emotion descriptor.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against spells and effects with the emotion descriptor.

twisted tattoo.name: Twisted Tattoo
twisted tattoo.description:
Your exposure to eldritch phenomena has caused your skin to develop a strange pattern.

Benefits: Once per day as you cast a spell whose casting time is 1 round or less, you can roll 1d4–2 (no minimum) and add the result to that spell’s effective caster level. In an area of primal magic, the minimum result of this roll is 0, not –1.

honest.name: Honest
honest.description:
A religious mentor instilled in you a sense of uncompromising integrity. You often speak your mind in defiance of any fear or hatred it might earn you, a habit that inspires others to trust you.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks. This bonus increases to +2 when the Diplomacy check is made to influence those who are already friendly or helpful toward you.

Suggested Characters

Diabolists, paladins, Sarenrae worshipers.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] when the check is made to influence those who are already friendly or helpful toward you.

shadow dissident (nidal).name: Shadow Dissident (Nidal)
shadow dissident (nidal).description:
Lifelong encounters with conjured shadows have made you especially adept at piercing their dark veils.

Requirements: Nidal

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on saving throws against spells and effects with the shadow descriptor (Pathfinder RPG Ultimate Magic 138).

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against spells and effects with the shadow descriptor.

ship's surgeon (skull and shackles).name: Ship's Surgeon (Skull and Shackles)
ship's surgeon (skull and shackles).description:
On a ship, a sailor often has to learn multiple skills, and this rule certainly applies to you. Your father was a woodworker, and you learned your first trade from him. But on your first voyage at sea you quickly found out that a carpenter is often a ship's doctor as well,after all, who knows more about sawing off limbs than someone who saws wood for a living? People are a lot bloodier than wood, that's for sure, but you haven't had many complaints,those sailors who have enjoyed your services are either happy to be alive or dead, and there's old salts who swear the peg legs you for made them are better than the real legs they used to have. You were between ships in Port Peril, and after stopping for a drink at a tavern called the Formidably Maid, you happened upon a damsel in distress in the alleyway outside. Thinking you could help, you didn't notice the thugs hiding nearby, and they caught you unawares. You've been on enough ships to know a press gang by the blow of the sap on the back of your neck,you just hope whatever ship you end up on needs a surgeon. Although the majority of your equipment was taken away when you were press-ganged, you managed to hang on to a fully stocked healer's kit, and you begin the campaign with it, regardless of your starting circumstances.

Benefits: You start with a fully stocked @UUID[Compendium.pf1.items.Item.qlweoovcvtklmsre]{healer's kit}, and you begin the campaign with it, regardless of your starting circumstances.

You gain a +1 trait bonus on Craft (carpentry) and Heal checks, and Heal is a class skill for you..

Usage: Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the Craft (carpentry) skill.

Update the target of the untargeted change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect Craft (carpentry).

truth's agent.name: Truth's Agent
truth's agent.description:
You are skilled at weeding out information.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on all Diplomacy checks made to gather information and all Knowledge (local) checks. Knowledge (local) is always a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to gather information.

failed aspirant.name: Failed Aspirant
failed aspirant.description:
You’ve failed the trials to gain entry to one of the Houses of Perfection. Though you are disappointed, you were able to pick up one trick from the monks.

Benefits: Choose a single combat maneuver.

Once per day, you can perform that combat maneuver without provoking an attack of opportunity and you gain a +2 bonus on that attempt. 

Usage: Select one combat maneuver (Bull Rush, Dirty Trick, Disarm, Drag, Grapple, Overrun, Reposition, Steal, Sunder, Trip) when you take this trait.

Update the text of the note on the changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you can perform <chosen combat maneuver> without provoking an attack of opportunity and you gain a +[[2]] bonus on that attempt.

worthless pawn (any great old one).name: Worthless Pawn (any Great Old One)
worthless pawn (any great old one).description:
Your existence is so meaningless to your chosen patron that it cares nothing for how you wield its power.

Requirements: Any Great Old One

Benefits: You may prepare and cast spells whose alignment is in opposition to your patron’s, but doing so causes you to take 1 point of Wisdom damage per spell level when the spell is cast. This does not permit you to use spell trigger or completion items of opposing alignments.

contextNotes.text: You may prepare and cast spells whose alignment is in opposition to your patron’s, but doing so causes you to take 1 point of Wisdom damage per spell level when the spell is cast.

always threatening (thamir gixx).name: Always Threatening (Thamir Gixx)
always threatening (thamir gixx).description:
You are fast on the draw.

Requirements: Thamir Gixx

Benefits: As a move action, you can draw a light, concealed weapon for which you have @UUID[Compendium.pf1.feats.Item.n250dFlbykAIAg5Z]{Weapon Focus}. If you have the @UUID[Compendium.pf1.feats.Item.WKIVfdosc5SqYzmP]{Quick Draw} feat, drawing this weapon is a free action.

practiced deception (norgorber).name: Practiced Deception (Norgorber)
practiced deception (norgorber).description:
Requirements: Norgorber

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Disguise checks, and you can apply and remove a disguise in half the normal time.

contextNotes.text: You can apply and remove a disguise in half the normal time.

divine warrior (iomedae).name: Divine Warrior (Iomedae)
divine warrior (iomedae).description:
From an early age, you were trained by a militaristic order of clerics. You are devoted both to the teachings of Iomadae and to spreading those teachings by force.

Requirements: Iomedae

Benefits: Whenever you cast a divine spell that affects any melee weapons, choose one of those weapons; it gains a +1 trait bonus on damage rolls for the duration of the spell.

contextNotes.text: Whenever you cast a divine spell that affects any melee weapons, choose one of those weapons; it gains a +1 trait bonus on damage rolls for the duration of the spell.

debauchery defier (imperial palace of ta.name: Debauchery Defier (Imperial Palace of Taldor)
debauchery defier (imperial palace of ta.description:
You are particularly adept at resisting addiction.

Requirements: Associate with the court of the Imperial Palace of Taldor 

Benefits: You must fail two saving throws in a row to become addicted to a drug.

contextNotes.text: You must fail two saving throws in a row to become addicted to a drug.

unflappable (sovereign court).name: Unflappable (Sovereign Court)
unflappable (sovereign court).description:
Whether it is a result of your dedication to your work or just pure guts, nothing seems to shake you.

Requirements: Sovereign Court

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saves against fear, and the DC to demoralize you with Intimidate checks increases by 3.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus vs fear.

furious vengeance (dranngvit).name: Furious Vengeance (Dranngvit)
furious vengeance (dranngvit).description:
Once wronged, your vengeance can be swift and brutal.

Requirements: Dranngvit

Benefits: Once per day as an immediate action, you gain a +1 trait bonus on a single attack roll.

In addition, if the target has damaged you within the last round, you deal 1 additional point of damage on a successful hit.

You must choose to use this ability before making the attack roll.

contextNotes.text:
Once per day as an immediate action, you gain a +[[1]] Trait bonus on a single attack roll.

In addition, if the target has damaged you within the last round, you deal 1 additional point of damage on a successful hit.

You must choose to use this ability before making the attack roll.

favored champion (human; shoanti).name: Favored Champion (Human; Shoanti)
favored champion (human; shoanti).description:
Omens from the spirits indicated you would be a great champion against your tribe’s enemies.

Requirements: Shoanti Human Heritage

Benefits: Once per day as an immediate action when you fail a combat maneuver check, you can reroll it. You must take the second result even if it is worse.

contextNotes.text: Once per day as an immediate action when you fail a combat maneuver check, you can reroll it. You must take the second result even if it is worse.

artistic dilettante (human; taldan).name: Artistic Dilettante (Human; Taldan)
artistic dilettante (human; taldan).description:
You come from money and because of this can quickly discern objects' value.

Requirements: Taldan Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Appraise checks, and on checks for one Craft skill of your choice.

Usage: Select one Craft skill when you take this trait.

Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the chosen skill.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

channel the earth (plane of earth).name: Channel the Earth (Plane of Earth)
channel the earth (plane of earth).description:
Perhaps you worship the Elemental Lord of Earth, or perhaps you have gleaned your fated powers via some other means of interacting with the Eternal Delve. Your divine powers are infused with the elemental powers of the Plane of Earth.

Requirements: Plane of Earth

Benefits: Whenever you channel positive energy to heal living creatures, affected targets gain a +2 profane bonus to CMD to resist bull rush, reposition, and trip combat maneuvers. Alternately, if you channel negative energy to harm living creatures, affected targets take a –2 penalty to CMD against such combat maneuvers. This effect lasts 1 round.

following in the footsteps (ruins of azl.name: Following in the Footsteps (Ruins of Azlant)
following in the footsteps (ruins of azl.description:
One of your dear friends or family members was part of the first wave of colonists to be selected by the Bountiful Venture Company to establish Talmandor's Bounty. You applied for the first wave as well, but weren’t selected. Now that you’ve made your way into the second wave, you can’t wait to be reunited with your family member. Your devotion to your family or friends is important to you, and it shows not only in how you feel about that friend or family member you are soon to reunite with, but also in how you treat other people with respect.

Benefits: Whenever you use the aid another action to aid an adjacent ally, increase the bonus you grant your ally by 1. In addition, due to your positive and helpful nature, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks.

contextNotes.text: Whenever you use the aid another action to aid an adjacent ally, increase the bonus you grant your ally by 1.
Whenever you use the aid another action to aid an adjacent ally, increase the bonus you grant your ally by 1.

shadow-scarred.name: Shadow-Scarred
shadow-scarred.description:
You were touched by terrible horrors that live in the darkness just outside the human sphere, such as shadow-beasts or aberrations linked to the Dark Tapestry, and feel your life-force ebb away ever so slightly whenever you return to the shadows.

Effects: Whenever you are in an area of dim light or darkness, you take a –1 penalty on saving throws.

contextNotes.text: -[[1]] penalty when in dim light or darkness.

goldsniffer (dwarf).name: Goldsniffer (Dwarf)
goldsniffer (dwarf).description:
Your keen senses lead you to hidden treasures.

Requirements: Dwarf

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Perception checks related to metals, jewels, and gemstones.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus  on checks related to metals, jewels, and gemstones.

vainglory.name: Vainglory
vainglory.description:
You compulsively seek recognition for your deeds, making it hard to act with subtlety.

Effects: You take a -1 penalty on Bluff, Disguise, and Stealth checks, and the save DC of any illusion you create is 1 lower than normal.

contextNotes.text: -[[1]] to DC of illusions you create

dawn renewal (sarenrae).name: Dawn Renewal (Sarenrae)
dawn renewal (sarenrae).description:
Your morning prayers often send a welcome spark of divine vitality into your most recent patients.

Requirements: Sarenrae

Benefits: When you use the Heal skill to provide long-term care, your patients heal an extra 2 hit points at sunrise.

contextNotes.text: Providing long-term care to your patients heals an extra 2 hit points at sunrise.

tunnel fighter (dwarf).name: Tunnel Fighter (Dwarf)
tunnel fighter (dwarf).description:
Caves and tunnels are a second home to you.

Requirements: Dwarf

Benefits: While underground, you receive a +2 trait bonus to initiative checks and a +1 trait bonus on weapon damage rolls for critical hits (this damage is multiplied on a critical hit).

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus while underground.
While underground +[[1]] Trait bonus  on weapon damage rolls for critical hits (this damage is multiplied on a critical hit).

ash bane (wastelands).name: Ash Bane (Wastelands)
ash bane (wastelands).description:
You have built up a resistance to the diseases that ash giants and their vermin guardians carry.

Requirements: Wastelands

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Fortitude saving throws to resist the disease abilities of ash giants and creatures with the vermin type. In addition, you gain a +1 trait bonus on melee attack rolls against vermin.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to resist the disease abilities of ash giants and creatures with the vermin type.
+[[1]] Trait bonus  on melee attack rolls against vermin.

inquisitive banterer (catfolk).name: Inquisitive Banterer (Catfolk)
inquisitive banterer (catfolk).description:
You are able to learn information by bombarding others with questions that come across as conversational.

Requirements: Catfolk

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks. In addition, you can use Diplomacy to gather information in 1d2 hours instead of 1d4.

contextNotes.text: You can  gather information in 1d2 hours instead of 1d4.

militia veteran (any town or village).name: Militia Veteran (Any Town or Village)
militia veteran (any town or village).description:
Your first job was serving in a civilian militia in your hometown. The skills you learned through daily drilling and protecting your fellow townsfolk gave you special insight into military life.

Requirements: Any Town or Village

Benefits: Select one of the following skills: Profession (soldier), Ride, or Survival. You gain a +1 trait bonus on checks with that skill, and it is always a class skill for you.

Usage

Select Profession (soldier), Ride, or Survival when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

good influence (aasimar; lawbringers).name: Good Influence (Aasimar; Lawbringers)
good influence (aasimar; lawbringers).description:
You possess an innate ability to motivate and inspire others to do the right thing.

Requirements: Lawbringer Aasimar Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks when attempting to persuade a nongood creature to make a decision that benefits the cause of good, and when persuading a nonlawful creature to make a decision in line with the laws of the region. These bonuses stack if you attempt to persuade a nongood, nonlawful creature to take a lawful good action.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus  when attempting to persuade a nongood creature to  benefit the cause of good or when persuading a nonlawful creature to make a decision in line with the laws of the region. (These stack to +2 if both apply.)

explorer (gnome).name: Explorer (Gnome)
explorer (gnome).description:
Though many gnomes settle down in communities large enough and interesting enough to provide them with all the stimulation they need to ward off the Bleaching, you’re never going to be one of them. You are always looking around the next bend in the road or over the next hill, past the borders of where you are to where you want to be.

Requirements: Gnome

Benefits: At the beginning of each character level, select one desired terrain type from the following list:

  • cold
  • desert
  • forest
  • jungle
  • mountains (including hills)
  • plains
  • planes (other than the Material Plane)
  • swamp
  • underground (including caves and dungeons)
  • urban
  • water

You gain a +2 bonus on all Survival checks made in your desired terrain type.

At the beginning of each level, you must select a new desired terrain type to replace the old one

(you cannot take the same desired terrain type two levels in a row).

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] bonus in your current desired terrain type.

assimilated native (human; mwangi).name: Assimilated Native (Human; Mwangi)
assimilated native (human; mwangi).description:
You have adopted the ways of the colonials. As a result, you feel that other, 'inferior' races such as your Mwangi brethren should obey you as they do the colonials.

Requirements: Mwangi Human Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Intimidate checks to influence Mwangi natives, but you suffer a -2 on Diplomacy checks to influence them.

contextNotes.text: -[[2]] Penalty to influence Mwangi natives
+[[2]] Trait bonus  to influence Mwangi natives

friend in every town.name: Friend in Every Town
friend in every town.description:
You have no problem making friends and learning information from them wherever you go.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on all Knowledge (local) checks and Diplomacy checks. One of these skills (your choice) is always a class skill for you.

Usage: Choose Knowledge (local) or Diplomacy when taking this trait. Add the chosen skill to the class skills section of the details tab of this trait.

driven by guilt (strange aeons).name: Driven By Guilt (Strange Aeons)
driven by guilt (strange aeons).description:
You awake after your ordeal with a lingering feeling that you have taken part in something outside the normal bounds of your morals. Whether you were corrupted at one point or compelled to perform some forgotten actions, this guilt drives you to fight against those forces in the world that prey upon the good. You see your present condition as a chance to redeem yourself and banish this unsettling emotion.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against any spells or spell-like abilities cast by evil creatures. In addition, once per day as a swift action, you can add your Charisma bonus to your attack rolls and deal 1 additional point of damage for each class level you have against evil creatures for 1 round.

You must have a good alignment to take this trait.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, as a swift action, for 1 round you can add ([[@abilities.cha.mod]]) to your attack rolls and deal ([[@attributes.hd.total]]) additional damage against evil creatures.  ([[@resources.drivenByGuiltStrangeAeons.value]] remaining uses)
+[[1]] Trait bonus against any spells or spell-like abilities cast by evil creatures.

candidate for perfection (jalmeray).name: Candidate for Perfection (Jalmeray)
candidate for perfection (jalmeray).description:
You have begun training to enter one of the Houses of Perfection, and have honed your body, mind, and spirit into one well-trained weapon.

Requirements: Jalmeray

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack of opportunity attack rolls made with unarmed strikes.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attacks of opportunity  made with unarmed strikes.

friends in low places.name: Friends in Low Places
friends in low places.description:
Outcasts know their own, and that knowledge opens doors and loosens tongues.

Benefits: Gathering information in lower quarters, such as vice dens and poorer districts, takes you 1d2 hours (instead of 1d4 hours). In addition, the attitudes of any destitute or impoverished NPCs you interact with begin one step closer to helpful.

contextNotes.text: Gathering information in lower quarters, such as vice dens and poorer districts, takes you 1d2 hours (instead of 1d4 hours). In addition, the attitudes of any destitute or impoverished NPCs you interact with begin one step closer to helpful.

regional recluse - sanos forest or whist.name: Regional Recluse - Sanos Forest or Whistledown (Varisia)
regional recluse - sanos forest or whist.description:
Your home community prides itself on remaining independent from Varisia’s city-states.

Requirements: Varisia

Benefits: Choose one of Varisia’s unaffiliated settlements from this list below. You gain a +1 trait bonus on Survival checks and one of the following benefits.

Sanos Forest or Whistledown

Gain Gnome as a bonus language. If you already have this language, gain a +1 trait bonus on Stealth checks made in forested areas.

Usage

  1. If you already have the Gnome language, Update the target of the untargeted context note on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect Stealth.

  2. if this trait is your only source of the Gnome language, delete the untargeted note.


Special Note

This is one of five versions of this trait. Other versions can be found here:

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.QezjzIjvbfIxTLPD]{Regional Recluse - Arsmeril or Crying Leaf (Varisia)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.SUxxga9Juql4kY3J]{Regional Recluse - Ilsurian (Varisia)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.EtUNYMr9ykH2XhZA]{Regional Recluse - Turtleback Ferry (Varisia)}

@UUID[Compendium.pf-content.pf-traits.Item.BDByEeeSK08bn8gp]{Regional Recluse - Urglin (Varisia)}

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on Stealth checks made in forested areas.

whistleblower (liberty's edge).name: Whistleblower (Liberty's Edge)
whistleblower (liberty's edge).description:
You are wise to the schemes of liars, thieves, and cheats.

Requirements: Liberty's Edge

Benefits: You gain a +1 bonus on Sense Motive checks, and that skill becomes a class skill for you.

tracker of the society (ranger, pathfind.name: Tracker of the Society (Ranger, Pathfinder Society)
tracker of the society (ranger, pathfind.description:
Thanks to your time as a Pathfinder, you've mastered the art of tracking.

Requirements: Ranger, Pathfinder Society

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Initiative checks and a +2 trait bonus on Survival skill checks made while traveling through any of your favored terrains. This is in addition to any bonuses you receive from your Favored Terrain class ability.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus made while traveling through any of your favored terrains.
+[[2]] Trait bonus made while traveling through any of your favored terrains.

mechanical aptitude.name: Mechanical Aptitude
mechanical aptitude.description:
You have learned much from tinkering with ancient technology from the Jistka Imperium.

Benefits: Choose one of the following skills: Disable Device, Knowledge (engineering), or Use Magic Device. Once per day, you can reroll a check with that skill before the outcome of the check is revealed. You must take the second result, even if it is worse.

Usage: The skill is set to Disable Device by default. If you wish it to be the other skills mentioned above, go to the changes tab and change the target to your chosen skill in the context notes.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you can reroll a check with this skill before the outcome of the check is revealed. You must take the second result, even if it is worse.

air-touched.name: Air-Touched
air-touched.description:
You share an affinity with elemental air.

Benefits: You gain DR 1/— against creatures and attacks with the air type.

contextNotes.text: DR 1/— against creatures and attacks with the air type.

angradd's flame (angradd).name: Angradd's Flame (Angradd)
angradd's flame (angradd).description:
Your faith in the Forge-Fire fans the passionate flames of war in your heart.

Requirements: Angradd

Benefits: Whenever you confirm a critical hit, you deal 1 additional point of damage to your target. This damage is considered precision damage.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] precision damage on a confirmed critical hit.

evasive sting.name: Evasive Sting
evasive sting.description:
Larger enemies have a hard time striking you.

Benefits: You gain a +1 dodge bonus to AC against enemies that are both your favored enemy and at least two size categories larger than you.

contextNotes.text: +1 Dodge bonus against enemies that are both your favored enemy and at least two size categories larger than you.

foul belch (goblin).name: Foul Belch (Goblin)
foul belch (goblin).description:
Requirements: Goblin

Benefits: Once per day as a standard action, you can force a particularly odious belch at a single opponent within 5 feet. The character affected must make a DC 12 Fortitude save or be sickened for 1d6 rounds. The save DC is Constitution-based.

black as night (fell pony).name: Black as Night (Fell Pony)
black as night (fell pony).description:
Requirements: Pony (Fell Pony)

Benefits: While astride a fell pony, you gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls against enemies you are flanking.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against enemies you are flanking while astride a fell pony

grit goggles (darklands).name: Grit Goggles (Darklands)
grit goggles (darklands).description:
Exposure to the constant hazards of airborne particles (such as during a sandstorm or cave-in) has made your eyes less susceptible to irritants, such as sand, dust, and even liquids and gases.

Requirements: Darklands, usually under deserts

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus against being blinded by foreign objects (such as from a Dirty Trick combat maneuver) and you halve any reductions in visibility from the environment (such as from certain storms) and their corresponding penalties. For example, if you were in a sandstorm that normally reduces visibility to 25 feet and imparts a –4 penalty on Perception checks, you reduce visibility to 50 feet and take only a –2 penalty on Perception checks.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against being blinded by foreign objects (such as from a Dirty Trick combat maneuver).
You halve any reductions in visibility from the environment (such as from certain storms) and their corresponding penalties.

empyreal pantheon.name: Empyreal Pantheon
empyreal pantheon.description:
Your faith in the empyreal lords accommodates numerous gods, and you are gifted at adapting their wide variety of focuses and lessons to your own life.

Benefits: Select either the law or chaos alignment descriptor. You may cast spells with that descriptor, even if your alignment or that of your god would normally not allow it. Doing so applies the good descriptor to the spell if it does not already apply.

absalom hotspur (absalom).name: Absalom Hotspur (Absalom)
absalom hotspur (absalom).description:
You grew up on the streets of Absalom and have worked as both guide and bodyguard for visitors to the city.

Requirements: Absalom

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Initiative checks and Knowledge (local) checks.

merchant's child (katapesh).name: Merchant's Child (Katapesh)
merchant's child (katapesh).description:
One of your close relatives was a gifted merchant in the bustling bazaars of Katapesh, and taught you early in life how to see the innate value in any object.

Requirements: Katapesh

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Appraise checks, and Appraise is always a class skill for you.

prophet of burrows (druma; ratfolk).name: Prophet of Burrows (Druma; Ratfolk)
prophet of burrows (druma; ratfolk).description:
While you might not be a practicing follower of the Prophecies of Kalistrade, you value human coin more than most ratfolk do and have taken the mercantile practices of the Prophecies to heart.

Requirements: Ratfolk native to Druma

Benefits: Whenever you roll a skill check to earn money, increase the amount of money that you earn by 5%.

jungle guide (sargava).name: Jungle Guide (Sargava)
jungle guide (sargava).description:
You've made your living outfitting and guiding expeditions deep into the Mwangi interior in search of ancient ruins and lost cities.

Requirements: Sargava

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Handle Animal checks, and a +1 trait bonus on Survival checks in jungle terrain. One of these skills is a class skill for you.

Usage

Select Handle Animal or Survival when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus in jungle terrain.
