Once per day,  +[[2]] Trait bonus on any single check. ([[@resources.secretsOfTheSphinxScarabSages.value]] remaining uses).

dusk dancer (halfling; nidal).name: Dusk Dancer (Halfling; Nidal)
dusk dancer (halfling; nidal).description:
Growing up in shadowy Nidal has given you the confidence to avoid hazards which you can barely see.

Requirements: Halfling; Nidal

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Reflex saves in dim light or darkness.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus in dim light or darkness.

best customer (jade regent).name: Best Customer (Jade Regent)
best customer (jade regent).description:
You’ve sat on your favorite barstool at the Rusty Dragon every night for as long as you can remember— and some nights you can’t remember thanks to a few too many drinks. In your various hazes, you’ve gotten to know the tap house’s proprietor, Ameiko, or one of her regulars, Sandru, quite well. Additionally, you’re well acquainted with a variety of uses for strong drink.

Benefits: Any time you have an alcoholic beverage on hand, you can expend one use of it to grant you a +2 trait bonus on a Heal check. This bonus increases to +4 if you’re treating your favorite drinking buddy (your NPC of choice).

NPC Choices

Ameiko, Sandru

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus if you expand one use of an alcoholic beverage. This bonus increases to +[[4]] when you’re treating your favorite drinking buddy. (Ameiko or Sandru)

educated druggist (alchemist).name: Educated Druggist (Alchemist)
educated druggist (alchemist).description:
Requirements: Alchemist healers who hail from Osibu, Thuvia, Tian Xia, or Ustalav, or who have lived there for at least 1 year

Benefits: Any alchemical remedies you administer that provide an alchemical bonus have that bonus increased by 1. These remedies also restore 1 hit point to the target in addition to their usual effects.

magic crafter.name: Magic Crafter
magic crafter.description:
Your early magical training involved study of the properties and creation of magic items.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Appraise checks and a +1 bonus on any Craft checks attempted while making magic items.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Bonus on any Craft checks attempted while making magic items.

voice of monsters (lamashtu).name: Voice of Monsters (Lamashtu)
voice of monsters (lamashtu).description:
Your faith leads you to see the power and wonder in even the most horrible abominations.

Requirement(s) Lamashtu

Benefits
Once per day, you can cast @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.ct9fhh8uawn8e4md]{speak with animals}. When you cast this spell, it can affect animals (as normal) as well as aberrations and magical beasts with an Intelligence of 2 or lower.

student of faith (rise of the runelords).name: Student of Faith (Rise of the Runelords)
student of faith (rise of the runelords).description:
While you have personally dedicated your life to a single deity, you study all religions and mortal faiths. Upon hearing that the town of Sandpoint recently completed a cathedral dedicated to the six deities most popular in the area, you had to see the place for yourself, and have arrived in time for the consecration of this holy edifice.

Benefits: Because of your strong faith and broad range of study, you cast all cure spells at +1 caster level, and whenever you channel energy, you gain a +1 trait bonus to the save DC of your channeled energy.

Usage: For each Cure Spell you can cast:

Edit the chosen spell. In the Offsets Section>Caster Level Offset box enter a 1

If you have the Channel Energy Class Feature

Edit the feature> Edit each Use. On the Use>Action Tab add + 1 to the DC Formula box

adrift (aasimar).name: Adrift (Aasimar)
adrift (aasimar).description:
You never felt like you belonged in your family. You were different, an outsider, and you grew up unable to bond with the people around you. Now, as an adult, you are still a little odd and others have a hard time understanding what makes you tick.

Requirements: Aasimar

Benefits: You receive a +1 trait bonus on saving throws made to resist charm and compulsion effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus to resist charm and compulsion effects.

glory of old (five kings mountains).name: Glory of Old (Five Kings Mountains)
glory of old (five kings mountains).description:
In your veins flows the blood of dwarven heroes from Tar Taargadth.

Requirements: Five Kings Mountains

Benefits: You receive a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against spells, spell-like abilities, and poison.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against spells, spell-like abilities, and poison.

aerial harrier (strix).name: Aerial Harrier (Strix)
aerial harrier (strix).description:
You are used to fighting enemies who cannot fly.

Requirements: Strix

Benefits: While flying, you gain a +1 trait bonus to AC against melee attacks from creatures on the ground.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus while flying,  against melee attacks from creatures on the ground.

celestial tracker (aasimar; angelkin).name: Celestial Tracker (Aasimar; Angelkin)
celestial tracker (aasimar; angelkin).description:
You share the same talent for tracking evildoers that your angelic ancestor possessed.

Requirements: Angelskin Aasimar Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Survival checks made to follow tracks. In addition, you treat trails as being up to 24 hours fresher than they actually are when using the Survival skill to follow tracks.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus  to follow tracks and you treat trails as being up to 24 hours fresher than they actually are.

sun orchid harvester (thuvia).name: Sun Orchid Harvester (Thuvia)
sun orchid harvester (thuvia).description:
You were employed briefly by Deyrubrujan’s alchemist cabal to harvest sun orchids deep in the Thuvian desert. Though you never worked your way deeper into the cabal, you retain the knowledge gained during your employment.

Requirements: Thuvia

Benefits: You gain a +1 bonus on Knowledge (nature) and Survival checks made in desert environments.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] bonus in desert environments.
+[[1]] bonus in desert environments.

armored soul.name: Armored Soul
armored soul.description: Benefits: When wearing armor or using a shield, you gain a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against divine spells that deal damage

contextNotes.text: If wearing armor or using a shield, gain [[+1]] Trait bonus against divine spells that deal damage.

rugged dragon hunter.name: Rugged Dragon Hunter
rugged dragon hunter.description: Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Survival checks to track dragons. If you have the favored terrain class ability, add a +1 bonus to check for skills affected by this ability, as long as you’re using these skills in regards to a dragon.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to track dragons.
+[[1]] Untyped bonus if in a favored terrain and made in  regards to a dragon.
+[[1]] Untyped bonus if in a favored terrain and made in  regards to a dragon.
+[[1]] Untyped bonus if in a favored terrain and made in  regards to a dragon.
+[[1]] Untyped bonus if in a favored terrain and made in  regards to a dragon.

focused mind.name: Focused Mind
focused mind.description:
Your childhood was either dominated by lessons of some sort (whether musical, academic, or other) or by a horrible home life that encouraged your ability to block out distractions and focus on the immediate task at hand.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on concentration checks.

absolute loyalty.name: Absolute Loyalty
absolute loyalty.description: Benefits: You gain the one-time ability to immediately cast @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.qr6qcx8zqum6jhc4]{atonement} upon yourself as a spell-like ability upon performing an act or being subjected to an effect that spell affects. This ability can only be used while you are acting in the service of your liege (an individual of higher social standing chosen when you take this feat).

lonely.name: Lonely
lonely.description:
You are far too easily convinced of the friendly intentions of others.

Effects: You take a -2 penalty on Sense Motive checks and on Perception checks to see through disguises, and on saving throws against charm spells and spell-like abilities.

contextNotes.text: -[[2]] to see through disguises
-[[2]] vs charm spells and spell-like abilities

hellknight aspirant (hell's vengeance).name: Hellknight Aspirant (Hell's Vengeance)
hellknight aspirant (hell's vengeance).description:
You have always admired the authority, discipline, and righteousness of the ebon-armored Hellknights. Whether it’s their unwavering self-control, uncompromising dedication to law and order, relentless pursuit of justice, or merciless administration of punishment, you have tried to model your own actions on the Hellknight philosophy called the Measure and the Chain. Your most fervent wish is to eventually join one of the Hellknight orders and take your place among the grim ranks dedicated to upholding and enforcing the laws of Hell and of Cheliax.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (planes) checks, and Knowledge (planes) is a class skill for you. In addition, choose one of the following Hellknight orders. You have trained yourself in the use of that order’s favored weapon (if more than one weapon is listed, pick one), and gain a +1 trait bonus to your Combat Maneuver Defense whenever an opponent tries to sunder or disarm you of that weapon.

Order of the Chain: Flail

Order of the Gate: Dagger

Order of the Godclaw: Morningstar

Order of the Nail: Lance or halberd

Order of the Pike: Longspear

Order of the Pyre: Glaive

Order of the Rack: Longsword or whip

Order of the Scourge: Heavy mace or whip

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus whenever an opponent tries to sunder or disarm you of your chosen weapon.

lost role model (famous dungeon).name: Lost Role Model (Famous Dungeon)
lost role model (famous dungeon).description:
As a youth, you marveled at the crusaders, spelunkers, and lore seekers who came through your town on their way to the nearby dungeon, tomb, or ruins.

Requirements: Settlement Near a Famous Dungeon

Benefits: One such intrepid role model in particular was of great import to you. On the last day you ever saw your hero, the adventurer gave you a unique dungeon guide (see page 14) worth no more than 750 gp and with an accuracy modifier of no greater than +2.

Suggestion

With the help of your GM and using the examples in this book, construct a unique dungeon guide tied to the dungeon near where you grew up.

stormrunner (the shackles).name: Stormrunner (The Shackles)
stormrunner (the shackles).description:
You have sailed on some of the roughest seas on Golarion, and have skirted the Eye of Abendego.

Requirements: The Shackles

Benefits: You're accustomed to the pitch and yaw of sailing vessels, even in a storm, and gain a +2 trait bonus on Acrobatics checks to keep your footing on uneven ground or unsteady surfaces.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to keep your footing on uneven ground or unsteady surfaces.

mutant eye.name: Mutant Eye
mutant eye.description:
One of your parents was exposed to uncontrolled magic or arcane pollution before you were born, causing you to develop a minor mutation that has marked you as different all your life.

Benefits: You have a third eye on your forehead. As long as it is uncovered and open, you can get a clearer sense of the emotions of those you see, granting you a +2 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks that increases to +4 on checks to notice whether a character is under a mind-affecting effect. However, the eye is unsettling, and you take a –1 penalty on Bluff and Diplomacy checks against humanoids who can see it.

Suggested Characters

Lamashtans, Mana Wastes natives, Nexians.

contextNotes.text: -[[1]] Penalty against humanoids who can see your third eye.
-[[1]] Penalty against humanoids who can see your third eye.
As long as your third eye is uncovered and open, you gain a +[[2]] trait bonus. This increases to +[[4]] on checks to notice whether a character is under a mind-affecting effect.

classically schooled.name: Classically Schooled
classically schooled.description:
Your apprenticeship or early education was particularly focused on the direct application of magic.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Spellcraft checks, and Spellcraft is always a class skill for you.

fiery dominance (plane of fire).name: Fiery Dominance (Plane of Fire)
fiery dominance (plane of fire).description:
Raised among the efreet of the Plane of Fire, you developed a similarly assured sense of superiority over people from other backgrounds.

Requirements: Plane of Fire

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Intimidate checks against humanoids of a subtype different from your own (or all non-human humanoids, if you are not a humanoid), and the DC for such creatures to demoralize you with Intimidate is 2 higher than usual.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against humanoids of a subtype different from your own (or all non-human humanoids, if you are not a humanoid).

bad reputation (sczarni faction).name: Bad Reputation (Sczarni Faction)
bad reputation (sczarni faction).description:
The reputation of the Sczarni precedes your arrival.

Requirements: Sczarni Faction

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Intimidate checks, and Intimidate becomes a class skill for you.

carpenden lobber (andoran; carpenden).name: Carpenden Lobber (Andoran; Carpenden)
carpenden lobber (andoran; carpenden).description:
Your regular participation in the moonmelon festival has made you wickedly effective at hurling harmless objects.

Requirements: Carpenden [in Andoran]

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on attack rolls when throwing items that do not deal normal hit point damage (such as weapons that deal nonlethal damage or items such as @UUID[Compendium.pf1.items.Item.utncinnlwzscpzbd]{tanglefoot bags} and @UUID[Compendium.pf1.items.Item.akgicrcsuznrhvkk]{thunderstones}).

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on attack rolls when throwing items that do not deal normal hit point damage.

warrior of old (elf).name: Warrior of Old (Elf)
warrior of old (elf).description:
As a child, you put in long hours in combat drills, and though time has made this training a dim memory, you still have a knack for quickly responding to trouble.

Requirements: Elf

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Initiative checks.

crisis of faith.name: Crisis of Faith
crisis of faith.description:
You were raised to uphold a specific set of tenets, and you never had reason to doubt their veracity. Recent events, however, have undermined that certainty, and you now find yourself wondering if any of the universal truths you once believed in can actually hold up to scrutiny. Perhaps you witnessed a mentor or church official fall from grace or violate your shared tenets. Maybe you watched a friend injured terribly despite (or because of ) their pure faith.

Benefits: Whatever the case, your shaken confidence grants you a +1 trait bonus on saving throws against illusions. You may replace any other faith trait with the Crisis of Faith trait whenever you gain a level. This replacement is permanent until you receive @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.qr6qcx8zqum6jhc4]{atonement}.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against illusions.

lucid dreamer (varisian wanderers, wonde.name: Lucid Dreamer (Varisian Wanderers, Wonderseekers)
lucid dreamer (varisian wanderers, wonde.description:
Your dreams are a special place of power that only you get to enjoy; driving away threats to your subconscious is as easy as slamming a door.

Requirements: Varisian Wanderers, Wonderseekers

Benefits: You receive a +1 trait bonus on Knowledge (planes) checks and a +2 trait bonus on Will saves against effects on your dreaming mind or subconscious, such as a night hag's dream haunting ability, the @UUID[Compendium.pf1.spells.Item.siv3ub7hbmcklf8c]{nightmare} spell, and illusions of the phantasm subschool.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against effects on your dreaming mind or subconscious, and illusions of the phantasm subschool.

vindictive strike (gyronna).name: Vindictive Strike (Gyronna)
vindictive strike (gyronna).description:
Requirements: Gyronna

Benfits

Once per day during a combat encounter, you can choose to gain a +1 trait bonus on a single attack roll against the creature that last hit you.

martial performer.name: Martial Performer
martial performer.description:
Your training has made you a martial performer.

Benefits: When you gain a monk bonus feat, you may instead take a performance feat for which you meet the prerequisites.

hellknight ancestry.name: Hellknight Ancestry
hellknight ancestry.description:
At least one of your parents was a Hellknight, and you see the orders as the embodiment of civic virtue.

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks to influence Hellknights and a +2 trait bonus on Intimidate checks to influence enemies of the Hellknights.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to influence Hellknights.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to influence enemies of the Hellknights.

lycanthropic bloodlust (skinwalker; were.name: Lycanthropic Bloodlust (Skinwalker; Werewolf-Kin)
lycanthropic bloodlust (skinwalker; were.description:
When your lycanthropic heritage awakened, you thrilled at any excuse for violence. Although you might have learned to focus or suppress this urge, it still gives you energy in a fight.

Requirements: Werewolf-Kin Skinwalker Heritage

Benefits: Once per day, when you deal damage to a foe with a natural attack, you may gain 1d6 temporary hit points as an immediate action. These temporary hit points last for 1 minute.

wary eye.name: Wary Eye
wary eye.description:
You know that you could be exposed at any time. Even your closest friends could someday become enemies, and so you have trained yourself to never be surprised by a betrayal.

Benefits: At the beginning of combat, before initiative checks are revealed, you can select one opponent you are aware of to be the subject of this trait. If you succeed at a Wisdom check opposed by the subject’s initiative check, you are not considered flat-footed against this subject for this combat, even if you normally would be. You can use this trait in a surprise round, even if you cannot otherwise act that round.

contextNotes.text: At the beginning of combat, before initiative checks are revealed, you can select one opponent you are aware of to be the subject of this trait. If you succeed at a Wisdom check opposed by the subject’s initiative check, you are not considered flat-footed against this subject for this combat, even if you normally would be. You can use this trait in a surprise round, even if you cannot otherwise act that round.

hongal bloodrider (hongal).name: Hongal Bloodrider (Hongal)
hongal bloodrider (hongal).description:
You belong to one of the nomadic groups of horseback riders that range throughout Hongal.

Requirements: Hongal

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Handle Animal and Ride checks.

faith healer (aasimar).name: Faith Healer (Aasimar)
faith healer (aasimar).description:
You have an unearthly appearance and soothing aura about you that make people believe you are gifted with divine powers. You learned at a young age to make use of your gift in order to make money, and you've found ways to ethically justify your work.

Requirements: Aasimar

Benefits: You can make a Heal check weekly to earn money as though making a Profession check. In addition, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Heal checks.

contextNotes.text: You can make a Heal check weekly to earn money as though making a Profession check.

eager performer (rise of the runelords).name: Eager Performer (Rise of the Runelords)
eager performer (rise of the runelords).description:
Hearing that Sandpoint had a theater rivaling those found in large cities like Magnimar and Korvosa, you decided to try your luck getting stage time there. After sending a letter to Cyrdak Drokkus requesting an audition and not hearing back, you’ve taken it upon yourself to travel to Sandpoint and meet him in person, trusting your force of will and charming inf luence will get you what you want.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on checks for any one Perform skill. Additionally, choose any one spell of the enchantment school; its save DC increases by +1.

Usage: Select one Perform skill when you take this trait.

Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the chosen skill.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

Select one spell of the enchantment school when you take this trait.

Edit the chosen spell. Edit the Use>Action tab> In the DC Offset Formula box enter a 1

log roller (forest).name: Log Roller (Forest)
log roller (forest).description:
The time you spent leaping between slippery logs as they whirled down the river to market taught you how to keep your footing.

Requirements: Forest

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Acrobatics checks and a +1 trait bonus to your CMB when attempting to resist trip attacks.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus when attempting to resist trip attacks.

effortless aid (human; garundi).name: Effortless Aid (Human; Garundi)
effortless aid (human; garundi).description:
You naturally observe others, and move to complement them.

Requirements: Garundi Human Heritage

Benefits: Once per day as a move action, you can select an adjacent ally. For the next minute, you can take 10 on attack rolls and skill checks to use the aid another action to aid that ally, even if you are distracted.

contextNotes.text: Once per day as a move action, you can select an adjacent ally. For the next minute, you can take 10 on attack rolls and skill checks to use the aid another action to aid that ally, even if you are distracted.

Once per day as a move action, you can select an adjacent ally. For the next minute, you can take 10 on attack rolls and skill checks to use the aid another action to aid that ally, even if you are distracted.

sphinxfriend (dwarf; pahmet).name: Sphinxfriend (Dwarf; Pahmet)
sphinxfriend (dwarf; pahmet).description:
You were raised near the lair of a proud sphinx. Well known for their wisdom, sphinxes are greatly respected by your people, and in times of uncertainty and crisis, their counsel has been invaluable. When you came of age, you were chosen to be the emissary between your settlement and the sphinx. Luckily for you, the proud creature enjoyed your company, and in addition to bartering for information, you learned to enjoy the riddles of your noble friend.

Requirements: Pahmet Dwarf Heritage

Benefits: As a result of your experiences, you gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks, Linguistics skill checks made to decipher writing in an unfamiliar language, and Sense Motive checks made to discern secret messages.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus  to decipher writing in  unfamiliar languages.
+[[1]] Trait bonus  to discern secret messages.

river rat (marsh or river).name: River Rat (Marsh or River)
river rat (marsh or river).description:
You learned to swim right after you learned to walk. As a youth, a gang of river pirates put you to work swimming in nighttime rivers and canals with a dagger in your teeth so you could sever the anchor ropes of merchant vessels.

Requirements: Marsh or river

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on damage rolls with a dagger and a +1 trait bonus on Swim checks. Swim is always a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on damage rolls with a dagger

divine denier.name: Divine Denier
divine denier.description:
You can’t bring yourself to accept the authority of the gods, even though friends might hound you relentlessly to change your ways. Perhaps you’re a Rahadoumi objecting on ideological grounds, or perhaps you just want to be left alone.

Benefits: Your doubt causes you to shrug off divine magic more quickly than others. Once per day when you are affected by a divine spell, you may reduce that spell’s duration to half (minimum 1 round) for you alone.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, when you are affected by a divine spell, you may reduce that spell’s duration to half (minimum 1 round) for you alone.

trade talk (half-elf, human-raised).name: Trade Talk (Half-Elf, Human-Raised)
trade talk (half-elf, human-raised).description:
You picked up not only the basics of your human parent’s profession, but also the lingo typical of the craft.

Requirements: Half-Elf, Human-Raised

Benefits: Choose a Craft or Profession skill. You gain a +1 trait bonus on checks with the chosen skill, and a +1 trait bonus on Bluff and Sense Motive checks when dealing with people in the context of that craft or profession.

Usage

Select one Craft or Profession skill when you take this trait.

Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the chosen skill.

Update the target of the change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

Update the text of the notes within the <> on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus when dealing with people in the context of <your chosen craft or profession skill>.
+[[1]] Trait bonus when dealing with people in the context of <your chosen craft or profession skill>.

sacred touch.name: Sacred Touch
sacred touch.description:
You were exposed to a potent source of positive energy as a child, perhaps by being born under the right cosmic sign, or maybe because one of your parents was a gifted healer.

Benefits: As a standard action, you may automatically stabilize a dying creature merely by touching it.

seafaring reputation.name: Seafaring Reputation
seafaring reputation.description:
The Wave Riders’ fame helps you convince seafarers to cooperate.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy and Intimidate checks against sailors and residents of Absalom.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus vs sailors and Absalom residents.
+[[1]] Trait bonus vs sailors and Absalom residents.

spirit talker (mwangi shamanistic faith).name: Spirit Talker (Mwangi shamanistic faith)
spirit talker (mwangi shamanistic faith).description:
You are a devotee of the shamanistic practices of the Mwangi, having little use for the deities of the colonials.

Requirements: Mwangi shamanistic faith

Benefits: You gain a +1 racial bonus on Will saves against divine spells and spell-like effects employed against you by clerics or paladins of Aroden, Abadar, Iomedae, Gozreh, and Shelyn but you suffer a -1 penalty on Will saves against divine spells cast by devotees of Mwangi faiths.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] racial bonus against divine spells and spell-like effects employed against you by clerics or paladins of Aroden, Abadar, Iomedae, Gozreh, and Shelyn.
-[[1]] penalty  against divine spells cast by devotees of Mwangi faiths.

oregent vandal (andoran; oregent).name: Oregent Vandal (Andoran; Oregent)
oregent vandal (andoran; oregent).description:
You find it quite easy to apply your carefully honed instincts for building and repairing to destruction instead.

Requirements: Oregent in Andoran

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on damage rolls made against targets with hardness. This bonus does not apply to attacks that ignore hardness.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on damage rolls made against targets with hardness. This bonus does not apply to attacks that ignore hardness.

wild stride (elf).name: Wild Stride (Elf)
wild stride (elf).description:
Requirements: Elf

Benefits: Choose a favored terrain from those listed in the ranger @UUID[Compendium.pf1.class-abilities.Item.U2YBSx1s8cT8iDq1]{favored terrain} class ability. You can move through naturally occurring difficult terrain without any penalty while within your favored terrain. Terrain created or modified by magic or traps affects you normally.

besmara's name (besmara).name: Besmara's Name (Besmara)
besmara's name (besmara).description:
Besmara sometimes answers your call.

Requirements: Besmara

Benefits: Once per day when casting a divine spell, you can speak or whisper her name in place of using a divine focus.

void child (diaspora).name: Void Child (Diaspora)
void child (diaspora).description:
You were raised in the Diaspora and are accustomed to being isolated from most other life in the vast void of space.

Requirements: Diaspora

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on saves against fear; this bonus increases to +2 whenever you are 30 feet or farther from your nearest ally.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against fear; this bonus increases to +2 whenever you are 30 feet or farther from your nearest ally.

wealthy dabbler (taldor).name: Wealthy Dabbler (Taldor)
wealthy dabbler (taldor).description:
You study magic at one of Taldor's many social clubs, wowing your friends with your expertise in the simplest of magical exploits.

Requirements: Taldor

Benefits: Select two non-harmful arcane cantrips. You can cast these two cantrips once per day each (caster level 1st). If you have levels in a class that can cast these cantrips, your caster level for these cantrips is equal to that class level.

child of two peoples (half-elf, half-orc.name: Child of Two Peoples (Half-Elf, Half-Orc, Human)
child of two peoples (half-elf, half-orc.description:
Your parents come from two visibly different cultures that maintain distinct identities regardless of their physical proximity (such as Tians and Ulfen). The tug of conflicting expectations has strengthened your sense of individuality and given you a gift for languages.

Requirements: Half-Elf, Half-Orc, or Human

Benefits: You receive a +2 trait bonus on Will saves against charm and compulsion effects, and you automatically know the racial or ethnic languages of both your parents. 

Usage: Determine the racial or ethnic languages of both your parents when you take this trait.

Update the Granted Proficiencies section Languages, on the details tab of this trait, to reflect these options.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against charm and compulsion effects.

gesture expertise.name: Gesture Expertise
gesture expertise.description:
You are adept at nonverbal communication.

Benefits: You can use a sign language or gestural system to send complex messages quickly in combat, and with a successful DC 15 Linguistics check, you can express yourself clearly enough to communicate basic messages to people who do not understand your gestures. You gain a +2 trait bonus to send secret messages via gestures using the Bluff skill, and to understand gestures with the Linguistics skill.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to understand gestures. With a successful DC 15 Linguistics check, you can express yourself clearly enough to communicate basic messages to people who do not understand your gestures.
+[[2]] Trait bonus to send secret messages via gestures.

asmodean acolyte (hell's vengeance).name: Asmodean Acolyte (Hell's Vengeance)
asmodean acolyte (hell's vengeance).description:
You grew up in the church of Asmodeus, and have served as a faithful follower your entire life. You’ve studied the unholy texts, committed the rigid hierarchies of Hell to memory, and striven to eliminate emotion and mercy from your mind in emulation of the Prince of Darkness. You may have continued your career in the church, officially joining the priesthood and aspiring to advance to positions of higher authority and greater power, or you may have left the church, deciding the life of a priest was not for you. In either case, the education you received in the church has remained with you.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy, Knowledge (planes), and Knowledge (religion) checks, and one of these skills (your choice) is a class skill for you. You also know the Infernal tongue (this does not count toward your number of languages).

Usage: Select Diplomacy, Knowledge (planes), or Knowledge (religion) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

tianjing temple guard (tianjing).name: Tianjing Temple Guard (Tianjing)
tianjing temple guard (tianjing).description:
You were a stalwart guard of one of Tianjing's magnificent temple cities, and your resolve proved to be of great use during bandit raids.

Requirements: Tianjing

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attacks of opportunity made with polearm weapons.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus on attacks of opportunity made with polearm weapons.

bifurcated magic (half-elf).name: Bifurcated Magic (Half-Elf)
bifurcated magic (half-elf).description:
You’ve studied the methods of both elven and human spellcasters.

Requirements: Half-Elf

Benefits: Pick two classes when you gain this trait.

Your caster level in each of those classes is treated as 1 higher as long as this doesn’t raise your caster level above your total Hit Dice.

This doesn’t stack with any similar increase from traits such as Magical Knack.

Usage: Pick two spellcasting classes when you gain this trait.

Once you have levels in at least two classes, you may add 1 to the caster level of either, or both if applicable, of your chosen classes.

To do so, go to the Spells tab of your actor, select the appropriate spellbook tab, edit by clicking on the button that looks like two gears, enter a 1 in the "Caster Level Bonus Formula"

shenmen prospector (shenmen).name: Shenmen Prospector (Shenmen)
shenmen prospector (shenmen).description:
You were a talented laborer in the silver mines of Shenmen, and have seen many wonders in the haunted caverns beneath the surface of the cursed nation.

Requirements: Shenmen

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Appraise and Knowledge (dungeoneering) checks.

bountiful herb-lore (cassomir).name: Bountiful Herb-Lore (Cassomir)
bountiful herb-lore (cassomir).description:
You have shown a significant talent for the magical arts, gaining the attention of the famed Acadamae of Korvosa.

Requirements: Cassomir

Benefits: Once per day after 10 minutes of foraging, you can sprinkle nutritious herbs into a magical consumable item (such as a potion of cure light wounds). If the item is consumed within 1 minute, it provides nourishment as if it were a normal meal to the creature that partakes of it.

contextNotes.text: After 10 minutes of foraging, you can sprinkle nutritious herbs into a magical consumable item. Doing so will provide nourishment as if it were a normal meal if consumed within 1 minute.

death's deputy (tiefling; grimspawn).name: Death's Deputy (Tiefling; Grimspawn)
death's deputy (tiefling; grimspawn).description:
When you send opponents to death's door, you often hurl their corpses right across its threshold.

Requirements: Grimspawn Tiefling Heritage

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on the damage dealt for any attack that would already reduce your target to negative hit points without this trait.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus to damage for any attack that would already reduce your target to negative hit points without this trait.

pattern seeker (hell's rebels).name: Pattern Seeker (Hell's Rebels)
pattern seeker (hell's rebels).description:
There are patterns in the world, both natural and artificial, that if only one can interpret them correctly, great secrets could be divined. You have long been fascinated by the idea of these hidden patterns, perhaps because a sibling or parent went to the grave obsessed with seeking a pattern, or maybe because you feel that you’ve uncovered a previously unknown pattern. Kintargo has a particularly unique pattern of its own; the belfry atop the Temple of Asmodeus rings at what seem to be random intervals. None know who or what rings the bells, and no true pattern by which the so-called Devil’s Bells has yet accurately predicted the tolling. Many have tried, and extensive but always incomplete documents exist that track the dates and times of recorded ringings back to the end of the Chelish Civil War, when the church of Asmodeus first claimed the abandoned temple of Aroden as their own. Maybe you will be the one to solve the pattern of the Devil’s Bells?

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on all Perception checks, and Perception is always a class skill for you. In addition, you increase the save DC of any illusion (pattern) spell you cast by 1, and you gain a +1 trait bonus on all saving throws against illusion effects.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus against illusion effects.
+[[1]] bonus to the save DC of any illusion (pattern) spell you cast.

valashmai veteran (valashmai jungle).name: Valashmai Veteran (Valashmai Jungle)
valashmai veteran (valashmai jungle).description:
You have traveled to the Valashmai Jungle on numerous occasions, and your prowess in traversing the jungle wilderness makes you a formidable guide and explorer.

Requirements: Valashmai Jungle

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Perception checks, and a +1 trait bonus on Survival checks in jungle terrain. One of these skills becomes a class skill for you.

Usage: Select Perception or Survival when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus in jungle terrain.

merchant.name: Merchant
merchant.description:
You lived your life as a merchant, buying and selling goods.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on any Appraise and Sense Motive checks you attempt while bargaining for the price of goods. Appraise is always a class skill for you.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus while bargaining for the price of goods.
+[[1]] Trait bonus while bargaining for the price of goods.

anatomist.name: Anatomist
anatomist.description:
You have studied the workings of anatomy, either as a student at university or as an apprentice mortician or necromancer.

Benefits: You know where to aim your blows to strike vital organs and you gain a +1 trait bonus on all rolls made to confirm critical hits.

local ties (iron gods).name: Local Ties (Iron Gods)
local ties (iron gods).description:
You have ties to a prominent local in the town of Torch—the missing wizard Khonnir Baine. If you’re a wizard, alchemist, or other scholarly type, he may have been your tutor or teacher. If you’re of a more martial bent, Khonnir could instead have been a friend or business associate of your mother, father, or patron. He may even have been your adoptive father, in which case you likely have a bond of friendship or rivalry with his adopted daughter Val (your GM has more information on her in this case if you wish to know more for your character’s background).

Benefits: Your association with Khonnir has given you insight into how technology works. Choose Disable Device or Knowledge (engineering). You gain a +1 trait bonus on checks with this skill, and it is a class skill for you. In addition, you are treated as if you possessed the Technologist feat for the purposes of resolving checks associated with that skill. If you gain the Technologist feat, your trait bonus for the selected skill increases to +3.

Usage

  1. Select Disable Device or Knowledge (engineering) when you take this trait.

  2. Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

  3. Update the target of the Context note on the changes tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

If you gain the Technologist feat, update the change on the Changes tab associated with the selected skill and change the bonus value from 1 to 3, then delete the context note.

contextNotes.text: You are treated as if you possessed the Technologist feat for the purposes of resolving checks associated with this skill. If you gain the Technologist feat, your trait bonus for the this skill increases to +3. (currently +1)

treasure mapper (ilizmagorti).name: Treasure Mapper (Ilizmagorti)
treasure mapper (ilizmagorti).description:
You’ve spent a great deal of time hunting down and following treasure maps—most false, but a few with legitimate information.

Requirements: Ilizmagorti

Benefits: You gain a +2 bonus on Survival or Profession (sailor) checks made to navigate using a map or chart, and one of them becomes a class skill for you.

Usage

Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add Profession (sailor).

Update the target of the context note on the Changes tab of this trait to Profession (sailor).

Select Survival or Profession (sailor) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] bonus on checks made to navigate using a map or chart.
+[[2]] bonus on checks made to navigate using a map or chart.

wharf rat (ostenso).name: Wharf Rat (Ostenso)
wharf rat (ostenso).description:
You may never have been on a boat larger than a garbage scow, but you've been helping them dock all your life.

Requirements: Ostenso

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on skill checks involving climbing and rope use, and a +4 trait bonus on Appraise checks to determine the value of large quantities of bulk goods without opening their containers.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus on skill checks involving climbing and rope use.
+[[4]] Trait bonus to determine the value of large quantities of bulk goods without opening their containers.

prehensile whip.name: Prehensile Whip
prehensile whip.description:
In your hand a whip has a life of its own, wrapping around pillars and beams with ease.

Benefits: You can use a whip as if it were a rope with a grappling hook at the end. Attaching your whip is a standard action, but detaching it is a full-round action.

wronged (gyronna).name: Wronged (Gyronna)
wronged (gyronna).description:
Somewhere in your past, you were taken advantage of because of your gender.

Requirements: Gyronna

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Sense Motive checks against male creatures of your race, and the DC for men to intimidate you increases by 2.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against male creatures of your race.

carnal attraction.name: Carnal Attraction
carnal attraction.description:
Benefits: You know how to ask the wrong questions the right way, granting you a +2 trait bonus on Diplomacy (gather information) and Knowledge (local) checks regarding whichever vice fascinates you: drugs, forbidden lore, gambling, illegal fighting, or a similar debauchery or indecency.

Usage: In Changes tab, change [vice] to your chosen vice.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus while gathering information regarding [vice]
+[[2]] Trait bonus regarding [vice]

elemental endowment (osirion).name: Elemental Endowment (Osirion)
elemental endowment (osirion).description:
You see the interactions between elementals as the epitome of balance; you’ve studied the traditional elemental bindings of Ancient Osirion and can draw upon their power.

Requirements: Osirion

Benefits: As an immediate action, once per day when you cast a spell with the air, earth, fire, or water descriptor, you can gain a number of temporary hit points equal to the spell’s level. This effect lasts for 1 minute.

contextNotes.text: As an immediate action, once per day when you cast a spell with the air, earth, fire, or water descriptor, you can gain a number of temporary hit points equal to the spell’s level. This effect lasts for 1 minute. ([[@resources.elementalEndowmentOsirion.value]] remaining uses)

blessed of the norns (lands of the linno.name: Blessed of the Norns (Lands of the Linnorm Kings)
blessed of the norns (lands of the linno.description:
You or one of your parents belonged to a coven that honored the norns—powerful fey seers—and you still bear their favor in the form of a subtle sense for danger.

Requirements: Lands of the Linnorm Kings

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Perception checks to notice traps and ambushes, and a +1 trait bonus to your AC against traps and during any surprise round in which you are caught unaware.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus  to notice traps and ambushes.
+[[1]] Trait bonus to your AC against traps, and during any surprise round in which you are caught unaware.

darklands trader.name: Darklands Trader
darklands trader.description:
You’re familiar with Nar- Voth’s trade routes and negotiation involving the denizens of the Darklands.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Diplomacy checks relating to subterranean trade and on Knowledge (dungeoneering) checks relating to subterranean creatures. Select one of these skills to be a class skill for you.

Usage: Select Diplomacy or Knowledge (dungeoneering) when you take this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the details tab of this trait to reflect your choice.

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Trait bonus relating to subterranean trade.
+[[1]] Trait bonus relating to subterranean creatures.

cliff-master (varisia; goblin).name: Cliff-Master (Varisia; Goblin)
cliff-master (varisia; goblin).description:
You are adept at scavenging along the cliffs by the sea, and have become a skilled climber.

Requirements: Goblin; Varisia

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on all Climb and Survival checks.

guarded (merfolk).name: Guarded (Merfolk)
guarded (merfolk).description:
You grew up believing air-breathers are not to be trusted.

Requirements: Merfolk

Benefits: You gain a +2 trait bonus on Bluff and Sense Motive checks against non-aquatic humanoids.

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Trait bonus against non-aquatic humanoids.
+[[2]] Trait bonus against non-aquatic humanoids.

touched by the sky (numeria).name: Touched by the Sky (Numeria)
touched by the sky (numeria).description:
Somehow, you managed to retrieve a rare vial of fluid from a piece of crashed wreckage on the plains of Numeria. Before you could be caught with the contraband, you drank it.

Requirements: Numeria

Benefits: You now have the ability to stabilize a dying creature with a touch as a standard action.

lamasaran performer (thuvia).name: Lamasaran Performer (Thuvia)
lamasaran performer (thuvia).description:
You are one of the famed performers of Lamasara, and your skill is known throughout Thuvia.

Requirements: Thuvia

Benefits: Once per day when you start a bardic performance, you can increase the DC of the saving throw to resist your performance’s effects by 1. This effect lasts until you switch performances or end your performance.

privileged slave (kaoling).name: Privileged Slave (Kaoling)
privileged slave (kaoling).description:
You were enslaved by the hobgoblins of Kaoling, and obeyed your masters dutifully, turning your back on comrades in order to find favor with your slavers - all the while plotting your escape.

Requirements: Kaoling

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on Bluff checks, and Bluff becomes a class skill for you.

unstable mutagen.name: Unstable Mutagen
unstable mutagen.description:
You discovered or were given a secret to make your mutagens more unstable—but also more potent.

Benefits: Once per day, you can create an unstable mutagen. It is like a normal mutagen in most ways, but also gains a single benefit or hindrance due to its instability. Roll 1d6 to determine the result of the instability.

d6

Result

1

The unstable mutagen lasts for 5 minutes per alchemist level.

2

The unstable mutagen lasts for 20 minutes per alchemist level.

3

The unstable mutagen does not grant a natural armor bonus.

4

The natural armor bonus granted by the mutagen increases by 2.

5

The penalty that the mutagen applies to the corresponding mental ability score increases by 2.

6

The unstable mutagen does not apply a penalty to the corresponding mental ability score.

dismantle order.name: Dismantle Order
dismantle order.description:
If you follow this code, your appreciation for unfettered social freedom allows you to locate supporters of law with supernatural ease.

Requirements: Code of Revolution

Benefits: Once per day, you can use detect law as a spell-like ability (caster level 1st). If you have levels in a class that can cast detect law, your caster level for this spell-like ability is equal to that class level.

Usage: to take this trait you must follow the Revolution Knightly code of conduct.

If a knight violates their code of conduct, they are disgraced and must perform penance to atone for their transgressions. until the penance is completed, disgraced knights no longer receive benefits from their knightly traits.

Revolution: Knights who follow the code of revolution believe that strict laws are an unnecessary bulwark standing in the way of social progress. According to these knights, intelligent and civilized people are inherently good-natured, and if left to their own devices, individuals would prove more wholesome and attuned to the natural order of things than when tainted by the unnatural strictures of organized society.

Their vows are as follows.

  • The wicked must be punished, though not by the hands of corrupted officials or easily swayed politicians.

  • I shall not let stand acts of unfairness performed by governments or their harbingers. Those who contribute to an

  • organized system of cruelty shall feel my wrath.

  • All prisoners, regardless of whether they were convicted or not, deserve a second chance.

  • Tribal or lawless societies exemplify the natural order, and under my watch shall not come to harm from those who would seek to rule them.

  • Those creatures who live free shall see no harm fall to them by myself or my sword.

  • I shall not place unnecessary value on material possessions, as such things are fleeting, whereas deeds are immortal.

dominator (belkzen).name: Dominator (Belkzen)
dominator (belkzen).description:
The blood of dominance runs thick in the savage home of orcs.

Requirements: Belkzen

Benefits: You receive a +2 trait bonus on all attempts to demoralize an opponent in combat using the Intimidate skill.

contextNotes.text:
+[[2]] Trait bonus 
to demoralize an opponent in combat.

cross-knowledge.name: Cross-Knowledge
cross-knowledge.description: Benefits: Once per day, you may treat an extract you drink as if your caster level were 1 level higher as long as that extract appears on the wizard spell list and the alchemist list of formulae.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you may treat an extract based on a spell that appears on both the wizard and alchemist spell lists with a +[[1]] bonus to your CL.

black powder bravado.name: Black Powder Bravado
black powder bravado.description:
You are particularly successful when using deeds.

Benefits: Once per day, when you perform a deed that requires an attack roll and you miss with that roll, you can reroll it. You must take the second result even if it is worse.

crusader.name: Crusader
crusader.description:
From studying “Prime Commander” and reading accounts of the battles in the Worldwound, you have trained to be a crusader against demonic forces.

Benefits: You gain a +1 trait bonus on attack rolls against outsiders with the chaotic subtype.

contextNotes.text: +[1]] Trait bonus against outsiders with the chaotic subtype.

faith's hunter.name: Faith's Hunter
faith's hunter.description:
Divine powers smile upon you each time you grievously wound a favored enemy.

Benefits: When you confirm a critical hit against a @UUID[Compendium.pf1.class-abilities.Item.XmALhWqXdN9yOmtd]{favored enemy}, you may extend the duration of any divine spell affecting you by 1 round.

The benefits from multiple critical hits stack.

Divine spells with an instantaneous duration can’t be extended in this manner.

contextNotes.text: When you confirm a critical hit against a favored enemy, you may extend the duration of any divine spell affecting you by 1 round. The benefits from multiple critical hits stack. Divine spells with an instantaneous duration can’t be extended in this manner.

chance encounter (wrath of the righteous.name: Chance Encounter (Wrath of the Righteous)
chance encounter (wrath of the righteous.description:
You always tended to get in over your head as a child, but your biggest youthful misadventure was the time you “accidentally” found yourself behind enemy lines in the Worldwound. You probably never would have made it back home to Kenabres if not for the help of a mysterious woman who helped you trick your way through a group of cultists. The woman never told you her name, but you remember her beauty and a deep sense of sadness she seemed to carry with her. Her skill with the bow was impressive as well, but the thing you remember most about her was the symbol of Desna she wore—she often held onto it without seeming to realize it, as if the connection to the goddess was something she clung to in a sense of need, as someone might clutch at a rope while dangling over a vast pit. She left your side a few moments before you were picked up by a patrol of crusaders, who finished the job of escorting you back to safety, and you’ve never seen her again. Ever since, you’ve just been lucky when it comes to trickery.

Benefits: Once per day, if you fail an Acrobatics, Bluff, Disguise, Sleight of Hand, or Stealth check, you may immediately reroll that check as a free action. You must take the second result, even if it is worse.

Associated Mythic Path

Trickster

Multiple Characters

You and other characters were all saved by the same mysterious woman in your childhood. This could have been the same event, or she could have saved you on separate occasions—the discovery that someone else was saved by the mysterious stranger has resulted in a long friendship (or perhaps friendly rivalry) with the others she rescued.

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you may immediately reroll a failed check as a free action. You must take the second result.  ([[@resources.chanceEncounterWrathOfTheRighteous.value]] remaining uses)
Once per day, you may immediately reroll a failed check as a free action. You must take the second result.  ([[@resources.chanceEncounterWrathOfTheRighteous.value]] remaining uses)
